sql: SELECT * FROM storm;

sql: SELECT Region_name, Region_code FROM region;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, providing a complete list of all regions with their full name and associated region code.

sql: SELECT Name, Number_Deaths FROM storm WHERE Number_Deaths > 50;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of storms that caused more than 50 deaths, along with the specific number of fatalities for each.

sql: SELECT Name, Max_speed FROM storm ORDER BY Max_speed DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each storm's name and its maximum speed, ordered from the highest speed to the lowest.

sql: SELECT * FROM storm WHERE Name = 'One';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete record, including ID, active dates, max speed, and impact data, for the specific storm named 'One'.

sql: SELECT Region_name FROM region WHERE Region_code = 'AL';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the full name of the region identified by the region code 'AL'.

sql: SELECT * FROM storm WHERE Damage_millions_USD = 13.0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows with all recorded details for storms that caused exactly $13 million in damages.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Number_Deaths = 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of storms that resulted in no deaths.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Max_speed FROM storm;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows showing the unique maximum speeds recorded for storms.

sql: SELECT * FROM region LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows providing a sample of regions, listing the region ID, code, and name for each.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Number_city_affected FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each storm by name, along with the number of cities impacted for each region affected by that storm.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name, T2.Number_city_affected FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each region's name and the corresponding number of cities affected within that region.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Region_id) FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each storm by name along with the total number of regions it affected.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name, SUM(T2.Number_city_affected) FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id GROUP BY T1.Region_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each region name along with the total number of cities affected within that region.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of storms that affected the region with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id WHERE T2.Storm_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the regions that were affected by the storm with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Max_speed, T1.Number_Deaths FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Number_city_affected > 10;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name, maximum speed, and number of deaths for storms that affected more than 10 cities.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id GROUP BY T1.Region_name HAVING COUNT(T2.Storm_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of regions that have been affected by at least one storm.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Damage_millions_USD FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and financial damage (in millions of USD) for any storm that affected the specific region identified by Region ID 3.

sql: SELECT T1.Storm_ID, AVG(T2.Number_city_affected) FROM affected_region AS T2 JOIN storm AS T1 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Storm_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing the average number of cities affected for each storm, identified by its Storm ID.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Region_name, T3.Number_city_affected FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T3 ON T1.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T2 ON T3.Region_id = T2.Region_id WHERE T1.Name = 'One';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the regions affected by the storm named 'One' and the number of cities that were impacted in each region.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID WHERE T3.Number_Deaths > 7;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a region affected by a storm that resulted in more than 7 fatalities.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Number_city_affected) FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each storm by name along with the total number of cities it affected.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all storms that have affected at least one region.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 WHERE T1.Region_id IN (SELECT Region_id FROM affected_region WHERE Storm_ID = 3);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the region affected by the storm with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Damage_millions_USD FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Damage_millions_USD > (SELECT AVG(Damage_millions_USD) FROM storm);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names and damage costs for storms that caused more financial damage than the average storm in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Region_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of storms that have impacted more than one region.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM affected_region AS T2 JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = T1.Region_id AND T3.Number_Deaths > 50);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a region that has been impacted by a storm which resulted in more than 50 deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region WHERE Region_id = 1) INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region WHERE Region_id = 2);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the storm that affected both the region with ID 1 and the region with ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region WHERE Region_id = 1) EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Number_Deaths > 0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a storm that affected Region 1 and resulted in zero deaths.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Number_Deaths > 0 UNION SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Damage_millions_USD > 10.75;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of storms that either caused any deaths or resulted in damages greater than 10.75 million USD.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Number_Deaths FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Number_Deaths = (SELECT MAX(Number_Deaths) FROM storm);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the deadliest storm event, showing its name and the corresponding number of deaths.

sql: SELECT T2.Region_name FROM affected_region AS T1 JOIN region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id WHERE T1.Storm_ID = (SELECT Storm_ID FROM storm ORDER BY Max_speed DESC LIMIT 1);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM affected_region AS T2 WHERE T2.Storm_ID = T1.Storm_ID);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of storms that are not associated with any affected regions.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 LEFT JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id WHERE T2.Storm_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of regions that have not been affected by any recorded storms.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Region_name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T1.Number_Deaths > (SELECT AVG(Number_Deaths) FROM storm);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a storm and the region it affected, specifically for storms that caused an above-average number of deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name, SUM(T3.Damage_millions_USD) AS TotalDamage FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Region_name ORDER BY TotalDamage DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each region alongside the total storm damage it has incurred (in millions of USD), ordered from the highest total damage to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region GROUP BY Storm_ID HAVING SUM(Number_city_affected) > 20);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of storms that have affected a total of more than 20 cities.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 WHERE T1.Region_id IN (SELECT Region_id FROM affected_region WHERE Number_city_affected > (SELECT AVG(Number_city_affected) FROM affected_region));
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of regions where the number of affected cities is greater than the average across all regions.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Max_speed FROM storm AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Storm_ID, SUM(Number_city_affected) AS TotalCities FROM affected_region GROUP BY Storm_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.TotalCities > 20;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and maximum speed of storms that have impacted more than 20 cities.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T3.Region_name = 'Algeria';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the storm that affected the region of Algeria.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Damage_millions_USD FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region WHERE Region_id IN (SELECT Region_id FROM region WHERE Region_name = 'Afghanistan'));
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and damage in millions of USD for storms that affected the 'Afghanistan' region.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM affected_region AS T2 WHERE T2.Storm_ID = T1.Storm_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows with the names of storms that have affected more than one region.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Number_Deaths FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Number_Deaths > (SELECT Number_Deaths FROM storm WHERE Name = 'Two');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and total number of deaths for storms that caused more fatalities than the storm named 'Two'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Max_speed = 972 AND Damage_millions_USD > 10.75 UNION SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Number_Deaths = 0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of storms that were either exceptionally powerful and damaging or resulted in zero deaths.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T3.Region_code = 'AF' OR T3.Region_code = 'DZ';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of storms that have affected regions with the specific region codes 'AF' or 'DZ'.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name, MAX(T3.Number_Deaths) FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Region_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each region alongside the maximum death toll from a single storm that has affected it.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Damage_millions_USD > 10 AND T1.Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region WHERE Region_id = 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a storm that affected the region with ID 1 and caused over $10 million in damages.

sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT T1.Name, T3.Region_name, T1.Number_Deaths FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T2.Region_id) AS T4 WHERE T4.Number_Deaths > 5;
result_summary: Returns 26 rows listing the regions affected by severe storms (those causing more than five deaths), showing the storm's name, the region's name, and the total number of deaths attributed to that storm.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 WHERE T1.Region_id IN (SELECT Region_id FROM affected_region) EXCEPT SELECT T2.Region_name FROM region AS T2 JOIN affected_region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id JOIN storm AS T4 ON T3.Storm_ID = T4.Storm_ID WHERE T4.Number_Deaths > 0;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT People_ID, Name, Height, Weight FROM people WHERE Name = 'Jack Campbell';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the ID, height, and weight for the individual named 'Jack Campbell'.

sql: SELECT * FROM body_builder WHERE Snatch > 140.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing all recorded details for a bodybuilder who can snatch over 140.0.

sql: SELECT Name, Birth_Place FROM people ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing people's names and their corresponding birthplaces, sorted alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT Body_Builder_ID, Total FROM body_builder ORDER BY Total DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each bodybuilder's ID and their total score, ordered from the highest total score to the lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Birth_Place FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each unique birthplace recorded for people in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people WHERE Height = 192.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and height for any person with a height of exactly 192.0.

sql: SELECT * FROM body_builder WHERE Total = 315.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all available details for bodybuilders who achieved a total lift weight of exactly 315.0.

sql: SELECT Name, Weight FROM people WHERE Birth_Place = 'Anchorage, Alaska';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and weight of a person born in 'Anchorage, Alaska'.

sql: SELECT People_ID, Name FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique ID and full name for each person.

sql: SELECT Body_Builder_ID, Snatch, Clean_Jerk FROM body_builder;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each bodybuilder's ID along with their corresponding Snatch and Clean & Jerk lift weights.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of people who are bodybuilders, along with their corresponding total score or weight.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Snatch, T2.Clean_Jerk FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Al Montoya';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the snatch and clean & jerk lift values for the specific bodybuilder named 'Al Montoya'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Total > 315.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and weight of any bodybuilder whose total lift score is greater than 315.0.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all people in the dataset.

sql: SELECT AVG(Weight) FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the calculated average weight for all people in the dataset.

sql: SELECT MAX(Total) FROM body_builder;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the maximum 'Total' value found across all bodybuilder records.

sql: SELECT Birth_Place, COUNT(*) FROM people GROUP BY Birth_Place;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each unique birthplace and the total count of people from that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Birth_Place, AVG(T2.Total) FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Birth_Place;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each unique birthplace alongside the average competition total for bodybuilders from that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Birth_Place, COUNT(T2.Body_Builder_ID) FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Birth_Place HAVING COUNT(T2.Body_Builder_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each birthplace that has produced at least one bodybuilder, along with the total count of bodybuilders from that location.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Weight > (SELECT AVG(Weight) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of people whose weight is greater than the average weight of all people.

sql: SELECT Body_Builder_ID FROM body_builder WHERE Total < (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM body_builder);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID of the bodybuilder whose total score is below the average total score across all bodybuilders.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T1.Height DESC LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and heights of the two tallest bodybuilders, ordered from highest to lowest height.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Weight / (T1.Height * T1.Height) AS WH_Ratio FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of people identified as bodybuilders, along with their calculated weight-to-height squared ratio.

sql: SELECT MIN(Snatch), MAX(Snatch) FROM body_builder;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the minimum and maximum snatch weights recorded across all bodybuilders.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Height), T1.Birth_Place FROM people AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Birth_Place;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each unique birthplace along with the average height of people born there.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Birth_Place FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT T2.People_ID FROM body_builder AS T2 WHERE T2.Total = 315.0);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and birthplace of bodybuilders who have a total score of exactly 315.0.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.Weight = (SELECT MAX(T2.Weight) FROM people AS T2 WHERE T2.Birth_Place = T1.Birth_Place);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying the heaviest person from each birthplace, showing their name and weight.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Birth_Place FROM people AS T1 JOIN (SELECT People_ID, AVG(Total) AS AvgTotal FROM body_builder GROUP BY People_ID) AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.AvgTotal > 315.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and birthplace of bodybuilders whose average total score is greater than 315.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM body_builder WHERE body_builder.People_ID = people.People_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all people who are also identified as bodybuilders.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height > 182.0 UNION SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Weight > 80.0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of people who are either taller than 182.0 or weigh more than 80.0.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height > 182.0 INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Weight >= 90.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of people who are both taller than 182.0 and weigh 90.0 or more.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height > 180.0 EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Weight > 80.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a person who is taller than 180.0 but weighs 80.0 or less.

sql: SELECT Birth_Place FROM people GROUP BY Birth_Place HAVING AVG(Weight) > (SELECT AVG(Weight) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing birthplaces where the average weight of people from that location is greater than the overall average weight across all people.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY T2.Total DESC) AS Rank FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows ranking each bodybuilder by their total score from highest to lowest, displaying their name, total score, and calculated rank.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T2.Total DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and total for the person with the second-highest body-building total.

sql: WITH AboveAverage AS (SELECT People_ID FROM body_builder WHERE Snatch > (SELECT AVG(Snatch) FROM body_builder) AND Clean_Jerk > (SELECT AVG(Clean_Jerk) FROM body_builder)) SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN AboveAverage AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of bodybuilders who perform above average in both the Snatch and Clean & Jerk lifts.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Snatch FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Birth_Place = 'Port Huron, Michigan' AND T2.Snatch > 140.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and snatch weight of a bodybuilder from 'Port Huron, Michigan' who can snatch more than 140.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Snatch > 140.0 UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Total = 315.0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of bodybuilders who can either snatch over 140.0 or have a total lift of exactly 315.0.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total, CASE WHEN T2.Total > 315.0 THEN 'Elite' ELSE 'Advanced' END AS Category FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each bodybuilder by name with their total score, classifying them as 'Elite' if their score is over 315 or 'Advanced' otherwise.

sql: SELECT Name, Height, Weight FROM people WHERE People_ID NOT IN (SELECT People_ID FROM body_builder);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name, height, and weight of a person who is not identified as a bodybuilder.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY (T2.Snatch + T2.Clean_Jerk) / T1.Weight DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the top pound-for-pound strongest bodybuilder, determined by ordering the ratio of their total lift weight (Snatch plus Clean and Jerk) to their body weight from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Total = (SELECT MAX(Total) FROM body_builder);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the bodybuilder with the highest total score, showing their name and that maximum score.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Snatch FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Snatch = (SELECT MIN(Snatch) FROM body_builder);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the body builder with the lowest snatch weight, showing their name and that minimum snatch value.

sql: SELECT MAX(Total) - MIN(Total) AS Total_Range FROM body_builder;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the calculated range of the 'Total' score for bodybuilders, representing the difference between the highest and lowest values.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Snatch BETWEEN 137.5 AND 140.0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and total score for bodybuilders whose 'Snatch' lift is between 137.5 and 140.0.

sql: WITH PlaceAvg AS (SELECT Birth_Place, AVG(Weight) as AvgWeight FROM people GROUP BY Birth_Place) SELECT T1.Name, T1.Weight, T2.AvgWeight FROM people AS T1 JOIN PlaceAvg AS T2 ON T1.Birth_Place = T2.Birth_Place WHERE T1.Weight >= T2.AvgWeight;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing people whose weight is at or above the average for their birthplace, showing each person's name, their individual weight, and the calculated average weight for their birthplace.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height, T2.Total FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Height IN (SELECT Height FROM people GROUP BY Height HAVING COUNT(*) = 1);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing bodybuilders who have a unique height not shared by any other person, showing their name, height, and 'Total' value.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Birth_Place FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM body_builder) EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name, T2.Birth_Place FROM people AS T2 WHERE T2.Weight <= 80.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and birthplaces of bodybuilders who weigh more than 80.0.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Clean_Jerk FROM people AS T1, body_builder AS T2 WHERE T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID AND T2.Clean_Jerk > (SELECT AVG(Clean_Jerk) FROM body_builder);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and 'Clean & Jerk' lift for each bodybuilder whose lift is greater than the average across all competitors.

sql: SELECT T1.Birth_Place, SUM(T2.Total) as TotalLift FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Birth_Place HAVING SUM(T2.Total) > 315.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each birthplace where the combined total lift of its bodybuilders exceeds 315.0, along with that aggregated total lift amount.

sql: SELECT ID, name, salary FROM instructor WHERE dept_name = 'Cybernetics';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID, name, and salary for all instructors within the 'Cybernetics' department.

sql: SELECT course_id, title, dept_name FROM course WHERE credits = 4;
result_summary: Returns 92 rows listing the course ID, title, and department name for all courses that are worth exactly 4 credits.

sql: SELECT name, dept_name FROM student WHERE tot_cred = 100;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the name and department of each student who has earned exactly 100 total credits.

sql: SELECT dept_name, budget FROM department ORDER BY budget DESC;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing each department and its budget, ordered from the highest budget to the lowest.

sql: SELECT course_id, sec_id, building, room_number FROM section WHERE semester = 'Fall' AND year = 2010;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the location (building and room number) for each course section offered during the Fall 2010 semester.

sql: SELECT ID, course_id, semester, year FROM takes WHERE grade = 'A-';
result_summary: Returns 3268 rows identifying each student course enrollment that resulted in a grade of 'A-', showing the student ID, course ID, semester, and year for each instance.

sql: SELECT room_number, capacity FROM classroom WHERE building = 'Chandler';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the room number and capacity for all classrooms located in the 'Chandler' building.

sql: SELECT course_id, title FROM course WHERE dept_name = 'Mech. Eng.';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the course ID and title for all courses offered by the Mechanical Engineering ('Mech. Eng.') department.

sql: SELECT prereq_id FROM prereq WHERE course_id = '376';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the prerequisite course ID required for course '376'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.building FROM student AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name;
result_summary: Returns 2000 rows listing each student's name alongside the name of the building where their department is located.

sql: SELECT dept_name, COUNT(course_id) FROM course GROUP BY dept_name;
