sql: SELECT T1.Vehicle_Flight_number, T1.Date, T2.name AS airport_name, T3.name AS company_name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id JOIN operate_company AS T3 ON T1.company_id = T3.id;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, providing a log of each flight's vehicle flight number and date, along with the corresponding airport and operating company names.

sql: SELECT Pilot, MAX(Velocity) FROM flight GROUP BY Pilot;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each pilot along with their maximum recorded flight velocity.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.City, T2.Country FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Pilot = 'Thompson';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique cities and countries of airports where the pilot 'Thompson' has completed a flight.

sql: SELECT Type, AVG("Group_Equity_Shareholding") FROM operate_company GROUP BY Type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each company type along with the average group equity shareholding for that type.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Altitude > 0;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique names of airports associated with flights that are currently in the air (having an altitude greater than zero).

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.airport_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the operating company that has a flight associated with airport ID 1.

sql: SELECT T2.name, MIN(T1.Velocity), MAX(T1.Velocity) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the minimum and maximum recorded flight velocities for each airport.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.City FROM airport AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.airport_id FROM flight AS T2 WHERE T2.Pilot = 'Thompson');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and city for each airport that has been used for a flight by the pilot named 'Thompson'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Country = 'Netherlands';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of airline companies that operate flights involving airports located in the Netherlands.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.City, T1.Country FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id WHERE T2.Velocity = (SELECT MAX(Velocity) FROM flight);
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the name, city, and country of airports associated with flights that achieved the maximum recorded velocity.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.id) AS flight_count FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each operating company that has at least one flight, along with the total count of flights for that company.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name, T2.Type FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Altitude > (SELECT AVG(Altitude) FROM flight);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the unique names and types of airline companies that have operated flights at an altitude greater than the overall average altitude.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.airport_id) AS distinct_airports FROM flight AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Pilot;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each pilot alongside the total count of distinct airports they have flown to.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T3.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T1.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT id FROM flight ORDER BY Altitude DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the operating company and airport for the single flight with the highest recorded altitude.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM airport AS T1 WHERE T1.Country = 'Iceland' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM flight AS T2 WHERE T2.airport_id = T1.id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an airport in Iceland that has at least one recorded flight.

sql: SELECT T2.Type, AVG(T1.Velocity) AS avg_velocity FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.Type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each type of operating company along with the average flight velocity for that company type.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.company_id FROM flight AS T2 JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Country = 'Iceland' OR T3.Country = 'Netherlands');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of airline companies that operate flights associated with airports in either Iceland or the Netherlands.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T2.Country FROM airport AS T2 WHERE T2.id IN (SELECT T1.airport_id FROM flight AS T1 GROUP BY T1.airport_id HAVING COUNT(T1.id) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and country for each airport that has more than one flight associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id WHERE T1.Type = 'Joint Venture' EXCEPT SELECT T3.name FROM operate_company AS T3 JOIN flight AS T4 ON T3.id = T4.company_id WHERE T4.Altitude < 3000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of 'Joint Venture' companies whose flights all operate at an altitude of 3000 or higher.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM airport AS T1 JOIN (SELECT airport_id, COUNT(*) AS num FROM flight GROUP BY airport_id) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id ORDER BY T2.num DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the busiest airport, determined by finding the airport with the highest total number of associated flights.

sql: SELECT Pilot FROM flight WHERE airport_id = 1 INTERSECT SELECT Pilot FROM flight WHERE airport_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the pilot who has flown flights associated with both airport ID 1 and airport ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Vehicle_Flight_number, T1.Date FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id JOIN operate_company AS T3 ON T1.company_id = T3.id WHERE T2.Country = 'Netherlands' AND T3.Type = 'Joint Venture';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the flight number and date for all flights operated by 'Joint Venture' companies that are associated with an airport in the Netherlands.

sql: SELECT T2.Country, COUNT(T1.id) AS total_flights FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.Country;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each country along with the total count of flights associated with airports in that country.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 WHERE T1."Group_Equity_Shareholding" = (SELECT MAX("Group_Equity_Shareholding") FROM operate_company WHERE Incorporated_in = 'China');
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the operating company that has the maximum group equity shareholding among all companies incorporated in China.

sql: SELECT Pilot FROM flight WHERE Altitude = 0 INTERSECT SELECT Pilot FROM flight WHERE Altitude > 0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the pilot who has flight records both on the ground (altitude of 0) and in the air (altitude greater than 0).

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM flight AS T2 WHERE T2.company_id = T1.id);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of operating companies that have no recorded flights.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1."Group_Equity_Shareholding" FROM operate_company AS T1 WHERE T1."Group_Equity_Shareholding" > (SELECT AVG("Group_Equity_Shareholding") FROM operate_company);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names and group equity shareholding of companies whose shareholding percentage is above the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot, AVG(T1.Velocity) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Country = 'Netherlands' GROUP BY T1.Pilot;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each pilot and their average flight velocity for all flights associated with airports in the Netherlands.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id UNION SELECT T3.name FROM operate_company AS T3 WHERE T3.Type = 'Corporate';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the unique names of companies that either operate a flight or are classified as the 'Corporate' type.

sql: SELECT T1.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM flight AS T2 WHERE T2.airport_id = T1.id) AS flight_count FROM airport AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each airport's name and the total number of flights associated with that airport.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING MIN(T2.Altitude) = 0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an airline company that has operated at least one flight with a minimum altitude of zero.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Country FROM airport AS T1 JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT airport_id FROM flight WHERE Pilot = 'Thompson') AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names and countries of all unique airports associated with flights piloted by 'Thompson'.

sql: SELECT customer_name, customer_email FROM Customers WHERE payment_method_code = 'Credit Card';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names and email addresses of all customers who use a credit card as their payment method.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products ORDER BY product_price DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each product's name and price, sorted from the most expensive to the least expensive.

sql: SELECT supplier_name, supplier_phone FROM Suppliers WHERE supplier_name = 'Lidl';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and phone number for the supplier specifically named 'Lidl'.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Staff;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of staff members.

sql: SELECT store_name, store_phone, store_email FROM Department_Stores LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing a sample of department stores, providing their name, phone number, and email address.

sql: SELECT order_id, customer_id, order_date FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status_code = 'New';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying all new customer orders, showing the order ID, the associated customer ID, and the date the order was placed.

sql: SELECT T1.department_name, T2.store_name FROM Departments AS T1 JOIN Department_Stores AS T2 ON T1.dept_store_id = T2.dept_store_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each department's name and the name of the department store it belongs to.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT job_title_code FROM Staff_Department_Assignments;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique job title codes assigned to staff members.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_type_code = 'Clothes';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and price for all products that are classified as 'Clothes'.

sql: SELECT staff_name FROM Staff WHERE staff_gender = '1';
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the names of staff members belonging to a specific gender, identified by the code '1'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each customer's name alongside a count of the total number of orders they have placed.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T3.supplier_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T3 ON T2.supplier_id = T3.supplier_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows that map each product to its supplier, listing the product name alongside the corresponding supplier's name.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, COUNT(T1.order_item_id) FROM Order_Items AS T1 GROUP BY T1.order_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each order ID and the corresponding total count of items within that order.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_email FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'Completed';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and email for each unique customer who has placed at least one completed order.

sql: SELECT product_type_code, AVG(product_price) FROM Products GROUP BY product_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each product type code along with the average price for all products of that type.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name, T3.department_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Departments AS T3 ON T2.department_id = T3.department_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each staff member's name and the name of the department they are assigned to, creating a comprehensive list of all staff-department pairings.

sql: SELECT T1.supplier_name, T3.address_details FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Supplier_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each supplier's name alongside the details for all of their associated addresses.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 LEFT JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of customers who have never placed an order.

sql: SELECT T1.department_name FROM Departments AS T1 JOIN Department_Stores AS T2 ON T1.dept_store_id = T2.dept_store_id JOIN Department_Store_Chain AS T3 ON T2.dept_store_chain_id = T3.dept_store_chain_id WHERE T3.dept_store_chain_name = 'South';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT customer_name, payment_method_code FROM Customers WHERE payment_method_code IN ('Credit Card', 'Direct Debit');
result_summary: Returns 15 rows identifying customers who pay by either 'Credit Card' or 'Direct Debit', listing each customer's name and their specific payment method.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.job_title_code = 'Department Manager';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all staff members who are designated as Department Managers.

sql: SELECT T1.store_name, COUNT(T2.department_id) AS NumberOfDepartments FROM Department_Stores AS T1 JOIN Departments AS T2 ON T1.dept_store_id = T2.dept_store_id GROUP BY T1.store_name;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the department store name along with the total number of departments it contains.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.product_price FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T3 ON T2.supplier_id = T3.supplier_id WHERE T3.supplier_name = 'Tesco';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the product name and price for all products supplied by 'Tesco'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T4.product_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T1.customer_name = 'Ahmed';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing a product purchased by the customer named 'Ahmed'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T3.address_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing customer names and their corresponding address details, with a separate row for each address associated with a given customer.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T3.product_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id WHERE T1.payment_method_code = 'Credit Card' GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(T3.product_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing credit card customers who have purchased more than one product item, showing each customer's name and the total count of items they bought.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.product_price, T3.supplier_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T3 ON T2.supplier_id = T3.supplier_id ORDER BY T1.product_price DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top three most expensive products, showing each product's name, price, and the name of its supplier.

sql: SELECT T1.supplier_name, SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) AS TotalValue FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id GROUP BY T1.supplier_name ORDER BY TotalValue DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each supplier and the total value of all products purchased from them, ordered from the highest total purchase value to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.department_name, COUNT(T2.staff_id) AS SalesPersonCount FROM Departments AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id WHERE T2.job_title_code = 'Sales Person' GROUP BY T1.department_name HAVING COUNT(T2.staff_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing departments that contain more than one staff member with the job title 'Sales Person', along with the total count of those sales people.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_email FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_name = 'yellow jeans';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and email of the unique customer who purchased the product 'yellow jeans'.

sql: SELECT T1.dept_store_chain_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T4.staff_id) AS NumberOfStaff FROM Department_Store_Chain AS T1 JOIN Department_Stores AS T2 ON T1.dept_store_chain_id = T2.dept_store_chain_id JOIN Departments AS T3 ON T2.dept_store_id = T3.dept_store_id JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T4 ON T3.department_id = T4.department_id GROUP BY T1.dept_store_chain_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each department store chain name alongside the total count of unique staff members employed by that chain.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.customer_id FROM Customer_Addresses AS T2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the IDs of customers who have placed an order but do not have a corresponding address on file.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_price = (SELECT MAX(product_price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and price of the most expensive product.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name, T3.department_name, T4.store_name, T5.dept_store_chain_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Departments AS T3 ON T2.department_id = T3.department_id JOIN Department_Stores AS T4 ON T3.dept_store_id = T4.dept_store_id JOIN Department_Store_Chain AS T5 ON T4.dept_store_chain_id = T5.dept_store_chain_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each staff member's name along with their assigned department, store, and the parent department store chain.

sql: SELECT supplier_name FROM Suppliers WHERE supplier_id IN (SELECT supplier_id FROM Product_Suppliers GROUP BY supplier_id HAVING count(product_id) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of suppliers who provide more than one product.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T4.product_price) AS total_spent FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single highest-spending customer and the total amount they have spent, calculated by summing the prices of all products they have ordered.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, COUNT(T1.order_id) AS order_count FROM Order_Items AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_id HAVING COUNT(T1.order_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing products that have been ordered more than once, along with the total count of orders for each product.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_name IN ('red jeans', 'black jeans') GROUP BY T1.customer_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T4.product_name) = 2;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.staff_id FROM Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 JOIN Departments AS T3 ON T2.department_id = T3.department_id WHERE T3.department_name = 'purchasing' INTERSECT SELECT staff_id FROM Staff_Department_Assignments WHERE job_title_code = 'Department Manager') AS T4 ON T1.staff_id = T4.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of staff members who are department managers within the purchasing department.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_price > (SELECT AVG(product_price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names and prices of products that cost more than the average price of all products.

sql: SELECT T1.department_name, count(T2.staff_id) AS staff_count FROM Departments AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id GROUP BY T1.department_id ORDER BY staff_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the department with the most staff members, along with the total count of staff for that department.

sql: SELECT T3.customer_name, T4.supplier_name, T5.address_details FROM Customer_Addresses AS T1 JOIN Supplier_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id JOIN Suppliers AS T4 ON T2.supplier_id = T4.supplier_id JOIN Addresses AS T5 ON T1.address_id = T5.address_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying customers and suppliers who share a common address, listing the customer's name, the supplier's name, and the details of the shared location.

sql: SELECT payment_method_code, COUNT(*) AS num_customers FROM Customers GROUP BY payment_method_code ORDER BY num_customers DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most common payment method by counting the number of customers who use it.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.order_id, T1.order_date FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id WHERE T2.product_id IN (SELECT T3.product_id FROM Product_Suppliers AS T3 JOIN Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.supplier_id = T4.supplier_id WHERE T4.supplier_name = 'Lidl');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique order ID and date for all orders that contain at least one product supplied by 'Lidl'.

sql: SELECT order_status_code, COUNT(order_id), COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) FROM Customer_Orders GROUP BY order_status_code;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each order status along with the total number of orders and the count of unique customers associated with that status.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Orders) AND T1.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status_code != 'Completed');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of customers who have placed at least one order and all of their orders have a 'Completed' status.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name, T2.job_title_code, T3.department_name, T4.store_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Departments AS T3 ON T2.department_id = T3.department_id JOIN Department_Stores AS T4 ON T3.dept_store_id = T4.dept_store_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each staff member's name, their job title code, their assigned department, and the store where that department is located.

sql: SELECT supplier_name FROM Suppliers WHERE supplier_id NOT IN (SELECT T1.supplier_id FROM Product_Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.product_type_code = 'Clothes');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, MAX(T2.order_date) AS most_recent_order FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each customer's name alongside the date of their most recent order.

sql: SELECT T3.customer_name, AVG(T1.item_count) AS avg_items_per_order FROM (SELECT order_id, COUNT(product_id) AS item_count FROM Order_Items GROUP BY order_id) AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id GROUP BY T3.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer's name along with the average number of items they purchase per order.

sql: SELECT Artist, Age FROM artist WHERE Age > 22;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing artists older than 22, showing each artist's name and age.

sql: SELECT Song, Issue_Date FROM volume;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each song title and its corresponding issue date from the volume collection.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Artist FROM artist;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each unique artist.

sql: SELECT Music_Festival, Category FROM music_festival WHERE Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each music festival that received a nomination, along with the specific award category for which it was nominated.

sql: SELECT Famous_Title, Artist FROM artist WHERE Famous_Release_date = '2009';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the famous title and corresponding artist for all works that had a famous release date in 2009.

sql: SELECT Artist, Age FROM artist ORDER BY Age DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the oldest artists by age, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Category FROM music_festival;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique award categories available at the music festival.

sql: SELECT Song, Weeks_on_Top FROM volume WHERE Weeks_on_Top = 1.0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the songs that spent exactly one week at the top of the charts, along with the week count.

sql: SELECT Artist, Famous_Title FROM artist WHERE Age = 35;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the artist's name and famous title for all artists who are 35 years old.

sql: SELECT Music_Festival, Category FROM music_festival WHERE Date_of_ceremony = '18 February 2011';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing music festivals and their award categories for all ceremonies that occurred on February 18, 2011.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each song and its corresponding artist.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, COUNT(T2.Volume_ID) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artist along with the total count of volumes they have produced.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist HAVING COUNT(T2.Volume_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of artists who have contributed to more than one volume.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, SUM(T2.Weeks_on_Top) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artist along with the total number of weeks their volumes have been on top.

sql: SELECT T1.Song, T2.Music_Festival, T2.Result FROM volume AS T1 JOIN music_festival AS T2 ON T1.Volume_ID = T2.Volume;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing songs along with the music festival they participated in and their corresponding result, such as 'Awarded' or 'Nominated'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique artists whose musical volumes have received an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Age) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top > 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average age of artists whose volumes spent more than two weeks on the top charts.

sql: SELECT Artist FROM artist WHERE Artist_ID IN (SELECT Artist_ID FROM artist WHERE Famous_Release_date = 'November 2007');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the artist whose famous release was in November 2007.

sql: SELECT T1.Issue_Date, T1.Song FROM volume AS T1 JOIN music_festival AS T2 ON T1.Volume_ID = T2.Volume WHERE T2.Category = 'Best Song' AND T2.Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the issue date and song title for songs that were nominated for the 'Best Song' award.

sql: SELECT T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Artist = 'Gorgoroth';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing all the songs performed by the artist 'Gorgoroth'.

sql: SELECT Category, COUNT(*) FROM music_festival GROUP BY Category;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each music festival category and the total number of festivals within that category.

sql: SELECT Artist, Age FROM artist WHERE Age = (SELECT MAX(Age) FROM artist);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the oldest artist in the database by showing their name and age.

sql: SELECT Music_Festival, Result, COUNT(*) FROM music_festival GROUP BY Music_Festival, Result;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows showing a count of each result (e.g., 'Awarded', 'Nominated') for each distinct music festival.

sql: SELECT T2.Song, T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID ORDER BY T2.Issue_Date;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each song and its corresponding artist, ordered chronologically by the song's issue date.

sql: SELECT T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Age = 34;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the songs by artists who are 34 years old.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Category = 'Best Song' AND T3.Result = 'Nominated' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the artist who was nominated for 'Best Song' and also had a volume spend more than one week at the top of the charts.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID EXCEPT SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Category = 'Best Lyrics';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an artist who has released a volume but has never won an award for 'Best Lyrics'.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, SUM(T2.Weeks_on_Top), COUNT(T3.ID) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume GROUP BY T1.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artist, the total number of weeks their music spent on top, and a count of their associated music festival appearances.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist ORDER BY SUM(T2.Weeks_on_Top) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the artist who has spent the most cumulative weeks at the top across all their volumes.

sql: SELECT T2.Song, T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Age < (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM artist);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing songs and their corresponding artists, for all artists who are younger than the average artist age.

sql: SELECT Category FROM music_festival GROUP BY Category HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT Music_Festival) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of music festival categories that are associated with more than one distinct festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the artists who have been both awarded and nominated at music festivals.

sql: SELECT T3.Music_Festival FROM volume AS T1 JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T1.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T1.Weeks_on_Top = (SELECT MAX(Weeks_on_Top) FROM volume);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the music festivals associated with the album volume that spent the most weeks at the top of the charts.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, COUNT(T2.Volume_ID) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Artist_ID IN (SELECT T2.Artist_ID FROM volume AS T2 JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume) GROUP BY T1.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artist who has performed at a music festival, along with a count of their total volumes.

sql: SELECT Artist FROM artist WHERE Famous_Title IS NOT NULL INTERSECT SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of artists who both have a recognized famous title and have received an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top > (SELECT AVG(T3.Weeks_on_Top) FROM volume AS T3 WHERE T3.Artist_ID = T1.Artist_ID);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the titles of songs that spent more weeks on top than the average for their respective artist.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded' UNION SELECT Artist FROM artist WHERE Age > 34;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of artists who have either won an award at a music festival or are older than 34.

sql: SELECT T3.Result, AVG(T1.Age) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume GROUP BY T3.Result;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the average age of artists, grouped by their music festival result ('Awarded' or 'Nominated').

sql: SELECT Artist, song_count FROM (SELECT T1.Artist, COUNT(T2.Volume_ID) AS song_count FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist) ORDER BY song_count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artist and their total number of songs, ordered from the artist with the most songs to the least.

sql: SELECT Artist FROM artist GROUP BY Artist HAVING COUNT(Famous_Title) > 1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T3.Category, T2.Song FROM volume AS T2 JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top = (SELECT MAX(T4.Weeks_on_Top) FROM volume AS T4 JOIN music_festival AS T5 ON T4.Volume_ID = T5.Volume WHERE T5.Category = T3.Category);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing music festival categories alongside the song(s) that spent the most weeks at the top for each respective category.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T1.Famous_Release_date != '2009' GROUP BY T1.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique artists who have performed at a music festival and did not have their famous release in 2009.

sql: SELECT T2.Song, T1.Artist, T3.Music_Festival FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top = 3.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the song, artist, and associated music festival for songs that were at the top for exactly three weeks.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded' ORDER BY T1.Age ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the youngest artist who has received an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top = 1.0 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top = 3.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying an artist who has had volumes that spent both exactly one week and exactly three weeks at the top.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, COUNT(T3.ID) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T1.Age > 30 GROUP BY T1.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each artist over the age of 30 and the total number of music festivals they have participated in.

sql: SELECT T2.Song FROM volume AS T2 JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume JOIN artist AS T1 ON T2.Artist_ID = T1.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Artist IN (SELECT Artist FROM artist GROUP BY Artist HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the song titles from music festivals performed by artists who have multiple entries in the artist database.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Category = 'Best Lyrics' AND T3.Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an artist who has won an award, excluding any artist who was also nominated for the 'Best Lyrics' category.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top > (SELECT AVG(Weeks_on_Top) FROM volume);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing the artists and titles for songs that have remained "on top" for a duration longer than the average across all songs.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, COUNT(T2.Volume_ID) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top >= 1 AND T2.Volume_ID IN (SELECT Volume FROM music_festival) GROUP BY T1.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artist and the total number of their volumes that were both featured in a music festival and spent at least one week on the top charts.

sql: SELECT Customer_name FROM Customers;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name of every customer in the database.

sql: SELECT Service_name FROM Services;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name of each available service.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT policy_type_code FROM Available_Policies;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, providing a distinct list of all available policy type codes.

sql: SELECT Claim_ID FROM Claims;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, providing a complete list of all unique claim identification numbers.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE Customer_name = 'Ellsworth Paucek';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available information for the customer named 'Ellsworth Paucek'.

sql: SELECT Policy_ID, Customer_Phone FROM Available_Policies WHERE policy_type_code = 'Property Insurance';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the policy ID and customer phone number for all policies classified as 'Property Insurance'.

sql: SELECT Settlement_ID, Settlement_Amount FROM Settlements WHERE Settlement_Amount > 5000;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the ID and amount for all settlements with a value greater than $5000.

sql: SELECT * FROM Services WHERE Service_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID and name for the service specifically identified by `Service_ID` 1.

sql: SELECT Customer_ID, Customer_name FROM Customers ORDER BY Customer_name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the ID and name for each customer, ordered alphabetically by customer name.

sql: SELECT * FROM First_Notification_of_Loss WHERE Customer_ID = 214;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for the First Notification of Loss (FNOL) report associated with customer ID 214.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, T2.Policy_ID FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing customer names and the ID for each policy they hold.

sql: SELECT T1.FNOL_ID, T2.Service_name FROM First_Notification_of_Loss AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, linking each First Notification of Loss (FNOL) ID to its corresponding service name.

sql: SELECT T1.Claim_ID, T2.Policy_ID FROM Claims AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.FNOL_ID = T2.FNOL_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, linking each claim ID to its corresponding policy ID from the initial 'First Notification of Loss' record.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, COUNT(T2.Policy_ID) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer by name and the total number of insurance policies they hold.

sql: SELECT T2.Service_name, COUNT(T1.FNOL_ID) FROM First_Notification_of_Loss AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID GROUP BY T2.Service_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each service name and the corresponding total count of initial loss notifications (claims) for that service.

sql: SELECT T1.FNOL_ID, T2.Customer_name FROM First_Notification_of_Loss AS T1 JOIN Customers AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows linking each First Notification of Loss (FNOL) ID to the name of the customer who filed the claim.

sql: SELECT AVG(Settlement_Amount) FROM Settlements;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average settlement amount across all recorded settlements.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID WHERE T3.policy_type_code = 'Property Insurance';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name of each customer who holds a 'Property Insurance' policy.

sql: SELECT policy_type_code, COUNT(Policy_ID) FROM Available_Policies GROUP BY policy_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each policy type and the total number of available policies for that type.

sql: SELECT FNOL_ID, COUNT(Claim_ID) FROM Claims GROUP BY FNOL_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, listing each First Notice of Loss (FNOL) identifier along with the total count of claims associated with that specific loss event.

sql: SELECT T1.Claim_ID, T3.Service_name FROM Claims AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.FNOL_ID = T2.FNOL_ID JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.Service_ID = T3.Service_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each claim ID alongside the name of the service associated with that claim.

sql: SELECT T1.Settlement_ID, T4.Customer_name FROM Settlements AS T1 JOIN Claims AS T2 ON T1.Claim_ID = T2.Claim_ID JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T3 ON T2.FNOL_ID = T3.FNOL_ID JOIN Customers AS T4 ON T3.Customer_ID = T4.Customer_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each settlement ID and the name of the customer who filed the associated claim.

sql: SELECT Claim_ID, SUM(Settlement_Amount) FROM Settlements GROUP BY Claim_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each claim by its ID, along with the corresponding total settlement amount for that claim.

sql: SELECT Customer_ID, Policy_ID, Date_Opened FROM Customers_Policies WHERE strftime('%Y', Date_Opened) = '2016';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing customer and policy IDs for all insurance policies that were opened during the year 2016, along with the specific date each was opened.

sql: SELECT T1.FNOL_ID, T2.Customer_name, T1.Policy_ID FROM First_Notification_of_Loss AS T1 JOIN Customers AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID WHERE T1.FNOL_ID = 532;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the customer's name and policy ID for the specific First Notification of Loss (FNOL) report with ID 532.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, COUNT(T2.Policy_ID) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Policy_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers who have more than one policy, along with the total count of policies for each.

sql: SELECT Customer_name FROM Customers WHERE Customer_ID IN (SELECT Customer_ID FROM Customers_Policies) AND Customer_ID NOT IN (SELECT Customer_ID FROM First_Notification_of_Loss);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers who have an active policy but have never filed a claim.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, SUM(T4.Settlement_Amount) AS Total_Settled FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claims AS T3 ON T2.FNOL_ID = T3.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS T4 ON T3.Claim_ID = T4.Claim_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each customer's name alongside the total monetary value of all their settled claims.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.Service_ID = T3.Service_ID WHERE T3.Service_name = 'Change a policy';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of customers who have requested the 'Change a policy' service.

sql: SELECT Service_name FROM Services WHERE Service_ID IN (SELECT Service_ID FROM First_Notification_of_Loss WHERE FNOL_ID IN (SELECT FNOL_ID FROM Claims WHERE Claim_ID IN (SELECT Claim_ID FROM Settlements)));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of services that have ultimately resulted in at least one settled insurance claim.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claims AS T3 ON T2.FNOL_ID = T3.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS T4 ON T3.Claim_ID = T4.Claim_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_name HAVING SUM(T4.Settlement_Amount) > (SELECT AVG(Settlement_Amount) FROM Settlements);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers whose total settlement payouts exceed the overall average settlement amount for all claims.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, COUNT(T3.Claim_ID) FROM Customers AS T1 LEFT JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID LEFT JOIN Claims AS T3 ON T2.FNOL_ID = T3.FNOL_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each customer's name alongside the total count of claims they have filed, including customers with zero claims.

sql: SELECT T2.Service_name, COUNT(T1.FNOL_ID) AS fnol_count FROM First_Notification_of_Loss AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID GROUP BY T2.Service_name ORDER BY fnol_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single service with the highest number of First Notification of Loss (FNOL) reports, along with the total count for that service.

sql: SELECT T3.policy_type_code, AVG(T5.Settlement_Amount) FROM Customers_Policies AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Policy_ID = T2.Policy_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T3 ON T1.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID JOIN Claims AS T4 ON T2.FNOL_ID = T4.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS T5 ON T4.Claim_ID = T5.Claim_ID GROUP BY T3.policy_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each policy type code along with the average settlement amount for claims filed under that policy type.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID WHERE T3.policy_type_code = 'Property Insurance' UNION SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID WHERE T2.Service_ID = 6;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of customers who either have a 'Property Insurance' policy or have submitted a First Notification of Loss for service ID 6.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID EXCEPT SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID WHERE T3.policy_type_code = 'Life Insurance';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of customers who have at least one policy but do not have a 'Life Insurance' policy.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID WHERE T3.policy_type_code = 'Property Insurance' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of customers who both have a 'Property Insurance' policy and have previously filed an insurance claim.

sql: SELECT Policy_ID FROM Available_Policies WHERE Policy_ID NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Policy_ID FROM First_Notification_of_Loss);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the policy IDs for available policies that have no recorded claims (First Notification of Loss).

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, T2.Policy_ID, julianday(T2.Date_Closed) - julianday(T2.Date_Opened) AS active_days FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID WHERE T2.Date_Closed IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows for all closed insurance policies, listing the customer's name, the policy ID, and the total number of days each policy was active.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, T4.Settlement_Amount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claims AS T3 ON T2.FNOL_ID = T3.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS T4 ON T3.Claim_ID = T4.Claim_ID ORDER BY T4.Settlement_Amount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the customer name and amount for the single largest claim settlement.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Service_name FROM Settlements AS T1 JOIN Claims AS T2 ON T1.Claim_ID = T2.Claim_ID JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T_FNOL ON T2.FNOL_ID = T_FNOL.FNOL_ID JOIN Services AS T3 ON T_FNOL.Service_ID = T3.Service_ID WHERE T1.Settlement_Amount > 5000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique services associated with claim settlements that exceeded a value of 5000.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, COUNT(T2.Policy_ID) AS policy_count, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Policy_ID) DESC) AS customer_rank FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows ranking each customer by their total number of policies, from most to fewest, and displays their name, policy count, and resulting rank.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Customers_Policies AS T2 WHERE T2.Customer_ID = T1.Customer_ID) AS NumberOfPolicies FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.Customer_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Customer_ID FROM First_Notification_of_Loss);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows for customers who have previously filed a claim, listing each customer's name and the total number of policies they hold.

sql: SELECT CAST(T1.settled_claims AS REAL) * 100 / T2.total_claims AS settlement_percentage FROM (SELECT count(DISTINCT Claim_ID) AS settled_claims FROM Settlements) AS T1, (SELECT count(Claim_ID) AS total_claims FROM Claims) AS T2;
result_summary: Returns a single value calculating the settlement percentage, which is the ratio of distinct settled claims to the total number of all claims filed.

sql: SELECT T1.policy_type_code FROM Available_Policies AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Policy_ID = T2.Policy_ID JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID JOIN Claims AS T4 ON T3.FNOL_ID = T4.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS T5 ON T4.Claim_ID = T5.Claim_ID GROUP BY T1.policy_type_code ORDER BY AVG(T5.Settlement_Amount) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the policy type code that corresponds to the highest average settlement amount.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, T2.Policy_ID FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID WHERE T2.Date_Closed IS NOT NULL AND strftime('%Y', T2.Date_Opened) = strftime('%Y', T2.Date_Closed);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Claim_ID, COUNT(T2.Settlement_ID) AS settlement_count FROM Claims AS T1 JOIN Settlements AS T2 ON T1.Claim_ID = T2.Claim_ID GROUP BY T1.Claim_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Settlement_ID) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID WHERE T2.Service_ID NOT IN (SELECT Service_ID FROM Services WHERE Service_name = 'New policy application');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of customers who have submitted a claim (First Notification of Loss) for any service that was not a 'New policy application'.

sql: SELECT T1.Claim_ID, MIN(julianday(T3.Effective_Date) - julianday(T1.Effective_Date)) AS days_to_settle FROM Claims AS T1 JOIN Settlements AS T3 ON T1.Claim_ID = T3.Claim_ID GROUP BY T1.Claim_ID ORDER BY days_to_settle DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single claim that took the longest time to settle, showing its ID and the calculated settlement duration in days.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.policy_type_code) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, COUNT(T2.student_id) FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T1.course_name;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T3 ON T2.student_id = T3.student_id JOIN Courses AS T4 ON T3.course_id = T4.course_id WHERE T4.course_name = 'statistics';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of all students who are registered for the 'statistics' course.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.line_1, T3.city, T3.country FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id;

sql: SELECT T1.candidate_id, T1.candidate_details, COUNT(T2.qualification) FROM Candidates AS T1 JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T2 ON T1.candidate_id = T2.candidate_id GROUP BY T1.candidate_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each candidate's ID and details, along with a count of the total number of qualifications they have on record.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, COUNT(T2.student_id) FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T1.course_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each course name along with the total number of students attending that course.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.city = 'Linnealand';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of people with an address in the city of 'Linnealand'.

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, COUNT(T1.course_id) FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS T1 GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING COUNT(T1.course_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.candidate_details, T2.qualification, T2.asessment_outcome_code FROM Candidates AS T1 JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T2 ON T1.candidate_id = T2.candidate_id;

sql: SELECT T1.person_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id INTERSECT SELECT T1.person_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidates AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the person ID, first name, and last name of individuals who are identified as both students and candidates.

sql: SELECT qualification, MIN(assessment_date), MAX(assessment_date) FROM Candidate_Assessments GROUP BY qualification;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing the first and last assessment dates for each type of candidate qualification.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T3 ON T2.student_id = T3.student_id JOIN Courses AS T4 ON T3.course_id = T4.course_id WHERE T4.course_name = 'statistics';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the first and last names of all students who attended the 'statistics' course.

sql: SELECT T1.student_details, T3.course_name, T2.registration_date FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T1.student_id = 121;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN (SELECT student_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations GROUP BY student_id HAVING COUNT(course_id) >= 2) AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T1.email_address, T4.line_1, T4.city FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidates AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T3 ON T2.candidate_id = T3.candidate_id JOIN People_Addresses AS T5 ON T1.person_id = T5.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T5.address_id = T4.address_id WHERE T3.asessment_outcome_code = 'Fail';

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, T1.course_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS T1 LEFT JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id AND T1.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE T2.student_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a course registration (by student and course ID) that has no corresponding attendance records.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.course_id = '301' UNION SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id WHERE T2.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of people who are either students registered for course '301' or candidates who passed their assessment.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T1.course_name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.student_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most popular course, determined by having the highest number of student registrations.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.course_id IN ('301', '303') GROUP BY T1.person_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.course_id) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the student who is enrolled in both course '301' and course '303'.

sql: SELECT T3.city, T3.state_province_county FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T1.first_name = 'Shannon';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the city and state/province/county for any person with the first name 'Shannon'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.student_id IN (SELECT candidate_id FROM Candidate_Assessments WHERE asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass');

sql: SELECT candidate_id, assessment_date FROM Candidate_Assessments WHERE assessment_date > (SELECT MIN(registration_date) FROM Student_Course_Registrations);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the candidate ID and assessment date for all assessments that occurred after the earliest student course registration date on record.

sql: SELECT T3.city, COUNT(T1.person_id) FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id GROUP BY T3.city HAVING COUNT(T1.person_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each city that has at least one resident, along with the total count of people living in that city.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T4 ON T1.person_id = T4.student_id WHERE T3.state_province_county = 'Texas';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of a student whose address is in the state of Texas.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T4 ON T1.person_id = T4.student_id JOIN Courses AS T5 ON T4.course_id = T5.course_id WHERE T3.country = 'USA' AND T5.course_name = 'English';

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, COUNT(T1.student_id) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT T3.course_name, T2.student_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T4 ON T2.student_id = T4.student_id AND T2.course_id = T4.course_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id) AS T1 GROUP BY T1.course_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each course name and the total count of distinct students who both registered for and attended that course.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T5.line_1, T5.city FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T4 ON T1.person_id = T4.candidate_id JOIN People_Addresses AS T6 ON T1.person_id = T6.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T5 ON T6.address_id = T5.address_id WHERE T3.course_name = 'statistics' AND T4.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass';

sql: SELECT T1.candidate_id, T1.candidate_details FROM Candidates AS T1 JOIN People AS T2 ON T1.candidate_id = T2.person_id WHERE T2.first_name IN (SELECT T4.first_name FROM Students AS T3 JOIN People AS T4 ON T3.student_id = T4.person_id);

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T3.course_id) AS courses_attended FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T3 ON T2.student_id = T3.student_id GROUP BY T1.person_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name;

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, T3.first_name, T3.last_name FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN People AS T3 ON T2.student_id = T3.person_id WHERE T2.registration_date = (SELECT MIN(registration_date) FROM Student_Course_Registrations WHERE course_id = T1.course_id);

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.skill_description FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T3 ON T1.engineer_id = T3.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T2 ON T3.skill_id = T2.skill_id WHERE T1.engineer_id = 10;

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T2.part_name FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Asset_Parts AS T3 ON T1.asset_id = T3.asset_id JOIN Parts AS T2 ON T3.part_id = T2.part_id WHERE T1.asset_id = 3;

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T2.fault_log_entry_datetime FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id WHERE T2.fault_description = 'system error';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the details of assets that have experienced a 'system error', along with the specific date and time each error was logged.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.visit_start_datetime FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id WHERE T2.fault_status = 'Return';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying maintenance engineers who made a return visit for a fault, showing their first and last name along with the start date and time of that visit.

sql: SELECT T1.part_name, T2.fault_description FROM Parts AS T1 JOIN Part_Faults AS T2 ON T1.part_id = T2.part_id WHERE T1.part_name = 'top';

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T2.contract_end_date FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Contracts AS T2 ON T1.maintenance_contract_id = T2.maintenance_contract_id WHERE T1.asset_id = 2;

sql: SELECT T2.skill_description, COUNT(T1.engineer_id) AS number_of_engineers FROM Engineer_Skills AS T1 JOIN Skills AS T2 ON T1.skill_id = T2.skill_id GROUP BY T2.skill_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each skill description along with the total count of engineers who possess that skill.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_id, T1.asset_details, COUNT(T2.part_id) AS number_of_parts FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Asset_Parts AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id GROUP BY T1.asset_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each asset's ID and details, along with a calculated total for the number of component parts associated with that asset.

sql: SELECT T3.fault_log_entry_id, T1.part_name, T2.fault_short_name FROM Parts AS T1 JOIN Part_Faults AS T2 ON T1.part_id = T2.part_id JOIN Fault_Log_Parts AS T3 ON T2.part_fault_id = T3.part_fault_id WHERE T3.fault_status = 'Reported';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing faults currently in 'Reported' status, showing the specific fault log entry ID, the name of the affected part, and the short name for the fault type.

sql: SELECT T1.fault_description, T2.staff_name, T3.company_name FROM Fault_Log AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.recorded_by_staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Assets AS T4 ON T1.asset_id = T4.asset_id JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T3 ON T4.supplier_company_id = T3.company_id WHERE T1.fault_log_entry_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row for a specific fault log entry (ID 1), showing the fault description, the name of the staff member who recorded it, and the name of the company that supplied the associated asset.

sql: SELECT T1.company_name, COUNT(T2.maintenance_contract_id) AS contract_count FROM Third_Party_Companies AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Contracts AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.maintenance_contract_company_id GROUP BY T1.company_name ORDER BY contract_count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each third-party company and its total count of maintenance contracts, ordered from the company with the most contracts to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T2.skill_id = T3.skill_id WHERE T3.skill_code = 'MECH';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of maintenance engineers who possess the skill identified by the code 'MECH'.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T1.asset_make FROM Assets AS T1 WHERE T1.asset_id IN (SELECT T2.asset_id FROM Asset_Parts AS T2 JOIN Parts AS T3 ON T2.part_id = T3.part_id WHERE T3.chargeable_yn = '1');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the details and make of assets that are composed of at least one chargeable part.

sql: SELECT T1.company_name, COUNT(T2.engineer_id) AS engineer_count FROM Third_Party_Companies AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id GROUP BY T1.company_name ORDER BY engineer_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the third-party company with the most maintenance engineers and the corresponding count of those engineers.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.recorded_by_staff_id JOIN Assets AS T3 ON T2.asset_id = T3.asset_id WHERE T3.supplier_company_id = 2;

sql: SELECT T1.skill_description FROM Skills AS T1 JOIN Skills_Required_To_Fix AS T2 ON T1.skill_id = T2.skill_id GROUP BY T1.skill_description ORDER BY COUNT(T2.part_fault_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single skill most frequently required to fix part faults.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 WHERE T1.engineer_id IN (SELECT T2.engineer_id FROM Engineer_Visits AS T2 JOIN Fault_Log AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Asset_Parts AS T4 ON T3.asset_id = T4.asset_id JOIN Parts AS T5 ON T4.part_id = T5.part_id WHERE T5.part_name = 'top');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of maintenance engineers who have performed a visit related to a fault involving a part named 'top'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id GROUP BY T1.engineer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name HAVING COUNT(T2.skill_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.part_name FROM Parts AS T1 JOIN Asset_Parts AS T2 ON T1.part_id = T2.part_id GROUP BY T1.part_name HAVING COUNT(T2.asset_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of parts that are used as components in more than one asset.

sql: SELECT T1.maintenance_contract_id, T1.contract_start_date FROM Maintenance_Contracts AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Assets AS T2 JOIN Fault_Log AS T3 ON T2.asset_id = T3.asset_id WHERE T2.maintenance_contract_id = T1.maintenance_contract_id);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the maintenance contract ID and start date for all contracts that cover assets with a recorded fault history.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T2.skill_id = T3.skill_id WHERE T3.skill_description = 'Electrical' INTERSECT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T2.skill_id = T3.skill_id WHERE T3.skill_description = 'Mechanical';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of maintenance engineers who possess both 'Electrical' and 'Mechanical' skills.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T2 JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T3 ON T2.engineer_id = T3.engineer_id WHERE T3.fault_status = 'Waiting';
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the first and last names of all maintenance engineers who are not assigned to a visit with a 'Waiting' fault status.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T2.company_name FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.supplier_company_id = T2.company_id WHERE T1.asset_id IN (SELECT T3.asset_id FROM Fault_Log AS T3 JOIN Fault_Log_Parts AS T4 ON T3.fault_log_entry_id = T4.fault_log_entry_id WHERE T4.fault_status = 'Reported');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the details and the supplier company name for all assets that currently have a fault status of 'Reported'.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name FROM Staff AS T1 WHERE T1.staff_id NOT IN (SELECT T2.contact_staff_id FROM Engineer_Visits AS T2 WHERE T2.contact_staff_id IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of staff members who have never been the designated contact for an engineer's visit.

sql: SELECT T1.part_name, T3.skill_description FROM Parts AS T1 JOIN Part_Faults AS T2 ON T1.part_id = T2.part_id JOIN Skills_Required_To_Fix AS T4 ON T2.part_fault_id = T4.part_fault_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T4.skill_id = T3.skill_id WHERE T1.part_name = 'package';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the descriptions of skills required to fix faults associated with the part named 'package'.

sql: SELECT first_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers UNION SELECT staff_name FROM Staff;
result_summary: Returns 30 rows, creating a single, consolidated list of unique first names for all personnel by combining maintenance engineers and general staff.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T1.asset_make FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN (SELECT asset_id, COUNT(*) AS fault_count FROM Fault_Log GROUP BY asset_id) AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id WHERE T2.fault_count > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the asset details and make for assets that have experienced more than one fault.

sql: SELECT T1.company_name FROM Third_Party_Companies AS T1 JOIN Assets AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.supplier_company_id JOIN Fault_Log AS T3 ON T2.asset_id = T3.asset_id GROUP BY T1.company_name ORDER BY COUNT(T3.fault_log_entry_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the third-party company whose supplied assets have accumulated the highest number of logged faults.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.company_name, T3.skill_description FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T4 ON T1.engineer_id = T4.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T4.skill_id = T3.skill_id WHERE T2.company_type = 'Maintenance Contractor' AND T3.skill_code = 'TV';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing maintenance engineers from contracting companies who possess the 'TV' skill, showing their full name, company name, and skill description.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T1.asset_acquired_date FROM Assets AS T1 WHERE T1.asset_id IN (SELECT asset_id FROM Fault_Log WHERE recorded_by_staff_id = (SELECT staff_id FROM Staff WHERE staff_name = 'Audreanne'));
