sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT engineer_id FROM Engineer_Skills GROUP BY engineer_id ORDER BY COUNT(skill_id) DESC LIMIT 1) AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the single maintenance engineer who possesses the most skills.

sql: SELECT AVG(part_counts.num_parts) FROM (SELECT COUNT(T1.part_id) as num_parts FROM Asset_Parts AS T1 GROUP BY T1.asset_id) AS part_counts;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single value representing the average number of parts per asset across all assets.

sql: SELECT T1.company_name FROM Third_Party_Companies AS T1 WHERE T1.company_id IN (SELECT T2.maintenance_contract_company_id FROM Maintenance_Contracts AS T2) AND T1.company_id IN (SELECT T3.company_id FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T3);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of third-party companies that both have active maintenance contracts and employ maintenance engineers.

sql: SELECT T1.part_name, T1.chargeable_amount FROM Parts AS T1 WHERE T1.chargeable_yn = '1' AND T1.chargeable_amount > (SELECT AVG(chargeable_amount) FROM Parts WHERE chargeable_yn = '1');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T2.fault_description, T4.first_name, T4.last_name, T5.company_name FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T4 ON T3.engineer_id = T4.engineer_id JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T5 ON T4.company_id = T5.company_id WHERE T1.asset_make = 'IN';
result_summary: Returns 1 row detailing a fault on an asset from the make 'IN', including the asset's details, the fault description, and the name and company of the third-party engineer assigned to it.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name, T2.fault_log_entry_id FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.recorded_by_staff_id WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Engineer_Visits AS T3 WHERE T3.fault_log_entry_id = T2.fault_log_entry_id AND T3.contact_staff_id = T1.staff_id);
result_summary: Returns 14 rows identifying fault logs and the staff member who recorded them, for incidents where that same staff member was not the designated contact for the follow-up engineer visit.

sql: SELECT * FROM Owners LIMIT 10;
result_summary: Returns a sample of 10 rows containing all recorded information for pet owners, including their name, address, and contact details.

sql: SELECT name, age, gender FROM Dogs WHERE name = 'Mavis';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name, age, and gender details for the dog specifically named 'Mavis'.

sql: SELECT name, age, weight FROM Dogs ORDER BY age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name, age, and weight of each dog, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT state FROM Owners;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing each unique state where an owner is located.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Dogs;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total count of all dogs.

sql: SELECT * FROM Charges WHERE charge_type = 'Daily Accommodation';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing all details (ID, type, and amount) for charges classified as 'Daily Accommodation'.

sql: SELECT name, weight FROM Dogs WHERE weight > 7.0;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and weight for all dogs weighing more than 7.0.

sql: SELECT * FROM Breeds WHERE breed_code IN ('ESK', 'BUL');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the breed code and name for the specific breeds matching the codes 'ESK' and 'BUL'.

sql: SELECT professional_id, first_name, last_name FROM Professionals WHERE first_name = 'Taryn' AND last_name = 'Braun';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the professional ID, first name, and last name for the professional specifically named 'Taryn Braun'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Owners AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id WHERE T1.dog_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the dog with ID 3, providing its name and the first and last name of its owner.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, COUNT(T1.dog_id) FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Owners AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id GROUP BY T2.owner_id;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each owner by their first and last name, along with a count of the total number of dogs they own.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, COUNT(T1.dog_id) FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Owners AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id WHERE T1.size_code = 'LGE' GROUP BY T2.owner_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first name of each owner who has large dogs, along with a count of how many large dogs each person owns.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.first_name, T3.breed_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Owners AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id JOIN Breeds AS T3 ON T1.breed_code = T3.breed_code WHERE T3.breed_name = 'Bulldog';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name of each dog identified as a 'Bulldog' along with the first name of its owner.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Owners AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id GROUP BY T2.owner_id HAVING COUNT(T1.dog_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of owners who have more than one dog.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Dogs WHERE dog_id IN (SELECT dog_id FROM Treatments WHERE professional_id = 4);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age for all dogs that have been treated by the professional with an ID of 4.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.breed_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Breeds AS T2 ON T1.breed_code = T2.breed_code WHERE T1.abandoned_yn = '1' AND T1.size_code = 'LGE';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and breed of a large-sized dog that has been abandoned.

sql: SELECT T2.treatment_type_description, AVG(T1.cost_of_treatment) FROM Treatments AS T1 JOIN Treatment_Types AS T2 ON T1.treatment_type_code = T2.treatment_type_code GROUP BY T1.treatment_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each treatment type description along with the calculated average cost for treatments of that type.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.treatment_id) FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id GROUP BY T1.dog_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each dog by name along with the total count of treatments they have received.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, COUNT(T1.treatment_id) FROM Treatments AS T1 JOIN Professionals AS T2 ON T1.professional_id = T2.professional_id GROUP BY T2.professional_id ORDER BY COUNT(T1.treatment_id) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each professional's full name and the total number of treatments they have performed, ordered from the highest number of treatments to the lowest.

sql: SELECT name, weight FROM Dogs WHERE weight > (SELECT AVG(weight) FROM Dogs);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and weight for each dog that is heavier than the overall average weight.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.first_name, T2.date_of_treatment FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id JOIN Professionals AS T3 ON T2.professional_id = T3.professional_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing a historical log of all treatments, showing the name of the dog treated, the first name of the professional who administered it, and the date of the treatment.

sql: SELECT T2.breed_name, COUNT(T1.dog_id) FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Breeds AS T2 ON T1.breed_code = T2.breed_code GROUP BY T2.breed_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each dog breed name along with the total count of dogs for that breed.

sql: SELECT T1.name, SUM(T2.cost_of_treatment) FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id GROUP BY T1.dog_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each dog's name along with the total cost of all their treatments.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, city FROM Owners WHERE state = 'Wisconsin';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first name, last name, and city for any pet owner located in the state of Wisconsin.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id WHERE T2.treatment_type_code = 'VAC';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all dogs that have received a vaccination treatment.

sql: SELECT T1.breed_name, T2.AvgAge FROM Breeds AS T1 JOIN (SELECT breed_code, AVG(age) AS AvgAge FROM Dogs GROUP BY breed_code) AS T2 ON T1.breed_code = T2.breed_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each dog breed's name and the calculated average age of all dogs belonging to that breed.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.breed_code, T1.age FROM Dogs AS T1 WHERE T1.age = (SELECT MAX(T2.age) FROM Dogs AS T2 WHERE T2.breed_code = T1.breed_code);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the oldest dog(s) within each breed, showing their name, breed code, and age.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, state FROM Owners WHERE state = 'Wisconsin' UNION SELECT first_name, last_name, state FROM Professionals WHERE state = 'Wisconsin';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first name, last name, and state for all individuals identified as either Owners or Professionals residing in Wisconsin.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id WHERE T2.treatment_type_code = 'EXAM' INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id WHERE T2.treatment_type_code = 'WALK';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of dogs that have received both an 'EXAM' and a 'WALK' treatment.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id WHERE T2.treatment_type_code = 'EXAM' EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id WHERE T2.treatment_type_code = 'VAC';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of dogs that have received an exam but have not received a vaccination.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name AS owner_first_name, T2.name AS dog_name, T4.first_name AS prof_first_name FROM Owners AS T1 JOIN Dogs AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id JOIN Treatments AS T3 ON T2.dog_id = T3.dog_id JOIN Professionals AS T4 ON T3.professional_id = T4.professional_id WHERE T1.state = 'Virginia';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.breed_name FROM Breeds AS T1 JOIN Dogs AS T2 ON T1.breed_code = T2.breed_code GROUP BY T1.breed_name HAVING AVG(T2.age) > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM Dogs);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the dog breed whose average age is greater than the overall average age of all dogs.

sql: SELECT T1.size_description, COUNT(T2.dog_id) AS dog_count, SUM(CASE WHEN T2.abandoned_yn = '1' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS abandoned_count FROM Sizes AS T1 LEFT JOIN Dogs AS T2 ON T1.size_code = T2.size_code GROUP BY T1.size_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows summarizing for each dog size category the total number of dogs and the count of those that were abandoned.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM Owners WHERE owner_id IN (SELECT owner_id FROM Dogs WHERE dog_id IN (SELECT dog_id FROM Treatments WHERE cost_of_treatment > 500));
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of owners whose dogs have received at least one treatment costing more than 500.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Treatments AS T1 JOIN Professionals AS T2 ON T1.professional_id = T2.professional_id JOIN Dogs AS T3 ON T1.dog_id = T3.dog_id GROUP BY T2.professional_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.breed_code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of professionals who have provided treatments to dogs of more than one distinct breed.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Owners AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id GROUP BY T1.owner_id ORDER BY COUNT(T1.dog_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the owner with the most dogs.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.first_name, T3.last_name, T2.date_of_treatment FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id JOIN Professionals AS T3 ON T2.professional_id = T3.professional_id WHERE T1.abandoned_yn = '1' AND T2.date_of_treatment = (SELECT MAX(date_of_treatment) FROM Treatments WHERE dog_id = T1.dog_id);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the name of each abandoned dog, the professional who administered their most recent treatment, and the date of that treatment.

sql: SELECT T2.treatment_type_description, AVG(T1.cost_of_treatment) AS avg_cost FROM Treatments AS T1 JOIN Treatment_Types AS T2 ON T1.treatment_type_code = T2.treatment_type_code GROUP BY T1.treatment_type_code ORDER BY avg_cost DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top three most expensive treatment types by their average cost, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Owners AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id GROUP BY T2.owner_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.size_code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of owners who have dogs of more than one distinct size.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.breed_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Breeds AS T2 ON T1.breed_code = T2.breed_code WHERE T1.abandoned_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique breeds of dogs that have been abandoned.

sql: SELECT first_name, state FROM Owners UNION ALL SELECT first_name, state FROM Professionals;
result_summary: Returns 30 rows, providing a combined list of the first names and states for all individuals from both the 'Owners' and 'Professionals' groups.

sql: SELECT T1.size_description, AVG(T2.age) FROM Sizes AS T1 JOIN Dogs AS T2 ON T1.size_code = T2.size_code GROUP BY T1.size_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each dog size description and the average age of dogs within that size group.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Treatments AS T1 JOIN Professionals AS T2 ON T1.professional_id = T2.professional_id WHERE T1.treatment_type_code = 'WALK' GROUP BY T1.professional_id ORDER BY COUNT(T1.treatment_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the professional who has administered the most treatments of type 'WALK'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.cost_of_treatment FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id WHERE T2.cost_of_treatment > (SELECT AVG(cost_of_treatment) FROM Treatments);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and treatment cost for dogs whose treatments were more expensive than the average cost across all treatments.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Owners AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id JOIN Breeds AS T3 ON T1.breed_code = T3.breed_code WHERE T2.first_name = 'Jaclyn' AND T3.breed_name = 'Eskimo';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the Eskimo dog owned by a person named Jaclyn.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM Owners EXCEPT SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Owners AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id WHERE T1.breed_code = 'BUL';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of all owners who do not own a dog of the 'BUL' breed.

sql: SELECT T3.first_name, T3.last_name, SUM(T1.cost_of_treatment) AS total_cost FROM Treatments AS T1 JOIN Dogs AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id JOIN Owners AS T3 ON T2.owner_id = T3.owner_id GROUP BY T3.owner_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each dog owner's first and last name, along with the total aggregated cost of all treatments for their dog(s).

sql: SELECT T2.breed_name, COUNT(T1.dog_id) FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Breeds AS T2 ON T1.breed_code = T2.breed_code GROUP BY T2.breed_name HAVING AVG(T1.weight) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each dog breed and the total count of dogs for that breed, filtered to only include breeds with an average weight greater than 5.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T4.professional_id, T4.first_name, T4.last_name FROM Owners AS T1 JOIN Dogs AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id JOIN Treatments AS T3 ON T2.dog_id = T3.dog_id JOIN Professionals AS T4 ON T3.professional_id = T4.professional_id WHERE T1.state = 'Wisconsin';

sql: SELECT name, breed_code FROM Dogs WHERE owner_id IN (SELECT owner_id FROM Owners WHERE state = 'Vermont') AND age > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM Dogs WHERE owner_id IN (SELECT owner_id FROM Owners WHERE state = 'Vermont'));
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and breed code of dogs from Vermont that are older than the average age of all dogs in that state.

result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all available products and their corresponding specifications, including dimensions, resolution, and scanning speed.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Ranking FROM store ORDER BY Ranking;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each store's name and its ranking, ordered from the lowest rank to the highest.

sql: SELECT District_name, Headquartered_City FROM district WHERE City_Area = 20.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the district name and its headquartered city for any district with a city area of exactly 20.0.

sql: SELECT product, dpi FROM product WHERE dpi = 600.0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing products that have a Dots Per Inch (DPI) of exactly 600.

sql: SELECT Store_Name FROM store WHERE Type = 'Village Store';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all stores classified as a 'Village Store'.

sql: SELECT District_name, City_Population FROM district WHERE City_Population > 100000;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing districts with a city population greater than 100,000, showing each district's name and its city population.

sql: SELECT * FROM store WHERE Store_Name = 'Miramichi';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details (including type, area size, and ranking) for the store named 'Miramichi'.

sql: SELECT product, interface FROM product WHERE interface = 'USB 2.0';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all products that use the 'USB 2.0' interface, showing the product name and its interface type.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Type FROM store;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each unique store type available in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Area_size FROM store ORDER BY Area_size DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the three largest stores by area size, ordered from largest to smallest.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, COUNT(T2.Product_ID) FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.Store_Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each store name alongside a count of the total number of products available in that store.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name, COUNT(T2.Store_ID) FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID GROUP BY T1.District_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each district's name and the total count of stores located within that district.

sql: SELECT T3.product FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T1.Store_Name = 'Miramichi';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the products available at the 'Miramichi' store.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.District_ID = 15;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of stores that are located in District 15.

sql: SELECT Type, COUNT(*) FROM store GROUP BY Type HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying store types with more than one location and providing a count of stores for each of those types.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, AVG(T3.pages_per_minute_color) FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id GROUP BY T1.Store_Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, providing each store's name and the average color printing speed (pages per minute) for the products it stocks.

sql: SELECT Store_Name FROM store WHERE Store_ID IN (SELECT Store_ID FROM store_product WHERE Product_ID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all stores that carry a specific product (Product ID 1).

sql: SELECT product FROM product WHERE product_id IN (SELECT Product_ID FROM store_product WHERE Store_ID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all products sold at the specific store with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name, COUNT(T2.Store_ID) AS NumberOfStores FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID GROUP BY T1.District_ID ORDER BY NumberOfStores DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each district and the total number of stores it contains, ordered from the district with the most stores to the one with the fewest.

sql: SELECT T1.product, COUNT(T2.Store_ID) AS NumberOfStores FROM product AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.Product_ID GROUP BY T1.product_id ORDER BY NumberOfStores DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each product and the total number of stores that carry it, ordered from the products carried in the most stores to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, T1.Type FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.District_ID = 11;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and type of the store located in District 11.

sql: SELECT T1.product, T3.Store_Name FROM product AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.Product_ID JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID WHERE T1.product_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the stores that carry a specific product (product ID 1), showing the product's name alongside each store's name.

sql: SELECT AVG(City_Population) FROM district WHERE City_Area > 15.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average population for all cities with a geographical area larger than 15.0 square units.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name, MAX(T3.Ranking) FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.District_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each district name along with the maximum ranking of a store located within that district.

sql: SELECT product, pages_per_minute_color FROM product WHERE pages_per_minute_color > (SELECT AVG(pages_per_minute_color) FROM product);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing products with an above-average color pages-per-minute speed, showing each product's name and its specific speed.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name, SUM(T3.Area_size) FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.District_name HAVING SUM(T3.Area_size) > 10;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing districts with a combined store area size greater than 10, along with that total calculated area for each district.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T3.dpi = 600.0 GROUP BY T1.Store_Name HAVING COUNT(T3.product_id) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of stores that stock more than two products with a 600 DPI resolution.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, T1.ProductCount FROM (SELECT T1.Store_Name, COUNT(T2.Product_ID) AS ProductCount FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.Store_Name) AS T1 ORDER BY T1.ProductCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single store that carries the most products, along with the total count of its products.

sql: SELECT T1.product FROM product AS T1 WHERE T1.product_id IN (SELECT T2.Product_ID FROM store_product AS T2 WHERE T2.Store_ID IN (SELECT T3.Store_ID FROM store AS T3 WHERE T3.Ranking > 1));
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of products available in stores with a ranking greater than 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 WHERE T1.Type = 'City Mall' UNION SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.District_ID = 11;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of all stores that are either a 'City Mall' type or are located within District 11.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 WHERE T1.Type = 'Village Store' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Store_Name FROM store AS T2 JOIN store_district AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID WHERE T3.District_ID = 15;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any store that is both classified as a 'Village Store' and is located in District 15.

sql: SELECT Store_Name FROM store EXCEPT SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.District_ID = 15;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all stores that are not located in District 15.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name, (SELECT T3.Store_Name FROM store_district AS T2 JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID WHERE T2.District_ID = T1.District_ID ORDER BY T3.Ranking ASC LIMIT 1) AS TopRankedStore FROM district AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, listing each district's name along with the name of the single highest-ranked store located within that district.

sql: SELECT T4.product FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN store_product AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID JOIN product AS T4 ON T3.Product_ID = T4.product_id WHERE T1.District_ID = 11;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all products sold in stores located in District ID 11.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, T1.Area_size, T1.Type FROM store AS T1 WHERE T1.Area_size > (SELECT AVG(T2.Area_size) FROM store AS T2 WHERE T2.Type = T1.Type);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying stores that have an area size larger than the average for their own specific store type, showing the store's name, area size, and type.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name FROM district AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM store_district AS T2 WHERE T2.District_ID = T1.District_ID);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all districts that contain at least one store.

sql: SELECT product_count_category, COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT T1.Store_ID, CASE WHEN COUNT(T2.Product_ID) <= 1 THEN 'Low' WHEN COUNT(T2.Product_ID) <= 3 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'High' END AS product_count_category FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.Store_ID) AS T1 GROUP BY product_count_category;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows that categorize stores by their number of products ('Low', 'Medium', or 'High') and then provides a count of how many stores fall into each of those categories.

sql: SELECT 'Products' AS Entity, COUNT(*) FROM product UNION ALL SELECT 'Stores' AS Entity, COUNT(*) FROM store;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows providing a summary count for key business entities, listing the total number of products and stores.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name FROM district AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.District_ID, AVG(T3.Ranking) as avg_rank FROM store_district AS T2 JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID GROUP BY T2.District_ID) AS T4 ON T1.District_ID = T4.District_ID WHERE T4.avg_rank < 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the district where the average ranking of its associated stores is less than 2.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.product FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T1.Type = 'Village Store';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique products available in stores classified as a 'Village Store'.

sql: SELECT T1.product FROM product AS T1 WHERE T1.product_id NOT IN (SELECT T3.Product_ID FROM store AS T2 JOIN store_product AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID WHERE T2.Type = 'City Mall');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the product that is not sold in any store of the 'City Mall' type.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM store_product AS T2 JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T2.Store_ID = T1.Store_ID AND T3.pages_per_minute_color < 10);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a store that does not carry any color printing products with a speed of less than 10 pages per minute.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name, AVG(T4.ProductCount) FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN (SELECT T1.Store_ID, COUNT(T2.Product_ID) AS ProductCount FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.Store_ID) AS T4 ON T2.Store_ID = T4.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.District_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each district and the average number of products carried by the stores within that district.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name, T3.Store_Name, T3.Area_size, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM store_district AS T4 JOIN store AS T5 ON T4.Store_ID = T5.Store_ID WHERE T4.District_ID = T1.District_ID AND T5.Area_size >= T3.Area_size) AS Rank FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing stores along with their area size and a calculated rank of their size within each district.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.District_ID = 15 EXCEPT SELECT Store_Name FROM store WHERE Type = 'Village Store';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a store located in district 15 that is not classified as a 'Village Store'.

sql: SELECT T1.product FROM product AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.Product_ID WHERE T2.Store_ID IN (SELECT Store_ID FROM store WHERE Ranking = 1) AND T2.Store_ID IN (SELECT Store_ID FROM store_district WHERE District_ID = 11);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the products sold in top-ranked stores (Ranking = 1) that are also located in District 11.

sql: WITH DistrictTopStore AS (SELECT T1.District_ID, (SELECT T3.Store_ID FROM store_district AS T2 JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID WHERE T2.District_ID = T1.District_ID ORDER BY T3.Ranking ASC LIMIT 1) AS TopStoreId FROM district AS T1) SELECT T1.District_name, T4.product FROM district AS T1 JOIN DistrictTopStore AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN store_product AS T3 ON T2.TopStoreId = T3.Store_ID JOIN product AS T4 ON T3.Product_ID = T4.product_id;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing all products sold by the highest-ranked store in each district, pairing each product with the corresponding district's name.

sql: SELECT T2.Type, SUM(T3.City_Population) FROM store_district AS T1 JOIN store AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN district AS T3 ON T1.District_ID = T3.District_ID GROUP BY T2.Type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each store type alongside the total population of all districts served by that store type.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.District_ID = 15 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product = 'Fujitsu fi-6130 A4 Series Scanner';

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, T2.Store_Name, T3.District_name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID < T2.Store_ID JOIN store_district AS T4 ON T1.Store_ID = T4.Store_ID JOIN store_district AS T5 ON T2.Store_ID = T5.Store_ID JOIN district AS T3 ON T4.District_ID = T3.District_ID WHERE T4.District_ID = T5.District_ID;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Other_Details FROM Locations AS T1 JOIN Events AS T2 ON T1.Location_ID = T2.Location_ID JOIN Finances AS T3 ON T2.Finance_ID = T3.Finance_ID JOIN Products_in_Events AS T4 ON T2.Event_ID = T4.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T5 ON T4.Product_ID = T5.Product_ID WHERE T3.Other_Details = 'Good' AND T5.Product_Type_Code = 'Food';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique 'Other Details' for locations that hosted events involving 'Food' products and had 'Good' financial details.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID FROM Agreements AS T1 INTERSECT SELECT T3.Event_ID FROM Assets AS T2 JOIN Assets_in_Events AS T3 ON T2.Asset_ID = T3.Asset_ID WHERE T2.Other_Details = 'Transportation Cars';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the Event IDs for events that have a formal agreement and also involve the use of 'Transportation Cars' as an asset.

sql: SELECT T1.Product_Name FROM Products AS T1 LEFT JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Product_ID = T2.Product_ID WHERE T2.Event_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all products that have not been included in any event.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Event_ID FROM Products_in_Events AS T1 LEFT JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID WHERE T2.Party_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique IDs for events that have associated products but no recorded parties.

sql: SELECT T2.address_details FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.Address_ID = T2.Address_ID GROUP BY T1.Address_ID HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.Event_Type_Code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the address details for locations that have hosted more than one distinct type of event.

sql: SELECT T2.Party_Details, SUM(CASE WHEN T1.Role_Code = 'Organizer' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), SUM(CASE WHEN T1.Role_Code = 'Participant' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Parties AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID GROUP BY T2.Party_Details;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, with each row showing a political party's details, the total number of events they organized, and the total number of events they participated in.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, count(T2.Asset_ID) FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Assets_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID ORDER BY count(T2.Asset_ID) DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the top 3 events with the most associated assets, ordered from the highest asset count to the lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Party_Details FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Parties AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T1.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Products_in_Events AS T4 ON T3.Event_ID = T4.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T5 ON T4.Product_ID = T5.Product_ID WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'Organizer' AND T5.Product_Name = 'Men suits';

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Type_Code FROM Events AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Assets_in_Events AS T2 WHERE T2.Event_ID = T1.Event_ID);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows identifying events that have no assets associated with them, showing each event's ID and type code.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T5.Product_Type_Code FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Products_in_Events AS T4 ON T3.Event_ID = T4.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T5 ON T4.Product_ID = T5.Product_ID WHERE T1.Party_Details = 'European Free Alliance' AND T2.Role_Code = 'Organizer';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique product types associated with events organized by the 'European Free Alliance'.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, T1.Party_ID, T2.Party_ID FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'Organizer' AND T2.Role_Code = 'Organizer' AND T1.Party_ID < T2.Party_ID;

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, SUM(T2.Product_Price) FROM Products_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.Product_ID = T2.Product_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each event ID and the total value of all products associated with that event.

sql: SELECT T2.Other_Details FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Locations AS T2 ON T1.Location_ID = T2.Location_ID GROUP BY T1.Location_ID ORDER BY count(T1.Event_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the 'Other_Details' of the single location that hosted the most events.

sql: SELECT T1.Product_Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Product_ID = T2.Product_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Event_Type_Code = 'Trade Show' AND T1.Product_Price > (SELECT AVG(Product_Price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of products with an above-average price that have been featured at a 'Trade Show' event.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID FROM Products_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.Product_ID = T2.Product_ID WHERE T2.Product_Type_Code IN ('Food', 'Clothes') GROUP BY T1.Event_ID HAVING count(DISTINCT T2.Product_Type_Code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID for an event that features both 'Food' and 'Clothes' product types.

sql: SELECT T3.Event_ID FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Product_ID = T2.Product_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T1.Product_Price = (SELECT MAX(Product_Price) FROM Products);

sql: SELECT T2.Party_Details FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Parties AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'Participant' GROUP BY T1.Party_ID HAVING count(T1.Event_ID) > 5;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Details, count(T3.Event_ID) FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T2.Role_Code = 'Organizer' AND T3.Event_Type_Code = 'Trade Show' GROUP BY T1.Party_Details;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each party's details alongside the total count of 'Trade Show' events they have organized.

sql: SELECT Channel_ID FROM Events GROUP BY Channel_ID ORDER BY count(Event_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID for the single channel with the most events.

sql: WITH ParticipantEvents (Event_ID) AS (SELECT Event_ID FROM Parties_in_Events WHERE Role_Code = 'Participant') SELECT T2.Event_Type_Code, count(T1.Event_ID) FROM ParticipantEvents AS T1 JOIN Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T2.Event_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each event type and the total count of events of that type that included a participant.

sql: SELECT Event_ID, Location_ID FROM Events WHERE Event_Type_Code = 'Press Conferenc' AND Event_ID IN (SELECT Event_ID FROM Agreements);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the event and location ID for a press conference ('Press Conferenc') that has an associated agreement.

sql: SELECT Role_Code FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID WHERE T2.Event_Type_Code = 'Press Conferenc' GROUP BY Role_Code ORDER BY count(T1.Party_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most common role for parties involved in press conferences, determined by the highest number of participants in that role.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.Address_ID = T2.Address_ID JOIN Products_in_Events AS T3 ON T1.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.Product_ID = T4.Product_ID WHERE T1.Address_ID = 3 AND T4.Product_Price = 2894.94;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID for an event that took place at a specific address (ID 3) and involved the sale of a product with a price of 2894.94.

sql: SELECT artist_name, gender FROM artist WHERE country = 'India';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and genders of all artists from India.

sql: SELECT song_name, artist_name FROM song WHERE rating = 8;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the song name and artist for all songs that have a rating of exactly 8.

sql: SELECT g_name, rating FROM genre ORDER BY rating DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all music genres and their ratings, ordered from the highest rating to the lowest.

sql: SELECT f_id, artist_name, file_size FROM files WHERE formats = 'mp3';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the file ID, artist name, and file size for every file identified as an MP3.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM artist;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single value, representing the total number of artists in the database.

sql: SELECT song_name, resolution FROM song ORDER BY resolution DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the song with the highest resolution, listing its name and resolution value.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT country FROM artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique countries where the artists are from.

sql: SELECT song_name, artist_name FROM song WHERE releasedate = '28-AUG-2011';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the song name and artist for any songs released on August 28, 2011.

sql: SELECT artist_name, country FROM artist WHERE preferred_genre = 'folk';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and country of any artist whose preferred genre is 'folk'.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM song WHERE languages = 'bangla';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of songs in the Bangla language.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T2.formats FROM song AS T1 JOIN files AS T2 ON T1.f_id = T2.f_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each song's name along with its corresponding file format.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T1.country, T2.song_name FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each song along with the name and country of the performing artist.

sql: SELECT artist_name, count(*) FROM song GROUP BY artist_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each artist's name along with the total number of songs they have.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T1.country FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T2.rating = 8;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and country of artists who have a song with a rating of 8.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T2.gender FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T1.genre_is = 'tagore';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the song name and the artist's gender for songs specifically in the 'tagore' genre.

sql: SELECT artist_name, AVG(rating) FROM song WHERE country = 'Bangladesh' GROUP BY artist_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each artist from Bangladesh along with their average song rating.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T2.file_size FROM song AS T1 JOIN files AS T2 ON T1.f_id = T2.f_id WHERE T1.rating > 6;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and file size for songs with a rating greater than 6.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T1.country, T1.gender FROM artist AS T1 WHERE T1.artist_name IN (SELECT DISTINCT T2.artist_name FROM song AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name, country, and gender for each artist who has at least one recorded song.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE artist_name IN (SELECT artist_name FROM song WHERE resolution > 500);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of artists who have released at least one song with a resolution greater than 500.

sql: SELECT genre_is, count(*) FROM song GROUP BY genre_is;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each music genre along with the total count of songs in that genre.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T2.most_popular_in FROM song AS T1 JOIN genre AS T2 ON T1.genre_is = T2.g_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each song's name along with the geographical region where its associated genre is most popular.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, count(T2.song_name) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T1.country = 'Bangladesh' GROUP BY T1.artist_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each artist from Bangladesh along with the total number of songs for each artist.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T1.artist_name FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T2.gender = 'Male';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the song name and artist name for all songs performed by male artists.

sql: SELECT T2.g_name FROM song AS T1 JOIN genre AS T2 ON T1.genre_is = T2.g_name WHERE T1.resolution = 1080;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the genres of songs that are available in 1080 resolution.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name FROM artist AS T1 JOIN genre AS T2 ON T1.preferred_genre = T2.g_name WHERE T2.rating = '9';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artists whose preferred genre has a top rating of '9'.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE country = 'India' UNION SELECT artist_name FROM song WHERE genre_is = 'folk';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of artists who are either from India or have a song in the 'folk' genre.

sql: SELECT artist_name, COUNT(*) AS song_count FROM song GROUP BY artist_name ORDER BY song_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single artist with the most songs, along with their total song count.

sql: SELECT T1.g_name, AVG(T2.rating) FROM genre AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.g_name = T2.genre_is GROUP BY T1.g_name HAVING AVG(T2.rating) > 6;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing music genres that have an average song rating greater than 6, showing the genre name and its calculated average rating.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T2.artist_name, T3.duration FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name JOIN files AS T3 ON T1.f_id = T3.f_id WHERE T2.gender = 'Female';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the song name, artist name, and duration for all songs performed by female artists.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T1.country FROM artist AS T1 WHERE T1.country = 'India' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.artist_name = T1.artist_name);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and country of artists from India who have at least one song in the database.

sql: SELECT song_name, artist_name, rating FROM song WHERE rating > (SELECT AVG(rating) FROM song);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name, artist, and rating for all songs with a rating that is higher than the average rating across all songs.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE country = 'Bangladesh' INTERSECT SELECT artist_name FROM song WHERE genre_is = 'folk';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a folk artist who is also from Bangladesh.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T1.song_name, T1.rating FROM song AS T1 JOIN (SELECT artist_name, MAX(rating) AS max_rating FROM song GROUP BY artist_name) AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name AND T1.rating = T2.max_rating;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying the highest-rated song(s) for each artist, showing the artist's name, the song's name, and its top rating.

sql: SELECT T1.g_name FROM genre AS T1 WHERE (SELECT count(*) FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.genre_is = T1.g_name) >= (SELECT count(*) FROM artist AS T3 WHERE T3.preferred_genre = T1.g_name);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of genres where the number of associated songs is greater than or equal to the number of artists who prefer that genre.

sql: SELECT artist_name, country FROM artist WHERE gender = 'Female' UNION SELECT artist_name, country FROM song WHERE rating > 7;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names and countries for artists who are either female or have a song with a rating greater than 7.

sql: SELECT T1.country, T1.gender, COUNT(T2.song_name) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name GROUP BY T1.country, T1.gender;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the total count of songs for each combination of an artist's country and gender.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name FROM song AS T1 INTERSECT SELECT T2.artist_name FROM artist AS T2 JOIN genre AS T3 ON T2.preferred_genre = T3.g_name WHERE T3.rating = '8';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artists who both have a recorded song and whose preferred genre has a rating of '8'.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T1.artist_name FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name JOIN genre AS T3 ON T2.preferred_genre = T3.g_name WHERE T3.rating >= '8';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing song names and their artists, specifically for artists whose preferred genre has a high rating of 8 or greater.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist EXCEPT SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE country = 'India';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all artists who are not from India.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, AVG(T1.resolution) FROM song AS T1 WHERE T1.country = 'India' GROUP BY T1.artist_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each artist from India alongside the average resolution of their songs.

sql: SELECT T1.g_name, COUNT(T2.song_name) FROM genre AS T1 LEFT JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.g_name = T2.genre_is GROUP BY T1.g_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each music genre and the total count of songs for that genre.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name FROM artist AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.artist_name = T1.artist_name AND T2.genre_is != T1.preferred_genre) AND T1.artist_name IN (SELECT artist_name FROM song);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of artists who have only ever produced songs that match their single, preferred genre.

sql: SELECT T2.genre_is FROM song AS T2 GROUP BY T2.genre_is ORDER BY AVG(T2.rating) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single genre with the highest average song rating.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T2.country = 'Bangladesh' AND SUBSTR(T1.releasedate, -4) > '2000';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of a song by a Bangladeshi artist that was released after the year 2000.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE country = 'Bangladesh' UNION SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE preferred_genre = 'tagore' EXCEPT SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE gender = 'Male';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of non-male artists who are either from Bangladesh or prefer the 'tagore' genre.

sql: SELECT T1.country, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.artist_name), COUNT(T2.song_name) FROM artist AS T1 LEFT JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name GROUP BY T1.country;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country along with the number of unique artists and the total count of songs associated with those artists.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name FROM song AS T1 JOIN genre AS T2 ON T1.genre_is = T2.g_name WHERE T1.rating < T2.rating;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of songs that have a lower rating than their respective genre's rating.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T1.gender, T2.g_name, T2.rating FROM artist AS T1, genre AS T2 WHERE T1.country = 'Bangladesh' AND T2.most_popular_in = 'Bangladesh';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows showing every combination of artists from Bangladesh and genres popular in Bangladesh, listing the artist's name and gender alongside the genre's name and rating.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.artist_name FROM song AS T1 JOIN files AS T2 ON T1.f_id = T2.f_id WHERE T2.formats = 'mp4' AND T1.artist_name IN (SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE country = 'India');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of artists from India who have at least one song available in MP4 format.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name GROUP BY T1.artist_name HAVING MIN(T2.rating) > 4 AND MAX(T2.rating) < 9;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying artists whose entire song catalog has a rating greater than 4 but less than 9.

sql: SELECT * FROM farm;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, providing a complete annual census of various livestock populations, including horses, cattle, pigs, and sheep/goats for each recorded year.

sql: SELECT Official_Name, Population FROM city ORDER BY Population DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each city's official name and its population, ordered from the most populated city to the least.

sql: SELECT Theme, Hosts FROM farm_competition WHERE Year = 2013;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the theme and hosts for the farm competition that was held in the year 2013.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM city;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all cities in the database.

sql: SELECT Farm_ID, Rank FROM competition_record WHERE Competition_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the final rank for each farm that participated in competition number 1.

sql: SELECT * FROM city WHERE Status = 'Village';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing all available details for cities that are classified as a 'Village'.

sql: SELECT Farm_ID, Year, Total_Horses FROM farm WHERE Total_Horses > 5500;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the Farm ID, Year, and Total Horses for any farm record where the total number of horses exceeded 5,500.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Farm_ID FROM competition_record;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique IDs of each farm that has participated in a competition.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme, T2.Official_Name FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_city_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T1.Host_city_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the theme of the farm competition and the official name of the host city, filtered specifically for the city with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT Year, Theme FROM farm_competition ORDER BY Year ASC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the year and theme for each farm competition, ordered chronologically from the earliest year.

sql: SELECT T2.Official_Name, COUNT(T1.Competition_ID) AS NumberOfCompetitions FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_city_ID = T2.City_ID GROUP BY T2.Official_Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the official name of each host city and the total number of farm competitions held there.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, COUNT(T1.Competition_ID) AS CompetitionsParticipated FROM competition_record AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Farm_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each farm's ID along with a count of the total number of competitions that farm has participated in.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Year, T1.Total_Cattle FROM farm AS T1 JOIN competition_record AS T2 ON T1.Farm_ID = T2.Farm_ID WHERE T2.Rank = 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying farms that have won a competition (Rank 1), showing their ID, the year of the farm's record, and its total number of cattle.

sql: SELECT AVG(Total_Horses), AVG(Total_Cattle), AVG(Pigs) FROM farm WHERE Year > 1927;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average total number of horses, cattle, and pigs across all farms for years after 1927.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme, T2.Farm_ID, T2.Rank FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN competition_record AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID ORDER BY T1.Year, T2.Rank;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the theme, participating farm ID, and achieved rank for various farm competitions, ordered chronologically by year and then by rank.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T1.Theme, T2.Official_Name FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_city_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T2.Status = 'Village';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing farm competitions that were hosted in a village, showing the year, theme, and official name of the host city for each event.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Rank FROM competition_record AS T1 WHERE T1.Competition_ID = (SELECT Competition_ID FROM farm_competition WHERE Theme = 'Codehunters');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the ID and rank for each farm that participated in the 'Codehunters' themed competition.

sql: SELECT * FROM farm WHERE Oxen > (SELECT AVG(Oxen) FROM farm);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing all farm details for farms that have more oxen than the overall average across all farms.

sql: SELECT T1.Official_Name, T1.Population FROM city AS T1 JOIN farm_competition AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_city_ID WHERE T2.Hosts LIKE '%Wang%';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the official name and population of the city that hosted a farm competition where a host's name includes 'Wang'.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Year, T1.Total_Cattle + T1.Pigs + T1.Sheep_and_Goats AS Total_Livestock FROM farm AS T1 ORDER BY Total_Livestock DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each farm's ID and year with a calculated total number of livestock, ordered from the highest total livestock count to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Rank FROM competition_record AS T1 WHERE T1.Farm_ID IN (SELECT Farm_ID FROM farm WHERE Year = 1928);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the Farm ID and competition rank for farms associated with the year 1928.

sql: SELECT Farm_ID FROM competition_record WHERE Rank <= 2;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the IDs of farms that achieved a first or second place ranking in a competition.

sql: SELECT T1.Host_city_ID, T1.Hosts FROM farm_competition AS T1 WHERE T1.Year IN (2005, 2006);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the host city ID and the names of the hosts for farm competitions that took place in either 2005 or 2006.

sql: SELECT * FROM city WHERE Population > 1500 AND Area_km_2 < 10;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for any city that is compact yet populous, specifically those with a population over 1,500 and an area less than 10 square kilometers.

sql: SELECT Year, COUNT(Farm_ID) FROM farm GROUP BY Year HAVING COUNT(Farm_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each year alongside the total number of farms recorded for that year.

sql: SELECT T2.Official_Name, T2.Population, T1.Theme FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_city_ID = T2.City_ID JOIN competition_record AS T3 ON T1.Competition_ID = T3.Competition_ID WHERE T3.Rank = 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the official name and population of cities that hosted winning (rank 1) farm competitions, along with each competition's theme.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Year FROM farm AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM competition_record AS T2 WHERE T2.Farm_ID = T1.Farm_ID AND T2.Rank = 1);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID and founding year for each farm that has achieved a first-place rank in at least one competition.

sql: SELECT T1.Official_Name FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT Host_city_ID FROM farm_competition WHERE YEAR = 2013) INTERSECT SELECT T2.Official_Name FROM city AS T2 WHERE T2.Status = 'Town';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the official name of the location that is both classified as a 'Town' and also hosted a farm competition in the year 2013.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID FROM farm AS T1 WHERE T1.Year = 1928 EXCEPT SELECT T2.Farm_ID FROM competition_record AS T2 WHERE T2.Rank != 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID of a farm established in 1928 which has only ever achieved a rank of 2 in competitions.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Year, T1.Total_Horses, (SELECT AVG(Total_Horses) FROM farm) AS Avg_Total_Horses FROM farm AS T1 WHERE T1.Total_Horses < (SELECT AVG(Total_Horses) FROM farm);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying farms with a total number of horses below the overall average, showing each farm's ID, year, and horse count alongside the overall average for comparison.

sql: SELECT T2.Official_Name, T3.Farm_ID, T3.Rank, T4.Total_Cattle FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_city_ID = T2.City_ID JOIN competition_record AS T3 ON T1.Competition_ID = T3.Competition_ID JOIN farm AS T4 ON T3.Farm_ID = T4.Farm_ID WHERE T1.Year = 2013;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows detailing the results of the 2013 farm competition, including the host city's name, each participating farm's ID, their rank, and their total number of cattle.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T1.Theme FROM farm_competition AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM competition_record AS T2 WHERE T2.Competition_ID = T1.Competition_ID) >= 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the year and theme for farm competitions that have three or more associated records.

sql: SELECT T1.Official_Name FROM city AS T1 JOIN farm_competition AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_city_ID GROUP BY T1.Official_Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Competition_ID) = (SELECT MAX(Cnt) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Competition_ID) AS Cnt FROM farm_competition GROUP BY Host_city_ID));
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the official name of the city that has hosted the most farm competitions.

sql: SELECT T2.Pigs FROM competition_record AS T1 JOIN farm AS T2 ON T1.Farm_ID = T2.Farm_ID WHERE T1.Rank = 1 AND T1.Competition_ID IN (SELECT Competition_ID FROM farm_competition WHERE Year = 2013);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the number of pigs from the farm that won a competition in the year 2013.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Hosts FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN competition_record AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID JOIN farm AS T3 ON T2.Farm_ID = T3.Farm_ID WHERE T3.Oxen > 850;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique hosts of competitions attended by farms that have more than 850 oxen.

sql: SELECT T1.Official_Name FROM city AS T1 JOIN farm_competition AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_city_ID EXCEPT SELECT Official_Name FROM city WHERE Status = 'Town';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the official names of cities that have hosted a farm competition, excluding any that are classified as a 'Town'.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T2.Theme FROM competition_record AS T1 JOIN farm_competition AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID WHERE T1.Farm_ID = 2 UNION SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T2.Theme FROM competition_record AS T1 JOIN farm_competition AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID WHERE T1.Farm_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the farm ID and the corresponding competition theme for all competitions involving either Farm ID 2 or Farm ID 3.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Year, T1.Total_Cattle / T1.Working_Horses AS Cattle_Per_Horse FROM farm AS T1 WHERE T1.Farm_ID IN (SELECT Farm_ID FROM competition_record) ORDER BY Cattle_Per_Horse DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows for farms with a competition record, showing their ID, year, and the ratio of cattle per working horse, sorted from the highest ratio to the lowest.

sql: WITH RankedFarms AS (SELECT Farm_ID, Rank, Competition_ID FROM competition_record WHERE Rank = 1) SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Year, T2.Theme FROM farm AS T1 JOIN RankedFarms AS RF ON T1.Farm_ID = RF.Farm_ID JOIN farm_competition AS T2 ON RF.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying first-place winning farms, showing each farm's ID, its founding year, and the theme of the competition it won.

sql: SELECT T1.Official_Name, T1.Population FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.City_ID = (SELECT Host_city_ID FROM farm_competition WHERE Competition_ID = (SELECT Competition_ID FROM competition_record WHERE Rank = 1 ORDER BY Farm_ID DESC LIMIT 1));
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the official name and population of the host city for the winning competition record associated with the highest farm ID.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Year FROM farm AS T1 WHERE (T1.Total_Cattle + T1.Pigs) > (SELECT AVG(Total_Cattle + Pigs) FROM farm);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the Farm ID and Year for farms where the combined count of cattle and pigs is greater than the average across all farms.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T1.Theme, T1.Hosts, (SELECT COUNT(T2.Farm_ID) FROM competition_record AS T2 WHERE T2.Competition_ID = T1.Competition_ID) AS Participant_Count FROM farm_competition AS T1 ORDER BY Participant_Count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each farm competition's year, theme, and hosts, along with the total number of participating farms, ordered from most to least popular.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID FROM competition_record AS T1 JOIN farm_competition AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID WHERE T2.Year = 2013 UNION SELECT T3.Farm_ID FROM farm AS T3 WHERE T3.Year = 1929;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique IDs of farms that either participated in a competition in the year 2013 or were established in 1929.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Competition_ID, T1.Rank FROM competition_record AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Competition_ID, Host_city_ID FROM farm_competition WHERE Hosts LIKE '%Alicia Keys%') AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the Farm ID, Competition ID, and Rank for farms that participated in competitions hosted by 'Alicia Keys'.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme, T1.Year, T3.Official_Name FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN competition_record AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID JOIN city AS T3 ON T1.Host_city_ID = T3.City_ID WHERE T2.Farm_ID = (SELECT Farm_ID FROM farm WHERE Year = 1928);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the theme, year, and official host city name for competitions attended by the farm that was established in 1928.

sql: SELECT T1.Official_Name, T1.Population FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.Population > (SELECT AVG(Population) FROM city WHERE Status = 'Village');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the official name and population for all cities that are more populous than the average village.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Year, T1.Total_Cattle FROM farm AS T1 WHERE T1.Farm_ID IN (SELECT Farm_ID FROM competition_record GROUP BY Farm_ID HAVING COUNT(*) > 0) AND T1.Total_Cattle > 8000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the farm ID, year, and total cattle count for farms that have participated in competitions and had a herd size of over 8000 cattle in a specific year.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Year FROM farm AS T1 WHERE T1.Total_Horses > (SELECT T2.Total_Horses FROM farm AS T2 WHERE T2.Year = 1927);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the Farm ID and Year for years when the farm's horse count was greater than its count in 1927.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T1.Theme, T1.Hosts FROM farm_competition AS T1 WHERE T1.Competition_ID NOT IN (SELECT Competition_ID FROM competition_record WHERE Rank > 2);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the year, theme, and hosts for farm competitions where no participant ranked lower than second place.

sql: SELECT c.Official_Name, f.Theme, f.Year FROM city AS c CROSS JOIN farm_competition AS f WHERE c.City_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing every recorded farm competition's theme and year, with each entry associated with the single city identified as 'Grand Falls/Grand-Sault'.

sql: SELECT Airline, Abbreviation FROM airlines WHERE Country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the full name and abbreviation for all airlines based in the USA.

sql: SELECT AirportName FROM airports WHERE City = 'Aberdeen ';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all airports located in the city of 'Aberdeen'.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM flights;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total count of all flights.

sql: SELECT FlightNo FROM flights WHERE Airline = 1;
result_summary: Returns 100 rows listing the flight numbers for all flights operated by the airline with ID 1.

sql: SELECT * FROM flights WHERE SourceAirport = ' ASY';
result_summary: Returns 19 rows containing the complete flight details (Airline, Flight Number, Source, and Destination) for all flights departing from the 'ASY' airport.

sql: SELECT Airline, Abbreviation FROM airlines WHERE Airline = 'Delta Airlines';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the airline name and abbreviation for 'Delta Airlines'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Country FROM airports;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique country name found in the airports data.

sql: SELECT T2.Airline FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid WHERE T1.FlightNo = 28;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the airlines that operate flight number 28.

sql: SELECT * FROM airports WHERE AirportCode = 'ABR';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with all available information for the airport identified by the code 'ABR'.

sql: SELECT AirportName FROM airports ORDER BY AirportName ASC;
result_summary: Returns 100 rows listing the names of airports, sorted in alphabetical order from A to Z.

sql: SELECT T2.Airline, count(*) FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid GROUP BY T2.Airline;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, listing each airline alongside the total number of flights it operates.

sql: SELECT T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode GROUP BY T1.SourceAirport HAVING count(*) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 100 rows listing the names of each unique airport that serves as a source for at least one flight.

sql: SELECT T2.City, T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode WHERE T1.FlightNo = 29;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the city and full name of the source airport for flight number 29.

sql: SELECT T1.FlightNo, T2.AirportName AS Source, T3.AirportName AS Destination FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T3.AirportCode;
result_summary: Returns 1200 rows listing each flight number along with the full names of its source and destination airports, translated from their airport codes.

sql: SELECT T1.FlightNo, T1.SourceAirport, T1.DestAirport FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid WHERE T2.Airline = 'United Airlines';
result_summary: Returns 100 rows listing flights operated by United Airlines, showing each flight's number, source airport, and destination airport.

sql: SELECT City, COUNT(AirportCode) FROM airports GROUP BY City;
result_summary: Returns 85 rows listing each city along with the total number of airports located in it.

sql: SELECT AirportName, City FROM airports WHERE AirportCode IN (SELECT DISTINCT TRIM(DestAirport) FROM flights);
result_summary: Returns 100 rows listing the name and city for each airport that is a destination in the flights table.

sql: SELECT T1.FlightNo, T1.Airline FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode WHERE T2.City = 'Aberdeen ';
result_summary: Returns 21 rows listing the flight number and airline for all flights originating from the city of Aberdeen.

sql: SELECT T2.Airline FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid GROUP BY T1.Airline HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of airlines that have operated more than one flight.

sql: SELECT T1.FlightNo, T2.FlightNo FROM flights AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.SourceAirport = T2.DestAirport AND T1.DestAirport = T2.SourceAirport WHERE T1.FlightNo < T2.FlightNo;
result_summary: Returns 668 rows listing pairs of flight numbers that represent round trips, where one flight's route (e.g., Airport A to B) is the exact reverse of the other's (Airport B to A).

sql: SELECT Airline AS Name FROM airlines WHERE Country = 'USA' UNION ALL SELECT AirportName AS Name FROM airports WHERE Country = 'United States ';
result_summary: Returns 112 rows, combining the names of all airlines and airports located in the United States into a single list.

sql: SELECT T2.Airline, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.DestAirport) FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid GROUP BY T2.Airline;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, with each row showing an airline and the total count of unique destinations it serves.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Abbreviation FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the unique abbreviations for each airline that has a recorded flight in the dataset.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.AirportName, T3.City FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T3.AirportCode WHERE T2.uid = 1;
result_summary: Returns 62 rows listing the unique destination airports and their cities for all flights operated by a specific airline (with ID 1).

sql: SELECT AirportName FROM airports WHERE AirportCode IN (SELECT DISTINCT TRIM(DestAirport) FROM flights) AND AirportCode NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT TRIM(SourceAirport) FROM flights);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.Airline, COUNT(*) AS flight_count FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid GROUP BY T2.Airline HAVING COUNT(*) = (SELECT MAX(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS c FROM flights GROUP BY Airline));
result_summary: Returns 12 rows identifying the airlines that operated the maximum number of flights, showing the airline's name and its total flight count.

sql: SELECT T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.Airline = T3.uid WHERE T3.Airline = 'United Airlines' INTERSECT SELECT T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.Airline = T3.uid WHERE T3.Airline = 'United Airlines';
result_summary: Returns 54 rows listing the names of airports that serve as both a departure and arrival point for flights operated by United Airlines.

sql: SELECT T3.Airline, count(*) FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.Airline = T3.uid WHERE T2.City = 'Aberdeen ' GROUP BY T3.Airline;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each airline that operates flights originating from Aberdeen, along with the total count of their flights from that city.

sql: SELECT T2.AirportName, COUNT(*) AS arrival_count FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode GROUP BY T2.AirportName ORDER BY arrival_count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top 3 airports by their total number of arriving flights, ordered from most arrivals to least.

sql: SELECT City, SUM(flight_count) AS total_flights FROM (SELECT T2.City, count(*) AS flight_count FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode GROUP BY T2.City UNION ALL SELECT T2.City, count(*) AS flight_count FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode GROUP BY T2.City) AS T GROUP BY City;
result_summary: Returns 85 rows listing each city along with the total count of flights that either departed from or arrived in that city.

sql: SELECT T2.Airline FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid WHERE TRIM(T1.DestAirport) IN (SELECT AirportCode FROM airports WHERE AirportName = 'Phillips AAF ');
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of airlines that operate flights landing at the 'Phillips AAF' airport.

sql: SELECT T1.Airline FROM airlines AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT T2.AirportCode FROM airports AS T2 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT T3.FlightNo FROM flights AS T3 WHERE T3.Airline = T1.uid AND TRIM(T3.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode));
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Airline FROM airlines WHERE Country = 'USA' EXCEPT SELECT T3.Airline FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.Airline = T3.uid WHERE T2.AirportName = 'Dyess AFB ';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of US-based airlines that do not have any flights originating from Dyess AFB.

sql: SELECT T1.FlightNo, T4.Airline FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T3.AirportCode JOIN airlines AS T4 ON T1.Airline = T4.uid WHERE T2.City = T3.City AND T2.AirportCode != T3.AirportCode;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the flight number and airline name for flights that travel between two different airports located within the same city.

sql: SELECT Incoming.AirportName FROM (SELECT T2.AirportName, count(*) AS cnt FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode GROUP BY T2.AirportName) AS Incoming JOIN (SELECT T2.AirportName, count(*) AS cnt FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode GROUP BY T2.AirportName) AS Outgoing ON Incoming.AirportName = Outgoing.AirportName WHERE Incoming.cnt > Outgoing.cnt;

sql: SELECT T3.Airline, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.City) AS cities_served FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.Airline = T3.uid GROUP BY T3.Airline;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each airline alongside the total count of distinct cities it flies from.

sql: SELECT T1.Abbreviation, T2.Abbreviation FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.uid < T2.uid WHERE T1.uid IN (SELECT Airline FROM flights WHERE TRIM(SourceAirport) = 'APG') AND T2.uid IN (SELECT Airline FROM flights WHERE TRIM(SourceAirport) = 'APG');
result_summary: Returns 21 rows listing all unique pairs of airline abbreviations for airlines that both operate flights originating from the 'APG' airport.

sql: SELECT T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode GROUP BY T1.SourceAirport HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Airline) = 1;

sql: SELECT AVG(flight_counts) FROM (SELECT COUNT(T1.FlightNo) AS flight_counts FROM flights AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Airline);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of flights operated per airline.

sql: SELECT T3.AirportName AS AirportA, T4.AirportName AS AirportB FROM flights AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.SourceAirport = T2.DestAirport AND T1.DestAirport = T2.SourceAirport JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T3.AirportCode JOIN airports AS T4 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T4.AirportCode WHERE T1.FlightNo < T2.FlightNo;
result_summary: Returns 668 rows, each identifying a unique pair of airports (AirportA and AirportB) that have round-trip flight service between them.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.AirportName, T4.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T3.AirportCode JOIN airports AS T4 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T4.AirportCode WHERE T2.Airline = 'United Airlines';
result_summary: Returns 98 rows listing the unique flight routes operated by United Airlines, showing the names of the source and destination airports for each route.

sql: SELECT T2.Airline, T3.AirportName, COUNT(*) AS flight_count FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T3.AirportCode GROUP BY T1.Airline, T1.SourceAirport ORDER BY flight_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single airline and source airport combination that has the highest number of departing flights.

sql: SELECT AirportName FROM airports WHERE AirportCode IN (SELECT DISTINCT TRIM(DestAirport) FROM flights EXCEPT SELECT DISTINCT TRIM(SourceAirport) FROM flights);

sql: SELECT T2.Airline FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid WHERE TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = 'ASY' AND T1.Airline NOT IN (SELECT Airline FROM flights WHERE TRIM(SourceAirport) = 'ASY');
