sql: SELECT T3.Airline, COUNT(*) FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.Airline = T3.uid WHERE T2.AirportName LIKE '%Municipal%' GROUP BY T3.Airline;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each airline and the total count of their flights that originate from airports with 'Municipal' in their name.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode WHERE T1.Airline IN (SELECT uid FROM airlines WHERE Country != 'USA');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Airline, T1.DestAirport, T1.c FROM (SELECT T2.Airline, T3.DestAirport, count(*) AS c FROM airlines AS T2 JOIN flights AS T3 ON T2.uid = T3.Airline GROUP BY T2.Airline, T3.DestAirport) AS T1 WHERE T1.c = (SELECT MAX(T4.c) FROM (SELECT T5.Airline, T5.DestAirport, count(*) AS c FROM airlines AS T6 JOIN flights AS T5 ON T6.uid = T5.Airline GROUP BY T6.Airline, T5.DestAirport) AS T4 WHERE T4.Airline = T1.Airline);

sql: SELECT T1.Airline FROM airlines AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT T2.City FROM (SELECT DISTINCT City FROM airports) AS T2 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT * FROM flights AS T3 JOIN airports AS T4 ON TRIM(T3.SourceAirport) = T4.AirportCode WHERE T3.Airline = T1.uid AND T4.City = T2.City));

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T4.Airline FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T3.AirportCode JOIN airlines AS T4 ON T1.Airline = T4.uid WHERE T2.City = 'Aberdeen ' AND T2.Country != T3.Country;

sql: SELECT T2.AirportName, COUNT(*) AS flight_count FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.Airline = T3.uid WHERE T3.Airline = 'United Airlines' GROUP BY T2.AirportName ORDER BY flight_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single departure airport with the most flights for 'United Airlines', showing the airport's name and the total flight count.

sql: SELECT name, meter_100 FROM swimmer WHERE Nationality = 'Poland';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and 100-meter time for each swimmer with Polish nationality.

sql: SELECT name, Capacity FROM stadium WHERE City = 'Baku';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and capacity of stadiums located in the city of 'Baku'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM event WHERE Year = '2016';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the event that occurred in the year 2016.

sql: SELECT name, Nationality FROM swimmer ORDER BY name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each swimmer's name and their nationality, sorted alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT name, Capacity FROM stadium ORDER BY Capacity DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing stadium names and their corresponding capacities, ordered from the highest capacity to the lowest.

sql: SELECT Name, Year FROM event ORDER BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and year of each event, ordered chronologically from the earliest to the most recent.

sql: SELECT name, Nationality FROM swimmer WHERE meter_100 = 57.31;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and nationality of swimmers who achieved a 100-meter time of exactly 57.31.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM stadium WHERE Opening_year = 2005;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and country for all stadiums that opened in the year 2005.

sql: SELECT Swimmer_ID FROM record WHERE Result = 'Win';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the IDs of all swimmers who have a recorded result of 'Win'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Nationality FROM swimmer;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each unique nationality represented by the swimmers in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Win';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of all swimmers who have won a competition.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM event AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.Country = 'Spain';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of events held in stadiums located in Spain.

sql: SELECT Nationality, COUNT(*) FROM swimmer GROUP BY Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each nationality and the corresponding total count of swimmers from that country.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.ID) FROM event AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.ID GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each stadium's name and the corresponding total number of events held there.

sql: SELECT Country, AVG(Capacity) FROM stadium GROUP BY Country;

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.Name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID WHERE T3.Year = '2016';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of swimmers and the corresponding events they competed in during the year 2016.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows providing a unique list of swimmer names who have at least one associated record.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T2.Capacity FROM event AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.Name = 'FINA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and capacity of the stadium that hosts the 'FINA' event.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.ID) FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each swimmer by name along with the total count of records they hold.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'Australia';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of Australian swimmers who have set official records.

sql: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM stadium GROUP BY City;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each city and the total number of stadiums located within it.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM event AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.Capacity > 70000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of an event held in a high-capacity stadium with more than 70,000 seats.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.Name, T2.Result FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each swimmer's name, the name of the event they participated in, and their result for that event.

sql: SELECT T2.Opening_year FROM event AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the opening year of the stadium that hosted the event with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Nationality FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Loss';

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID JOIN stadium AS T4 ON T3.Stadium_ID = T4.ID WHERE T4.Opening_year = 2005;

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.Name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID WHERE T2.Result <> 'Win';

sql: SELECT T4.name FROM record AS T1 JOIN swimmer AS T2 ON T1.Swimmer_ID = T2.ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T1.Event_ID = T3.ID JOIN stadium AS T4 ON T3.Stadium_ID = T4.ID WHERE T2.ID = 3;

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.Jan) FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T1.Regional_Population > 8000000;

sql: SELECT T1.Venue FROM match AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.Match_ID = T2.Match_ID WHERE T2.Year = 2009;

sql: SELECT avg(T1.GDP), sum(T1.Regional_Population) FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T2.Dec > 19;

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.GDP FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = 'Friendly match' ORDER BY T1.GDP DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year IN (2009, 2010) GROUP BY T1.City HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Year) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the city that hosted an event in both 2009 and 2010.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.GDP) FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT T2.Host_City FROM hosting_city AS T2 JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification');

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.Regional_Population FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T1.Regional_Population > (SELECT AVG(Regional_Population) FROM city) GROUP BY T1.City_ID;

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = 'Friendly match' EXCEPT SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the city that hosted a 'Friendly match' but did not host a '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification' match.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T1.Competition, T1.Match_Count FROM (SELECT T2.Year, T3.Competition, COUNT(T3.Match_ID) AS Match_Count FROM hosting_city AS T2 JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID GROUP BY T2.Year, T3.Competition) AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Year, MAX(Match_Count) AS Max_Count FROM (SELECT T2.Year, T3.Competition, COUNT(T3.Match_ID) AS Match_Count FROM hosting_city AS T2 JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID GROUP BY T2.Year, T3.Competition) AS T4 GROUP BY Year) AS T5 ON T1.Year = T5.Year AND T1.Match_Count = T5.Max_Count;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying, for each year, the competition that held the most matches and the total count of those matches.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.GDP, COUNT(T3.Match_ID) AS Num_Matches FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID JOIN hosting_city AS T3 ON T1.City_ID = T3.Host_City WHERE T2.Dec < 20 GROUP BY T1.City, T1.GDP;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows for cities with a December temperature below 20, listing each city's name, its GDP, and the total number of matches it has hosted.

sql: SELECT T1.City, CASE WHEN T2.Year = 2008 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS Hosted_In_2008 FROM city AS T1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT * FROM hosting_city WHERE Year = 2008) AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City GROUP BY T1.City_ID;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each city and indicating with a 'Yes' or 'No' if it hosted an event in the year 2008.

sql: SELECT Competition, SUM(CAST(SUBSTR(Score, 1, INSTR(Score, '-') - 1) AS INTEGER) + CAST(SUBSTR(Score, INSTR(Score, '-') + 1) AS INTEGER)) AS TotalGoals FROM match GROUP BY Competition ORDER BY TotalGoals DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single competition with the highest total number of goals scored across all its matches, along with that total goal count.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.GDP FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.GDP > (SELECT GDP FROM city WHERE City = 'Hangzhou ( Zhejiang )') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM hosting_city AS T2 WHERE T2.Host_City = T1.City_ID);

sql: SELECT T3.* FROM temperature AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID JOIN hosting_city AS T4 ON T2.City_ID = T4.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T4.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T1.Aug > T1.Jul;

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year = 2009 UNION SELECT City FROM city WHERE GDP < 700;

sql: SELECT T1.City, MAX(T2.Jan, T2.Feb, T2.Mar, T2.Apr, T2.Jun, T2.Jul, T2.Aug, T2.Sep, T2.Oct, T2.Nov, T2.Dec) AS Max_Temp, MIN(T2.Jan, T2.Feb, T2.Mar, T2.Apr, T2.Jun, T2.Jul, T2.Aug, T2.Sep, T2.Oct, T2.Nov, T2.Dec) AS Min_Temp FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT Host_City FROM hosting_city);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing cities that have served as a host city, along with their highest and lowest monthly average temperatures recorded during the year.

sql: SELECT SUM(T1.Regional_Population) FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT T2.Host_City FROM hosting_city AS T2 JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = 'Friendly match');
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total regional population for all cities that have hosted a 'Friendly match'.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT Host_City FROM hosting_city) AND T1.GDP > (SELECT AVG(GDP) FROM city);

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T2.City FROM hosting_city AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_City = T2.City_ID WHERE T1.Year = (SELECT Year FROM hosting_city GROUP BY Year ORDER BY COUNT(Match_ID) DESC LIMIT 1) LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the year with the highest number of matches and the corresponding host city for that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Match_ID, T2.Match_ID, T1.Host_City FROM hosting_city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.Host_City = T2.Host_City AND T1.Match_ID < T2.Match_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a pair of distinct matches that occurred in the same host city, showing the two match IDs and the shared city ID.

sql: SELECT T1.City, ABS(T2.Jul - T2.Jan) AS Temp_Diff FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Host_City FROM hosting_city) ORDER BY Temp_Diff DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the host city with the largest absolute temperature difference between July and January, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: WITH YearHost AS (SELECT DISTINCT Year, Host_City FROM hosting_city) SELECT T1.Year, SUM(T2.GDP) FROM YearHost AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_City = T2.City_ID GROUP BY T1.Year;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each year an event was hosted, along with the total combined GDP of all host cities for that year.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification' AND T1.Regional_Population > 8000000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of large cities (with a regional population over 8 million) that hosted a match for the '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification'.

sql: SELECT T1.City, MAX(T3.Date) AS Last_Match_Date FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID GROUP BY T1.City;

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = 'Friendly match' AND T1.Regional_Population > (SELECT Regional_Population FROM city WHERE City = 'Hangzhou ( Zhejiang )');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the city that has hosted a 'Friendly match' and has a regional population greater than that of Hangzhou (Zhejiang).

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification' INTERSECT SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year = 2009;

sql: SELECT SUBSTR(T1.Hanyu_Pinyin, 1, 1) AS Pinyin_Initial, AVG(T1.Regional_Population) FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City GROUP BY Pinyin_Initial;

sql: SELECT T1.Committee FROM election AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Committee HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2;

sql: SELECT T2.Party FROM election AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.Committee = 'Appropriations' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Party FROM election AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.First_Elected = 2002.0;

sql: SELECT Delegate FROM election WHERE First_Elected = (SELECT MIN(First_Elected) FROM election);

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate, T1.Committee, T2.Party, T2.Year FROM election AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party = T2.Party_ID ORDER BY T2.Year DESC, T1.Delegate ASC;

sql: SELECT T2.Party, T2.Governor FROM party AS T2 WHERE Party_ID IN (SELECT Party FROM election GROUP BY Party HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Delegate) AS c FROM election GROUP BY Party) AS T1;

sql: SELECT Delegate, Committee FROM election WHERE First_Elected = 2002.0 INTERSECT SELECT Delegate, Committee FROM election WHERE Party = 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate, T1.First_Elected FROM election AS T1 WHERE T1.Party = (SELECT Party_ID FROM party WHERE Governor = 'Peter Vallone');

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate, T1.Committee FROM election AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Party_ID FROM party WHERE Year = 1998.0) AS T2 ON T1.Party = T2.Party_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the delegate's name and their assigned committee for all delegates belonging to a political party active in the year 1998.

sql: SELECT T2.Party FROM party AS T2 JOIN election AS T1 ON T2.Party_ID = T1.Party GROUP BY T2.Party_ID, T2.Party HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Committee) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate, T2.Party, T2.Year FROM election AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.First_Elected > 2002.0;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Governor FROM party AS T2 JOIN election AS T1 ON T2.Party_ID = T1.Party WHERE T1.District = 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate, T2.Party FROM election AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party = T2.Party_ID WHERE T2.US_Senate <> '(no election)';

sql: SELECT T1.Headquarter, SUM(T1.Revenue) FROM Manufacturers AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Headquarter HAVING SUM(T1.Revenue) > (SELECT AVG(Revenue) FROM Manufacturers);

sql: SELECT T1.*, T2.AvgProdPrice FROM Manufacturers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Manufacturer, AVG(Price) AS AvgProdPrice FROM Products GROUP BY Manufacturer) AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Manufacturer WHERE T1.Revenue > 100;

sql: WITH ManufacturerProductCounts AS (SELECT Manufacturer, COUNT(*) AS PCount FROM Products GROUP BY Manufacturer) SELECT T1.Name FROM Manufacturers AS T1 JOIN ManufacturerProductCounts AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Manufacturer WHERE T2.PCount = (SELECT MAX(PCount) FROM ManufacturerProductCounts);

sql: SELECT T1.Party, AVG(T3.Num_of_Audience) FROM people AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Affirmative OR T1.People_ID = T2.Negative JOIN debate AS T3 ON T2.Debate_ID = T3.Debate_ID GROUP BY T1.Party;

result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single venue that hosted the largest total audience across all debates.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age, T1.Party FROM people AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Affirmative WHERE T2.Debate_ID = 1 UNION SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age, T1.Party FROM people AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Negative WHERE T2.Debate_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows with the name, age, and party of the participants from both the affirmative and negative sides of the debate with ID 1.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID NOT IN (SELECT Affirmative FROM debate_people WHERE If_Affirmative_Win = 'F');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of people who have never lost a debate while arguing the affirmative position.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Party FROM people AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM debate_people AS T2 JOIN debate AS T3 ON T2.Debate_ID = T3.Debate_ID WHERE (T2.Affirmative = T1.People_ID OR T2.Negative = T1.People_ID) AND T3.Num_of_Audience < (SELECT AVG(Num_of_Audience) FROM debate));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and party of people who have participated in at least one debate with a below-average audience size.

sql: SELECT T.Debate_ID, AVG(T.Age) AS Avg_Participant_Age FROM (SELECT T1.Debate_ID, T2.Age FROM debate_people AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Affirmative = T2.People_ID UNION ALL SELECT T1.Debate_ID, T2.Age FROM debate_people AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Negative = T2.People_ID) AS T GROUP BY T.Debate_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, with each row listing a unique debate ID and the corresponding average age of all participants in that debate.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Affirmative AS pid FROM debate_people UNION ALL SELECT Negative AS pid FROM debate_people) AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.pid GROUP BY T1.People_ID, T1.Name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the person who has participated in the most debates, determined by counting their total appearances and selecting the top result.

sql: SELECT T1.Date, T1.Venue FROM debate AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.Debate_ID = T2.Debate_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.Affirmative = T3.People_ID JOIN people AS T4 ON T2.Negative = T4.People_ID WHERE T3.Party = T4.Party;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the date and venue of debates where both the affirmative and negative speakers represented the same political party.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Party FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT Affirmative FROM debate_people WHERE If_Affirmative_Win = 'T') UNION SELECT T1.Name, T1.Party FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT Negative FROM debate_people);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and political party for any person who either won a debate on the affirmative side or participated in a debate on the negative side.

sql: SELECT p.Party, COUNT(DISTINCT p.People_ID) AS NumberOfDebaters FROM people AS p JOIN (SELECT Affirmative AS pid FROM debate_people UNION SELECT Negative AS pid FROM debate_people) AS dp ON p.People_ID = dp.pid GROUP BY p.Party;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each political party and the total number of unique individuals from that party who have participated in a debate.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Party FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT p_id FROM (SELECT Affirmative as p_id, Debate_ID FROM debate_people UNION ALL SELECT Negative as p_id, Debate_ID FROM debate_people) AS all_participants JOIN debate AS d ON all_participants.Debate_ID = d.Debate_ID WHERE d.Venue = 'Doha , Qatar');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Date, T2.Venue FROM people AS T1, debate AS T2, debate_people AS T3 WHERE T1.People_ID = T3.Affirmative AND T2.Debate_ID = T3.Debate_ID AND T1.Age = (SELECT MIN(Age) FROM people WHERE Party = 'Republican');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the youngest Republican party member, along with the date and venue of a debate in which they argued on the affirmative side.

sql: SELECT Name, Height, Country FROM mountain ORDER BY Height DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name, height, and country of each mountain, ordered from the tallest to the shortest.

sql: SELECT Name, Country, Points FROM climber ORDER BY Points DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing climbers' names, countries, and points, ordered from the highest to the lowest number of points.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mountain;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of mountains.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM climber;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total number of climbers.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM mountain WHERE Country = 'Tanzania';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and height for each mountain located in Tanzania.

sql: SELECT Name, Points FROM climber WHERE Country = 'West Germany';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and points for each climber from West Germany.

sql: SELECT AVG(Height) FROM mountain;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average height calculated across all mountains in the dataset.

sql: SELECT SUM(Points) FROM climber;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total sum of points accumulated by all climbers.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM mountain WHERE Height > 5500;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and height of mountains taller than 5500 meters.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM climber WHERE Points = 10.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and country of any climber with a score of exactly 10.0 points.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each climber's name alongside the name of the mountain they are associated with.

sql: SELECT Country, COUNT(*) FROM mountain GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each country along with a count of the total number of mountains located within it.

sql: SELECT Country, SUM(Points) FROM climber GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each country along with the total points accumulated by climbers from that country.

sql: SELECT Name FROM climber WHERE Mountain_ID IN (SELECT Mountain_ID FROM mountain WHERE Country = 'Tanzania');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of climbers who have climbed mountains located in Tanzania.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'West Germany';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of mountains that have been climbed by someone from 'West Germany'.

sql: SELECT Name, Points FROM climber WHERE Points > (SELECT AVG(Points) FROM climber);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and point scores for climbers whose points are higher than the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Kibo (Uhuru Pk)';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the climbers who have climbed the mountain named 'Kibo (Uhuru Pk)'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Climber_ID) AS NumberOfClimbers FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each mountain by name along with the total number of climbers associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, AVG(T2.Points) AS AveragePoints FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each mountain by name, along with the average points awarded to climbers for that mountain.

sql: SELECT Country FROM mountain GROUP BY Country ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single country that has the most mountains in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID ORDER BY T1.Height DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and height of the single tallest mountain that has an associated climber record.

sql: SELECT Name FROM mountain WHERE Mountain_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Mountain_ID FROM climber);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all mountains that have been climbed by at least one person.

sql: SELECT Country, AVG(Prominence) FROM mountain GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country along with the average prominence of the mountains located there.

sql: SELECT Country, MAX(Points) FROM climber GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each country along with the highest point score achieved by a climber from that country.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Name, T1.Points FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID ORDER BY T1.Points DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each climb with the mountain's name, the climber's name, and the points awarded, ordered from the highest number of points to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T1.Mountain_ID, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Climber_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of mountains that have been climbed by more than one person.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Name, T1.Points FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T1.Points = (SELECT MAX(T3.Points) FROM climber AS T3 WHERE T3.Mountain_ID = T1.Mountain_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the top-scoring climber for each mountain, listing the mountain name, the climber's name, and the points they achieved.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Country FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Height > 5500;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique country of climbers who have ascended mountains with a height greater than 5500.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Height) FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'West Germany';
result_summary: Returns a single row calculating the average height of all mountains climbed by climbers from 'West Germany'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T1.Prominence > (SELECT AVG(Prominence) FROM mountain);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of mountains that have been climbed and have a prominence greater than the average of all mountains.

sql: SELECT T1.Range, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Country) FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T1.Range;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each mountain range along with the number of distinct countries that climbers of that range are from.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'Tanzania' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'Kenya';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of climbers who have climbed mountains in Tanzania but have not climbed any mountains in Kenya.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T1.Mountain_ID, T1.Name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Climber_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the single mountain with the most climbers, determined by counting climbers for each mountain.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Points FROM climber AS T1 WHERE T1.Points > (SELECT AVG(T2.Points) FROM climber AS T2 WHERE T2.Country = T1.Country);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and points for each climber who has a higher score than the average score for climbers from their own country.

sql: SELECT Country FROM climber GROUP BY Country HAVING AVG(Points) > 11;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T3.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID AND T1.Climber_ID < T2.Climber_ID JOIN mountain AS T3 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T3.Mountain_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing unique pairs of climbers who have climbed the same mountain, showing the names of both climbers and the name of the mountain.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T2.Name, T2.Points FROM (SELECT Mountain_ID, MAX(Points) AS MaxPoints FROM climber GROUP BY Mountain_ID) AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID AND T1.MaxPoints = T2.Points JOIN mountain AS T3 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T3.Mountain_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each mountain's name alongside the name and score of the climber who achieved the highest points for that specific mountain.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T1.Range = 'Mount Kenya' GROUP BY T2.Climber_ID, T2.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Mountain_ID) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mountain WHERE Range = 'Mount Kenya');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Range, SUM(T2.Points) FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T1.Range;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each mountain range and the total points accumulated by climbers on mountains within that range.

sql: SELECT Name FROM climber WHERE Mountain_ID IN (SELECT Mountain_ID FROM mountain WHERE Height BETWEEN 5000 AND 6000) UNION SELECT Name FROM climber WHERE Points > 12;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of climbers who have either climbed a mountain with a height between 5000 and 6000, or have accumulated more than 12 points.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Points - T3.AvgPoints AS PointDifference FROM climber AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Mountain_ID, AVG(Points) AS AvgPoints FROM climber GROUP BY Mountain_ID) AS T3 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T3.Mountain_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, providing each climber's name and the calculated difference between their individual points and the average points scored on the mountain they climbed.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Country FROM mountain AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM climber AS T2 WHERE T2.Country = T1.Country);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique countries that contain mountains but do not have any registered climbers from those same countries.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Prominence FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T1.Points > (SELECT AVG(Points) FROM climber);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of climbers with above-average points, along with the prominence of the mountain they climbed.

sql: SELECT Range FROM mountain GROUP BY Range HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY AVG(Height) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the mountain range with the highest average elevation, considering only ranges with more than one recorded peak.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country AS ClimberCountry, T2.Name AS MountainName, T2.Country AS MountainCountry FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T1.Country != T2.Country;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing climbers who have climbed mountains located outside their home country, showing the climber's name and country alongside the mountain's name and its country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS Climber, T2.Name AS Mountain, T1.Points, (SELECT COUNT(T3.Points) + 1 FROM climber AS T3 WHERE T3.Mountain_ID = T1.Mountain_ID AND T3.Points > T1.Points) AS Rank FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID ORDER BY T2.Name, Rank;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows ranking climbers on each mountain based on their points, showing the climber, mountain, points, and their resulting rank. The data is ordered alphabetically by mountain name, with climbers ranked from best to worst for each mountain.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Prominence FROM mountain AS T1 WHERE T1.Mountain_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Mountain_ID FROM climber) AND T1.Prominence = (SELECT MIN(T2.Prominence) FROM mountain AS T2 WHERE T2.Mountain_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Mountain_ID FROM climber));
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and prominence of the climbed mountain with the minimum prominence.

sql: SELECT Name FROM climber WHERE Climber_ID NOT IN (SELECT T1.Climber_ID FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Mount Kenya (Batian)');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of all climbers who have not climbed 'Mount Kenya (Batian)'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Name FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T1.Range = 'Kilimanjaro';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of climbers and the specific mountains they climbed within the 'Kilimanjaro' range.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Climber_ID), MAX(T2.Points) - MIN(T2.Points) AS PointRange FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN climber AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T1.Mountain_ID, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Climber_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows for mountains climbed by more than one person, listing the mountain name, the total number of climbers, and the range between the highest and lowest points awarded.

sql: SELECT * FROM Person;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name, age, city, gender, and job for every person in the dataset.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Person WHERE city = 'new york city';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and age of a person who lives in 'new york city'.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Person WHERE age > 30;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age for each person who is older than 30.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT job FROM Person;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each unique job title recorded for individuals in the dataset.

sql: SELECT name FROM Person WHERE gender = 'female';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all individuals identified as female.

sql: SELECT name, friend FROM PersonFriend WHERE year > 8;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the pairs of friends whose friendship has lasted for more than 8 years.

sql: SELECT friend FROM PersonFriend WHERE name = 'Alice';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a friend for the person named 'Alice'.

sql: SELECT name, city FROM Person WHERE job = 'engineer';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and city for each person identified as an engineer.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Person ORDER BY age DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and age of the single oldest person in the dataset.

sql: SELECT * FROM PersonFriend WHERE name = 'Bob' OR friend = 'Bob';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all friendship records that include 'Bob', showing the names of both individuals and the duration of their friendship in years.

sql: SELECT name, COUNT(*) FROM PersonFriend GROUP BY name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each person by name along with a count of their total number of friends.

sql: SELECT AVG(year) FROM PersonFriend;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average duration, in years, of all recorded friendships.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.friend FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each person and one of their friends, effectively mapping out friendship relationships.

sql: SELECT name FROM PersonFriend WHERE friend = 'Zach';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the person who is friends with 'Zach'.

sql: SELECT city, COUNT(*) FROM Person GROUP BY city;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each city along with the total number of people in that city.

sql: SELECT job, AVG(age) FROM Person GROUP BY job;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each job title along with the average age of people with that job.

sql: SELECT name FROM Person WHERE name IN (SELECT name FROM PersonFriend);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of people who are also recorded as being a friend to someone else.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Person WHERE job = 'engineer' AND city = 'salt lake city';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and age of an engineer living in Salt Lake City.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name, T1.age FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.friend;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique individuals, by name and age, who are listed as a friend to at least one other person.

sql: SELECT gender, COUNT(name) FROM Person GROUP BY gender;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each gender and the total number of people for that gender.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.job FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name WHERE T2.friend = 'Bob';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and job of a person who is a friend of 'Bob'.

sql: SELECT T1.friend FROM PersonFriend AS T1 JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.friend = T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the names of all people who are listed as someone's friend, after verifying they exist in the main person directory.

sql: SELECT city FROM Person WHERE age = (SELECT MAX(age) FROM Person);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city where the oldest person lives.

sql: SELECT T1.city, AVG(T2.year) FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name GROUP BY T1.city;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each city and the average friendship year for the people residing in that city.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name WHERE T3.job = 'doctor';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a person who has a friend that is a doctor.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name WHERE T1.city <> T3.city AND T1.gender = 'male' AND T3.gender = 'male';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing pairs of male friends who live in different cities, showing the name of the first person and the name of their friend.

sql: SELECT T2.friend FROM PersonFriend AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.friend = T2.name WHERE T1.name = 'Alice';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the friends of friends for the person named 'Alice'.

sql: SELECT name FROM PersonFriend GROUP BY name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the person who has the most friends.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name WHERE T1.age < T3.age;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of people who have at least one friend older than themselves.

sql: SELECT name FROM PersonFriend INTERSECT SELECT friend FROM PersonFriend;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of individuals who are present as both a person and a friend in the friendship records.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name WHERE T1.job = 'student' AND T3.job = 'engineer';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a student who has at least one friend working as an engineer.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name WHERE T3.age > 30;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of people who have at least one friend older than 30.

sql: SELECT name, age, city FROM Person WHERE job = 'student' UNION SELECT name, age, city FROM Person WHERE city = 'salt lake city';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows with the name, age, and city of all people who are either students or who live in 'salt lake city'.

sql: SELECT name FROM Person EXCEPT SELECT name FROM PersonFriend WHERE year > 8;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of people who do not have any friendships that have lasted longer than 8 years.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Person WHERE age > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM Person);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of each person whose age is greater than the average age of all people in the table.

sql: SELECT name FROM PersonFriend GROUP BY name HAVING COUNT(friend) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of each person who has at least one friend.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.age FROM Person AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM PersonFriend AS T2 WHERE T2.name = T1.name AND T2.year > 5);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of individuals who have friendships lasting longer than 5 years.

sql: SELECT city, avg_age FROM (SELECT city, AVG(age) as avg_age FROM Person GROUP BY city) AS T1 WHERE T1.avg_age > 30;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the cities where the average age of the population is over 30, along with that calculated average age.

sql: SELECT T2.friend FROM PersonFriend AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.friend = T2.name WHERE T1.name = 'Alice' EXCEPT SELECT friend FROM PersonFriend WHERE name = 'Alice';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing a suggested friend for 'Alice', defined as a person who is a friend of one of her friends but not her direct friend.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name, T2.year FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name WHERE T2.year = (SELECT MIN(year) FROM PersonFriend);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the pair of friends with the longest-standing friendship, showing both names and the year they became friends, which is the earliest year recorded in the dataset.

sql: SELECT name FROM Person WHERE name NOT IN (SELECT friend FROM PersonFriend);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM Person AS T1 JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.gender = T2.gender AND T1.name < T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing unique pairs of people who share the same gender.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name, ABS(T1.age - T3.age) AS age_diff FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name ORDER BY age_diff DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the pair of friends with the largest age difference, showing both of their names and the calculated difference in their ages.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name WHERE T1.gender = 'female' AND T3.gender = 'male';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a female person who has at least one male friend.

sql: SELECT T1.job, COUNT(*) FROM Person AS T1 WHERE T1.city IN (SELECT T2.city FROM Person AS T2 WHERE T2.age > 30) GROUP BY T1.job;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows counting the number of people per job, filtered to include only those living in cities that are also home to residents over the age of 30.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM PersonFriend AS T2 JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name WHERE T2.name = T1.name AND T3.city = 'salt lake city');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a person who has at least one friend living in 'salt lake city'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.age, T2.friend, T3.age FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name WHERE T1.name = 'Alice';
result_summary: Returns 1 row for the person named 'Alice', listing her age, the name of her friend, and that friend's age.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.city, T3.name, T3.city FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name WHERE T2.year = 10;
result_summary: Returns 1 row that lists a person's name and city alongside their friend's name and city, for friendships that were established in year 10.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name WHERE T2.friend IN (SELECT T3.name FROM Person AS T3 WHERE T3.job = 'doctor');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a person who has at least one friend with the job of 'doctor'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.job, T1.city FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name EXCEPT SELECT T3.name, T3.job, T3.city FROM Person AS T3 WHERE T3.gender = 'male';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name, job, and city of non-male individuals who have at least one friend.

sql: SELECT * FROM Services;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing all available services, showing the ID and type code for each service.

sql: SELECT Participant_ID, Participant_Details FROM Participants;

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details, count(T2.Event_ID) FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID GROUP BY T1.Participant_ID HAVING count(T2.Event_ID) >= 1;

sql: SELECT T4.Service_Type_Code, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Participant_ID) FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Services AS T4 ON T3.Service_ID = T4.Service_ID GROUP BY T4.Service_Type_Code;

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Participants_in_Events AS T2 JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T2.Participant_ID = T1.Participant_ID AND T3.Event_Details = 'Success');

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Event_Details = 'Success' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Event_Details = 'Fail';

sql: SELECT T2.Service_Type_Code, T3.num_participants FROM (SELECT Event_ID, COUNT(Participant_ID) AS num_participants FROM Participants_in_Events GROUP BY Event_ID) AS T3 JOIN Events AS T1 ON T1.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID ORDER BY T3.num_participants DESC;
