sql: SELECT * FROM Student_Course_Enrolment WHERE date_of_completion IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing all details for student course enrolments that have been marked as complete.

sql: SELECT T2.subject_name, count(T1.course_id) FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Subjects AS T2 ON T1.subject_id = T2.subject_id GROUP BY T2.subject_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each subject name along with the total number of courses offered for that subject.

sql: SELECT T2.course_name, count(T1.student_id) FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T2.course_name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each course by name and the total number of students enrolled in it.

sql: SELECT T2.personal_name, T2.family_name, count(T1.course_id) FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id GROUP BY T2.author_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each course author by their first and last name, along with a count of the total number of courses they have authored.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id WHERE T3.test_result = 'Pass';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique personal and family names of all students who have passed at least one test.

sql: SELECT course_name FROM Courses WHERE course_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT course_id FROM Student_Course_Enrolment);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of all courses that have at least one student enrolled.

sql: SELECT T2.subject_name FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Subjects AS T2 ON T1.subject_id = T2.subject_id GROUP BY T2.subject_name HAVING count(T1.course_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all subjects that contain more than one course.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T3.course_name = 'advanced database';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the first and last names of all students enrolled in the 'Advanced Database' course.

sql: SELECT test_result, count(*) FROM Student_Tests_Taken GROUP BY test_result;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each test result category ('Pass', 'Fail') and the total number of students who achieved that outcome.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id JOIN Subjects AS T3 ON T2.subject_id = T3.subject_id WHERE T3.subject_name = 'Computer Science';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique personal and family names of all authors and tutors who have created courses within the 'Computer Science' subject.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name FROM Courses AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 WHERE T2.course_id = T1.course_id);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of all courses that have at least one student enrolled.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, 'Passed' as Status FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id WHERE T3.test_result = 'Pass' UNION SELECT T1.personal_name, 'Failed' as Status FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id WHERE T3.test_result = 'Fail';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing student names and a corresponding status indicating whether they 'Passed' or 'Failed' a test.

sql: SELECT avg(CourseCount) FROM (SELECT count(T1.course_id) as CourseCount FROM Courses AS T1 GROUP BY T1.author_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of courses created per author.

sql: SELECT strftime('%Y', date_of_registration), COUNT(student_id) FROM Students GROUP BY strftime('%Y', date_of_registration);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each registration year alongside the total number of students who registered in that year.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.course_id IN (SELECT T3.course_id FROM Courses AS T3 JOIN Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T4 ON T3.author_id = T4.author_id WHERE T4.personal_name = 'Cathrine');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, MAX(T1.date_of_latest_logon) FROM Students AS T1 GROUP BY T1.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each student's name along with the date and time of their most recent login.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id JOIN Courses AS T4 ON T2.course_id = T4.course_id JOIN Subjects AS T5 ON T4.subject_id = T5.subject_id WHERE T3.test_result = 'Pass' AND T5.subject_name = 'Computer Science';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of students who have passed a test in the 'Computer Science' subject.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, T3.course_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN (SELECT author_id FROM Courses GROUP BY author_id HAVING count(*) > 1) AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T1.author_id = T3.author_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the full names of authors who have created more than one course, along with the names of all courses they have authored.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING count(DISTINCT T3.subject_id) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of students who are enrolled in courses from two or more different subjects.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.registration_id IN (SELECT registration_id FROM Student_Tests_Taken) AND T2.registration_id NOT IN (SELECT registration_id FROM Student_Tests_Taken WHERE test_result = 'Fail');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of students who have taken at least one test and have passed all of them.

sql: SELECT T3.subject_name FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN Subjects AS T3 ON T2.subject_id = T3.subject_id GROUP BY T3.subject_name ORDER BY count(T1.student_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the subject with the most student enrolments.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T3.course_name = 'database' EXCEPT SELECT T1.personal_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T3.course_name = 'advanced database';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the personal names of students who are enrolled in the 'database' course but not the 'advanced database' course.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN T3.test_result = 'Pass' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(T3.registration_id) AS pass_percentage FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id GROUP BY T1.course_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, calculating the student test pass percentage for each course by dividing the number of 'Pass' results by the total number of tests taken.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.registration_id NOT IN (SELECT registration_id FROM Student_Tests_Taken);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the personal names of students who are enrolled in a course but have not taken any tests for that specific enrollment.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id JOIN Subjects AS T3 ON T2.subject_id = T3.subject_id WHERE T3.subject_name = 'Computer Science' EXCEPT SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id JOIN Subjects AS T3 ON T2.subject_id = T3.subject_id WHERE T3.subject_name != 'Computer Science';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of course authors who have exclusively written for the 'Computer Science' subject.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.date_of_completion IS NOT NULL AND julianday(T2.date_of_completion) - julianday(T2.date_of_enrolment) <= 150;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the first and last names of students who have completed a course within 150 days of their enrollment date.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T5.personal_name AS author_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id JOIN Courses AS T4 ON T2.course_id = T4.course_id JOIN Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T5 ON T4.author_id = T5.author_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows that pair student names with the names of the authors for each course in which the student has taken a test.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id WHERE T3.test_result = 'Fail' GROUP BY T1.course_name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the course with the highest total number of failed tests.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.test_result) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows with the first and last names of students who have achieved exactly two unique types of test results (e.g., 'Pass' and 'Fail') across all their enrollments.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.student_id) AS total_students FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id GROUP BY T1.author_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, listing each course author by name and the total number of unique students enrolled across all courses they have created.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING COUNT(T2.course_id) > (SELECT AVG(enroll_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(course_id) AS enroll_count FROM Student_Course_Enrolment GROUP BY student_id));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of students who are enrolled in more courses than the average student.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING count(DISTINCT T3.subject_id) = (SELECT count(*) FROM Subjects);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT course_name FROM Courses WHERE course_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT course_id FROM Student_Course_Enrolment);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all courses that currently have no student enrollments.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T3.course_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T1.date_of_registration = (SELECT MAX(date_of_registration) FROM Students);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the most recently registered student and the name of a course they are enrolled in.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T2.personal_name, T4.course_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T3a ON T1.student_id = T3a.student_id JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T3b ON T3a.course_id = T3b.course_id JOIN Students AS T2 ON T2.student_id = T3b.student_id JOIN Courses AS T4 ON T3a.course_id = T4.course_id WHERE T1.student_id < T2.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing pairs of students, along with the name of the specific course they are taking together.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T4 ON T3.registration_id = T4.registration_id WHERE T4.test_result = 'Pass' GROUP BY T1.author_id ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T3.student_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the first and last name of the top course author, ranked by the number of unique students who have passed a test in any of their courses.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN Subjects AS T4 ON T3.subject_id = T4.subject_id WHERE T4.subject_name = 'Computer Science' INTERSECT SELECT T1.personal_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN Subjects AS T4 ON T3.subject_id = T4.subject_id WHERE T4.subject_name = 'Arts';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the personal name of any student enrolled in courses from both the 'Computer Science' and 'Arts' subjects.

sql: SELECT AVG(julianday(T1.date_of_completion) - julianday(T1.date_of_enrolment)) FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN Subjects AS T3 ON T2.subject_id = T3.subject_id WHERE T3.subject_name = 'Language' AND T1.date_of_completion IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average number of days it takes for a student to complete a 'Language' course, measured from the date of enrolment to the date of completion.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id WHERE T2.course_id NOT IN (SELECT course_id FROM Student_Course_Enrolment);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of authors who have created courses that currently have no student enrollments.

sql: SELECT T1.subject_name, T2.course_name, COUNT(T3.registration_id) AS enrollment_count FROM Subjects AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.subject_id = T2.subject_id JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id GROUP BY T1.subject_name, T2.course_name HAVING COUNT(T3.registration_id) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T4 JOIN Courses AS T5 ON T4.course_id = T5.course_id WHERE T5.subject_id = T1.subject_id GROUP BY T5.course_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying the course(s) with the highest student enrollment within each subject, showing the subject name, course name, and that top enrollment count.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T4 ON T3.registration_id = T4.registration_id GROUP BY T1.author_id HAVING count(DISTINCT T4.test_result) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the full names of course authors whose students have collectively achieved exactly two distinct types of test results across all courses.

sql: SELECT * FROM captain;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, providing a complete list of all captains with their recorded details, including their assigned ship, age, class, and rank.

sql: SELECT Name, Type, Built_Year FROM ship ORDER BY Built_Year DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each ship's name, type, and build year, ordered from the newest to the oldest.

sql: SELECT Name, age, Rank FROM captain WHERE Rank = 'Midshipman';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name, age, and rank of all captains who hold the rank of 'Midshipman'.

sql: SELECT * FROM ship WHERE Type = 'Panamax';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing all available details (ID, Name, Type, Built Year, Class, and Flag) for ships that are classified as 'Panamax' type.

sql: SELECT Name, Class FROM captain WHERE age = '40';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and ship class for any captain who is 40 years old.

sql: SELECT Name, Flag FROM ship WHERE Built_Year = 1997.0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and flag of each ship built in the year 1997.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM ship;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of ships.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Class FROM captain;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique ship classes recorded for captains.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Name LIKE 'Captain%';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of captains whose recorded name begins with the title 'Captain'.

sql: SELECT * FROM ship WHERE Flag = 'Panama' LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Returns a sample of 2 rows showing full details for ships registered under the Panama flag.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each captain's name alongside the name of their assigned ship.

sql: SELECT T2.Type, COUNT(T1.Captain_ID) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Type;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each ship type alongside a count of the captains assigned to ships of that type.

sql: SELECT T2.Flag, AVG(T1.age) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Flag;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the average age of captains, grouped by their ship's flag of registration.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Built_Year = 1998.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of captains who command ships that were built in the year 1998.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Name, T1.age FROM ship AS T2 JOIN captain AS T1 ON T2.Ship_ID = T1.Ship_ID ORDER BY T1.age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each ship's name, its captain's name, and the captain's age, ordered from the oldest captain to the youngest.

sql: SELECT T2.Class, COUNT(*) FROM ship AS T2 GROUP BY T2.Class;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each ship class along with the total number of ships belonging to that class.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM ship AS T2 JOIN captain AS T1 ON T2.Ship_ID = T1.Ship_ID WHERE T1.age = '54';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the ship whose captain is 54 years old.

sql: SELECT T1.Class, COUNT(*) FROM captain AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Class;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each ship class along with the total number of captains associated with that class.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 WHERE T1.Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM ship WHERE Flag = 'Panama');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of captains who command ships registered under the 'Panama' flag.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE CAST(age AS INTEGER) > 40;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the name and age of captains who are older than 40.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Rank = 'Midshipman' UNION SELECT Name FROM ship WHERE Type = 'Panamax';
result_summary: Returns 13 rows containing a combined list of names for all captains with the rank 'Midshipman' and all ships of type 'Panamax'.

sql: SELECT Class, MAX(age) FROM captain GROUP BY Class;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows showing the age of the oldest captain for each distinct ship class.

sql: SELECT T2.Built_Year, COUNT(T1.Captain_ID) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Built_Year;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the number of captains associated with ships, grouped by the year each ship was built.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Rank FROM ship AS T2 JOIN captain AS T1 ON T2.Ship_ID = T1.Ship_ID WHERE T1.Class = 'Cutter';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ship name and its captain's rank, for captains whose assigned class is 'Cutter'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Ship_ID IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of all captains who are currently assigned to a ship.

sql: SELECT T2.Type, COUNT(T1.Captain_ID) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Type HAVING COUNT(T1.Captain_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing ship types that have more than one captain assigned across them, along with the total count of captains for each of those types.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE CAST(age AS REAL) > (SELECT AVG(CAST(age AS REAL)) FROM captain);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and ages of captains who are older than the average age across all captains.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.age FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Built_Year = (SELECT MIN(Built_Year) FROM ship);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age of each captain who commands the oldest ship or ships.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Class FROM captain AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ship AS T2 WHERE T2.Ship_ID = T1.Ship_ID AND T2.Type = 'Panamax');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and class of each captain in command of a 'Panamax' type ship.

sql: SELECT T2.Flag, AVG(T1.age) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Flag HAVING AVG(T1.age) > 45;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the ship's flag and the corresponding average captain age, for flags where the average captain age is greater than 45.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM captain AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Class, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM captain GROUP BY Class) AS T2 ON T1.Class = T2.Class WHERE T2.cnt > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the full details for all captains who belong to a ship class that has more than one captain assigned to it.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE Class = 'Third-rate ship of the line' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name, T1.age FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Flag = 'Panama';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the name and age of captains who both command a 'Third-rate ship of the line' and are on a ship registered under the 'Panama' flag.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Class = 'Third-rate ship of the line' EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Name = 'Captain Sir Henry Langford';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of captains commanding a 'Third-rate ship of the line', with 'Captain Sir Henry Langford' specifically excluded from the list.

sql: SELECT Class FROM captain GROUP BY Class HAVING MIN(CAST(age AS INTEGER)) > 30;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the ship classes that are exclusively commanded by captains older than 30.

sql: SELECT Name, CASE WHEN CAST(age AS INTEGER) > 50 THEN 'Veteran' ELSE 'Experienced' END FROM captain WHERE Rank = 'Midshipman';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of captains with the rank 'Midshipman', categorizing each as 'Veteran' if over 50 years old or 'Experienced' otherwise.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN captain AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.age = (SELECT MAX(age) FROM captain);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the ship commanded by the oldest captain.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM ship AS T2 WHERE T2.Built_Year > (SELECT AVG(T3.Built_Year) FROM ship AS T3) AND T2.Ship_ID IN (SELECT T1.Ship_ID FROM captain AS T1 WHERE T1.Rank = 'Midshipman');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of ships that were built more recently than the fleet's average build year and are captained by an officer with the rank of 'Midshipman'.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE Ship_ID = (SELECT Ship_ID FROM ship WHERE Name = 'HM Cutter Avenger');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age for all captains who have served on the ship named 'HM Cutter Avenger'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM ship WHERE Type = 'Panamax') AND CAST(age AS INTEGER) < 45;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of captains younger than 45 who are in command of a 'Panamax' type ship.

sql: SELECT T1.Class, T2.Flag, COUNT(*) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T1.Class, T2.Flag;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows counting the number of ships for each combination of ship class and flag.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Class, T1.Rank FROM captain AS T1 WHERE (T1.Class, T1.age) IN (SELECT Class, MAX(age) FROM captain GROUP BY Class);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the oldest captain for each ship class, listing their name, class, and rank.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Name FROM captain AS T1, ship AS T2 WHERE T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID AND T2.Built_Year = (SELECT Built_Year FROM ship WHERE Name = 'HMS Manxman') AND T2.Name != 'HMS Manxman';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying ships and their captains that share the same build year as the ship 'HMS Manxman', excluding 'HMS Manxman' itself from the results.

sql: SELECT Name, 'Captain' as Role FROM captain UNION SELECT Name, 'Vessel' as Role FROM ship;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows combining the names of all captains and vessels into a single list, with a 'Role' column to identify each entry as either a 'Captain' or a 'Vessel'.

sql: SELECT Rank FROM captain GROUP BY Rank HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT Class) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the captain ranks that have commanded more than one distinct ship class.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Class = 'KR' EXCEPT SELECT T3.Name FROM captain AS T3 WHERE T3.age = '40';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of captains who command a 'KR' class ship, excluding any of those captains who are 40 years old.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE Class = 'Third-rate ship of the line' AND Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM ship WHERE Flag = 'Panama');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age of captains commanding 'Third-rate ship of the line' class vessels that sail under the 'Panama' flag.

sql: SELECT T1.Built_Year, count(T2.Captain_ID) FROM ship AS T1 LEFT JOIN captain AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T1.Built_Year HAVING count(T2.Captain_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each ship-building year that is associated with more than one captain, along with the total count of captains for that year.

sql: SELECT Name FROM ship WHERE Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM captain WHERE Class = 'Third-rate ship of the line');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the names of all ships that are classified as a 'Third-rate ship of the line'.

sql: SELECT T1.Flag FROM ship AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Flag HAVING COUNT(T1.Ship_ID) = (SELECT COUNT(T2.Captain_ID) FROM captain AS T2 JOIN ship AS T3 ON T2.Ship_ID = T3.Ship_ID WHERE T3.Flag = T1.Flag);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN captain AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE CAST(T2.age AS INTEGER) > 42;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of ships that are commanded by captains older than 42.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Location FROM festival_detail;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and location for each festival in the database.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork WHERE Type = 'Program Music/Variety Show';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all artworks classified as 'Program Music/Variety Show'.

sql: SELECT * FROM festival_detail WHERE Year = 2007;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the full details for all festivals that occurred in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Type FROM artwork;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the distinct types of artwork recorded.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM festival_detail;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all festival details.

sql: SELECT * FROM artwork WHERE Name = 'Indonesian Idol';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available information for the artwork specifically named 'Indonesian Idol'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of all artworks, sorted in ascending alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT * FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all nominations that resulted in a win, including the corresponding artwork and festival IDs.

sql: SELECT avg(Num_of_Audience) FROM festival_detail;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of audience members across all recorded festival events.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Location FROM festival_detail;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the distinct location where festivals are held.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of artworks that have received a nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, count(T2.Artwork_ID) FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each festival's name along with the total count of artworks nominated for that festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns the names of the 2 artworks that have won a nomination.

sql: SELECT Type, count(*) FROM artwork GROUP BY Type;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing the total number of artworks for each distinct artwork type.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Chair_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of festival chairs whose festivals have had at least one winning nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Result FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name of each nominated artwork and its corresponding nomination result.

sql: SELECT sum(Num_of_Audience) FROM festival_detail WHERE Year = 2007;
result_summary: Returns a single value representing the total number of audience members for all festivals that occurred in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT T1.Type, count(*) FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID GROUP BY T1.Type;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artwork type along with the total count of nominations received for that type.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_ID IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of festivals that have received at least one nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Festival_ID = (SELECT Festival_ID FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_Name = 'Flower Awards');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated for the 'Flower Awards' festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, count(T2.Artwork_ID) FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE T1.Year = 2007 GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total count of artwork nominations for each festival that took place in 2007.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name HAVING count(*) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of festivals that have received two or more nominations.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Festival_ID IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM festival_detail WHERE Num_of_Audience > 152);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated for festivals attended by more than 152 people.

sql: SELECT T3.Festival_Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'I Know' AND T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the festival where the artwork 'I Know' won an award.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, T1.Year FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'United States' GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and a corresponding year for unique festivals held in the United States that have received nominations.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Nominated' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated for an award but did not win.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Location = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and type of artworks that were nominated for festivals held in the United States.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the festival that received the most nominations.

sql: SELECT Chair_Name FROM festival_detail WHERE Num_of_Audience = (SELECT max(Num_of_Audience) FROM festival_detail);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the chairperson of the festival that attracted the largest audience.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Chair_Name = 'Charles Coody' AND T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the artwork that won an award at a festival chaired by 'Charles Coody'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T1.Type = 'Program Music/Variety Show' AND T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of 'Program Music/Variety Show' artworks that have won a nomination.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_ID IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Nominated') AND Festival_ID NOT IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a festival that has received nominations but no wins.

sql: SELECT T1.Artwork_ID, T2.Artwork_ID FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE T1.Artwork_ID < T2.Artwork_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing unique pairs of artwork IDs that were nominated together for the same festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 WHERE T1.Type = 'Presenter Music/Variety Show' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of artworks that are either of the type 'Presenter Music/Variety Show' or have won a nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name ORDER BY count(*) ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the festival that received the fewest nominations.

sql: SELECT Location FROM festival_detail GROUP BY Location ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single location that hosts the most festivals.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Chair_Name LIKE 'Raymond%';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated for festivals chaired by someone whose name starts with 'Raymond'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Year = 2006 UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Year = 2007 AND T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of artworks that were either nominated in a 2006 festival or won an award in a 2007 festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Festival_Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Nominated' AND T1.Artwork_ID NOT IN (SELECT Artwork_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing artworks that have been nominated for an award but have never won, showing the artwork's name and the name of the festival where it was nominated.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE T1.Num_of_Audience > 152 AND T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a festival that won a nomination and had an audience of more than 152 people.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Festival_Name IN ('Panasonic Awards', 'Flower Awards');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of artworks that were nominated in either the 'Panasonic Awards' or the 'Flower Awards'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Year = 2007;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of artworks that were nominated for a festival award in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, count(T2.Artwork_ID) FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T1.Artwork_ID IN (SELECT Artwork_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won') GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each artwork that has won an award, showing its name and the total count of all nominations it received.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, T2.Result, count(*) FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name, T2.Result;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows detailing the number of nominations for each festival, broken down by the result of the nomination (e.g., 'Won', 'Nominated').

sql: SELECT Year, avg(Num_of_Audience) FROM festival_detail GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each year along with the average number of audience members for festivals held in that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name HAVING count(T2.Artwork_ID) > (SELECT avg(Nomination_Count) FROM (SELECT count(Artwork_ID) AS Nomination_Count FROM nomination GROUP BY Festival_ID));
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Festival_Name, T3.Year FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each artwork nomination, detailing the artwork's name, the festival's name, and the year of the festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Num_of_Audience = 152;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated at festivals with an audience of exactly 152.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Festival_ID NOT IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_Name = 'Panasonic Awards');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of artworks that have been nominated for any festival other than the 'Panasonic Awards'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Festival_Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of winning artworks and the corresponding festivals they won.

sql: SELECT * FROM Student WHERE Age = 18;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing all available details (ID, name, major, etc.) for every student who is 18 years old.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing the first and last names of all students, ordered by age from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT AllergyType FROM Allergy_Type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each unique type of allergy recorded in the database.

sql: SELECT StuID, Allergy FROM Has_Allergy WHERE StuID = 1002;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the known allergies for the student with an ID of 1002.

sql: SELECT Allergy FROM Allergy_Type WHERE AllergyType = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of all allergies that are classified as food allergies.

sql: SELECT * FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows containing all available student details for every female student in the dataset.

sql: SELECT LName, Fname, Major FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'HKG';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the last name, first name, and major for all students from the city represented by the code 'HKG'.

sql: SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy WHERE Allergy = 'Cat';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the student IDs of all students who are allergic to cats.

sql: SELECT Fname, Age FROM Student ORDER BY Fname;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing the first name and age of each student, ordered alphabetically by first name.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Student;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total count of all students.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Allergy FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 58 rows listing the first name, last name, and specific allergy for each student with a recorded allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Sex, COUNT(*) FROM Student AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Sex;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each sex ('F', 'M') and the corresponding total count of students for that sex.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, T1.Fname, T1.LName, COUNT(T2.Allergy) AS NumberOfAllergies FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 29 rows listing each student's ID and name, along with a total count of the number of allergies each student has.

sql: SELECT T2.AllergyType, COUNT(T1.StuID) AS StudentCount FROM Has_Allergy AS T1 JOIN Allergy_Type AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy GROUP BY T2.AllergyType;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each allergy type along with the total count of students who have that type of allergy.

sql: SELECT Age, COUNT(*) FROM Student GROUP BY Age;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each distinct student age and the total count of students at that age.

sql: SELECT * FROM Student WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing all available details for students who are older than the average student age.

sql: SELECT T1.Allergy FROM Has_Allergy AS T1 WHERE T1.StuID IN (SELECT T2.StuID FROM Student AS T2 WHERE T2.Age > 18);
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing the allergies of students who are older than 18.

sql: SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average age of all female students.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Shellfish';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of students who have a shellfish allergy.

sql: SELECT StuID FROM Student WHERE Major = 600 UNION SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy WHERE Allergy = 'Cat';
result_summary: Returns 19 rows, providing a unique list of student IDs for all students who are either in major 600 or are allergic to cats.

sql: SELECT T1.Allergy, T1.AllergyType FROM Allergy_Type AS T1 WHERE T1.Allergy IN (SELECT T2.Allergy FROM Has_Allergy AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the name and type for each allergy that is actively recorded for at least one person in the system.

sql: SELECT city_code, count(*) FROM Student GROUP BY city_code;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing each city code along with the total number of students from that city.

sql: SELECT MAX(Age), MIN(Age) FROM Student;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the maximum and minimum age found across all students.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.StuID = 1001 OR T1.StuID = 1002;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of the students with the specific student IDs 1001 and 1002.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T1.Age FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T3.AllergyType = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 28 rows listing the first name, last name, and age of all students who have a food allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 WHERE T2.StuID = T1.StuID AND T2.Allergy = 'Cat');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows with the student ID, first name, and last name for all students who are recorded as having a cat allergy.

sql: SELECT T2.AllergyType, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.StuID) AS NumberOfAffectedStudents FROM Has_Allergy AS T1 JOIN Allergy_Type AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy GROUP BY T2.AllergyType HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.StuID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing allergy types that affect more than one student, along with the total count of students for each of those allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.StuID IN (SELECT T2.StuID FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 GROUP BY T2.StuID HAVING COUNT(T2.Allergy) >= 2);
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the first and last names of students who have two or more recorded allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T1.Age FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.Age = (SELECT MIN(T2.Age) FROM Student AS T2 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T3 ON T2.StuID = T3.StuID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the first name, last name, and age of the student(s) who are the youngest among all students with a recorded allergy.

sql: SELECT Allergy FROM Has_Allergy WHERE StuID = 1002 EXCEPT SELECT Allergy FROM Allergy_Type WHERE AllergyType != 'food';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the food-related allergies for the student with an ID of 1002.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code, AVG(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy) GROUP BY T1.city_code;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each city code along with the average age of students in that city who have a recorded allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 WHERE T2.StuID = T1.StuID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of all students who do not have any recorded allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Allergy FROM Allergy_Type AS T1 WHERE T1.AllergyType = 'food' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Allergy FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 WHERE T2.StuID = 1002;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the specific food allergies that student 1002 has.

sql: SELECT T1.Major, T1.Sex, COUNT(T1.StuID) FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.StuID IN (SELECT T2.StuID FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T3.AllergyType = 'food') GROUP BY T1.Major, T1.Sex;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the number of students who have a 'food' allergy, broken down by their major and sex.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.Age < (SELECT AVG(T2.Age) FROM Student AS T2) AND T1.StuID IN (SELECT T3.StuID FROM Has_Allergy AS T3);
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the first and last names of students who have a recorded allergy and are younger than the average student age.

sql: SELECT T1.Allergy, T1.AllergyType FROM Allergy_Type AS T1 WHERE T1.Allergy NOT IN (SELECT Allergy FROM Has_Allergy);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and type of all allergies that are not recorded for any patient.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, T1.Fname, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 WHERE T2.StuID = T1.StuID) AS AllergyCount FROM Student AS T1 ORDER BY AllergyCount DESC;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing each student's ID and first name along with a count of their total allergies, ordered to show the students with the most allergies first.

sql: WITH StudentAllergyCounts AS (SELECT StuID, COUNT(Allergy) as NumAllergies FROM Has_Allergy GROUP BY StuID) SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.NumAllergies FROM Student AS T1 JOIN StudentAllergyCounts AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.NumAllergies > 1;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows identifying students who have more than one allergy, showing their first name, last name, and the total count of their allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.city_code = 'BAL' AND T1.StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of students from the city 'BAL' who are known to have an allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Allergy, T3.AllergyType FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T1.Age = 19;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first name, last name, specific allergy, and allergy type for students who are 19 years old.

sql: SELECT a.Allergy, a.AllergyType FROM Allergy_Type a LEFT JOIN Has_Allergy h ON a.Allergy = h.Allergy WHERE h.StuID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and types of allergies from the master list that no student is recorded as having.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE (SELECT count(*) FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 WHERE T2.StuID = T1.StuID) = (SELECT MAX(c) FROM (SELECT count(*) AS c FROM Has_Allergy GROUP BY StuID));
result_summary: Returns 2 rows with the student ID and last name for the students who have the most recorded allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Shellfish' UNION SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Cat';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of all students who have an allergy to either 'Shellfish' or 'Cat'.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy GROUP BY StuID HAVING count(*) = 1) AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the first and last names of students who have exactly one recorded allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Advisor, COUNT(T1.StuID) AS NumStudents, AVG(T1.Age) AS AvgAge FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy) GROUP BY T1.Advisor;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows, listing each advisor along with the total number and average age of their students who have allergies.

sql: SELECT * FROM Has_Allergy WHERE StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F' AND Age > 18);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing all recorded allergies for female students over the age of 18, showing the student's ID for each allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.Major = 600 EXCEPT SELECT T3.Fname, T3.LName FROM Student AS T3 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T4 ON T3.StuID = T4.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of students with Major ID 600 who do not have any recorded allergies.

sql: SELECT * FROM editor;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, providing a complete list of all editors along with their ID, name, and age.

sql: SELECT Theme, Sales FROM journal;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing various themes, which appear to be sports-related, and their corresponding sales figures.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM editor WHERE Age > 30;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and age for all editors who are over 30 years old.

sql: SELECT * FROM journal WHERE Theme = 'Miami Dolphins';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing all details (Journal ID, Date, Theme, and Sales) for journal entries specifically about the 'Miami Dolphins' theme.

sql: SELECT * FROM journal ORDER BY Sales DESC;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows detailing all journal entries, including their ID, date, theme, and sales figures, sorted from the highest sales to the lowest.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM journal;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of journals.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Work_Type FROM journal_committee;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the distinct types of work performed for a journal committee.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID WHERE T2.Work_Type IN ('Photo', 'Article') GROUP BY T1.Editor_ID, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Work_Type) = 2;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID JOIN journal AS T3 ON T2.Journal_ID = T3.Journal_ID ORDER BY T3.Sales DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T3.Sales) FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID JOIN journal AS T3 ON T2.Journal_ID = T3.Journal_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID JOIN journal AS T3 ON T2.Journal_ID = T3.Journal_ID WHERE T3.Sales > (SELECT AVG(Sales) FROM journal);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of editors who are associated with journals that have sales greater than the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING count(T2.Journal_ID) = (SELECT count(Journal_ID) FROM journal_committee GROUP BY Editor_ID ORDER BY count(Journal_ID) DESC LIMIT 1);

sql: SELECT T1.Theme FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID GROUP BY T1.Theme HAVING COUNT(T2.Editor_ID) = (SELECT COUNT(Editor_ID) FROM journal_committee GROUP BY Journal_ID ORDER BY COUNT(Editor_ID) DESC LIMIT 1);

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID GROUP BY T1.Editor_ID, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.Work_Type <> 'Photo' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) = 0;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, AVG(T3.Sales) FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID JOIN journal AS T3 ON T2.Journal_ID = T3.Journal_ID GROUP BY T1.Editor_ID, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Journal_ID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and average journal sales for an editor who serves on the committee of two or more journals.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid JOIN Has_amenity AS T3 ON T2.dormid = T3.dormid JOIN Dorm_amenity AS T4 ON T3.amenid = T4.amenid WHERE T4.amenity_name = 'Study Room';

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Student AS T3 JOIN Lives_in AS T4 ON T3.StuID = T4.stuid WHERE T1.StuID != T3.StuID AND T2.dormid = T4.dormid AND T1.Major = T3.Major);

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.stuid = T2.stuid WHERE T2.dormid IN (SELECT dormid FROM Has_amenity WHERE amenid = 900 INTERSECT SELECT dormid FROM Has_amenity WHERE amenid = 901);

sql: SELECT T2.amenity_name FROM Has_amenity AS T1 JOIN Dorm_amenity AS T2 ON T1.amenid = T2.amenid GROUP BY T1.amenid HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.dormid) >= 2;

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, SUM(T2.Total) AS TotalSpending FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId WHERE T1.Country = 'Germany' GROUP BY T1.CustomerId;

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, SUM(T2.Total) AS TotalSpending FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.CustomerId ORDER BY TotalSpending DESC LIMIT 5;

sql: SELECT T3.FirstName, T3.LastName, SUM(T1.Total) AS TotalSales FROM Invoice AS T1 JOIN Customer AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId JOIN Employee AS T3 ON T2.SupportRepId = T3.EmployeeId WHERE T3.Title = 'Sales Support Agent' GROUP BY T3.EmployeeId ORDER BY TotalSales DESC;

sql: SELECT T3.Name, COUNT(T1.PlaylistId) AS PlaylistAppearances FROM PlaylistTrack AS T1 JOIN Track AS T2 ON T1.TrackId = T2.TrackId JOIN Album AS T4 ON T2.AlbumId = T4.AlbumId JOIN Artist AS T3 ON T4.ArtistId = T3.ArtistId GROUP BY T3.ArtistId ORDER BY PlaylistAppearances DESC LIMIT 3;

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName AS EmployeeFirstName, T1.LastName AS EmployeeLastName, T2.FirstName AS ManagerFirstName, T2.LastName AS ManagerLastName FROM Employee AS T1 LEFT JOIN Employee AS T2 ON T1.ReportsTo = T2.EmployeeId;

sql: SELECT T5.Name, SUM(T3.UnitPrice * T3.Quantity) AS Revenue FROM Invoice AS T1 JOIN Customer AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T1.InvoiceId = T3.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T4 ON T3.TrackId = T4.TrackId JOIN MediaType AS T5 ON T4.MediaTypeId = T5.MediaTypeId WHERE T2.Country = 'Germany' GROUP BY T5.Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, CAST(COUNT(T3.TrackId) AS REAL) / COUNT(DISTINCT T2.AlbumId) AS AvgTracksPerAlbum FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId JOIN Track AS T3 ON T2.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId GROUP BY T1.ArtistId HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.AlbumId) > 0;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Artist AS T1 WHERE T1.ArtistId IN (SELECT T3.ArtistId FROM Track AS T2 JOIN Album AS T3 ON T2.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId JOIN Genre AS T4 ON T2.GenreId = T4.GenreId WHERE T4.Name = 'Rock') AND 1 = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T4.GenreId) FROM Track AS T2 JOIN Album AS T3 ON T2.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId JOIN Genre AS T4 ON T2.GenreId = T4.GenreId WHERE T3.ArtistId = T1.ArtistId);
result_summary: Returns 39 rows listing the names of artists who exclusively produce music within the 'Rock' genre.

sql: WITH MonthlySales AS (SELECT STRFTIME('%Y-%m', InvoiceDate) AS SaleMonth, SUM(Total) AS MonthlyTotal FROM Invoice GROUP BY SaleMonth) SELECT T1.SaleMonth, T1.MonthlyTotal, T2.MonthlyTotal AS PreviousMonthTotal, (T1.MonthlyTotal - T2.MonthlyTotal) / T2.MonthlyTotal * 100.0 AS GrowthPercentage FROM MonthlySales AS T1 LEFT JOIN MonthlySales AS T2 ON T1.SaleMonth = STRFTIME('%Y-%m', DATE(T2.SaleMonth || '-01', '+1 month')) WHERE PreviousMonthTotal IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 59 rows, providing a month-over-month sales analysis by showing the total sales for each month, the total from the prior month, and the calculated percentage growth.

sql: WITH TrackSales AS (SELECT T2.GenreId, T1.TrackId, COUNT(T1.InvoiceLineId) AS SalesCount FROM InvoiceLine AS T1 JOIN Track AS T2 ON T1.TrackId = T2.TrackId GROUP BY T2.GenreId, T1.TrackId), RankedTracks AS (SELECT T1.GenreId, T1.TrackId, T1.SalesCount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY T1.GenreId ORDER BY T1.SalesCount DESC) AS Rank FROM TrackSales AS T1) SELECT T3.Name AS Genre, T2.Name AS TopTrack, T1.SalesCount FROM RankedTracks AS T1 JOIN Track AS T2 ON T1.TrackId = T2.TrackId JOIN Genre AS T3 ON T1.GenreId = T3.GenreId WHERE T1.Rank = 1;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows, identifying the single best-selling track for each music genre based on sales volume, and listing the genre name, track name, and total sales count.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.rid) AS outgoing_routes FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.src_apid = T2.apid GROUP BY T2.name ORDER BY outgoing_routes DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T3.name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.country) AS country_count FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.dst_apid = T2.apid JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.alid = T3.alid GROUP BY T3.name;

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM airlines AS T1 WHERE T1.alid IN (SELECT alid FROM routes WHERE src_apid = 2966 INTERSECT SELECT alid FROM routes WHERE dst_apid = 2990);
