sql: SELECT T2.country, COUNT(T1.dst_apid) AS dest_count FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.dst_apid = T2.apid GROUP BY T2.country ORDER BY dest_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the country that is the destination for the highest number of flight routes, along with the total count of those incoming routes.

sql: SELECT name, country FROM airlines WHERE active = 'Y' AND alid NOT IN (SELECT T1.alid FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.dst_apid = T2.apid WHERE T2.country = 'Papua New Guinea');
result_summary: Returns 1251 rows listing the name and country of active airlines that do not operate any routes with a destination in Papua New Guinea.

sql: SELECT T2.name AS source_airport, T3.name AS destination_airport FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.src_apid = T2.apid JOIN airports AS T3 ON T1.dst_apid = T3.apid WHERE T1.airline = '2B';
result_summary: Returns 40 rows listing the source and destination airport names for all flight routes operated by the airline with code '2B'.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T4.avg_elevation FROM (SELECT T1.alid, AVG(T2.elevation) AS avg_elevation FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.src_apid = T2.apid GROUP BY T1.alid) AS T4 JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T4.alid = T3.alid ORDER BY T4.avg_elevation DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the airline with the highest average source airport elevation, listing its name and the calculated average elevation.

sql: SELECT name, city, country FROM airports WHERE country = 'Papua New Guinea' UNION SELECT name, city, country FROM airports WHERE city = 'Goroka';
result_summary: Returns 26 rows listing the name, city, and country for a combined set of airports from 'Papua New Guinea' and the city of 'Goroka'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.name, T4.name FROM routes AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.src_apid = T2.src_apid AND T1.dst_apid = T2.dst_apid AND T1.alid < T2.alid JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.alid = T3.alid JOIN airlines AS T4 ON T2.alid = T4.alid;
result_summary: Returns 4865 rows, listing unique pairs of airlines that directly compete by operating the exact same routes from a source to a destination airport.

sql: SELECT country, COUNT(*) FROM airlines WHERE active = 'Y' GROUP BY country HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 197 rows listing the number of active airlines for each country.

sql: SELECT T1.rid, T3.name AS airline_name, T2.name AS destination_airport FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.dst_apid = T2.apid JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.alid = T3.alid WHERE T3.country = 'United States' AND T2.country = 'Papua New Guinea';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T4.name, COUNT(T1.rid) AS international_routes FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.src_apid = T2.apid JOIN airports AS T3 ON T1.dst_apid = T3.apid JOIN airlines AS T4 ON T1.alid = T4.alid WHERE T2.country != T3.country GROUP BY T4.name ORDER BY international_routes DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the airline with the most international routes, along with the total count of those routes.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM airports AS T1 WHERE T1.apid IN (SELECT dst_apid FROM routes WHERE src_ap = 'AER') AND T1.apid NOT IN (SELECT src_apid FROM routes WHERE dst_ap = 'KZ');
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of airports that are direct destinations from airport 'AER' but do not serve as origins for flights to airport 'KZ'.

sql: SELECT T1.country, T1.name, T1.elevation FROM airports AS T1 WHERE T1.elevation = (SELECT MAX(T2.elevation) FROM airports AS T2 WHERE T2.country = T1.country);
result_summary: Returns 245 rows, identifying the airport with the highest elevation within each country and listing its country, name, and elevation.

sql: SELECT T1.rid, T2.iata, T3.iata FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.src_apid = T2.apid JOIN airports AS T3 ON T1.dst_apid = T3.apid WHERE T2.iata IS NOT NULL AND T3.iata IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 66067 rows, providing a list of all flight routes by their unique ID, along with the corresponding IATA codes for their source and destination airports.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.rid) FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.alid = T2.alid GROUP BY T2.name HAVING COUNT(T1.rid) > (SELECT AVG(route_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(rid) AS route_count FROM routes GROUP BY alid) AS T3);
result_summary: Returns 122 rows listing each airline and its total route count, for all airlines that operate a higher-than-average number of routes.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T4.name, T2.name FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.alid = T2.alid JOIN airports AS T3 ON T1.src_apid = T3.apid JOIN airports AS T4 ON T1.dst_apid = T4.apid WHERE T2.name LIKE '%flight%';
result_summary: Returns 128 flight routes, listing the source airport, destination airport, and airline name for all airlines that have the word 'flight' in their name.

sql: SELECT name, Trade_Name FROM medicine;
result_summary: Returns 21 rows containing the generic name and corresponding trade name for each medicine.

sql: SELECT name, Location FROM enzyme;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each enzyme by name and its corresponding cellular location.

sql: SELECT name, Trade_Name FROM medicine WHERE FDA_approved = 'Yes';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the generic name and trade name for all medicines that are approved by the FDA.

sql: SELECT name, Product FROM enzyme WHERE Location = 'Mitochondrion';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of enzymes located in the Mitochondrion and the corresponding products they create.

sql: SELECT id, name, Trade_Name FROM medicine WHERE name = 'Amisulpride';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID, name, and trade name for the medicine named 'Amisulpride'.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM medicine;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all medicines.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM enzyme;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of enzymes in the dataset.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT interaction_type FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique types of interactions that can occur between medicines and enzymes.

sql: SELECT name, Chromosome FROM enzyme WHERE OMIM = 125270;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and chromosome location of the enzyme associated with the specific OMIM ID 125270.

sql: SELECT * FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction WHERE enzyme_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing all recorded medicine interactions for a specific enzyme (ID 1), including the medicine ID and the type of interaction.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.enzyme_id) FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, listing each medicine by name along with the total count of enzymes it interacts with.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.medicine_id) FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each enzyme by name along with a count of the total number of medicines it interacts with.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.medicine_id JOIN enzyme AS T2 ON T3.enzyme_id = T2.id WHERE T1.FDA_approved = 'Yes';
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing pairs of FDA-approved medicines and the enzymes they interact with, showing the name for each.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.enzyme_id JOIN medicine AS T2 ON T3.medicine_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Location = 'Mitochondrion';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing medicines and the specific mitochondrial enzymes they are known to interact with.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Trade_Name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id WHERE T2.interaction_type = 'inhibitor';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the generic name and trade name for all medicines that are classified as enzyme inhibitors.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Location FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id WHERE T2.interaction_type = 'inhibitor';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows showing the name and location of enzymes that are inhibited by a medicine.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, COUNT(T2.medicine_id) FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id GROUP BY T1.Location;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each enzyme's cellular location alongside the total count of medicines that interact with enzymes found in that specific location.

sql: SELECT FDA_approved, COUNT(*) FROM medicine GROUP BY FDA_approved;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total count of medicines for each FDA approval status ('Yes' or 'No').

sql: SELECT T2.Trade_Name FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.enzyme_id JOIN medicine AS T2 ON T3.medicine_id = T2.id WHERE T1.name = 'ALA synthase';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the trade names of medicines that interact with the enzyme named 'ALA synthase'.

sql: SELECT T1.Porphyria FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.enzyme_id JOIN medicine AS T2 ON T3.medicine_id = T2.id WHERE T2.name = 'Aripiprazole';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the Porphyria classifications associated with enzymes that interact with the medicine 'Aripiprazole'.

sql: SELECT T2.interaction_type, COUNT(T1.id) FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id GROUP BY T2.interaction_type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing a count of medicines for each distinct medicine-enzyme interaction type.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T2.Trade_Name FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T1 JOIN medicine AS T2 ON T1.medicine_id = T2.id JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T1.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Chromosome = '3p21.1';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the official name and trade name of medicines that interact with enzymes located on the specific chromosome '3p21.1'.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T2.Trade_Name FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T1 JOIN medicine AS T2 ON T1.medicine_id = T2.id WHERE T2.FDA_approved = 'No';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the generic name and trade name for medicines that are not approved by the FDA.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Location FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id WHERE T2.medicine_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and cellular locations of all enzymes that interact with the medicine identified by ID 3.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name, T3.interaction_type FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.medicine_id JOIN enzyme AS T2 ON T3.enzyme_id = T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the interactions between medicines and enzymes, showing the name of the medicine, the name of the enzyme it interacts with, and the type of interaction.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Location FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING COUNT(T2.medicine_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and location of enzymes that interact with more than one medicine.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.enzyme_id FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 GROUP BY T2.enzyme_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 2);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of enzymes that interact with more than two different medicines.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id JOIN medicine AS T3 ON T2.medicine_id = T3.id WHERE T3.FDA_approved = 'Yes' INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id JOIN medicine AS T3 ON T2.medicine_id = T3.id WHERE T3.FDA_approved = 'No';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the names of enzymes that interact with both FDA-approved and non-FDA-approved medicines.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 JOIN medicine AS T3 ON T2.medicine_id = T3.id WHERE T2.enzyme_id = T1.id AND T3.FDA_approved = 'Yes') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 JOIN medicine AS T3 ON T2.medicine_id = T3.id WHERE T2.enzyme_id = T1.id AND T3.FDA_approved = 'No');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of enzymes that interact with both FDA-approved and non-FDA-approved medicines.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 WHERE T1.FDA_approved = 'No' AND T1.id IN (SELECT T2.medicine_id FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of medicines that are not FDA approved but are known to have an enzyme interaction.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id GROUP BY T1.id ORDER BY COUNT(T2.medicine_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the single enzyme that interacts with the highest number of medicines.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id GROUP BY T1.id ORDER BY COUNT(T2.enzyme_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the medicine that interacts with the highest number of enzymes.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 WHERE T1.id NOT IN (SELECT T2.enzyme_id FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of enzymes that do not have any recorded interactions with medicines.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 LEFT JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id WHERE T2.medicine_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of medicines that have no recorded enzyme interactions.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T3 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T4 ON T3.enzyme_id = T4.enzyme_id AND T3.medicine_id < T4.medicine_id JOIN medicine AS T1 ON T3.medicine_id = T1.id JOIN medicine AS T2 ON T4.medicine_id = T2.id WHERE T3.enzyme_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing the unique pairs of medicines that both interact with a specific enzyme (enzyme ID 1).

sql: SELECT T2.interaction_type FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id WHERE T1.FDA_approved = 'Yes' GROUP BY T2.interaction_type ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most common enzyme interaction type for medicines that are approved by the FDA.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Trade_Name FROM medicine AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.medicine_id FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T2.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T2.interaction_type = 'inhibitor' AND T3.Location = 'Mitochondrion');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the generic and trade names of medicines that inhibit enzymes located in the Mitochondrion.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T1 JOIN medicine AS T2 ON T1.medicine_id = T2.id WHERE T1.enzyme_id = (SELECT id FROM enzyme WHERE name = 'ALA synthase') EXCEPT SELECT name FROM medicine WHERE FDA_approved = 'No';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of FDA-approved medicines that interact with the 'ALA synthase' enzyme.

sql: WITH InteractionCounts AS (SELECT T2.enzyme_id, COUNT(T2.medicine_id) as InteractionCount FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 GROUP BY T2.enzyme_id) SELECT T1.name, T3.InteractionCount FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN InteractionCounts AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.enzyme_id WHERE T3.InteractionCount > 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing enzymes that interact with more than two medicines, showing the enzyme's name and the specific count of its medicine interactions.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Trade_Name FROM medicine AS T1 WHERE T1.FDA_approved = 'Yes' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T2.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T2.medicine_id = T1.id AND T3.Location = 'Mitochondrion');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and trade name for all FDA-approved medicines that interact with enzymes located in the mitochondrion.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T3.FDA_approved) FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 JOIN medicine AS T3 ON T2.medicine_id = T3.id WHERE T2.enzyme_id = T1.id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of enzymes that interact with both FDA-approved and non-FDA-approved medicines.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T1 JOIN medicine AS T2 ON T1.medicine_id = T2.id WHERE T1.enzyme_id = (SELECT id FROM enzyme WHERE name = 'ALA synthase') UNION SELECT T2.name FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T1 JOIN medicine AS T2 ON T1.medicine_id = T2.id WHERE T1.enzyme_id = (SELECT id FROM enzyme WHERE name = 'ALA dehydratase');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of unique medicines that interact with either the 'ALA synthase' or 'ALA dehydratase' enzymes.

sql: SELECT name FROM medicine WHERE FDA_approved = 'Yes' EXCEPT SELECT T2.name FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T1 JOIN medicine AS T2 ON T1.medicine_id = T2.id WHERE T2.FDA_approved = 'Yes';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of FDA-approved medicines that have no recorded enzyme interactions.

sql: SELECT T1.Location FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id JOIN medicine AS T3 ON T2.medicine_id = T3.id WHERE T3.FDA_approved = 'Yes' GROUP BY T1.Location ORDER BY COUNT(T2.medicine_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single enzyme location that interacts with the highest number of FDA-approved medicines.

sql: SELECT T1.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 WHERE T2.enzyme_id = T1.id) AS interaction_count FROM enzyme AS T1 ORDER BY interaction_count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each enzyme's name along with its total count of medicine interactions, ordered from the most interactive enzyme to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Chromosome FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id WHERE T2.medicine_id = (SELECT id FROM medicine WHERE name = 'Aripiprazole');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and chromosome location for each enzyme that interacts with the medicine 'Aripiprazole'.

sql: SELECT T3.name FROM medicine AS T3 WHERE T3.id IN (SELECT T1.medicine_id FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T1 JOIN enzyme AS T2 ON T1.enzyme_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Product = 'δ-Aminolevulinic acid');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of medicines that interact with enzymes producing 'δ-Aminolevulinic acid'.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T1 JOIN enzyme AS T2 ON T1.enzyme_id = T2.id WHERE T1.medicine_id IN (SELECT id FROM medicine WHERE FDA_approved = 'No') AND T1.interaction_type = 'inhibitor';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of enzymes that are inhibited by medicines not yet approved by the FDA.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT medicine_id FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction WHERE enzyme_id IN (SELECT id FROM enzyme WHERE Porphyria = 'none'));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of medicines that interact with enzymes not associated with Porphyria.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 WHERE T1.FDA_approved = 'Yes' AND T1.id IN (SELECT T2.medicine_id FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T2.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Chromosome = '3p21.1');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of FDA-approved medicines that interact with enzymes located on chromosome '3p21.1'.

sql: SELECT property_id, property_address FROM Properties WHERE property_type_code = 'house';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the property ID and address for all properties that are classified as a 'house'.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Customers;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total number of customers.

sql: SELECT T1.service_details, T2.organization_details FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the details for each service alongside the details of the organization that provides it.

sql: SELECT customer_details FROM Customers WHERE customer_id = 5;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the customer details for the specific customer identified by ID 5.

sql: SELECT thing_id, service_details FROM Things WHERE Type_of_Thing_Code = 'Electronics';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the ID and service details for all things classified as 'Electronics'.

sql: SELECT resident_id, other_details FROM Residents WHERE date_moved_in = '2015-03-27 12:00:00';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the resident ID and other details for all residents who moved in at noon on March 27, 2015.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT service_type_code FROM Services;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each of the unique service types available.

sql: SELECT * FROM Organizations WHERE organization_id = 7;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for the specific organization with an ID of 7, including its parent organization ID and descriptive details.

sql: SELECT thing_id, Date_and_Date FROM Timed_Status_of_Things WHERE Status_of_Thing_Code = 'Open';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID for each thing with an 'Open' status, along with the date and time that status was recorded.

sql: SELECT T1.other_details, T2.property_address FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Properties AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id LIMIT 10;
result_summary: Returns a sample of 10 rows matching resident details with their corresponding property address.

sql: SELECT T2.organization_details, COUNT(T1.service_id) FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id GROUP BY T2.organization_details;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each organization's details along with the total count of services it provides.

sql: SELECT T1.resident_id, T1.other_details FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Properties AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id WHERE T2.property_type_code = 'house';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the ID and other details for all residents who live in properties classified as 'house'.

sql: SELECT T1.organization_details AS Organization, T2.organization_details AS Parent_Organization FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.parent_organization_id = T2.organization_id;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows that list each organization alongside its corresponding parent organization, effectively mapping out the company hierarchy.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details, COUNT(T2.Customer_Event_ID) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows showing the details for each customer along with a count of their associated events.

sql: SELECT T1.other_details, T3.service_details FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Residents_Services AS T2 ON T1.resident_id = T2.resident_id JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id WHERE T3.service_type_code = 'Check';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing the other details of residents who have received a 'Check' type service, along with the details of that specific service.

sql: SELECT T1.thing_id, T1.Type_of_Thing_Code FROM Things AS T1 JOIN Timed_Status_of_Things AS T2 ON T1.thing_id = T2.thing_id WHERE T2.Status_of_Thing_Code = 'In Progress';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the ID and type code for all things that currently have a status of 'In Progress'.

sql: SELECT T1.property_address, COUNT(T2.resident_id) AS NumberOfResidents FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id GROUP BY T1.property_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each property address along with the total number of residents living at that property.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.thing_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the details of customers associated with events for a specific item (thing_id = 1).

sql: SELECT organization_details FROM Organizations WHERE organization_id IN (SELECT organization_id FROM Services WHERE service_type_code = 'Check');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the details of organizations that offer a 'Check' type service.

sql: SELECT T2.organization_details, COUNT(T1.organization_id) FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.parent_organization_id = T2.organization_id GROUP BY T2.organization_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each parent organization along with the total count of its direct sub-organizations.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Event_ID, T1.resident_id FROM Customer_Events AS T1 JOIN Customer_Event_Notes AS T2 ON T1.Customer_Event_ID = T2.Customer_Event_ID;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the customer event ID and resident ID for every customer event that has an associated note.

sql: SELECT T1.organization_id, COUNT(T2.thing_id) FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Things AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id GROUP BY T1.organization_id;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, with each row listing an organization's ID and the total count of 'things' associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.resident_id, T2.service_details FROM Residents_Services AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id WHERE T1.other_details = 'Unsatisfied';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows identifying residents who were 'Unsatisfied' with a service, showing their ID and the details of that specific service.

sql: SELECT T2.property_address FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Properties AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id ORDER BY T1.date_moved_in DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the property address of the most recently moved-in resident.

sql: SELECT property_id, COUNT(resident_id) FROM Residents GROUP BY property_id HAVING COUNT(resident_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing properties that have more than one resident, along with the total count of residents for each of those properties.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Things AS T3 ON T2.thing_id = T3.thing_id JOIN Organizations AS T4 ON T3.organization_id = T4.organization_id WHERE T4.organization_details = 'Denesik and Sons Party';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the details of all customers associated with the 'Denesik and Sons Party' organization.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id JOIN Residents_Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id JOIN Residents AS T4 ON T3.resident_id = T4.resident_id JOIN Properties AS T5 ON T4.property_id = T5.property_id WHERE T5.property_type_code = 'house';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique details of organizations that provide services to residents living in houses.

sql: SELECT T1.other_details FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Residents_Services AS T2 ON T1.resident_id = T2.resident_id JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id WHERE T3.service_type_code = 'Check' AND T2.other_details = 'Unsatisfied';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the residents who were marked as 'Unsatisfied' after receiving a 'Check' type service.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Customer_Event_Notes AS T3 ON T2.Customer_Event_ID = T3.Customer_Event_ID WHERE T3.service_type_code = 'Cleanning';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the customer details for each recorded instance of a 'Cleaning' service event.

sql: SELECT T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Things AS T2 JOIN Timed_Status_of_Things AS T3 ON T2.thing_id = T3.thing_id WHERE T2.organization_id = T1.organization_id AND T3.Status_of_Thing_Code = 'Close');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the details of organizations that have at least one associated item or 'thing' with a status of 'Close'.

sql: SELECT T1.other_details FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Residents_Services AS T2 ON T1.resident_id = T2.resident_id JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id JOIN Organizations AS T4 ON T3.organization_id = T4.organization_id WHERE T4.parent_organization_id = 7;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing details for residents who have used services provided by any organization under the parent organization with ID 7.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Customer_Event_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the details of the single customer with the most recorded events.

sql: SELECT T1.property_address FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Residents_Services AS T3 ON T2.resident_id = T3.resident_id JOIN Services AS T4 ON T3.service_id = T4.service_id JOIN Organizations AS T5 ON T4.organization_id = T5.organization_id WHERE T5.organization_details = 'Robel-Schulist Group';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the addresses of properties where residents use services provided by the 'Robel-Schulist Group'.

sql: SELECT T1.thing_id FROM Things AS T1 JOIN Timed_Status_of_Things AS T2 ON T1.thing_id = T2.thing_id WHERE T1.Type_of_Thing_Code = 'Electronics' GROUP BY T1.thing_id HAVING count(T2.Status_of_Thing_Code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique IDs for all electronic items that have had more than one status update recorded.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details, T3.property_address FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Properties AS T3 ON T2.property_id = T3.property_id WHERE T3.property_type_code = 'apartment';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the details of customers who have had an event at an apartment, along with the corresponding property's address.

sql: SELECT organization_details FROM Organizations WHERE organization_id IN (SELECT organization_id FROM Services WHERE service_type_code = 'Check') AND organization_id NOT IN (SELECT organization_id FROM Services WHERE service_type_code = 'Cleanning');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the details for an organization that provides a 'Check' service but not a 'Cleanning' service.

sql: SELECT T1.property_type_code, COUNT(T3.resident_id) FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Residents_Services AS T3 ON T2.resident_id = T3.resident_id WHERE T3.other_details = 'Unsatisfied' GROUP BY T1.property_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each property type alongside the total count of 'Unsatisfied' service reports from residents living in that property category.

sql: SELECT other_details FROM Residents WHERE CAST(julianday(date_moved_out) - julianday(date_moved_in) AS REAL) > (SELECT AVG(CAST(julianday(date_moved_out) - julianday(date_moved_in) AS REAL)) FROM Residents WHERE date_moved_out IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows containing the details of residents whose length of stay was longer than the average residency duration.

sql: SELECT T1.resident_id, T1.service_id FROM Residents_Services AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.resident_id = T2.resident_id WHERE T1.date_provided > T2.date_moved_out;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying erroneous records where a service was provided to a resident after their move-out date, listing the corresponding resident and service IDs.

sql: SELECT T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.parent_organization_id JOIN Things AS T3 ON T2.organization_id = T3.organization_id WHERE T3.Type_of_Thing_Code = 'Electronics';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows showing the details of parent organizations that have a sub-organization associated with items of the 'Electronics' type.

sql: SELECT customer_details FROM Customers WHERE customer_id = 5 UNION SELECT other_details FROM Residents WHERE resident_id = 6;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the combined details for a specific customer (ID 5) and a specific resident (ID 6) from their respective tables.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Things AS T3 ON T2.thing_id = T3.thing_id JOIN Timed_Status_of_Things AS T4 ON T3.thing_id = T4.thing_id WHERE T4.Status_of_Thing_Code = 'In Progress';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the details of customers who are associated with an item currently marked as 'In Progress'.

sql: SELECT T1.other_details FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Properties AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Residents_Services AS T3 ON T1.resident_id = T3.resident_id JOIN Services AS T4 ON T3.service_id = T4.service_id WHERE T2.property_address = '2580 Yundt Plains' AND T4.service_type_code = 'Cleanning';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.service_details FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Residents_Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id GROUP BY T1.service_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.resident_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the service details for each service that has been used by more than one distinct resident.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Things AS T1 WHERE T1.thing_id IN (SELECT thing_id FROM Timed_Locations_of_Things WHERE Location_Code = '288 Lang Ferry') AND T1.thing_id IN (SELECT thing_id FROM Timed_Status_of_Things WHERE Status_of_Thing_Code = 'Open');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for any 'thing' that has been located at '288 Lang Ferry' and also recorded with an 'Open' status.

sql: SELECT T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 WHERE T1.organization_id IN (SELECT parent_organization_id FROM Organizations) AND T1.organization_id NOT IN (SELECT organization_id FROM Services);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT resident_id FROM Residents_Services INTERSECT SELECT resident_id FROM Customer_Events;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the IDs of residents who have both used a service and attended a customer event.

sql: SELECT T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 WHERE T1.organization_id IN (SELECT organization_id FROM Things WHERE Type_of_Thing_Code = 'Commen') INTERSECT SELECT T2.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T2 WHERE T2.organization_id IN (SELECT organization_id FROM Services WHERE service_type_code = 'Check');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the details for organizations that are associated with both 'Commen' type things and 'Check' type services.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id WHERE strftime('%Y', T2.date_moved_out) = '2017';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the full details of properties from which a resident moved out during the year 2017.

sql: SELECT T1.service_details FROM Services AS T1 JOIN (SELECT organization_id FROM Things GROUP BY organization_id ORDER BY COUNT(thing_id) DESC LIMIT 1) AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the service details for the single organization that possesses the highest count of "things".

sql: SELECT * FROM train WHERE name = 'Guruvayur Express';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing all available details for the train named 'Guruvayur Express'.

sql: SELECT network_name, services FROM station WHERE local_authority = 'Watford';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the network name and services for stations located within the 'Watford' local authority.

sql: SELECT * FROM weekly_weather WHERE day_of_week = 'Monday';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing all weekly weather details (station ID, temperatures, precipitation, wind speed) for each station that recorded data on a Monday.

sql: SELECT name, origin, destination FROM train ORDER BY name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each train's name, origin, and destination, sorted alphabetically by train name.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM station;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all stations.

sql: SELECT station_id, day_of_week, high_temperature FROM weekly_weather ORDER BY high_temperature DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the station ID, day of the week, and temperature for the top five hottest recorded days, ordered from highest to lowest temperature.

sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT origin) FROM train;
result_summary: Returns a single row with the total count of unique departure origins for trains.

sql: SELECT name, origin, destination FROM train WHERE interval = 'Daily';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing trains that operate on a daily schedule, showing each train's name, origin, and destination.

sql: SELECT station_id, day_of_week, wind_speed_mph FROM weekly_weather WHERE wind_speed_mph > 12;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the station, day of the week, and wind speed for all weekly weather readings where winds were stronger than 12 mph.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT local_authority FROM station;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique local authorities that have a station.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.train_number FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T2.station_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and train number for all trains that pass through station ID 1.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, T2.day_of_week, T2.high_temperature FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T1.network_name = 'Amersham';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the high temperature for each day of the week for the 'Amersham' station network.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, COUNT(T2.train_id) AS number_of_trains FROM station AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.network_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each rail network name along with a total count of the trains that operate on it.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.station_id) AS number_of_stops FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each train's name along with the total number of stops on its route.

sql: SELECT day_of_week, AVG(high_temperature) AS avg_high_temp FROM weekly_weather GROUP BY day_of_week;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows showing the average high temperature calculated for each day of the week.

sql: SELECT station_id, MAX(precipitation) AS max_precipitation FROM weekly_weather GROUP BY station_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each weather station ID along with the maximum weekly precipitation recorded for that station.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, AVG(T2.wind_speed_mph) AS avg_wind FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.network_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each station network name along with the average weekly wind speed recorded across all stations within that network.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.destination FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T2.station_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and final destination of each train that passes through the station with ID 3.

sql: SELECT network_name FROM station WHERE id IN (SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather WHERE day_of_week = 'Tuesday' AND high_temperature > 60);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the network name for any station that recorded a high temperature above 60 on a Tuesday.

sql: SELECT station_id, AVG(high_temperature) AS avg_high FROM weekly_weather GROUP BY station_id HAVING AVG(high_temperature) > 55;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the weather stations where the average weekly high temperature exceeds 55 degrees, showing each station's ID and its specific average high temperature.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.station_id) AS stop_count FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.station_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of trains that have more than one stop, along with the total count of stops for each train.

sql: SELECT name, origin FROM train WHERE id IN (SELECT train_id FROM route WHERE station_id = 2);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and origin of all trains that pass through the station with ID 2.

sql: SELECT T2.day_of_week, T2.precipitation FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T1.local_authority = 'Chiltern';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the precipitation amount for each day of the week from weather stations located in the 'Chiltern' local authority.

sql: SELECT origin, COUNT(*) AS train_count FROM train GROUP BY origin;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each train origin city along with the total count of trains that depart from that origin.

sql: SELECT station_id, MIN(low_temperature) FROM weekly_weather GROUP BY station_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each weather station's ID along with the lowest temperature ever recorded for that station.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.train_number, T2.network_name, T2.services FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.train_id JOIN station AS T2 ON T3.station_id = T2.id WHERE T1.origin = 'Trivandrum';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows detailing the stations served by trains originating from 'Trivandrum', including the train's name and number, the station's network name, and the services provided at that station.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.network_name, T4.day_of_week, T4.high_temperature, T4.precipitation FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.train_id JOIN station AS T2 ON T3.station_id = T2.id JOIN weekly_weather AS T4 ON T2.id = T4.station_id WHERE T1.name = 'Ananthapuri Express';
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing the weekly weather forecast (day, high temperature, precipitation) for each station along the route of the 'Ananthapuri Express' train, including the station's network name.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.origin FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T2.station_id IN (SELECT id FROM station WHERE local_authority = 'Watford');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and origin of trains that pass through any station located in the 'Watford' local authority.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM weekly_weather AS T2 WHERE T2.station_id = T1.id AND T2.precipitation > 50);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of station networks that have experienced at least one week with precipitation greater than 50 units.

sql: SELECT T.name, T.num_stops FROM (SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.station_id) AS num_stops FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id GROUP BY T1.name) AS T WHERE T.num_stops > 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of trains that make more than two stops, along with the specific number of stops for each train.

sql: SELECT T1.name, AVG(T4.high_temperature) AS avg_route_temp FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.train_id JOIN station AS T2 ON T3.station_id = T2.id JOIN weekly_weather AS T4 ON T2.id = T4.station_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING AVG(T4.high_temperature) > 60;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT name FROM train WHERE origin = 'Trivandrum' UNION SELECT name FROM train WHERE destination = 'Chennai';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of all trains that either originate in 'Trivandrum' or have 'Chennai' as their destination.

sql: SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather WHERE high_temperature > 60 INTERSECT SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather WHERE wind_speed_mph > 12;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the ID of a weather station that recorded both a high temperature above 60 and wind speeds greater than 12 mph.

sql: SELECT id, network_name FROM station EXCEPT SELECT T1.id, T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and network name for all stations that are not part of any travel route.

sql: SELECT network_name, services FROM station WHERE id IN (SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather WHERE high_temperature > (SELECT AVG(high_temperature) FROM weekly_weather));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the network name and services for stations that have recorded a weekly high temperature greater than the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.local_authority) AS authorities_served FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.train_id JOIN station AS T2 ON T3.station_id = T2.id GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY authorities_served DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each train by name and the count of unique local authorities its route passes through, ordered from the train serving the most authorities to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, AVG(T2.high_temperature) AS avg_temp FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.id, T1.network_name ORDER BY avg_temp DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the station network with the highest average weekly high temperature, along with that calculated average.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T2.station_id = (SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather ORDER BY wind_speed_mph DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of trains that have a route passing through the station with the highest recorded weekly wind speed.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.origin, T1.destination FROM train AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(T2.station_id) FROM route AS T2 WHERE T2.train_id = T1.id) >= 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name, origin, and destination of trains whose routes include three or more stations.

sql: SELECT name FROM train WHERE id IN (SELECT train_id FROM route WHERE station_id IN (SELECT id FROM station WHERE local_authority = 'Chiltern')) AND id IN (SELECT train_id FROM route WHERE station_id IN (SELECT id FROM station WHERE local_authority = 'Watford'));
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a train that travels through stations in both the 'Chiltern' and 'Watford' local authorities.

sql: SELECT T1.day_of_week, T1.station_id, (T1.high_temperature - T1.low_temperature) AS temp_range FROM weekly_weather AS T1 WHERE (T1.high_temperature - T1.low_temperature) = (SELECT MAX(high_temperature - low_temperature) FROM weekly_weather AS T2 WHERE T2.station_id = T1.station_id);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying the day of the week and the calculated temperature range for the day(s) that experienced the largest temperature swing at each station.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN weekly_weather AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.station_id GROUP BY T1.id, T1.name HAVING AVG(T3.precipitation) > 30;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of trains that operate on routes where the average weekly precipitation across the stations on that route is greater than 30.

sql: SELECT name FROM train WHERE origin = 'Chennai' UNION SELECT network_name FROM station WHERE local_authority = 'Watford';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the combined names of all trains originating from 'Chennai' and all stations located within the 'Watford' local authority.

sql: SELECT T1.station_id FROM route AS T1 WHERE T1.train_id = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.station_id FROM route AS T2 WHERE T2.train_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the station ID for a station that is on the route of train 1 but not on the route of train 2.

sql: SELECT T2.network_name FROM route AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.station_id = T2.id WHERE T1.train_id = (SELECT train_id FROM route GROUP BY train_id ORDER BY COUNT(station_id) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the network name of each station on the route for the train that makes the most stops.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.origin, T1.destination FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T2.station_id IN (SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather WHERE wind_speed_mph > 13);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique origin and destination pairs for trains that travel through stations experiencing high winds (over 13 mph).

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, T2.day_of_week, T2.high_temperature FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T2.high_temperature = (SELECT MAX(high_temperature) FROM weekly_weather AS T3 WHERE T3.station_id = T1.id);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying the hottest day of the week for each station, listing the station's network name, the specific day, and its corresponding maximum high temperature.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.network_name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.train_id JOIN station AS T2 ON T3.station_id = T2.id WHERE T1.interval = 'Daily' AND T2.id IN (SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather WHERE low_temperature < 55);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique network names for stations that are serviced by daily trains and have experienced a weekly low temperature below 55 degrees.

sql: SELECT T1.local_authority, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.train_id) AS train_count, AVG(T4.high_temperature) AS avg_high_temp FROM station AS T1 JOIN route AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.station_id JOIN weekly_weather AS T4 ON T1.id = T4.station_id GROUP BY T1.local_authority;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, with each row listing a local authority, the number of distinct trains that service its stations, and the average weekly high temperature for those stations.

sql: SELECT T1.train_id, T2.train_id FROM route AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.station_id = T2.station_id AND T1.train_id < T2.train_id GROUP BY T1.train_id, T2.train_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, with each row listing a unique pair of train IDs that share at least one common station on their respective routes.

sql: SELECT Name, Capacity FROM stadium ORDER BY Capacity DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and capacity for all stadiums, ordered from largest to smallest capacity.

sql: SELECT Name, Song_Name FROM singer WHERE Country = 'United States';

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM singer WHERE Is_male = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and age for each male singer.

sql: SELECT Name FROM stadium WHERE Location = 'Raith Rovers';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Country FROM singer;

sql: SELECT Song_Name, Name FROM singer WHERE Song_release_year = '1992';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the song and the singer for all songs released in the year 1992.

sql: SELECT concert_Name, Theme, Year FROM concert ORDER BY Year ASC;

sql: SELECT Name, Capacity FROM stadium WHERE Capacity > 10000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and capacity for all stadiums with a capacity greater than 10,000.

sql: SELECT T1.concert_Name, T2.Name FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.Stadium_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each concert's name along with the name of the stadium where it is held.

sql: SELECT Country, COUNT(*) FROM singer GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the total number of singers from each country.

sql: SELECT T4.Name FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.Stadium_ID JOIN singer_in_concert AS T3 ON T1.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID JOIN singer AS T4 ON T3.Singer_ID = T4.Singer_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Somerset Park';

sql: SELECT AVG(Age) FROM singer;

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM singer WHERE Age = (SELECT MAX(Age) FROM singer);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and age of the oldest singer in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM singer WHERE Country = 'France' UNION SELECT Name, Country FROM singer WHERE Country = 'Netherlands';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names and countries of all singers who are from either France or the Netherlands.

sql: SELECT Name FROM stadium ORDER BY Capacity DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the stadium with the highest capacity.

sql: SELECT Year, COUNT(*) FROM concert GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each year along with the total number of concerts held in that year.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T3.concert_Name FROM singer_in_concert AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T1.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each singer's name alongside the name of the concert they performed in.

sql: SELECT concert_Name FROM concert WHERE Stadium_ID IN (SELECT Stadium_ID FROM stadium WHERE Capacity < 11000);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of concerts held in stadiums with a capacity of less than 11,000.

sql: SELECT T1.concert_Name, T2.Name FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.Stadium_ID WHERE T1.Theme = 'Free choice';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the concert name and its corresponding stadium for concerts with the theme 'Free choice'.

sql: SELECT Name, Song_Name, Song_release_year FROM singer WHERE Song_release_year > '2000';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the singer's name, song title, and release year for all songs released after the year 2000.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM singer WHERE Age = (SELECT MIN(Age) FROM singer);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the youngest singer by their name and country.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.concert_ID) FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.Stadium_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each stadium name and the total number of concerts held there.

sql: SELECT Name FROM singer AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM singer_in_concert AS T2 WHERE T2.Singer_ID = T1.Singer_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all singers who have participated in at least one concert.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.concert_ID) FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID GROUP BY T1.Singer_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.concert_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing singers who have performed in more than one concert, along with the total number of concerts for each.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID WHERE T3.Year = '2014' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID WHERE T3.Year = '2015';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a singer who performed in a concert in 2014 but did not perform in one in 2015.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.Stadium_ID GROUP BY T1.Stadium_ID ORDER BY COUNT(T1.concert_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the stadium that has hosted the most concerts.

sql: SELECT Name, Location FROM stadium EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name, T2.Location FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.Stadium_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and locations of all stadiums that have not hosted a concert.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM singer WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM singer);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age for each singer who is older than the average age of all singers.

sql: SELECT T2.concert_Name, COUNT(T1.Singer_ID) FROM singer_in_concert AS T1 JOIN concert AS T2 ON T1.concert_ID = T2.concert_ID GROUP BY T1.concert_ID HAVING COUNT(T1.Singer_ID) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the names of concerts that feature more than two singers, along with the total count of singers for each of those concerts.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID GROUP BY T1.Singer_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.concert_ID) = (SELECT MAX(cnt) FROM (SELECT COUNT(concert_ID) AS cnt FROM singer_in_concert GROUP BY Singer_ID));
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the singer who has performed in the most concerts.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 WHERE T1.Country = 'France' AND T1.Singer_ID IN (SELECT T2.Singer_ID FROM singer_in_concert AS T2 JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID WHERE T3.Theme = 'Free choice');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of singers from France who have also performed in a concert with the theme 'Free choice'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM singer_in_concert AS T2 JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID WHERE T3.Year = '2014' AND T2.Singer_ID = T1.Singer_ID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a singer who did not perform in any concert during the year 2014.

sql: SELECT Name, Country, Age FROM singer AS T1 WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM singer AS T2 WHERE T2.Country = T1.Country);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, country, and age of singers who are older than the average age of singers from their respective country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID WHERE T3.Year = '2014' INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM singer WHERE Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the singer from the 'United States' who also performed in a concert during the year 2014.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T3.Location FROM singer_in_concert AS T1 JOIN singer AS T4 ON T1.Singer_ID = T4.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T2 ON T1.concert_ID = T2.concert_ID JOIN stadium AS T3 ON T2.Stadium_ID = T3.Stadium_ID WHERE T4.Name = 'Timbaland';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location of stadiums where the singer 'Timbaland' has performed.

sql: SELECT Name, Song_Name FROM singer WHERE Song_release_year = (SELECT MIN(Song_release_year) FROM singer);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the singer and their song that corresponds to the earliest release year in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Singer_ID) FROM concert AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.concert_ID = T2.concert_ID GROUP BY T1.Year;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each year and the total number of distinct singers who performed in a concert during that year.

result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the singer who performed in both concert 1 and concert 2.

sql: SELECT Name, Average FROM stadium WHERE Average > (SELECT MIN(Lowest) FROM stadium) AND Stadium_ID IN (SELECT Stadium_ID FROM concert);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and average value for stadiums that have hosted a concert, provided their average value is greater than the minimum lowest value recorded across all stadiums.

sql: SELECT T2.Theme, AVG(T3.Age) FROM singer_in_concert AS T1 JOIN concert AS T2 ON T1.concert_ID = T2.concert_ID JOIN singer AS T3 ON T1.Singer_ID = T3.Singer_ID GROUP BY T2.Theme;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN membership_register_branch AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Register_Year = '2016' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN membership_register_branch AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Register_Year = '2017';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT city, state_province_county FROM Addresses WHERE state_province_county = 'Georgia';

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.city, T2.country FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_address_id = T2.address_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, providing a list of all customers with their corresponding city and country of residence.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.lesson_date, T2.price FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the full name of each staff member alongside the date and price for every lesson they have conducted.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.lesson_id) AS number_of_lessons FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, SUM(T1.amount_payment) AS total_paid FROM Customer_Payments AS T1 GROUP BY T1.customer_id;

sql: SELECT T1.nickname, T1.first_name, COUNT(T2.lesson_id) AS lessons_taught FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id;

sql: SELECT T1.vehicle_details, AVG(T2.price) AS avg_lesson_price FROM Vehicles AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.vehicle_id = T2.vehicle_id GROUP BY T1.vehicle_details;

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T2.amount_payment) AS total_payments FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each customer's ID, first name, and last name, along with the total sum of payments made by that customer.

sql: SELECT T2.city, COUNT(T1.customer_id) AS number_of_customers FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_address_id = T2.address_id GROUP BY T2.city;

sql: SELECT T1.lesson_date, T1.price, T2.vehicle_details FROM Lessons AS T1 JOIN Vehicles AS T2 ON T1.vehicle_id = T2.vehicle_id WHERE T2.vehicle_details = 'Truck';

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.lesson_date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.lesson_status_code = 'Cancelled';

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.line_1_number_building, T2.city FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.staff_address_id = T2.address_id;

sql: SELECT MAX(amount_payment), MIN(amount_payment) FROM Customer_Payments WHERE payment_method_code = 'Direct Debit';

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.datetime_payment FROM Customer_Payments AS T1 WHERE T1.amount_payment > (SELECT AVG(amount_payment) FROM Customer_Payments);

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.customer_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING count(T2.customer_id) > 0;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Payments);
