sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Vehicles AS T3 ON T2.vehicle_id = T3.vehicle_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.vehicle_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who have taught lessons using more than one distinct vehicle.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T2.amount_payment) AS total_paid FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id ORDER BY total_paid DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single customer who has paid the most in total, showing their first name, last name, and the total sum of their payments.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T1.amount_outstanding, T3.total_paid FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount_payment) AS total_paid FROM Customer_Payments GROUP BY customer_id) AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T1.amount_outstanding > T3.total_paid;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying customers whose outstanding balance is greater than the total amount they have paid. The result lists the customer's name, their outstanding balance, and the sum of their total payments.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id HAVING COUNT(T2.lesson_id) > (SELECT AVG(lesson_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(lesson_id) AS lesson_count FROM Lessons GROUP BY staff_id));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the first and last names of staff members who have taught a number of lessons greater than the average number of lessons taught per staff member.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Staff AS T3 ON T2.staff_id = T3.staff_id JOIN Vehicles AS T4 ON T2.vehicle_id = T4.vehicle_id WHERE T3.first_name = 'Janessa' AND T4.vehicle_details = 'Car';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_status_code = 'Good Customer' INTERSECT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.payment_method_code = 'Direct Debit';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have a 'Good Customer' status and also use 'Direct Debit' for payments.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_address_id = T2.address_id WHERE T2.state_province_county = 'Georgia' EXCEPT SELECT first_name, last_name FROM Customers WHERE customer_status_code = 'Good Customer';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows with the first and last names of customers from Georgia who are not considered 'Good Customers'.

sql: SELECT T2.vehicle_details FROM Lessons AS T1 JOIN Vehicles AS T2 ON T1.vehicle_id = T2.vehicle_id ORDER BY T1.price DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the vehicle used for the single most expensive lesson, sorted by price from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.city, T3.price FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_address_id = T2.address_id JOIN Lessons AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T3.lesson_status_code = 'Cancelled';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the first name, last name, and city of customers who have cancelled a lesson, along with the price of that specific cancelled lesson.

sql: SELECT T1.nickname, T1.first_name, SUM(T2.price) AS total_revenue FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.lesson_status_code != 'Cancelled' GROUP BY T1.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each staff member's nickname and first name, along with the total revenue they have generated from all non-cancelled lessons.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Lessons GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT staff_id) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have taken lessons with more than one distinct staff member.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 WHERE T1.staff_id NOT IN (SELECT staff_id FROM Lessons WHERE lesson_status_code = 'Cancelled');
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the first and last names of all staff members who have never had a lesson cancelled.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T1.amount_outstanding FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_status_code = 'Bad Customer' ORDER BY T1.amount_outstanding DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns the top 3 customers with a 'Bad Customer' status, listing their first name, last name, and outstanding balance, ordered from the highest to the lowest amount owed.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 LEFT JOIN Customer_Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.customer_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of all customers who have not made any payments.

sql: SELECT T2.city, SUM(T1.amount_outstanding) AS total_outstanding FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_address_id = T2.address_id GROUP BY T2.city ORDER BY total_outstanding DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the total outstanding customer payment amounts for each city, ordered from the city with the highest total amount to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Lessons AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have not taken any lessons.

sql: SELECT T1.nickname, T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.customer_id) AS distinct_customers_taught FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id ORDER BY distinct_customers_taught DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each staff member's name and nickname, along with the total count of distinct customers they have taught, ordered from the highest count to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.lesson_id) as lesson_count FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id ORDER BY lesson_count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top three customers by their total number of lessons attended, ordered from highest to lowest count.

sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN lesson_status_code = 'Cancelled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*) FROM Lessons;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value that represents the overall percentage of lessons with a 'Cancelled' status.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Payments) EXCEPT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Lessons);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have made a payment but have not yet taken any lessons.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.lesson_date = (SELECT MIN(lesson_date) FROM Lessons);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of the staff member who taught a lesson on the earliest recorded date.

sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', datetime_payment) AS payment_month, SUM(amount_payment) AS total_paid FROM Customer_Payments GROUP BY payment_month;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each payment month and the corresponding total amount paid by customers during that month.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.lesson_id) as lesson_count FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 on T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.customer_address_id = T4.address_id WHERE T4.country = 'USA' GROUP BY T1.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each staff member's first and last name along with the total number of lessons they have taught to customers located in the USA.

sql: SELECT Club_ID, name, Region, Start_year FROM club;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows providing a complete list of all clubs, showing each club's ID, name, region, and start year.

sql: SELECT name, Position, Points FROM player;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name, position, and total points for each player in the dataset.

sql: SELECT name, Region FROM club WHERE Region = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and region for the club located in the USA.

sql: SELECT name, Start_year FROM club ORDER BY Start_year DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and start year of each club, ordered from the most to least recent start year.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Country FROM competition;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique countries where competitions are held.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM player;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total number of players.

sql: SELECT name, Apps, Tries FROM player WHERE Position = 'Full Back';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying players who are 'Full Backs', showing their name, number of appearances (Apps), and tries scored.

sql: SELECT Competition_type, Country FROM competition WHERE Year = 2006;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the competition type and host country for all competitions that occurred in the year 2006.

sql: SELECT Rank, Club_ID, Total FROM club_rank WHERE Total > 20;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying clubs with a total score or metric greater than 20, listing each club's rank, ID, and total value.

sql: SELECT name, Position, Points FROM player WHERE Club_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, position, and points for each player belonging to a specific club (Club ID 1).

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.Player_ID) FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each club's name along with the total number of players registered for that club.

sql: SELECT T1.Club_ID, COUNT(T1.Player_ID) FROM player AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Club_ID HAVING COUNT(T1.Player_ID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the club ID and total player count for all clubs that have two or more players.

sql: SELECT name FROM club WHERE Region = 'USA' UNION SELECT name FROM club WHERE Region = 'UK';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of all clubs located in either the 'USA' or the 'UK'.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T3.name, T1.Score FROM competition_result AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID_1 = T2.Club_ID JOIN club AS T3 ON T1.Club_ID_2 = T3.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the results of competitions, showing the names of the two competing clubs and their final score for each match.

sql: SELECT name FROM club WHERE Club_ID IN (SELECT Club_ID FROM club_rank WHERE Gold > 10);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the club that has won more than 10 gold medals.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.Rank FROM club AS T1 JOIN club_rank AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Rank < 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and rank for each club that is ranked in the top two positions (i.e., a rank of 1 or 2).

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 JOIN club_rank AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Gold = T2.Silver;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of clubs that have an equal number of gold and silver awards.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Region = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all players who belong to clubs located in the 'USA' region.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T1.Country, T2.Score FROM competition AS T1 JOIN competition_result AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID WHERE T1.Competition_type = 'Friendly';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the year, host country, and final score for all competitions classified as 'Friendly'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, CASE WHEN T2.Total > 20 THEN 'High Medal Count' ELSE 'Low Medal Count' END FROM club AS T1 JOIN club_rank AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each club's name and a classification of 'High Medal Count' or 'Low Medal Count' based on whether their total medal count is greater than 20.

sql: SELECT name, Points FROM player WHERE Points > (SELECT AVG(Points) FROM player);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and point totals for players who have scored more than the average number of points across all players.

sql: SELECT name FROM club WHERE Club_ID NOT IN (SELECT Club_ID FROM player);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any club that does not have any players.

sql: SELECT T2.name, SUM(T1.Points) FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each club's name along with the total points accumulated by all players in that club.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name FROM competition_result AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID_1 = T2.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of all clubs that have participated in at least one competition.

sql: SELECT Competition_ID, Competition_type, Country FROM competition WHERE Country <> 'Italy';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing all competitions held outside of Italy, showing each competition's ID, type, and country.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.Region, T3.Total FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID JOIN club_rank AS T3 ON T1.Club_ID = T3.Club_ID WHERE T1.Position = 'Full Back';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name, club region, and the club's total rank score for any player whose position is 'Full Back'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM player AS T2 WHERE T2.Club_ID = T1.Club_ID AND T2.Points > 30);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of clubs that have at least one player who has scored more than 30 points.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Position FROM player AS T1 WHERE T1.Apps > (SELECT AVG(T2.Apps) FROM player AS T2 WHERE T2.Club_ID = T1.Club_ID);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names and positions for players whose number of appearances is greater than the average for their own club.

sql: SELECT T1.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM player AS T2 WHERE T2.Club_ID = T1.Club_ID) AS PlayerCount FROM club AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each club's name along with a calculated count of the total number of players belonging to that club.

sql: WITH ClubMedals AS (SELECT Club_ID, Gold, Silver FROM club_rank WHERE Total > 15) SELECT T1.name, T2.Gold, T2.Silver FROM club AS T1 JOIN ClubMedals AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID ORDER BY T2.Gold DESC;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, gold, and silver medal counts for clubs that have won more than 15 total medals, ordered from the most gold medals to the least.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM competition_result AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID_1 = T2.Club_ID OR T1.Club_ID_2 = T2.Club_ID JOIN competition AS T3 ON T1.Competition_ID = T3.Competition_ID WHERE T3.Country = 'Italy' INTERSECT SELECT T2.name FROM competition_result AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID_1 = T2.Club_ID OR T1.Club_ID_2 = T2.Club_ID JOIN competition AS T3 ON T1.Competition_ID = T3.Competition_ID WHERE T3.Country = 'Spain';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the club that has participated in competitions in both Italy and Spain.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 JOIN club_rank AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID EXCEPT SELECT name FROM club WHERE Start_year = '2009';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all ranked clubs, excluding any that were established in 2009.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Region FROM club AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Club_ID FROM (SELECT Club_ID_1 AS Club_ID FROM competition_result UNION ALL SELECT Club_ID_2 AS Club_ID FROM competition_result) GROUP BY Club_ID HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and region of clubs that have participated in more than one competition or match.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Club_ID = (SELECT Club_ID FROM club_rank ORDER BY Gold DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all players belonging to the club that has won the most gold medals.

sql: SELECT T2.Competition_type, T2.Country, T3.name AS Club1, T4.name AS Club2, T1.Score FROM competition_result AS T1 JOIN competition AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID JOIN club AS T3 ON T1.Club_ID_1 = T3.Club_ID JOIN club AS T4 ON T1.Club_ID_2 = T4.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing individual competition results, showing the competition type, country, the names of the two competing clubs, and the final score for each match.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 WHERE (SELECT Gold FROM club_rank AS T2 WHERE T2.Club_ID = T1.Club_ID) > (SELECT Silver FROM club_rank AS T3 WHERE T3.Club_ID = T1.Club_ID);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of clubs that have won more gold medals than silver medals.

sql: SELECT name, Region FROM club WHERE Club_ID IN (SELECT Club_ID_1 FROM competition_result) UNION SELECT name, Region FROM club WHERE Club_ID IN (SELECT Club_ID_2 FROM competition_result);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and region for all clubs that have participated in a competition.

sql: SELECT T2.name, AVG(T1.Points) AS AveragePoints FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T2.name HAVING AVG(T1.Points) > 30;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing clubs where the average player point score is greater than 30, showing the club's name and the calculated average score.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name AS ClubName, T2.Start_year FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Club_ID IN (SELECT Club_ID FROM club WHERE Start_year < '2000');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing players, their associated club, and the club's start year, specifically for clubs that were established before the year 2000.

sql: SELECT name, Apps, Club_ID, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY Club_ID ORDER BY Apps DESC) AS App_Rank_In_Club FROM player;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each player, their number of appearances ('Apps'), and their club ID, along with a calculated rank of their appearances within their respective club from most to fewest.

sql: SELECT T1.Region, AVG(T2.Total) AS AvgTotalMedals FROM club AS T1 JOIN club_rank AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.Region ORDER BY AvgTotalMedals DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the region with the highest average total medals per club, including that calculated average.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Country) FROM competition_result AS T2 JOIN competition AS T3 ON T2.Competition_ID = T3.Competition_ID WHERE T2.Club_ID_1 = T1.Club_ID OR T2.Club_ID_2 = T1.Club_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of clubs that have participated in competitions located in more than one country.

sql: SELECT name, Points, Apps FROM player WHERE Club_ID = (SELECT Club_ID FROM club WHERE name = 'AIB');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the name, points, and number of appearances for players belonging to the 'AIB' club.

sql: SELECT name FROM club WHERE Club_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Club_ID FROM player) AND Club_ID IN (SELECT Club_ID FROM club_rank WHERE Total < 20);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of clubs that have players and a total rank score of less than 20.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T1.Country FROM competition AS T1 JOIN competition_result AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID WHERE CAST(SUBSTR(T2.Score, 1, INSTR(T2.Score, ':') - 1) AS INT) + CAST(SUBSTR(T2.Score, INSTR(T2.Score, ':') + 1) AS INT) = (SELECT MAX(CAST(SUBSTR(Score, 1, INSTR(Score, ':') - 1) AS INT) + CAST(SUBSTR(Score, INSTR(Score, ':') + 1) AS INT)) FROM competition_result);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the year and hosting country of the competition that contained the single highest-scoring match, which is found by summing the two numbers in the 'score' field.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Position, T1.Tries FROM player AS T1 WHERE T1.Tries > (SELECT AVG(T2.Tries) FROM player AS T2 WHERE T2.Position = T1.Position);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying players with an above-average number of tries compared to others in their same position, showing their name, position, and number of tries.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name AS ClubName, T2.Start_year, T3.Rank FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID JOIN club_rank AS T3 ON T1.Club_ID = T3.Club_ID WHERE T2.Start_year > '2000';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing players, their club's name, the club's start year, and its rank, for all clubs that were founded after the year 2000.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name, T3.name AS ClubName FROM player AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID AND T1.Player_ID < T2.Player_ID JOIN club AS T3 ON T1.Club_ID = T3.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing all unique pairs of teammates, showing the names of both players and the name of the club they play for together.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.Total FROM club AS T1 JOIN club_rank AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Total > (SELECT Total FROM club_rank WHERE Club_ID = (SELECT Club_ID FROM club WHERE name = 'AIB'));
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of clubs and their total scores, specifically for clubs that have a higher total score than the club named 'AIB'.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM competition_result AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID_1 = T2.Club_ID EXCEPT SELECT T2.name FROM competition_result AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID_2 = T2.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of clubs that have participated in competitions only as the first listed club (Club_ID_1) and never as the second (Club_ID_2).

sql: SELECT * FROM Student;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows, providing the complete record for every student, including their ID, name, age, gender, major, advisor, and city code.

sql: SELECT PetType, pet_age FROM Pets WHERE weight > 13.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the type and age for any pet weighing over 13.0.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT PetType FROM Pets;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each unique type of pet available in the dataset.

sql: SELECT LName FROM Student ORDER BY LName ASC;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing the last names of all students, ordered alphabetically.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Student;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of students.

sql: SELECT AVG(pet_age) FROM Pets;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average age of all pets in the dataset.

sql: SELECT * FROM Pets WHERE PetType = 'dog';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing all recorded information (ID, type, age, and weight) for every pet identified as a 'dog'.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first name, last name, and age of students from the city with code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT MAX(weight) FROM Pets;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the maximum weight found among all pets.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Age > 20;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last names of all students who are older than 20.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T3.PetType FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last name of each student alongside the type of pet they own, with a separate row for each pet.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, count(T2.PetID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, providing a count of pets for each student identified by their ID.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = 'cat';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of students who own a cat.

sql: SELECT T1.PetType, AVG(T1.pet_age) FROM Pets AS T1 GROUP BY T1.PetType;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each pet type along with the average age for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.PetType, COUNT(*) FROM Pets AS T1 GROUP BY T1.PetType;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each distinct pet type and the total count of pets for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID FROM Has_Pet AS T1 GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING count(T1.PetID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the student ID for any student who owns more than one pet.

sql: SELECT T1.PetType FROM Pets AS T1 GROUP BY T1.PetType HAVING AVG(T1.weight) > 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the types of pets whose average weight is greater than 10.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 LEFT JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.PetID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 32 rows listing the first and last names of all students who do not have a pet.

sql: SELECT T1.PetType, MAX(T1.weight) FROM Pets AS T1 GROUP BY T1.PetType;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each pet type along with the maximum weight recorded for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1, Has_Pet AS T2, Pets AS T3 WHERE T1.StuID = T2.StuID AND T2.PetID = T3.PetID AND T3.pet_age < 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of students who own a pet that is younger than two years old.

sql: SELECT T1.Sex, COUNT(T3.PetID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID GROUP BY T1.Sex;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing a breakdown of the total number of pets owned by students, grouped by the student's sex.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code, COUNT(T2.StuID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.city_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each city code alongside the total number of pets owned by students in that city.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T1.Age, T3.PetType, T3.pet_age FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID ORDER BY T1.Age;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing students and the details of their pets (type and age), ordered by the student's age from youngest to oldest.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.Major = 600;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of students in major code 600 who have a pet.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Pet);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students who own a pet.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID NOT IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Pet);
result_summary: Returns 32 rows containing the first and last names of all students who do not own a pet.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.weight = (SELECT MAX(weight) FROM Pets);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the first and last name of the student who owns the heaviest pet.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.pet_age > (SELECT AVG(pet_age) FROM Pets);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of a student who owns a pet that is older than the average age of all pets.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student WHERE StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Pet));
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the first and last names of students whose age is greater than the average age of all students who own a pet.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Has_Pet AS T2 JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T2.StuID = T1.StuID AND T3.PetType = 'dog');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of each student who is registered as owning a dog.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Pet WHERE PetID IN (SELECT PetID FROM Pets WHERE PetType = 'dog')) AND StuID NOT IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Pet WHERE PetID IN (SELECT PetID FROM Pets WHERE PetType = 'cat'));
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of students who own a dog but do not own a cat.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Pet) UNION SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Age > 20;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows containing the first and last names of all unique students who either own a pet or are older than 20.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.city_code = 'HKG' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = 'dog';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of students who both live in the city with code 'HKG' and own a dog.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID EXCEPT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = 'cat';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of a student who owns a pet that is not a cat.

sql: SELECT T1.Major, AVG(T3.pet_age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID GROUP BY T1.Major;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing student majors alongside the average age of pets owned by students within each major.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Pet GROUP BY StuID HAVING COUNT(PetID) = (SELECT MAX(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(PetID) as c FROM Has_Pet GROUP BY StuID)));
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of the student who owns the maximum number of pets.

sql: SELECT * FROM Pets WHERE PetType = (SELECT PetType FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all details for every pet belonging to the most common pet type in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Fname, T2.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.Major = T2.Major AND T1.StuID < T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 180 rows, with each row representing a unique pair of students who share the same academic major.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T3.PetType, T3.pet_age FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T1.StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Pet GROUP BY StuID HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first name, last name, pet type, and pet age for all pets belonging to students who own more than one pet.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code, AVG(T3.weight) AS avg_pet_weight FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID GROUP BY T1.city_code ORDER BY avg_pet_weight DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city code with the highest average pet weight, calculated across all pets owned by students in that city.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = (SELECT T3.PetType FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T1.Fname = 'Linda');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of all students who own the same type of pet as the student named 'Linda'.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = 'dog';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average age of all students who own a dog.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Major IN (SELECT Major FROM Student GROUP BY Major HAVING count(*) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 33 rows listing the first and last names of all students who are enrolled in majors that have more than one student.

sql: SELECT T1.PetType, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.StuID) FROM Pets AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.PetID = T2.PetID GROUP BY T1.PetType;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each pet type alongside a count of the distinct students who own a pet of that type.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Pets AS T1 WHERE T1.weight > (SELECT AVG(T2.weight) FROM Pets AS T2 WHERE T2.PetType = T1.PetType);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for pets that weigh more than the average weight of their specific pet type.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Has_Pet AS T2 WHERE T2.StuID = T1.StuID) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of the student who owns exactly two pets.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code, avg(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.city_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each city code along with the average age of students in that city who own a pet.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Age < (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the first and last names of students who are younger than the average student age.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = (SELECT city_code FROM Student WHERE Fname = 'Linda' AND LName = 'Smith') AND StuID != 1001;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of students who live in the same city as 'Linda Smith', excluding the student with ID 1001.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, count(T3.PetID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType IN (SELECT PetType FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType HAVING count(*) > 1) GROUP BY T1.Fname, T1.LName;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing students by full name and the number of pets they own, but only counting pets that belong to a common type (a pet type that appears more than once in the database).

sql: SELECT * FROM railway;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, providing a complete record for each railway, including details on its builder, build date, location, and object number.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM manager WHERE Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all managers who are from the United States, along with their country.

sql: SELECT Name, `From`, Arrival FROM train WHERE `From` = 'Wardha';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name, departure station, and arrival time for the train departing from 'Wardha'.

sql: SELECT Railway, Builder, Location FROM railway WHERE Location = 'York';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name, builder, and location for the railway located in 'York'.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM manager ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing all managers and their ages, ordered from the oldest to the youngest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Location FROM railway;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each unique location of the railways.

sql: SELECT * FROM train WHERE Railway_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing all available details for trains belonging to a specific railway, identified by Railway ID 1.

sql: SELECT Name, Level FROM manager WHERE Level = 5;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and level for all managers who are at Level 5.

sql: SELECT * FROM railway_manage ORDER BY From_Year ASC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows detailing railway management assignments, including the railway ID, manager ID, and their start year, ordered chronologically from the earliest assignment.

sql: SELECT Name, Train_Num FROM train WHERE Name = 'Sewagram Exp';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and number for the train specifically named 'Sewagram Exp'.

sql: SELECT T1.Railway, T2.Name FROM railway AS T1 JOIN train AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each train's name along with the name of the railway it is operated by.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country, T2.From_Year FROM manager AS T1 JOIN railway_manage AS T2 ON T1.Manager_ID = T2.Manager_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each railway manager's name, their country, and the year their management period began.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, COUNT(T2.Train_ID) FROM railway AS T1 JOIN train AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID GROUP BY T1.Location;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each railway location and the total number of trains associated with that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Railway, COUNT(T2.Train_ID) FROM railway AS T1 LEFT JOIN train AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID GROUP BY T1.Railway;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each railway company and the total number of trains it operates, including railways that have no trains.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Railway_ID) FROM manager AS T1 JOIN railway_manage AS T2 ON T1.Manager_ID = T2.Manager_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each manager's name along with the total count of railways they manage.

sql: SELECT * FROM train WHERE Railway_ID IN (SELECT Railway_ID FROM railway WHERE Location = 'York');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all details for trains associated with railways located in 'York'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM manager AS T1 JOIN railway_manage AS T2 ON T1.Manager_ID = T2.Manager_ID WHERE T2.From_Year = '2010';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the manager who started their railway management position in the year 2010.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Arrival FROM train AS T1 JOIN railway AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID WHERE T2.Railway = 'SECR';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and arrival time for all trains operating on the 'SECR' railway.

sql: SELECT Wheels, COUNT(*) FROM railway GROUP BY Wheels;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, providing a count of railways for each distinct wheel arrangement.

sql: SELECT `From`, COUNT(*) FROM train GROUP BY `From` ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows counting the number of trains departing from each station, ordered from the station with the most departures to the least.

sql: SELECT * FROM railway WHERE Built LIKE '%1902%';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all available details for railways whose construction history mentions the year 1902.

sql: SELECT Country, AVG(Age) FROM manager GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country along with the average age of managers for that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Railway, T2.Name FROM railway AS T1 JOIN train AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID WHERE T2.`From` = 'Mumbai CST';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of trains that originate from 'Mumbai CST' and the corresponding railway operator for each train.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.From_Year FROM manager AS T1, railway_manage AS T2 WHERE T1.Manager_ID = T2.Manager_ID AND T1.Name = 'Ben Curtis';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the manager 'Ben Curtis' and the year they began their management role in the railway.

sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Builder) FROM railway;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value: the total count of unique railway builders.

sql: SELECT T1.Railway, T1.Location FROM railway AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM train AS T2 WHERE T2.Railway_ID = T1.Railway_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location of railways that operate more than one train.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Train_Num FROM train AS T1 WHERE T1.Railway_ID IN (SELECT T2.Railway_ID FROM railway AS T2 WHERE T2.Builder = 'SECR Ashford');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and train number for all trains that operate on railways built by 'SECR Ashford'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM manager AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM railway_manage AS T2 WHERE T1.Manager_ID = T2.Manager_ID AND T2.From_Year > '2009');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of managers who began a management role at a railway starting after the year 2009.

sql: SELECT T1.Railway, T1.Builder FROM railway AS T1 WHERE T1.Wheels = '4-4-0' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Railway, T2.Builder FROM railway AS T2 WHERE T2.Location = 'York';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the railway and its builder for any railway that both has a '4-4-0' wheel arrangement and is located in 'York'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM manager AS T1 WHERE T1.Age > 45 EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM manager AS T2 WHERE T2.Country = 'United States' AND T2.Working_year_starts = '2004';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of managers over the age of 45, excluding any from the United States who started working in 2004.

sql: SELECT 'Manager' AS Type, Name FROM manager WHERE Age > 50 UNION SELECT 'Railway' AS Type, Railway FROM railway WHERE Built < '1902';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows combining a list of managers older than 50 with a list of railways built before 1902, identifying each record's type.

sql: WITH RailTrainCount AS (SELECT T1.Railway_ID, COUNT(T2.Train_ID) as NumTrains FROM railway AS T1 JOIN train AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID GROUP BY T1.Railway_ID) SELECT T3.Railway, T3.Location, T4.NumTrains FROM railway AS T3 JOIN RailTrainCount AS T4 ON T3.Railway_ID = T4.Railway_ID WHERE T4.NumTrains > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing railways that operate more than one train, showing each railway's name, location, and its total train count.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM manager AS T1 WHERE T1.Age = (SELECT MAX(Age) FROM manager WHERE Country = 'United States');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and age of the oldest manager from the United States.

sql: SELECT T1.`From`, COUNT(T1.Train_ID) FROM train AS T1 GROUP BY T1.`From` HAVING COUNT(T1.Train_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each train's origin location along with a count of how many trains depart from that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Level FROM manager AS T1 WHERE T1.Level = 5 AND T1.Manager_ID IN (SELECT Manager_ID FROM railway_manage WHERE From_Year = '2010');
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and level of managers at 'Level 5' who began managing a railway in the year 2010.

sql: SELECT T1.Railway FROM railway AS T1 LEFT JOIN train AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID WHERE T2.Train_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of railways that do not have any trains associated with them.

sql: SELECT Name FROM train WHERE Railway_ID IN (SELECT Railway_ID FROM railway WHERE Location = 'Bo''ness');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the train that operates on railways located in 'Bo''ness'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM manager AS T1 WHERE T1.Working_year_starts > '2003' AND EXISTS (SELECT T2.Manager_ID FROM railway_manage AS T2 WHERE T2.Manager_ID = T1.Manager_ID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of managers who started working after 2003 and are currently assigned to manage a railway.

sql: SELECT T1.Railway FROM railway AS T1 WHERE T1.Railway_ID IN (SELECT Railway_ID FROM train WHERE Name = 'Sewagram Exp') AND T1.Wheels = '4-4-0';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the railway that operates the 'Sewagram Exp' train with a '4-4-0' wheel configuration.

sql: SELECT Name FROM manager WHERE Manager_ID IN (SELECT Manager_ID FROM railway_manage) INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM manager WHERE Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of managers who are from the 'United States' and also manage a railway.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM manager AS T1 WHERE T1.Manager_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Manager_ID FROM railway_manage AS T2 WHERE T2.From_Year = '2010');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of managers who were not assigned to manage any railway in the year 2010.

sql: SELECT Name, 'Manager' AS Role FROM manager WHERE Age > 45 UNION SELECT Name, 'Train' AS Role FROM train WHERE `From` = 'Mumbai CST';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, providing a combined list of managers older than 45 and trains departing from 'Mumbai CST', identifying each entry with a derived 'Role' column.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country FROM manager AS T1 WHERE T1.Country = 'United States' AND T1.Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM manager WHERE Country = 'United States');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and country of a manager from the United States whose age is greater than the average age of all US-based managers.

sql: WITH ManagerYears AS (SELECT Manager_ID, From_Year FROM railway_manage WHERE From_Year > '2009') SELECT T1.Name, T2.From_Year FROM manager AS T1 JOIN ManagerYears AS T2 ON T1.Manager_ID = T2.Manager_ID ORDER BY T2.From_Year DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of managers who began their tenure after 2009, along with their start year, ordered from most to least recent.

sql: SELECT * FROM train WHERE Arrival > '09:00' AND Railway_ID IN (SELECT Railway_ID FROM railway WHERE Wheels = '4-4-0');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing all details for trains that use a '4-4-0' wheel configuration and are scheduled to arrive after 9 AM.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Working_year_starts FROM manager AS T1 WHERE T1.Country = 'United States' AND T1.Manager_ID IN (SELECT Manager_ID FROM railway_manage WHERE From_Year = '2012');
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and career start year for any manager from the 'United States' who was actively managing a railway in 2012.

sql: SELECT T1.Railway FROM railway AS T1 WHERE T1.Railway_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Railway_ID FROM train AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of railways that do not have any associated trains.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Level, T1.Age FROM manager AS T1 WHERE T1.Level > (SELECT AVG(Level) FROM manager WHERE T1.Country = 'United States') OR T1.Age < 46;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name, level, and age for any manager who is either younger than 46 or has a level greater than the average level of all managers in the United States.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM manager WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM manager);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age for all managers who are older than the average age of all managers.

sql: SELECT * FROM Rooms;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, providing a complete inventory of all available rooms and their corresponding details.

sql: SELECT * FROM Reservations;
result_summary: Returns 600 rows, providing a complete list of all reservations with full details for each, including guest name, stay dates, room, rate, and party size.

sql: SELECT RoomId, roomName, decor FROM Rooms WHERE bedType = 'King';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID, name, and decor style for all rooms that are equipped with a 'King' size bed.

sql: SELECT * FROM Reservations WHERE LastName = 'SELBIG';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all reservation details for the guest with the last name 'SELBIG'.

sql: SELECT roomName, beds, bedType FROM Rooms WHERE beds > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the room name, number of beds, and bed type for all rooms that contain more than one bed.

sql: SELECT * FROM Reservations WHERE Adults = 2;
result_summary: Returns 149 rows listing the complete details for all reservations made for exactly two adults.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT roomName FROM Rooms;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing all unique room names available.

sql: SELECT Code, Room, CheckIn, Rate FROM Reservations WHERE Rate = 150.0;
result_summary: Returns 71 rows containing the reservation code, room, check-in date, and rate for all bookings with a rate of exactly $150.

sql: SELECT RoomId, roomName, basePrice FROM Rooms ORDER BY basePrice DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each room's ID, name, and base price, ordered from the most to least expensive.

sql: SELECT Code, LastName, Adults, Kids FROM Reservations WHERE Kids = 0;
result_summary: Returns 374 rows for all reservations that do not include any children, listing each reservation's code, the booker's last name, and the number of adults.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T2.CheckIn, T2.CheckOut, T2.LastName FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room;
result_summary: Returns 600 rows listing all reservations, showing the guest's last name, the name of their reserved room, and the corresponding check-in and check-out dates.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, COUNT(T2.Code) FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.roomName;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each room by name along with the total number of reservations made for that room.

sql: SELECT T1.bedType, AVG(T2.Rate) AS AverageRate FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.bedType;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each bed type along with the average reservation rate for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T2.Adults + T2.Kids AS TotalGuests FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room;
result_summary: Returns 600 rows, listing the room name for each reservation along with the total number of guests calculated by summing the adults and children for that stay.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, SUM(T2.Rate) AS TotalRevenue FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.roomName ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the total revenue generated by each room, ordered from the most to least profitable.

sql: SELECT LastName, COUNT(Code) AS NumReservations FROM Reservations GROUP BY LastName;
result_summary: Returns 537 rows, listing each customer's last name along with the total count of reservations they have made.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T1.maxOccupancy, COUNT(T2.Code) FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.decor = 'traditional' GROUP BY T1.roomName;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows for rooms with a 'traditional' decor, showing each room's name, its maximum occupancy, and a total count of reservations made for that room.

sql: SELECT T2.FirstName, T2.LastName FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.bedType = 'King';
result_summary: Returns 256 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have made a reservation for a room with a King-sized bed.

sql: SELECT roomName FROM Rooms WHERE RoomId IN (SELECT DISTINCT Room FROM Reservations);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of all rooms that have at least one reservation.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, AVG(T2.Adults) AS AvgAdults FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.roomName;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each room name along with the average number of adults per reservation for that room.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, SUM(T2.Adults), SUM(T2.Kids) FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.roomName;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each room by name, along with the total count of adults and kids from all reservations associated with that room.

sql: SELECT roomName, basePrice FROM Rooms WHERE basePrice >= 150;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing rooms with a base price of $150 or more, showing each room's name and its corresponding price.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT LastName FROM Reservations WHERE Rate = 100.0;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows containing a unique list of last names for guests whose reservation rate was exactly $100.

sql: SELECT bedType, COUNT(*) FROM Rooms WHERE bedType IN ('King', 'Queen') GROUP BY bedType;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total count of rooms available for 'King' and 'Queen' bed types, grouped by each type.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, MIN(T2.CheckIn), MAX(T2.CheckOut) FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.roomName;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, providing a booking history summary for each room by showing its name, its earliest recorded check-in date, and its latest recorded check-out date.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T1.basePrice, COUNT(T2.Code) FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.basePrice > 150 GROUP BY T1.RoomId HAVING COUNT(T2.Code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows for premium rooms (base price over $150) that have been booked more than once, showing the room name, its base price, and the total count of reservations.

sql: SELECT T2.FirstName, T2.LastName FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T2.FirstName, T2.LastName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.decor) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 38 rows, identifying guests by first and last name who have stayed in rooms with more than one distinct decor style across all their reservations.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T1.basePrice, AVG(T2.Rate) AS AvgRate FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.RoomId HAVING AVG(T2.Rate) < T1.basePrice;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT RoomId, roomName FROM Rooms WHERE RoomId NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Room FROM Reservations);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Code, FirstName, LastName, Adults FROM Reservations WHERE Adults > (SELECT AVG(Adults) FROM Reservations);
result_summary: Returns 235 rows listing reservations with an above-average number of adults, showing the reservation code, the guest's first and last name, and the adult count.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.RoomId ORDER BY SUM(T2.Rate) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the single room with the highest total revenue, calculated by summing the rates of all its reservations.

sql: SELECT T2.FirstName, T2.LastName FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.decor = 'modern' INTERSECT SELECT T2.FirstName, T2.LastName FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.decor = 'traditional';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first and last names of guests who have made reservations in both a modern-decorated room and a traditional-decorated room.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T1.maxOccupancy - MAX(T2.Adults + T2.Kids) AS OccupancyDifference FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.RoomId;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows for each room, calculating the difference between the room's maximum occupancy and the largest number of guests (adults plus kids) from any single reservation for that room.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T2.LastName = 'KLESS' EXCEPT SELECT T1.roomName FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T2.LastName = 'GABLER';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a room that has been reserved by a guest with the last name 'KLESS' but not by a guest with the last name 'GABLER'.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T1.decor, COUNT(T2.Code) AS ReservationCount FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.RoomId ORDER BY ReservationCount DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name, decor, and total reservation count for the top three most-booked rooms, ordered from highest to lowest reservation count.

sql: SELECT T2.* FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.basePrice = (SELECT MIN(basePrice) FROM Rooms);
result_summary: Returns 59 rows containing all reservation details for bookings made in the rooms with the lowest base price.

sql: SELECT FirstName, LastName FROM Reservations WHERE Room IN (SELECT RoomId FROM Rooms WHERE bedType = 'Queen') UNION SELECT FirstName, LastName FROM Reservations WHERE Adults > 1;
result_summary: Returns 343 rows listing the first and last names of guests who are either staying in a room with a 'Queen' bed or whose reservation is for more than one adult.

sql: SELECT T1.decor, T1.roomName, T1.basePrice FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN (SELECT decor, MAX(basePrice) AS MaxPrice FROM Rooms GROUP BY decor) AS T2 ON T1.decor = T2.decor AND T1.basePrice = T2.MaxPrice;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, identifying the room with the highest base price for each decor style by listing its decor, room name, and price.

sql: SELECT T2.FirstName, T2.LastName FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.decor = 'traditional' EXCEPT SELECT T2.FirstName, T2.LastName FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.decor = 'modern';
result_summary: Returns 220 rows listing the first and last names of guests who have stayed in a room with 'traditional' decor but have never stayed in a room with 'modern' decor.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.beds) FROM Rooms AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Reservations AS T2 WHERE T2.Room = T1.RoomId);
result_summary: Returns a single row calculating the average number of beds across all rooms that have at least one reservation.

sql: SELECT T2.Code, T1.roomName, T2.LastName, T1.basePrice, T2.Rate FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T2.Rate != T1.basePrice;
result_summary: Returns 364 rows identifying all reservations where the rate paid was different from the room's standard base price, listing the reservation code, room name, guest's last name, the base price, and the actual rate charged.

sql: SELECT LastName FROM Reservations GROUP BY LastName ORDER BY COUNT(Code) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the last name of the guest who has made the most reservations.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T1.maxOccupancy FROM Rooms AS T1 WHERE T1.maxOccupancy > (SELECT AVG(maxOccupancy) FROM Rooms) AND T1.RoomId IN (SELECT Room FROM Reservations);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and maximum occupancy for all reserved rooms that have a capacity greater than the average across all rooms.

sql: SELECT roomName, TotalRevenue, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC) AS RevenueRank FROM (SELECT T1.roomName, SUM(T2.Rate) AS TotalRevenue FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.roomName);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each room, its total revenue generated from all reservations, and its corresponding revenue rank from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT LastName, COUNT(Code), SUM(Adults + Kids) FROM Reservations GROUP BY LastName HAVING COUNT(Code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 60 rows for customers who have made more than one reservation, listing their last name, the total count of their bookings, and the combined number of guests across all those reservations.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, SUM(T2.Adults + T2.Kids) FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.RoomId HAVING SUM(T2.Adults + T2.Kids) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows identifying rooms that have hosted a total of more than 5 guests across all reservations, showing each room's name and its corresponding total guest count.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T2.FirstName, T2.LastName, T2.CheckIn FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room JOIN (SELECT Room, MAX(CheckIn) AS MaxCheckIn FROM Reservations GROUP BY Room) AS T3 ON T2.Room = T3.Room AND T2.CheckIn = T3.MaxCheckIn;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows identifying the most recent guest for each room, listing the room name, the guest's first and last name, and the date of that latest check-in.

sql: SELECT 100.0 * COUNT(*) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Rooms) FROM Rooms WHERE bedType = 'King';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single calculated value representing the percentage of all rooms that have a 'King' bed type.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T1.bedType, T1.decor, T2.CheckIn, T2.CheckOut FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T2.LastName = 'GABLER';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the room details (name, bed type, decor) and the check-in/check-out dates for the reservation associated with the customer 'GABLER'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.roomName, T1.decor FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.decor = 'traditional' AND T2.Adults > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique, traditionally decorated rooms that have been reserved by parties of more than one adult.

sql: SELECT Name, Open_Year FROM museum ORDER BY Open_Year DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing museum names and their opening years, ordered from the most recently opened to the oldest.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM visitor WHERE Age > 30;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age for each visitor who is older than 30.

sql: SELECT Name, Level_of_membership FROM visitor ORDER BY Level_of_membership DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each visitor's name and their membership level, ordered from the highest level of membership to the lowest.

sql: SELECT * FROM museum WHERE Name = 'Plaza Museum';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details (ID, name, number of staff, and opening year) for the museum named 'Plaza Museum'.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM visitor WHERE Name = 'Guti Midfielder';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and age for the visitor named 'Guti Midfielder'.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM museum;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all museums.

sql: SELECT AVG(Age) FROM visitor;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average age of all visitors.

sql: SELECT SUM(Num_of_Ticket) FROM visit;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the total number of tickets used across all visits.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Num_of_Ticket FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID WHERE T1.Museum_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the museum with ID 1 and the number of tickets recorded for a single visit.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total_spent FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID WHERE T2.Total_spent = 320.44;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the visitor's name for any visit where the total amount spent was exactly 320.44.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.visitor_ID) FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each museum by name along with the total count of visitors for that museum.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Total_spent) FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each visitor by name and the total amount they have spent across all of their visits.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, AVG(T2.Num_of_Ticket) FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY AVG(T2.Num_of_Ticket) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each museum and its average number of tickets per visit, ordered from the highest average to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID WHERE T2.Num_of_Ticket > 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of museums that have had at least one visit where more than 10 tickets were sold.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID WHERE T2.Total_spent > 300;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of visitors who have spent over 300 on any single visit.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM visitor WHERE ID IN (SELECT visitor_ID FROM visit WHERE Num_of_Ticket > 5);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of visitors who have purchased more than five tickets in a single visit.

sql: SELECT Name FROM museum WHERE Museum_ID IN (SELECT Museum_ID FROM visit WHERE Total_spent < 100);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of museums that have had at least one visit where the total amount spent was less than $100.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID WHERE T2.Museum_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the name and age of visitors who have visited the museum with ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Num_of_Ticket) AS TotalTickets FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING SUM(T2.Num_of_Ticket) > 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of visitors who have purchased more than 10 tickets in total, along with the corresponding total ticket count for each.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Museum_ID) AS NumberOfMuseumsVisited FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing each visitor's name along with the total number of museums they have visited.

sql: SELECT Name, Num_of_Staff FROM museum WHERE Open_Year > '2005' ORDER BY Num_of_Staff DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing museums that opened after 2005, showing their name and number of staff, ordered from the highest staff count to the lowest.

sql: SELECT Level_of_membership, COUNT(*) FROM visitor GROUP BY Level_of_membership;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each membership level and the total count of visitors associated with that level.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name, T2.Num_of_Ticket FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID JOIN museum AS T3 ON T2.Museum_ID = T3.Museum_ID WHERE T1.Age < 30;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing museum visits made by visitors younger than 30, showing the visitor's name, the museum's name, and the number of tickets purchased for that visit.

sql: SELECT Name FROM museum WHERE Num_of_Staff > (SELECT AVG(Num_of_Staff) FROM museum);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of museums that have more staff members than the average for all museums.

sql: SELECT Name FROM visitor WHERE Age < (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM visitor);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all visitors whose age is below the average age of all visitors.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID JOIN museum AS T3 ON T2.Museum_ID = T3.Museum_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Plaza Museum';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and age of the visitor who has been to the 'Plaza Museum'.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, AVG(T1.Age) FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID JOIN museum AS T3 ON T2.Museum_ID = T3.Museum_ID GROUP BY T3.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each museum by name, along with the average age of the visitors for that museum.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM visitor AS T1 WHERE T1.ID IN (SELECT T2.visitor_ID FROM visit AS T2 GROUP BY T2.visitor_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Museum_ID) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of visitors who have been to more than one museum.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM museum AS T1 WHERE T1.Museum_ID IN (SELECT T2.Museum_ID FROM visit AS T2 GROUP BY T2.Museum_ID HAVING SUM(T2.Total_spent) > 100);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of museums where the total amount spent by all visitors is greater than 100.

sql: SELECT Name, Level_of_membership FROM visitor WHERE ID NOT IN (SELECT visitor_ID FROM visit);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and membership levels for visitors who have never made a visit.

sql: SELECT Name, Num_of_Staff FROM museum WHERE Museum_ID NOT IN (SELECT Museum_ID FROM visit WHERE Museum_ID IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and number of staff for each museum that has had no recorded visits.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID GROUP BY T1.ID, T1.Name HAVING SUM(T2.Total_spent) > (SELECT AVG(Total_spent) FROM visit);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of visitors whose total spending across all their visits is greater than the average spending per individual visit.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.TotalSpent FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN (SELECT visitor_ID, SUM(Total_spent) AS TotalSpent FROM visit GROUP BY visitor_ID) AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID ORDER BY T2.TotalSpent DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the top-spending visitor by name and the total amount they have spent across all visits.

sql: SELECT Name FROM museum WHERE Museum_ID = (SELECT Museum_ID FROM visit GROUP BY Museum_ID ORDER BY SUM(Num_of_Ticket) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the museum with the highest total ticket sales.

sql: SELECT Name, 'High Membership' AS Membership_Category FROM visitor WHERE Level_of_membership > 5 UNION ALL SELECT Name, 'Low Membership' AS Membership_Category FROM visitor WHERE Level_of_membership <= 5;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each visitor's name and assigning them a 'High Membership' (level > 5) or 'Low Membership' (level <= 5) category.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID WHERE T2.Museum_ID = 1 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID WHERE T2.Museum_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the visitor who has visited both the museum with ID 1 and the museum with ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID WHERE T2.visitor_ID = 5 EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM museum AS T1 WHERE T1.Open_Year < '2010';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a museum visited by a specific visitor (ID 5), excluding any museums that opened before 2010.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM visitor WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM visitor) AND ID IN (SELECT visitor_ID FROM visit);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and age of visitors who have previously made a visit and are older than the average age of all visitors.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, COUNT(T1.ID) FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID JOIN museum AS T3 ON T2.Museum_ID = T3.Museum_ID WHERE T1.Level_of_membership = 8 GROUP BY T3.Name;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Level_of_membership, AVG(T2.Total_spent), COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ID) FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID GROUP BY T1.Level_of_membership HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing membership levels with more than one visitor, showing the average amount spent per visit and the total count of visitors for each level.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM museum AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM visit AS T2 WHERE T2.Museum_ID = T1.Museum_ID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of museums that have had two or more recorded visits.

sql: SELECT Name FROM visitor WHERE Age = (SELECT MIN(Age) FROM visitor WHERE ID IN (SELECT visitor_ID FROM visit));
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the youngest visitor who has a recorded visit.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Num_of_Ticket) AS TotalTickets FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING SUM(T2.Num_of_Ticket) > 15;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name of each museum that has sold more than 15 tickets, along with its total ticket count.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID WHERE T1.Level_of_membership > 5 GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Museum_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any premium member (membership level over 5) who has visited more than one museum.

sql: SELECT T3.Open_Year, AVG(T1.Age) AS AvgVisitorAge FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID JOIN museum AS T3 ON T2.Museum_ID = T3.Museum_ID GROUP BY T3.Open_Year HAVING AVG(T1.Age) < 30;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying museum opening years where the average visitor age is under 30, showing each qualifying year and its corresponding average visitor age.

sql: SELECT Name FROM visitor WHERE ID IN (SELECT visitor_ID FROM visit GROUP BY visitor_ID HAVING SUM(Num_of_Ticket) > (SELECT AVG(Num_of_Ticket) FROM visit));
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of visitors whose total number of purchased tickets exceeds the average number of tickets bought per visit.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM museum AS T1 WHERE T1.Num_of_Staff > 20 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM visit AS T2 JOIN visitor AS T3 ON T2.visitor_ID = T3.ID WHERE T2.Museum_ID = T1.Museum_ID AND T3.Age < 30);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a museum that employs more than 20 staff members and has had at least one visitor under the age of 30.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID WHERE T2.Total_spent > (SELECT T3.Num_of_Staff * 5 FROM museum AS T3 WHERE T3.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age of visitors whose spending at a museum exceeded a threshold calculated as five times the number of staff at that specific museum.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM visitor AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM visit AS T2 WHERE T2.visitor_ID = T1.ID AND T2.Num_of_Ticket = 20);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of any visitor who has purchased exactly 20 tickets for a single visit.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age, T1.Level_of_membership FROM visitor AS T1 WHERE T1.Age > 25 AND T1.ID IN (SELECT T2.visitor_ID FROM visit AS T2 JOIN museum AS T3 ON T2.Museum_ID = T3.Museum_ID WHERE T3.Open_Year = '2012');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, age, and membership level for visitors older than 25 who have visited a museum that opened in the year 2012.

sql: SELECT * FROM Campuses ORDER BY Year ASC;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing all details for each campus, ordered chronologically by their founding year from oldest to newest.

sql: SELECT Campus, Location FROM Campuses WHERE County = 'Kern';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the campus name and location for all university campuses situated in Kern County.

sql: SELECT CampusFee FROM csu_fees WHERE Campus = 1 AND Year = 1996;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the specific campus fee for the campus with ID 1 during the year 1996.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Campuses;

sql: SELECT Degrees FROM degrees WHERE Campus = 1 AND Year = 1990;

sql: SELECT TotalEnrollment_AY, FTE_AY FROM enrollments WHERE Campus = 1 AND Year = 1956;

sql: SELECT Campus, Year FROM Campuses WHERE Year > 2000;

sql: SELECT AVG(CampusFee) FROM csu_fees;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average campus fee across all records.

sql: SELECT Undergraduate, Graduate FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Campus = 1 AND Discipline = 5 AND Year = 2004;

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, T2.CampusFee FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN csu_fees AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T2.Year = 1996;

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, SUM(T2.Degrees) FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN degrees AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus GROUP BY T1.Campus;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing each campus along with the total number of degrees it has awarded.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, SUM(T2.Undergraduate) FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN discipline_enrollments AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T2.Year = 2004 GROUP BY T1.Campus;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing each campus and its total undergraduate enrollment for the year 2004.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, T2.TotalEnrollment_AY, T3.Faculty FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN enrollments AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus JOIN faculty AS T3 ON T1.Id = T3.Campus WHERE T2.Year = 2002 AND T3.Year = 2002;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing each campus's name, total student enrollment, and faculty count for the year 2002.

sql: SELECT Year, AVG(CampusFee) FROM csu_fees GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each year from the dataset along with the corresponding average campus fee for that year.

sql: SELECT Year, SUM(Degrees) FROM degrees GROUP BY Year HAVING SUM(Degrees) > 2000;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, listing each year where the total number of degrees awarded exceeded 2,000, along with the total count of degrees for that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus FROM Campuses AS T1 WHERE T1.Id IN (SELECT T2.Campus FROM csu_fees AS T2 WHERE T2.Year = 2003);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the campus that had associated fees recorded in the year 2003.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, MAX(T2.CampusFee) FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN csu_fees AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus GROUP BY T1.Campus;

sql: SELECT Campus, Year, TotalEnrollment_AY FROM enrollments WHERE TotalEnrollment_AY = (SELECT MAX(TotalEnrollment_AY) FROM enrollments);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the campus and year that had the single highest total academic year enrollment on record.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, SUM(T2.Undergraduate), SUM(T2.Graduate) FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN discipline_enrollments AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T2.Year = 2004 GROUP BY T1.Campus;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing the total undergraduate and graduate student enrollment for each campus during the year 2004.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, T2.FTE_AY FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN enrollments AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T1.Year < 1900;
result_summary: Returns 280 rows listing the campus name and its corresponding full-time equivalent academic year enrollment for all campuses founded before the year 1900.

sql: SELECT Discipline, SUM(Graduate) FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Year = 2004 GROUP BY Discipline;
result_summary: Returns 22 rows showing the total number of graduates for each academic discipline in the year 2004.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, SUM(T2.Degrees) FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN degrees AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T1.County = 'Butte' GROUP BY T1.Campus;

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, T2.Faculty FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN faculty AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T2.Year = 2002;

sql: SELECT Campus FROM Campuses WHERE Id IN (SELECT DISTINCT Campus FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Graduate > 0);

sql: SELECT Discipline FROM discipline_enrollments GROUP BY Discipline ORDER BY SUM(Undergraduate) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT Campus, Year, Discipline, CAST(Graduate AS REAL) / Undergraduate FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Undergraduate > 0 ORDER BY CAST(Graduate AS REAL) / Undergraduate DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the specific campus, year, and discipline with the highest ratio of graduate to undergraduate students, along with that calculated ratio.

sql: SELECT Id FROM Campuses WHERE County = 'Kern' EXCEPT SELECT Campus FROM csu_fees WHERE Year = 2003;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID of the campus in 'Kern' county that did not have any recorded CSU fees in the year 2003.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN degrees AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus GROUP BY T1.Campus HAVING AVG(T2.Degrees) > (SELECT AVG(Degrees) FROM degrees);

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, T2.TotalEnrollment_AY, T3.Faculty FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN enrollments AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus JOIN faculty AS T3 ON T1.Id = T3.Campus WHERE T2.Year = 2002 AND T3.Year = 2002;

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, SUM(T2.Undergraduate) / T3.Faculty FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN discipline_enrollments AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus JOIN faculty AS T3 ON T1.Id = T3.Campus WHERE T2.Year = 2002 AND T3.Year = 2002 AND T3.Faculty > 0 GROUP BY T1.Campus;
