sql: SELECT T1.Campus FROM Campuses AS T1 WHERE T1.Id IN (SELECT Campus FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Year = 2004) AND T1.Id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Campus FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Year = 2004 AND Graduate > 0);

sql: SELECT T1.Degrees / T2.Faculty FROM degrees AS T1 JOIN faculty AS T2 ON T1.Campus = T2.Campus WHERE T1.Campus = 3 AND T1.Year = 2002 AND T2.Year = 2002;

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name, T3.compatible_since_year FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.accelerator_id JOIN browser AS T2 ON T3.browser_id = T2.id WHERE T3.compatible_since_year = 1996;

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.accelerator_id JOIN browser AS T2 ON T3.browser_id = T2.id GROUP BY T1.id, T1.name HAVING SUM(T2.market_share) > 30;

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM architect AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM bridge AS T2 WHERE T2.architect_id = T1.id) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM mill AS T3 WHERE T3.architect_id = T1.id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an architect who has designed a bridge but has not designed a mill.

sql: SELECT T1.name, count(T2.id) AS bridge_count, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY count(T2.id) DESC) as rnk FROM architect AS T1 LEFT JOIN bridge AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each architect, the total number of bridges they designed, and their corresponding rank based on this count from most to fewest.

sql: SELECT location, count(*), avg(built_year) FROM mill GROUP BY location;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each mill location, the total number of mills within it, and the average year those mills were built.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM architect AS T1 JOIN bridge AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING count(T2.id) > (SELECT avg(bridge_count) FROM (SELECT count(id) AS bridge_count FROM bridge GROUP BY architect_id));
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of architects who have designed more bridges than the average number of bridges per architect.

sql: SELECT name, location, built_year FROM mill WHERE built_year = (SELECT max(built_year) FROM mill);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most recently built mill, including its name, location, and the year it was built.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name FROM architect AS T1 JOIN (SELECT architect_id, max(length_meters) AS max_len FROM bridge GROUP BY architect_id) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id JOIN bridge AS T3 ON T2.architect_id = T3.architect_id AND T2.max_len = T3.length_meters;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the longest bridge designed by each architect, listing the architect's name alongside the name of that bridge.

sql: SELECT T1.nationality, count(T2.id) AS total_structures FROM architect AS T1 JOIN (SELECT architect_id, id FROM bridge UNION ALL SELECT architect_id, id FROM mill) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.nationality;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each architect nationality alongside the total count of structures (bridges and mills) designed by architects of that nationality.

sql: SELECT name FROM bridge WHERE location LIKE '%Utah , USA' AND length_meters < ANY (SELECT length_meters FROM bridge WHERE location = 'Guangxi , China');
error_message: Discarded: SQL Runtime Error - near "SELECT": syntax error

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM (SELECT architect_id, 'bridge' AS type FROM bridge UNION ALL SELECT architect_id, 'mill' AS type FROM mill) AS T1 JOIN architect AS T2 ON T1.architect_id = T2.id GROUP BY T1.architect_id, T2.name HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.type) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of architects who have designed both a bridge and a mill.

sql: SELECT T1.name, sum(T2.built_year) AS total_length FROM architect AS T1 JOIN mill AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY total_length DESC LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the top two architects, ranked by the cumulative sum of the construction years for all mills they designed, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT name, location FROM mill WHERE type = (SELECT type FROM mill WHERE name = 'Moulin de Fexhe') AND name != 'Moulin de Fexhe';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows with the names and locations of mills that share the same type as the 'Moulin de Fexhe' mill.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM architect AS T1 JOIN (SELECT architect_id, id FROM bridge UNION ALL SELECT architect_id, id FROM mill) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.id ORDER BY count(T2.id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the architect who has designed the most structures in total, considering both bridges and mills.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM architect AS T1 JOIN mill AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.id, T1.name HAVING max(T2.built_year) < 1850;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of an architect whose entire portfolio of designed mills was completed before the year 1850.

sql: SELECT T3.nationality FROM (SELECT T1.nationality, avg(T2.length_meters) AS avg_len FROM architect AS T1 JOIN bridge AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.nationality) AS T3 ORDER BY T3.avg_len DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the nationality of architects whose designed bridges have the highest average length.

sql: SELECT player_id, name_first, name_last, birth_year FROM player WHERE birth_year = 1934 AND birth_country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 76 rows listing players born in the USA in 1934, showing each player's ID, first name, last name, and birth year.

sql: SELECT player_id, team_id, salary FROM salary WHERE year = 1985;
result_summary: Returns 550 rows listing the player ID, team ID, and salary for all players active during the year 1985.

sql: SELECT player_id, votedby, votes FROM hall_of_fame WHERE yearid = 1936 AND inducted = 'Y';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the players successfully inducted into the Hall of Fame in 1936, showing each player's ID, the voting body, and their total vote count.

sql: SELECT name_full, city, state FROM college WHERE college_id = 'rice';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the full name, city, and state for the college identified by the ID 'rice'.

sql: SELECT award_id, year, league_id FROM player_award WHERE player_id = 'hinespa01';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the award ID, year, and league ID for all awards won by the player with the ID 'hinespa01'.

sql: SELECT team_id, name, w, l FROM team WHERE league_id = 'NL' AND year = 1981;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the team ID, name, wins (w), and losses (l) for all teams in the National League for the 1981 season.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM team_franchise WHERE active = 'Y';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of team franchises that are currently active.

sql: SELECT year, team_id_winner, team_id_loser FROM postseason WHERE wins = 3;
result_summary: Returns 123 rows identifying postseason series that were won in exactly 3 games, listing the year of the series along with the IDs of the winning and losing teams.

sql: SELECT park_name, city FROM park WHERE state = 'CA';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and city for each park located in California.

sql: SELECT g, ab, r, h, hr, rbi FROM batting WHERE player_id = 'abercda01' AND year = 1871;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the batting statistics (games, at-bats, runs, hits, home runs, and RBIs) for the player with ID 'abercda01' during the 1871 season.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.team_id FROM all_star AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.year = 1933;
result_summary: Returns 36 rows listing the first name, last name, and team ID for all players who were selected as all-stars in the year 1933.

sql: SELECT team_id, SUM(salary) FROM salary WHERE year = 1985 GROUP BY team_id;
result_summary: Returns 26 rows listing the total salary for each team specifically for the year 1985.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, COUNT(T1.award_id) FROM player_award AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id;
result_summary: Returns 1323 rows, listing each player by their first and last name along with the total count of awards they have received.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.team_id FROM manager AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.year = 1871;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the first and last names of players who were managers in the year 1871, along with the ID of the team they managed.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last FROM player_college AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.college_id = 'rice';
result_summary: Returns 83 rows listing the first and last names of all players who attended Rice University.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.points_won FROM player_award_vote AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.award_id = 'Cy Young' AND T1.year = 1956;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of players and the points they won for the 'Cy Young' award in the year 1956.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.g_all FROM appearances AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.team_id = 'BS1' AND T1.year = 1871;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the first name, last name, and total games played for each player on team 'BS1' during the 1871 season.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.year, T1.ab FROM batting AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.ab > 500;
result_summary: Returns 13773 rows identifying individual player seasons with more than 500 at-bats, listing the player's first and last name, the year, and the number of at-bats.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.yearid FROM hall_of_fame AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.votedby = 'BBWAA' AND T1.inducted = 'Y';
result_summary: Returns 116 rows, listing the first name, last name, and induction year for all players who were successfully inducted into the Hall of Fame by the Baseball Writers' Association (BBWAA).

sql: SELECT T2.franchise_name, T1.w, T1.l FROM team AS T1 JOIN team_franchise AS T2 ON T1.franchise_id = T2.franchise_id WHERE T1.year = 1981 ORDER BY T1.w DESC;
result_summary: Returns 26 rows showing each franchise's name along with their win and loss record for the 1981 season, ordered from the most wins to the fewest.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.salary FROM salary AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.year = 1985 AND T1.salary > 800000;
result_summary: Returns 82 rows listing the first name, last name, and salary for players who earned over $800,000 in 1985.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.g, T1.e FROM fielding AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.year = 1871 AND T1.pos = 'SS';
result_summary: Returns 30 rows listing players who played the shortstop position in the year 1871, showing their first and last names, games played ('g'), and errors committed ('e').

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.year, T1.league_id FROM manager_award AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.award_id = 'BBWAA Manager of the year';
result_summary: Returns 66 rows listing all managers who have won the 'BBWAA Manager of the Year' award, including their first and last name, the year they won, and the league they were in.

sql: SELECT league_id, AVG(salary) FROM salary WHERE year = 1985 GROUP BY league_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the average salary for each baseball league during the 1985 season.

sql: SELECT T1.year, T1.round, T1.team_id_winner, T1.team_id_loser FROM postseason AS T1 WHERE T1.league_id_loser = 'AA';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the details of postseason games where the losing team belonged to the 'AA' league, showing the year, round, and the IDs of the winning and losing teams for each matchup.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last FROM player AS T1 JOIN hall_of_fame AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id JOIN player_award AS T3 ON T1.player_id = T3.player_id WHERE T2.inducted = 'Y' AND T3.award_id = 'Triple Crown';
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the first and last names of players who have been inducted into the Hall of Fame and have also won the 'Triple Crown' award.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, T2.salary FROM player AS T1 JOIN salary AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T2.year = 1985 AND T2.salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM salary WHERE year = 1985);
result_summary: Returns 235 rows listing the first name, last name, and salary of players who earned an above-average salary in the year 1985.

sql: SELECT T3.name_first, T3.name_last, T1.name, T1.w FROM team AS T1 JOIN manager AS T2 ON T1.team_id = T2.team_id AND T1.year = T2.year JOIN player AS T3 ON T2.player_id = T3.player_id WHERE T1.year = 1871 AND T1.w = (SELECT MAX(w) FROM team WHERE year = 1871 AND league_id = T1.league_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the manager's full name, their team's name, and the total number of wins for the team with the most wins in its league during the 1871 season.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.year) AS all_star_seasons FROM all_star AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.year) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 225 rows listing players selected as an all-star in more than five seasons, showing each player's first name, last name, and the total count of their all-star seasons.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, T3.year, T3.w, T3.l, T3.era FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_award AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id JOIN pitching AS T3 ON T1.player_id = T3.player_id AND T2.year = T3.year WHERE T2.award_id = 'Pitching Triple Crown';
result_summary: Returns 38 rows listing the first name, last name, and key pitching statistics (wins, losses, ERA) for every player in the specific year they won the 'Pitching Triple Crown' award.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, T3.year, T3.name FROM player AS T1 JOIN appearances AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id JOIN team AS T3 ON T2.team_id = T3.team_id AND T2.year = T3.year WHERE T1.birth_country = 'USA' AND T3.ws_win = 'Y';
result_summary: Returns 3516 rows listing the unique first name, last name, year, and team name for each US-born player who played on a World Series-winning team.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last FROM player AS T1 WHERE T1.player_id IN (SELECT player_id FROM player_college WHERE college_id = 'rice') AND T1.player_id IN (SELECT player_id FROM player_award_vote WHERE award_id = 'Cy Young');

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, SUM(T1.hr) AS career_hr FROM batting AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id HAVING SUM(T1.hr) > 500 ORDER BY career_hr DESC;
result_summary: Returns 27 rows listing players with more than 500 career home runs, including their first name, last name, and career total, ordered from the highest number of home runs to the lowest.

sql: SELECT player_id FROM manager_award UNION SELECT player_id FROM player_award;
result_summary: Returns 1383 rows listing the unique IDs of all individuals who have received either a player award or a manager award.

sql: SELECT player_id FROM batting WHERE year = 1871 INTERSECT SELECT player_id FROM pitching WHERE year = 1871;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the player IDs for all players who had both batting and pitching records during the year 1871.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last FROM hall_of_fame AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.player_id IN (SELECT player_id FROM hall_of_fame WHERE inducted = 'Y' EXCEPT SELECT player_id FROM all_star);
result_summary: Returns 770 rows listing the first and last names of players who were inducted into the Hall of Fame but were never selected as an All-Star.

sql: SELECT T1.park_name, T2.year, T2.games FROM park AS T1 JOIN home_game AS T2 ON T1.park_id = T2.park_id WHERE T2.games = (SELECT MAX(games) FROM home_game);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the park, year, and number of games for seasons that had the all-time maximum number of home games played.

sql: SELECT T1.year, T1.name, T1.hr FROM team AS T1 JOIN (SELECT year, MAX(hr) AS max_hr FROM team WHERE year >= 1980 GROUP BY year) AS T2 ON T1.year = T2.year AND T1.hr = T2.max_hr ORDER BY T1.year DESC;
result_summary: Returns 38 rows identifying the team with the most home runs for each year since 1980, showing the year, team name, and home run total, ordered from the most recent year to the oldest.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, T2.award_id, T2.year FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_award AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE CAST(SUBSTR(T1.final_game, 1, 4) AS INTEGER) = T2.year;
result_summary: Returns 82 rows identifying players who received an award in the same year as their final game, listing each player's name, the award ID, and the year.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.yearid, T1.votes, T1.needed FROM hall_of_fame AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.inducted = 'N' AND T1.votes > T1.needed / 2.0;
result_summary: Returns 547 rows identifying players who came close to Hall of Fame induction but were not elected, specifically those who received more than half the required votes. The output includes the player's first and last name, the ballot year, the votes received, and the votes needed.

sql: SELECT T1.year, T2.franchise_name, T1.name, T1.fp FROM team AS T1 JOIN team_franchise AS T2 ON T1.franchise_id = T2.franchise_id WHERE T1.year = 1871 ORDER BY T1.fp DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing all baseball teams from the year 1871, showing their franchise name, team name, and fielding percentage (fp), ordered from highest to lowest performance.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.year, T1.round, T1.so FROM pitching_postseason AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id ORDER BY T1.so DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the top single-postseason pitching performances based on strikeouts, ordered from most to fewest. The data includes the pitcher's name, the year, the postseason round, and their total number of strikeouts for that series.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.team_id, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.year) AS seasons FROM manager AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id, T1.team_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.year) > 10;
result_summary: Returns 32 rows identifying managers who have managed a single team for more than 10 seasons, listing each manager's name, their team ID, and the total number of seasons they managed that team.

sql: SELECT T3.name_full, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.player_id) AS hof_inductees FROM player_college AS T1 JOIN hall_of_fame AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id JOIN college AS T3 ON T1.college_id = T3.college_id WHERE T2.inducted = 'Y' GROUP BY T1.college_id;
result_summary: Returns 57 rows listing each college alongside the total count of its players who have been inducted into the Hall of Fame.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, T2.year, T3.award_id FROM player AS T1 JOIN manager AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id JOIN player_award AS T3 ON T1.player_id = T3.player_id AND T2.year = T3.year WHERE T2.plyr_mgr = 'Y';
result_summary: Returns 91 rows listing the first name, last name, year, and award ID for players who won an award in the same year they also served as a player-manager.

sql: SELECT T1.year, T1.name, T1.w, T1.l, T1.era FROM team AS T1 JOIN team_franchise AS T2 ON T1.franchise_id = T2.franchise_id WHERE T1.ws_win = 'Y' AND T1.era < 2.77;
result_summary: Returns 22 rows identifying World Series champion teams with an exceptionally low team Earned Run Average (ERA) below 2.77, including their name, year, win-loss record, and ERA.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.year FROM player_award_vote AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.award_id = 'Mgr of the year' AND T1.year = 1983 EXCEPT SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.year FROM manager_award AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.award_id = 'BBWAA Manager of the year' AND T1.year = 1983;

sql: SELECT T3.name, T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.salary FROM salary AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id JOIN team AS T3 ON T1.team_id = T3.team_id AND T1.year = T3.year WHERE T1.year = 1985 AND T1.salary = (SELECT MAX(salary) FROM salary AS T4 WHERE T4.team_id = T1.team_id AND T4.year = 1985);
result_summary: Returns 26 rows identifying the highest-paid player for each team in the year 1985, listing the team name, player's full name, and their salary.

sql: SELECT T2.name_first, T2.name_last, T1.points_won, T1.points_max, (CAST(T1.points_won AS REAL) / T1.points_max) AS vote_share FROM player_award_vote AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.award_id = 'Cy Young' AND T1.year = 1956 ORDER BY vote_share DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing players who received votes for the 1956 'Cy Young' award, showing their points won, the maximum possible points, and their calculated vote share, ordered from highest to lowest vote share.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, T2.year, (CAST(T2.h AS REAL) / T2.ab) AS regular_avg, (CAST(T3.h AS REAL) / T3.ab) AS postseason_avg FROM player AS T1 JOIN batting AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id JOIN batting_postseason AS T3 ON T1.player_id = T3.player_id AND T2.year = T3.year WHERE T2.ab > 0 AND T3.ab > 0 AND (CAST(T3.h AS REAL) / T3.ab) > (CAST(T2.h AS REAL) / T2.ab);
result_summary: Returns 3399 rows identifying players who had a higher batting average in the postseason than in the regular season for a given year, including their name, the year, and both calculated averages.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, COUNT(T2.order_item_id) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name;

sql: SELECT T3.shipment_date, T3.shipment_tracking_number FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Shipments AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id WHERE T1.customer_name = 'George';

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM Order_Items WHERE order_item_status = 'Finish');

sql: SELECT T1.shipment_id, T1.order_id, T1.shipment_date FROM Shipments AS T1 JOIN Invoices AS T2 ON T1.invoice_number = T2.invoice_number WHERE T2.invoice_number = 2;

sql: SELECT * FROM Orders WHERE customer_id = (SELECT customer_id FROM Customers WHERE customer_name = 'Alberto');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete order information for the customer named 'Alberto'.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, COUNT(T2.order_item_id) AS item_count FROM Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id GROUP BY T1.order_id;

sql: SELECT T1.shipment_tracking_number, T2.order_id, T2.date_order_placed FROM Shipments AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows matching each shipment to its original order, listing the shipment's tracking number, the order ID, and the date the order was placed.

result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the invoice date for the shipment with the tracking number '0114'.

sql: SELECT T1.Company, count(T2.Station_ID) FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID GROUP BY T1.Company;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each company name along with the total count of stations associated with that company.

sql: SELECT Main_Industry, AVG(Sales_billion) FROM company GROUP BY Main_Industry;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each main industry along with the average sales in billions for companies within that industry.

sql: SELECT Company, Market_Value FROM company WHERE Market_Value > (SELECT AVG(Market_Value) FROM company);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the company name and market value for all companies whose market value is greater than the average across all companies.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, T1.Open_Year FROM gas_station AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID WHERE T2.Rank_of_the_Year = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the location and opening year for gas stations that are operated by the top-ranked company of the year.

sql: SELECT Company FROM company WHERE Company_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Company_ID FROM station_company);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all companies that operate at least one station.

sql: SELECT Open_Year, count(*) FROM gas_station GROUP BY Open_Year;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each opening year alongside the total count of gas stations that opened in that year.

sql: SELECT Headquarters, SUM(Market_Value) FROM company GROUP BY Headquarters;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each company headquarters location along with the total market value of all companies based in that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Manager_Name FROM gas_station AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID WHERE T2.Company_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the manager for a gas station belonging to the company with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T2.Station_ID FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID WHERE T1.Headquarters = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the Station IDs associated with companies headquartered in the USA.

sql: SELECT T1.Company FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID WHERE T2.Rank_of_the_Year = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the company that was ranked number one for the year.

sql: SELECT T1.Company, T1.Headquarters FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID WHERE T1.Profits_billion > 15;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and headquarters for companies with profits exceeding 15 billion.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, T1.Manager_Name FROM gas_station AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID ORDER BY T2.Rank_of_the_Year DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the location and manager's name for gas stations, ordered by their parent company's yearly rank from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Company FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID GROUP BY T1.Company_ID HAVING count(T2.Station_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of companies that operate at least one station.

sql: SELECT T1.Company, T1.Sales_billion, T3.Location FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each company, their sales in billions, and the location of each gas station they are associated with.

sql: SELECT T1.Company FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T3.Open_Year = 1998;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the company that opened a gas station in the year 1998.

sql: SELECT T1.Company FROM company AS T1 LEFT JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID WHERE T2.Station_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of companies that are not associated with any station.

sql: SELECT T1.Company, T1.Market_Value FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID WHERE T1.Market_Value > (SELECT AVG(Market_Value) FROM company);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing companies with a market value greater than the average market value of all companies, showing each company's name and specific market value.

sql: SELECT T3.Location FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T1.Main_Industry = 'Oil and gas';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the locations of gas stations that are associated with companies in the 'Oil and gas' industry.

sql: SELECT T1.Company, T1.Profits_billion FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T3.Location = 'Herne Hill' ORDER BY T1.Profits_billion DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and profit in billions for the single most profitable company that operates a gas station in 'Herne Hill'.

sql: SELECT Company_ID FROM company INTERSECT SELECT Company_ID FROM station_company;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the IDs of companies that are present in both the main company list and the station-company association list.

sql: SELECT Company FROM company WHERE Headquarters = 'USA' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Company FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID WHERE T2.Rank_of_the_Year = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of US-based companies that have never achieved the #1 rank in any given year.

sql: SELECT T1.Headquarters, SUM(T1.Sales_billion) FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID GROUP BY T1.Headquarters;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each company headquarters location alongside the total sales in billions for all companies based there.

sql: SELECT T3.Manager_Name, T3.Location, T1.Company FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T3.Open_Year > 1998;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the manager, location, and parent company for all gas stations that opened after 1998.

sql: SELECT T1.Main_Industry, AVG(T2.Rank_of_the_Year) FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID GROUP BY T1.Main_Industry;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each main industry along with the average annual rank for the companies within that industry.

sql: SELECT T1.Company FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID WHERE T1.Rank = T2.Rank_of_the_Year;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the company whose overall rank matches its rank for the year from the station data.

sql: SELECT Company FROM company WHERE Company_ID = (SELECT Company_ID FROM station_company GROUP BY Company_ID ORDER BY COUNT(Station_ID) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the company that operates the most stations.

sql: SELECT Company, Headquarters FROM company WHERE Headquarters = 'USA' UNION SELECT Company, Headquarters FROM company WHERE Sales_billion > 400;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and headquarters of companies that are either headquartered in the 'USA' or have sales exceeding 400 billion.

sql: SELECT T1.Company, T1.Sales_billion FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T3.Manager_Name = 'BrianWingrave';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the company name and its sales in billions for the gas station managed by 'BrianWingrave'.

sql: SELECT T1.Company, MIN(T3.Open_Year) FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID GROUP BY T1.Company;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each company along with the opening year of its oldest gas station.

sql: SELECT T1.Company FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID WHERE T1.Profits_billion > (SELECT Profits_billion FROM company WHERE Company = 'General Electric');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of companies that have profits greater than those of General Electric.

sql: SELECT T1.Company, T3.Manager_Name FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T1.Assets_billion > 340;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a company with assets exceeding 340 billion and the name of a manager at one of its associated gas stations.

sql: SELECT T1.Company FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID WHERE T1.Market_Value > T1.Assets_billion;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of companies whose market value is greater than their total assets.

sql: SELECT Headquarters FROM company GROUP BY Headquarters ORDER BY SUM(Market_Value) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the headquarters location with the highest combined market value, aggregated across all companies.

sql: SELECT T3.Location, T3.Open_Year FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T1.Company = 'Royal Dutch Shell';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the location and opening year for each gas station operated by the company 'Royal Dutch Shell'.

sql: SELECT T1.Company FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T1.Headquarters = 'USA' AND T3.Open_Year = 2000;

sql: SELECT T1.Company, count(T2.Station_ID) FROM company AS T1 LEFT JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID GROUP BY T1.Company;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each company and the total number of stations associated with it, including companies that have zero stations.

sql: SELECT T3.Location FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T1.Sales_billion = (SELECT MAX(Sales_billion) FROM company);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the location of a gas station associated with the company that has the highest sales in billions.

sql: SELECT T3.Open_Year, AVG(T1.Profits_billion) FROM company AS T1 JOIN station_company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN gas_station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID GROUP BY T3.Open_Year;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each gas station opening year alongside the average profits (in billions) of the companies associated with stations opened in that year.

sql: SELECT * FROM Individuals WHERE individual_first_name = 'Oscar';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete record for any individual with the first name 'Oscar'.

sql: SELECT organization_name, date_formed FROM Organizations ORDER BY date_formed ASC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each organization's name and its formation date, ordered chronologically from the oldest to the newest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT service_type_code FROM Services;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the distinct types of services available, identified by their service type code.

sql: SELECT party_email FROM Parties LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows containing a sample of email addresses for parties.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Addresses;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single value representing the total count of all addresses in the table.

sql: SELECT form_name, form_number FROM Forms WHERE form_type_code = 'Complex';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the name and number for all forms that are classified as 'Complex'.

sql: SELECT party_id, party_email FROM Parties WHERE payment_method_code = 'Cheque';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the ID and email address for parties who have 'Cheque' as their registered payment method.

sql: SELECT T1.individual_first_name, T1.individual_last_name FROM Individuals AS T1 JOIN Organization_Contact_Individuals AS T2 ON T1.individual_id = T2.individual_id WHERE T2.organization_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the first and last names for all individuals who are contacts for the organization with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT service_name, service_descriptio FROM Services WHERE service_type_code = 'Education';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and description for all services classified under the 'Education' service type.

sql: SELECT line_1_number_building, town_city FROM Addresses WHERE state_province_county = 'Colorado';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the street address and city for all addresses located in the state of Colorado.

sql: SELECT T1.party_id, COUNT(T2.form_id) FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Party_Forms AS T2 ON T1.party_id = T2.party_id GROUP BY T1.party_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each party ID and the total number of forms associated with that party.

sql: SELECT T1.party_id, COUNT(T2.address_id) FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Party_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.party_id = T2.party_id GROUP BY T1.party_id HAVING COUNT(T2.address_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying parties with more than one registered address, showing each party's ID and the corresponding count of their addresses.

sql: SELECT T1.individual_first_name, T1.individual_last_name FROM Individuals AS T1 JOIN Organization_Contact_Individuals AS T2 ON T1.individual_id = T2.individual_id JOIN Organizations AS T3 ON T2.organization_id = T3.organization_id WHERE T3.organization_name = 'Labour Party';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of individuals who are contacts for the 'Labour Party' organization.

sql: SELECT service_name FROM Services WHERE service_id IN (SELECT service_id FROM Party_Services);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name of each service that is associated with a party.

sql: SELECT T1.form_name, T3.party_email FROM Forms AS T1 JOIN Party_Forms AS T2 ON T1.form_id = T2.form_id JOIN Parties AS T3 ON T2.party_id = T3.party_id WHERE T2.form_status_code = 'Completed';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of completed forms and the email addresses of the parties who submitted them.

sql: SELECT T1.party_id, T1.party_email FROM Parties AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Party_Addresses AS T2 WHERE T2.party_id = T1.party_id AND T2.address_type_code = 'Residence');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the party ID and email for each party confirmed to have a residence address on file.

sql: SELECT T1.service_type_code, COUNT(T2.booking_id) FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Party_Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id GROUP BY T1.service_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each service type code along with the total number of bookings made for that type of service.

sql: SELECT individual_first_name, individual_last_name FROM Individuals WHERE individual_id NOT IN (SELECT individual_id FROM Organization_Contact_Individuals);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of individuals who are not registered as a contact for any organization.

sql: SELECT 'Completed', COUNT(*) FROM Party_Forms WHERE form_status_code = 'Completed' UNION ALL SELECT 'Partially Completed', COUNT(*) FROM Party_Forms WHERE form_status_code = 'Partially Completed';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows providing the total count of party forms for both 'Completed' and 'Partially Completed' statuses.

sql: SELECT T1.organization_name, COUNT(T2.individual_id) FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Organization_Contact_Individuals AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id GROUP BY T1.organization_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each organization's name and the total number of contact individuals associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.form_name FROM Forms AS T1 JOIN Party_Forms AS T2 ON T1.form_id = T2.form_id WHERE T2.form_status_code = 'Partially Completed';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all forms that have a 'Partially Completed' status.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.party_email FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Party_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.party_id = T2.party_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the unique email addresses for all parties with a registered address in the USA.

sql: SELECT AVG(form_count) FROM (SELECT party_id, COUNT(form_id) AS form_count FROM Party_Forms GROUP BY party_id) AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value, which is the average number of forms submitted per party.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.service_name, T1.service_type_code FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Party_Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique combinations of service names and their type codes for all services that are actively being used by a party.

sql: SELECT T1.form_name FROM Forms AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id WHERE T2.service_type_code = 'Education';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of forms associated with services that are classified under the 'Education' service type.

sql: SELECT party_id FROM Party_Forms WHERE form_status_code = 'Completed' INTERSECT SELECT customer_id FROM Party_Services;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the IDs for parties who have both completed a form and have used a service.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.party_email FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Party_Services AS T2 ON T1.party_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id JOIN Party_Forms AS T4 ON T1.party_id = T4.party_id JOIN Forms AS T5 ON T4.form_id = T5.form_id WHERE T3.service_type_code = 'Education' AND T5.form_type_code = 'Complex';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the unique email addresses for parties who have both used an 'Education' type service and submitted a 'Complex' type form.

sql: SELECT T1.party_id FROM Party_Addresses AS T1 WHERE T1.address_type_code = 'Billing' EXCEPT SELECT T2.party_id FROM Party_Forms AS T2 WHERE T2.form_status_code = 'Completed';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the IDs for parties that have a 'Billing' address but do not have a form with a 'Completed' status.

sql: SELECT T1.organization_name, T3.individual_first_name, T3.individual_last_name FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Organization_Contact_Individuals AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id JOIN Individuals AS T3 ON T2.individual_id = T3.individual_id WHERE T2.date_contact_from = (SELECT MAX(date_contact_from) FROM Organization_Contact_Individuals WHERE organization_id = T1.organization_id);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each organization's name along with the first and last name of their most recent contact person.

sql: SELECT T1.service_name, COUNT(T2.booking_id) FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Party_Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id JOIN Party_Addresses AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.party_id JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.address_id WHERE T4.country = 'USA' GROUP BY T1.service_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each service name alongside a count of its total bookings from customers located in the USA.

sql: SELECT T1.organization_name FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN (SELECT organization_id, COUNT(*) AS contact_count FROM Organization_Contact_Individuals GROUP BY organization_id ORDER BY contact_count DESC LIMIT 1) AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the organization with the highest number of associated individual contacts.

sql: SELECT T1.party_email FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Party_Forms AS T2 ON T1.party_id = T2.party_id JOIN Forms AS T3 ON T2.form_id = T3.form_id JOIN Party_Addresses AS T4 ON T1.party_id = T4.party_id JOIN Addresses AS T5 ON T4.address_id = T5.address_id WHERE T3.form_name = 'SSN Application' AND T5.state_province_county = 'Colorado' AND T2.form_status_code = 'Completed';

sql: SELECT T1.individual_first_name, T1.individual_last_name FROM Individuals AS T1 JOIN Organization_Contact_Individuals AS T2 ON T1.individual_id = T2.individual_id GROUP BY T1.individual_id HAVING COUNT(T2.organization_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of individuals who are designated as contacts for more than one organization.

sql: SELECT T3.service_name, COUNT(T2.booking_id) AS booking_count FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Party_Services AS T2 ON T1.party_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id WHERE T1.payment_method_code = 'Credit Card' GROUP BY T3.service_name ORDER BY booking_count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing up to the top 3 most booked services for parties paid by credit card, showing each service's name and its total number of bookings, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.party_email FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Party_Services AS T2 ON T1.party_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.party_id NOT IN (SELECT party_id FROM Party_Forms);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique email addresses of customers who have used a service but have not yet submitted a form.

sql: SELECT T3.service_type_code, COUNT(T2.form_id) FROM Party_Forms AS T1 JOIN Forms AS T2 ON T1.form_id = T2.form_id JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id GROUP BY T3.service_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each service type code along with the total count of forms associated with that service type.

sql: SELECT T1.party_id FROM Party_Addresses AS T1 WHERE T1.address_type_code = 'Residence' INTERSECT SELECT T2.party_id FROM Party_Addresses AS T2 WHERE T2.address_type_code = 'Billing';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the IDs of parties that have both a residence address and a billing address on file.

sql: SELECT T2.service_name FROM Forms AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id WHERE T1.form_type_code = 'Complex' GROUP BY T2.service_name ORDER BY COUNT(T1.form_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the service name that has the highest count of 'Complex' type forms.

sql: SELECT T1.individual_first_name, T1.individual_last_name FROM Individuals AS T1 JOIN Organization_Contact_Individuals AS T2 ON T1.individual_id = T2.individual_id JOIN Organizations AS T3 ON T2.organization_id = T3.organization_id WHERE T3.organization_name = 'Conservative' AND T2.date_contact_from > '2016-09-01 00:00:00';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last name of an individual who was contacted by the 'Conservative' organization after September 1, 2016.

sql: SELECT party_id FROM Party_Forms WHERE form_status_code = 'Partially Completed' EXCEPT SELECT party_id FROM Party_Forms WHERE form_status_code = 'Completed';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying parties who have partially completed forms but have never submitted a fully completed one.

sql: SELECT T1.service_type_code, T1.form_status_code FROM (SELECT T4.service_type_code, T2.form_status_code, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY T4.service_type_code ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) as rn FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Party_Forms AS T2 ON T1.party_id = T2.party_id JOIN Forms AS T3 ON T2.form_id = T3.form_id JOIN Services AS T4 ON T3.service_id = T4.service_id GROUP BY T4.service_type_code, T2.form_status_code) AS T1 WHERE T1.rn = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows that identify the single most common form status for each type of service.

sql: SELECT T1.party_email FROM Parties AS T1 WHERE T1.party_id IN (SELECT T2.customer_id FROM Party_Services AS T2 JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id WHERE T3.service_type_code = 'Education') AND T1.party_id IN (SELECT T4.party_id FROM Party_Addresses AS T4 JOIN Addresses AS T5 ON T4.address_id = T5.address_id WHERE T5.state_province_county = 'NorthCarolina');
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the email address for a customer located in North Carolina who is also subscribed to an 'Education' type service.

sql: SELECT T3.service_type_code, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.form_status_code = 'Completed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.form_id) AS completion_percentage FROM Party_Forms AS T1 JOIN Forms AS T2 ON T1.form_id = T2.form_id JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id GROUP BY T3.service_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each service type code and the calculated completion percentage for all associated forms.

sql: SELECT T1.individual_first_name, T1.individual_last_name FROM Individuals AS T1 JOIN Organization_Contact_Individuals AS T2 ON T1.individual_id = T2.individual_id JOIN Organizations AS T3 ON T2.organization_id = T3.organization_id WHERE T3.date_formed > '2016-10-01 00:00:00' AND T1.individual_email LIKE '%.org';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of contacts with a '.org' email address who are associated with organizations formed after October 1, 2016.

sql: SELECT T1.service_name, COUNT(T2.booking_id) AS booking_count FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Party_Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id WHERE T1.service_type_code = 'Welfare' GROUP BY T1.service_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the total number of bookings for each service classified as a 'Welfare' type.

sql: SELECT T3.party_email FROM Forms AS T1 JOIN Party_Forms AS T2 ON T1.form_id = T2.form_id JOIN Parties AS T3 ON T2.party_id = T3.party_id WHERE T1.form_type_code = 'Basic' AND T2.form_status_code = 'Partially Completed';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the email address of a party whose 'Basic' type form is 'Partially Completed'.

sql: SELECT T1.service_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.customer_id) AS unique_parties FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Party_Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id GROUP BY T1.service_name ORDER BY unique_parties DESC;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Position FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Affiliated_With AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician JOIN Department AS T3 ON T2.Department = T3.DepartmentID WHERE T3.Name = 'Surgery';

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS PhysicianName, T2.Name AS PatientName, T3.Start FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Patient AS T2 JOIN Appointment AS T3 ON T1.EmployeeID = T3.Physician AND T2.SSN = T3.Patient WHERE T3.ExaminationRoom = 'A';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Medication) AS NumberOfPrescriptions FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Prescribes AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM Nurse AS T1 JOIN Undergoes AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.AssistingNurse;

sql: SELECT T1.Patient, SUM(T2.Cost) AS TotalProcedureCost FROM Undergoes AS T1 JOIN Procedures AS T2 ON T1.Procedures = T2.Code GROUP BY T1.Patient;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.StayStart, T2.StayEnd FROM Patient AS T1 JOIN Stay AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Patient;

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS PhysicianName, T3.Name AS ProcedureName FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Procedures AS T3 JOIN (SELECT Physician, Treatment FROM Trained_In EXCEPT SELECT Physician, Procedures FROM Undergoes) AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician AND T3.Code = T2.Treatment;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM Patient AS T1 JOIN Stay AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Patient JOIN Room AS T3 ON T2.Room = T3.RoomNumber JOIN Undergoes AS T4 ON T1.SSN = T4.Patient JOIN Physician AS T5 ON T4.Physician = T5.EmployeeID JOIN Affiliated_With AS T6 ON T5.EmployeeID = T6.Physician JOIN Department AS T7 ON T6.Department = T7.DepartmentID WHERE T3.BlockFloor = 1 AND T7.Name = 'General Medicine';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Patient) AS TimesPrescribed FROM Medication AS T1 JOIN Prescribes AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Medication GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY TimesPrescribed DESC LIMIT 2;

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS PatientName, T2.Name AS ProcedureName, T3.Name AS NurseName FROM Patient AS T1 JOIN Procedures AS T2 JOIN Nurse AS T3 JOIN Undergoes AS T4 ON T1.SSN = T4.Patient AND T2.Code = T4.Procedures AND T3.EmployeeID = T4.AssistingNurse;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM Procedures AS T1 JOIN Undergoes AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Procedures JOIN Physician AS T3 ON T2.Physician = T3.EmployeeID WHERE T1.Cost > (SELECT Cost FROM Procedures WHERE Name = 'Reverse Rhinopodoplasty') AND T3.Position = 'Surgical Attending Physician';

sql: WITH DailyCounts AS (SELECT Physician, DATE(Start) AS ApptDate, COUNT(DISTINCT Patient) AS PatientCount FROM Appointment GROUP BY Physician, ApptDate) SELECT T1.Name, AVG(T2.PatientCount) AS AvgPatientsPerDay FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN DailyCounts AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS Physician1, T2.Name AS Physician2, T3.Name AS DepartmentName FROM Physician AS T1, Physician AS T2, Department AS T3, Affiliated_With AS T4, Affiliated_With AS T5 WHERE T1.EmployeeID = T4.Physician AND T2.EmployeeID = T5.Physician AND T3.DepartmentID = T4.Department AND T4.Department = T5.Department AND T1.EmployeeID < T2.EmployeeID AND T4.PrimaryAffiliation = 1 AND T5.PrimaryAffiliation = 1;
