sql: SELECT DISTINCT payment_method FROM Customers;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each unique payment method used by customers.

sql: SELECT mailshot_id, product_category, mailshot_start_date FROM Mailshot_Campaigns WHERE mailshot_name = 'US';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows detailing the campaign ID, product category, and start date for all mailshot campaigns named 'US'.

sql: SELECT premise_id, premise_details FROM Premises WHERE premises_type = 'Warehouse';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID and details for all premises that are classified as 'Warehouse'.

sql: SELECT item_id, order_id, product_id, item_order_quantity FROM Order_Items WHERE item_order_quantity > 5;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying individual order items purchased in quantities greater than 5, showing the item's ID, its associated order and product IDs, and the exact quantity.

sql: SELECT customer_id, premise_id, date_address_from FROM Customer_Addresses WHERE address_type_code = 'Billing';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the customer ID, premise ID, and effective start date for all customer addresses designated as 'Billing'.

sql: SELECT customer_id, customer_name, customer_phone FROM Customers ORDER BY customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows containing the ID, name, and phone number for each customer, ordered alphabetically by customer name.

sql: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(order_id) FROM Customer_Orders GROUP BY customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each customer ID along with the total count of orders they have placed.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_email FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the unique names and email addresses for all customers who have placed an order.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.item_order_quantity FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the product name and the quantity for each individual line item from all orders.

sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) FROM Mailshot_Customers WHERE outcome_code != 'No Response';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of unique customers who responded to the mailshot campaign.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_phone FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.address_type_code = 'Billing';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and phone number for each customer with a designated 'Billing' address on file.

sql: SELECT product_category, COUNT(mailshot_id) FROM Mailshot_Campaigns GROUP BY product_category;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each product category and the total number of mailshot campaigns associated with it.

sql: SELECT shipping_method_code, AVG(order_shipping_charges) FROM Customer_Orders GROUP BY shipping_method_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each shipping method code alongside the average shipping charge for all orders using that method.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.payment_method = 'Direct Debit';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of customers who are on the mailshot list and pay using 'Direct Debit'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Premises AS T3 ON T2.premise_id = T3.premise_id WHERE T3.premises_type = 'Warehouse';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of customers whose address is classified as a 'Warehouse'.

sql: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(order_id) FROM Customer_Orders GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(order_id) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer's ID and the total number of orders they have placed, filtered to include only customers with one or more orders.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, COUNT(T2.item_id) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name HAVING COUNT(T2.item_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each product name alongside the total number of times that product has been ordered, including only products with at least one sale.

sql: SELECT T1.mailshot_name, T1.product_category, T2.mailshot_customer_date FROM Mailshot_Campaigns AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.mailshot_id = T2.mailshot_id WHERE T2.outcome_code = 'Order';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows detailing successful mailshot campaigns that resulted in a customer order, including the campaign name, product category, and the date of the interaction.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'Cancelled';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of customers who have had at least one order with a 'Cancelled' status.

sql: SELECT customer_id, MAX(order_placed_datetime) FROM Customer_Orders GROUP BY customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each unique customer ID along with the date and time of their most recent order.

sql: SELECT T1.mailshot_name, COUNT(T2.customer_id) FROM Mailshot_Campaigns AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.mailshot_id = T2.mailshot_id WHERE T1.product_category = 'food' GROUP BY T1.mailshot_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each 'food' product mailshot campaign name and the total number of customers targeted in that campaign.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_email FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_category = 'Electronics';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique customer's name and email for those who have purchased a product in the 'Electronics' category.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T2.order_shipping_charges) AS total_shipping FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id ORDER BY total_shipping DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top customers who have incurred the highest total shipping charges, ordered from most to least expensive.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Orders);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of customers who are on the mailshot list but have not yet placed an order.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.item_order_quantity FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.item_order_quantity = (SELECT MAX(item_order_quantity) FROM Order_Items);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the product ordered in the largest single quantity, showing the product's name and that maximum quantity.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2A ON T1.customer_id = T2A.customer_id JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2B ON T1.customer_id = T2B.customer_id WHERE T2A.address_type_code = 'Billing' AND T2B.address_type_code = 'Shipping' AND T2A.premise_id != T2B.premise_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers who have separate addresses for billing and shipping.

sql: SELECT T1.mailshot_name, COUNT(T3.order_id) AS number_of_orders FROM Mailshot_Campaigns AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.mailshot_id = T2.mailshot_id JOIN Customer_Orders AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T2.outcome_code = 'Order' GROUP BY T1.mailshot_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing each mailshot campaign's name alongside the total number of orders generated as a direct result of that campaign.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_category IN ('Food', 'Electronics') GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.customer_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T4.product_category) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the customer who has purchased products from both the 'Food' and 'Electronics' categories.

sql: SELECT shipping_method_code, AVG(julianday(order_delivered_datetime) - julianday(order_placed_datetime)) AS avg_delivery_days FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_delivered_datetime IS NOT NULL GROUP BY shipping_method_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each shipping method and the corresponding average delivery time in days for completed orders.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Premises AS T3 ON T2.premise_id = T3.premise_id WHERE T3.premises_type = 'Residence' INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Mailshot_Campaigns AS T3 ON T2.mailshot_id = T3.mailshot_id WHERE T3.product_category = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer who both lives at a 'Residence' and was targeted by a 'food' product mailshot campaign.

sql: SELECT T1.mailshot_name, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.outcome_code = 'Order' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T2.customer_id) AS conversion_rate_percent FROM Mailshot_Campaigns AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.mailshot_id = T2.mailshot_id GROUP BY T1.mailshot_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, listing each mailshot campaign name and its corresponding conversion rate, calculated as the percentage of customers who placed an 'Order'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T4.product_category FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_id, T3.product_category FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Mailshot_Campaigns AS T3 ON T2.mailshot_id = T3.mailshot_id;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows identifying customer and product category pairs from purchases, excluding cases where the customer was targeted by a mailshot for that same product category.

sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', order_placed_datetime) AS year_month, COUNT(order_id) AS cancellation_count FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status_code = 'Cancelled' GROUP BY year_month ORDER BY cancellation_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single month with the highest number of cancelled orders, showing the specific year-month and the total cancellation count.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T2.order_shipping_charges) AS total_shipping FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING SUM(T2.order_shipping_charges) > (SELECT AVG(order_shipping_charges) FROM Customer_Orders);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing customers whose total spending on shipping charges is greater than the average shipping charge across all orders, showing each customer's name and their total amount spent on shipping.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, T1.customer_id, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.product_id) AS distinct_products FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id GROUP BY T1.order_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, with each row showing an order ID, the associated customer ID, and a count of the distinct products purchased in that order.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Customer_Orders AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE (julianday(T2.date_address_to) - julianday(T2.date_address_from)) / 365 > 5;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of customers who have lived at a single address for more than five years and have also placed an order.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.product_name, T1.product_category FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Customer_Orders AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id WHERE T3.order_status_code = 'Cancelled';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique product names and categories for all products that have appeared in a cancelled order.

sql: SELECT customer_name FROM Customers WHERE payment_method = 'Credit Card' INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Premises AS T3 ON T2.premise_id = T3.premise_id WHERE T3.premises_type = 'Warehouse';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer who both pays with a credit card and has an address at a warehouse premise.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, SUM(T2.item_order_quantity) AS total_quantity, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(T2.item_order_quantity) DESC) AS sales_rank FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each product's name, the total quantity ordered, and its corresponding sales rank, with rank 1 being the most frequently ordered product.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, AVG(T3.item_count) FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN (SELECT order_id, COUNT(item_id) AS item_count FROM Order_Items GROUP BY order_id) AS T3 ON T1.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, showing each customer ID and the average number of items they purchase per order.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE date(T1.order_placed_datetime) = date(T2.mailshot_customer_date);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.mailshot_id, T1.mailshot_name FROM Mailshot_Campaigns AS T1 LEFT JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.mailshot_id = T2.mailshot_id GROUP BY T1.mailshot_id HAVING COUNT(T2.customer_id) = 0;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the ID and name for mailshot campaigns that have no associated customers.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_placed_datetime = (SELECT MIN(order_placed_datetime) FROM Customer_Orders);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the customer who placed the very first order ever recorded.

sql: SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Orders EXCEPT SELECT customer_id FROM Mailshot_Customers;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the customer IDs for customers who have placed an order but are not included in the mailshot list.

sql: SELECT mailshot_id, outcome_code, outcome_count FROM (SELECT mailshot_id, outcome_code, COUNT(*) as outcome_count, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY mailshot_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) as rn FROM Mailshot_Customers GROUP BY mailshot_id, outcome_code) WHERE rn = 1;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, identifying the single most frequent customer outcome for each mailshot campaign and the total count of customers who had that outcome.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_id AS customer1, T2.customer_id AS customer2, T1.premise_id FROM Customer_Addresses AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.premise_id = T2.premise_id WHERE T1.customer_id < T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying pairs of distinct customers who share the same address, listing the two customer IDs and their shared premise ID.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and age for each person in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE Hometown = 'Miami';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and age of the person whose hometown is 'Miami'.

sql: SELECT Name, Hometown FROM people WHERE Age = 24.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and hometown of all people who are 24 years old.

sql: SELECT * FROM gymnast WHERE Gymnast_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the detailed scores across all gymnastic events for the specific gymnast with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people ORDER BY Height DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing people's names and heights, ordered from the tallest to the shortest.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the person whose specific People ID is 2.

sql: SELECT Gymnast_ID, Total_Points FROM gymnast WHERE Total_Points > 57.9;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and total points for each gymnast who achieved a score greater than 57.9.

sql: SELECT Name, Hometown FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each person's name and their corresponding hometown.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE Age < 22;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and age of each person younger than 22.

sql: SELECT AVG(Total_Points) FROM gymnast;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average total points calculated across all gymnasts.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and total points for each gymnast by joining their personal information with their gymnast-specific records.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID ORDER BY T2.Total_Points DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and total points for the single top-scoring gymnast.

sql: SELECT AVG(Floor_Exercise_Points), AVG(Rings_Points), AVG(Vault_Points) FROM gymnast;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average points for the Floor Exercise, Rings, and Vault events across all gymnasts.

sql: SELECT Hometown, COUNT(*) FROM people GROUP BY Hometown;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each hometown and the corresponding number of people from that town.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Total_Points > (SELECT AVG(Total_Points) FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age of each gymnast whose total points are greater than the average across all gymnasts.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Floor_Exercise_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID ORDER BY T2.Floor_Exercise_Points DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each gymnast by name along with their floor exercise points, ordered from the highest score to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID ORDER BY T1.Age DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the oldest gymnast.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Floor_Exercise_Points > T2.Vault_Points;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of gymnasts who scored more points in their floor exercise than in their vault event.

sql: SELECT MAX(Age) - MIN(Age) FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the age range of all people, calculated as the difference between the maximum and minimum age.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM gymnast;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all gymnasts in the table.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T1.Hometown = 'Santo Domingo';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the name and total points for gymnasts whose hometown is 'Santo Domingo'.

sql: SELECT Hometown, AVG(Height) FROM people GROUP BY Hometown;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each hometown along with the average height for people from that hometown.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Pommel_Horse_Points > 9.7;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of gymnasts who scored higher than 9.7 points on the Pommel Horse.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Gymnast_ID = 4;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and total points for the specific gymnast with an ID of 4.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM gymnast WHERE Total_Points > 57.7;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of gymnasts who have more than 57.7 total points.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age, T2.Vault_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Vault_Points = (SELECT MAX(Vault_Points) FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the gymnast who achieved the highest vault score, showing their name, age, and that specific score.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID ORDER BY T2.Total_Points DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top three gymnasts by their total points, ordered from the highest score to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Floor_Exercise_Points > (SELECT AVG(Floor_Exercise_Points) FROM gymnast) AND T2.Rings_Points > (SELECT AVG(Rings_Points) FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the gymnast who scored above the average in both the Floor Exercise and the Rings events.

sql: SELECT T1.Hometown FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID GROUP BY T1.Hometown ORDER BY AVG(T2.Total_Points) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single hometown whose gymnasts have the highest average total points.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Rings_Points > (SELECT T4.Rings_Points FROM people AS T3 JOIN gymnast AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.Gymnast_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Paul Hamm');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of gymnasts who scored higher on the Rings event than the gymnast 'Paul Hamm'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T1.Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of gymnasts whose age is greater than the average age of all people in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Hometown, MAX(T2.Total_Points) FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID GROUP BY T1.Hometown;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each hometown along with the maximum total points achieved by a gymnast from that town.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT Gymnast_ID FROM gymnast WHERE Total_Points > 58);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any gymnast who has achieved more than 58 total points.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Total_Points > (SELECT T4.Total_Points FROM people AS T3 JOIN gymnast AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.Gymnast_ID WHERE T3.People_ID = 2);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the gymnast whose total points are greater than the total points of the gymnast with People_ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Floor_Exercise_Points > (SELECT AVG(Pommel_Horse_Points) FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of gymnasts whose floor exercise score is greater than the average pommel horse score across all gymnasts.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Age) FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Total_Points > 57.5;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average age of all gymnasts who have scored more than 57.5 total points.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age for every person who is not registered as a gymnast.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Age = 24.0 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of people who are 24 years old and are also gymnasts.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Gymnast_ID, MAX(scores) - MIN(scores) AS score_range FROM (SELECT Gymnast_ID, Floor_Exercise_Points AS scores FROM gymnast UNION ALL SELECT Gymnast_ID, Pommel_Horse_Points FROM gymnast UNION ALL SELECT Gymnast_ID, Rings_Points FROM gymnast UNION ALL SELECT Gymnast_ID, Vault_Points FROM gymnast UNION ALL SELECT Gymnast_ID, Parallel_Bars_Points FROM gymnast UNION ALL SELECT Gymnast_ID, Horizontal_Bar_Points FROM gymnast) AS T3 GROUP BY Gymnast_ID) AS T4 ON T1.People_ID = T4.Gymnast_ID ORDER BY T4.score_range ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the gymnast with the smallest score range (the difference between their highest and lowest scores) across all events, identifying the most consistent performer.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, (T2.Floor_Exercise_Points + T2.Pommel_Horse_Points + T2.Rings_Points + T2.Vault_Points + T2.Parallel_Bars_Points + T2.Horizontal_Bar_Points) / 6 AS Avg_Discipline_Score FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID ORDER BY Avg_Discipline_Score DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each gymnast's name and their average score across six disciplines, ordered from the highest to the lowest average score.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Hometown = 'Miami' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Total_Points > 58.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of people who are either from 'Miami' or are gymnasts with a total score greater than 58.0.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T1.Height > (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM people) AND T2.Total_Points > (SELECT AVG(Total_Points) FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any gymnast who is both taller than the average person and has a total points score higher than the average for all gymnasts.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS G1 ON T1.People_ID = G1.Gymnast_ID, people AS T2 JOIN gymnast AS G2 ON T2.People_ID = G2.Gymnast_ID WHERE G1.Pommel_Horse_Points = G2.Horizontal_Bar_Points AND G1.Gymnast_ID != G2.Gymnast_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing pairs of different gymnasts where the first gymnast's pommel horse score is equal to the second gymnast's horizontal bar score.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Parallel_Bars_Points > 9.7 AND T2.Horizontal_Bar_Points > 9.7;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of gymnasts who scored higher than 9.7 points on both the Parallel Bars and the Horizontal Bars.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T1.Hometown = 'Santo Domingo' ORDER BY T2.Total_Points DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the gymnast from Santo Domingo who has the highest total points.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Total_Points < (SELECT AVG(T4.Total_Points) FROM people AS T3 JOIN gymnast AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.Gymnast_ID WHERE T3.Hometown = 'Santo Domingo');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of gymnasts who scored below the average total points of gymnasts from the hometown 'Santo Domingo'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Floor_Exercise_Points, T2.Pommel_Horse_Points, T2.Rings_Points, T2.Vault_Points, T2.Parallel_Bars_Points, T2.Horizontal_Bar_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Total_Points = (SELECT MAX(Total_Points) FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and individual event scores for the gymnast who achieved the highest overall total score.

sql: SELECT T1.Hometown, COUNT(T2.Gymnast_ID) FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID GROUP BY T1.Hometown HAVING COUNT(T2.Gymnast_ID) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows providing a count of gymnasts for each hometown that has at least one gymnast.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T1.Hometown = (SELECT T3.Hometown FROM people AS T3 JOIN gymnast AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.Gymnast_ID ORDER BY T4.Total_Points DESC LIMIT 1) AND T2.Total_Points < (SELECT MAX(Total_Points) FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all gymnasts from the same hometown as the overall top-scoring gymnast, excluding the top scorer from the list.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, CASE WHEN T2.Total_Points > 58 THEN 'Elite' WHEN T2.Total_Points > 57.7 THEN 'Excellent' ELSE 'Good' END AS Performance_Label FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each gymnast's name alongside a performance label ('Elite', 'Excellent', or 'Good') calculated based on their total points.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name, T3.killed, T3.injured FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id;

sql: SELECT T1.name, SUM(T3.killed + T3.injured) AS total_casualties FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each battle name and the total number of casualties (killed plus injured) caused by ships that were lost in that battle.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle GROUP BY T1.name HAVING count(T2.id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of battles in which more than one ship was lost.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.killed FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T2.killed = (SELECT max(killed) FROM death);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the ship responsible for the maximum number of recorded kills, along with that kill count.

sql: SELECT T1.bulgarian_commander FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T1.result = 'Bulgarian victory' GROUP BY T1.bulgarian_commander HAVING SUM(T3.killed) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the Bulgarian commanders who won battles where the total number of deaths caused by ships lost in that battle was greater than 5.

sql: SELECT name, ship_type FROM ship WHERE disposition_of_ship = 'Captured' AND id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT caused_by_ship_id FROM death WHERE caused_by_ship_id IS NOT NULL);

sql: SELECT T_agg.battle_name FROM (SELECT T1.name AS battle_name, SUM(T3.killed) AS total_killed FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.name) AS T_agg ORDER BY T_agg.total_killed DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.killed FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T2.killed > (SELECT AVG(killed) FROM death);

sql: SELECT commander FROM (SELECT T1.bulgarian_commander AS commander FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle UNION ALL SELECT T1.latin_commander AS commander FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle) AS T3 WHERE commander IS NOT NULL GROUP BY commander ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.name, SUM(T2.killed) AS total_killed FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY total_killed DESC LIMIT 3;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle WHERE T2.id NOT IN (SELECT caused_by_ship_id FROM death);

sql: SELECT T1.ship_type, CAST(SUM(T2.killed) AS REAL) * 100 / (SELECT SUM(killed) FROM death) AS percentage_of_total_deaths FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.ship_type;

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN battle AS T2 ON T1.lost_in_battle = T2.id WHERE T1.ship_type = 'Brig' AND T1.disposition_of_ship = 'Captured' AND T2.result = 'Bulgarian victory';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of a 'Brig' type ship and the corresponding battle where it was captured in a 'Bulgarian victory'.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T1.name, T2.name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.lost_in_battle = T2.lost_in_battle JOIN battle AS T3 ON T1.lost_in_battle = T3.id WHERE T1.id < T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing pairs of ships that were lost in the same battle, showing the battle's name and the names of the two ships involved.

sql: SELECT T1.location, COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.disposition_of_ship = 'Captured' THEN T1.id END), SUM(T2.killed + T2.injured) FROM ship AS T1 LEFT JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.location;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows detailing naval engagement outcomes for each geographical location, providing a count of captured ships and the total sum of associated casualties (killed and injured).

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.date, SUM(T3.killed) FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T1.bulgarian_commander = 'Kaloyan' AND T1.latin_commander != 'Baldwin I' GROUP BY T1.name, T1.date;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name, date, and total number of casualties for each battle commanded by 'Kaloyan' where the opposing Latin commander was not 'Baldwin I'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T1.lost_in_battle NOT IN (SELECT id FROM battle);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.result, AVG(T3.killed) FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.result;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each unique battle outcome alongside the average number of deaths caused by ships that were lost in battles with that outcome.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.ship_type FROM ship AS T1 JOIN (SELECT caused_by_ship_id, SUM(killed) AS total_killed FROM death GROUP BY caused_by_ship_id) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T1.location = 'English Channel' AND T1.disposition_of_ship = 'Captured' AND T2.total_killed > 20;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and type for ships that were captured in the English Channel and caused more than 20 deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.name, SUM(T3.killed + T3.injured) AS battle_casualties FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T1.id > 2 GROUP BY T1.name HAVING SUM(T3.killed + T3.injured) > (SELECT AVG(total_casualties) FROM (SELECT SUM(T6.killed + T6.injured) AS total_casualties FROM battle AS T4 JOIN ship AS T5 ON T4.id = T5.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T6 ON T5.id = T6.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T4.id));
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing battles with a higher-than-average casualty count, showing the battle name and the total number of people killed or injured.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T1.name, T3.killed FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T3.killed = (SELECT MAX(killed) FROM death);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single deadliest event, showing the name of the responsible ship, the number of people killed, and the name of the battle where that ship was eventually lost.

sql: SELECT T1.Team, T1.Name, T1.Age FROM technician AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Team, MAX(Age) AS max_age FROM technician GROUP BY Team) AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team AND T1.Age = T2.max_age;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id GROUP BY T1.technician_id, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.repair_ID) >= 1;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Making_Year, T3.Class, T3.Team FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN machine AS T3 ON T2.Machine_ID = T3.Machine_ID WHERE T1.Team = 'NYY';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN machine AS T3 ON T2.Machine_ID = T3.Machine_ID WHERE T3.quality_rank > (SELECT AVG(quality_rank) FROM machine);

sql: SELECT T1.Team, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.repair_ID) FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id WHERE T1.Team IN (SELECT Team FROM technician GROUP BY Team HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) GROUP BY T1.Team;

sql: SELECT T1.Machine_ID, T1.Machine_series, T4.Name, T3.Notes FROM machine AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.Machine_ID = T2.Machine_ID JOIN repair AS T3 ON T2.repair_ID = T3.repair_ID JOIN technician AS T4 ON T2.technician_id = T4.technician_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows that link each machine (by ID and series) to the name of the assigned technician and the specific notes for that repair job.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Team) AS NumTeams FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN machine AS T3 ON T2.Machine_ID = T3.Machine_ID GROUP BY T1.technician_id, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T3.Machine_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each technician's name and the total count of distinct teams whose machines they have serviced, including only technicians with at least one repair assignment.

sql: SELECT T1.Starting_Year, COUNT(T3.repair_ID) FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN repair AS T3 ON T2.repair_ID = T3.repair_ID WHERE T3.Notes LIKE '%Failed%' GROUP BY T1.Starting_Year;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Team FROM technician AS T1 WHERE T1.technician_id NOT IN (SELECT T2.technician_id FROM repair_assignment AS T2 JOIN machine AS T3 ON T2.Machine_ID = T3.Machine_ID WHERE T3.value_points <= 100);

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code WHERE T2.role_description = 'Editor' UNION SELECT T1.employee_name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code WHERE T2.role_description = 'Manager';

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Circulation_History AS T2 ON T1.employee_id = T2.employee_id WHERE T2.document_id = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.employee_name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Circulation_History AS T2 ON T1.employee_id = T2.employee_id WHERE T2.document_id = 2;

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name, COUNT(T2.copy_number) AS copies_handled FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Circulation_History AS T2 ON T1.employee_id = T2.employee_id GROUP BY T1.employee_name ORDER BY copies_handled DESC LIMIT 3;

sql: SELECT document_type_description FROM Ref_Document_Types WHERE document_type_code IN (SELECT T1.document_type_code FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Drafts AS T2 ON T1.document_id = T2.document_id GROUP BY T1.document_type_code HAVING COUNT(T2.document_id) > 1);

sql: SELECT T1.role_description, COUNT(T3.document_id) AS circulation_count FROM Roles AS T1 JOIN Employees AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code JOIN Circulation_History AS T3 ON T2.employee_id = T3.employee_id GROUP BY T1.role_description;

result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the status description for all documents that have been mailed to the address with an ID of 8.

sql: SELECT T3.role_description, T4.document_type_description FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Circulation_History AS T2 ON T1.employee_id = T2.employee_id JOIN Roles AS T3 ON T1.role_code = T3.role_code JOIN Documents AS T5 ON T2.document_id = T5.document_id JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T4 ON T5.document_type_code = T4.document_type_code GROUP BY T3.role_description, T4.document_type_description;

sql: SELECT document_id, draft_number FROM Document_Drafts AS T1 WHERE draft_number > (SELECT AVG(draft_number) FROM Document_Drafts AS T2 WHERE T2.document_id = T1.document_id);

sql: SELECT T4.role_description, COUNT(T3.document_id) AS mailed_doc_circulations FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_Mailed AS T2 ON T1.document_id = T2.document_id JOIN Circulation_History AS T3 ON T1.document_id = T3.document_id JOIN Employees AS T5 ON T3.employee_id = T5.employee_id JOIN Roles AS T4 ON T5.role_code = T4.role_code GROUP BY T4.role_description;

sql: SELECT T1.document_id, T1.receipt_number FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN (SELECT document_type_code, AVG(receipt_number) AS avg_num FROM Documents GROUP BY document_type_code) AS T2 ON T1.document_type_code = T2.document_type_code WHERE T1.receipt_number > T2.avg_num;

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Circulation_History AS T2 JOIN Documents AS T3 ON T2.document_id = T3.document_id WHERE T2.employee_id = T1.employee_id AND T3.document_type_code = 'Paper');

sql: SELECT T1.shipping_agent_name FROM Ref_Shipping_Agents AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.shipping_agent_code = T2.shipping_agent_code JOIN Documents_Mailed AS T3 ON T2.document_id = T3.document_id GROUP BY T1.shipping_agent_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.mailed_to_address_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT transaction_id, COUNT(lot_id) FROM Transactions_Lots GROUP BY transaction_id HAVING COUNT(lot_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying transactions that involve more than one lot, listing each transaction's ID and the corresponding count of lots.

sql: SELECT T2.investor_id, T2.Investor_details FROM Transactions AS T1 JOIN Investors AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id GROUP BY T1.investor_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.transaction_type_code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying investors who have engaged in exactly two distinct types of transactions, showing their ID and associated details.

sql: SELECT investor_id, SUM(amount_of_transaction), COUNT(transaction_id) FROM Transactions GROUP BY investor_id HAVING COUNT(transaction_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing investors who have made more than one transaction, showing each investor's ID, the total value of their transactions, and their total number of transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, T2.Investor_details FROM Transactions AS T1 JOIN Investors AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id WHERE T1.amount_of_transaction = (SELECT MAX(amount_of_transaction) FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'SALE');
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the investor (ID and details) who conducted the single largest transaction classified as a 'SALE'.

sql: SELECT T1.lot_id, T2.lot_details FROM Transactions_Lots AS T1 JOIN Lots AS T2 ON T1.lot_id = T2.lot_id GROUP BY T1.lot_id HAVING COUNT(T1.transaction_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID and details for lots that have been involved in more than one transaction.

sql: SELECT investor_id, SUM(amount_of_transaction) AS total_amount FROM Transactions GROUP BY investor_id HAVING SUM(amount_of_transaction) > (SELECT AVG(amount_of_transaction) FROM Transactions);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying investors whose total transaction value is greater than the average of a single transaction, showing each qualifying investor's ID and their total transaction sum.

sql: SELECT T1.transaction_id, T1.amount_of_transaction, T1.transaction_type_code FROM Transactions AS T1 WHERE T1.amount_of_transaction > (SELECT AVG(T2.amount_of_transaction) FROM Transactions AS T2 WHERE T2.transaction_type_code = T1.transaction_type_code);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying transactions with an amount greater than the average for their respective transaction type, including the transaction ID, amount, and type code.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, T1.Investor_details FROM Investors AS T1 WHERE T1.investor_id IN (SELECT investor_id FROM Transactions) AND T1.investor_id NOT IN (SELECT investor_id FROM Lots);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the ID and details for investors who have made a transaction but do not own a lot.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, SUM(T1.share_count) AS total_shares_purchased FROM Transactions AS T1 WHERE T1.transaction_type_code = 'PUR' GROUP BY T1.investor_id ORDER BY total_shares_purchased DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top three investors by the total number of shares purchased, ordered from the highest number of shares to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.*, T3.lot_details, T4.Investor_details FROM Transactions AS T1 JOIN Transactions_Lots AS T2 ON T1.transaction_id = T2.transaction_id JOIN Lots AS T3 ON T2.lot_id = T3.lot_id JOIN Investors AS T4 ON T1.investor_id = T4.investor_id WHERE T2.lot_id = 11;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the full transaction details, along with the corresponding lot and investor information, for all transactions involving lot ID 11.

sql: SELECT investor_id FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'SALE' EXCEPT SELECT investor_id FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'PUR';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the IDs of investors who have made at least one sale transaction but have never made a purchase transaction.

sql: SELECT T2.lot_id, T3.lot_details, SUM(T1.amount_of_transaction) FROM Transactions AS T1 JOIN Transactions_Lots AS T2 ON T1.transaction_id = T2.transaction_id JOIN Lots AS T3 ON T2.lot_id = T3.lot_id GROUP BY T2.lot_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, with each row listing a lot's ID and details, along with the total sum of all transaction amounts associated with that lot.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, SUM(CASE WHEN T1.transaction_type_code = 'SALE' THEN T1.amount_of_transaction ELSE 0 END) AS total_sales, SUM(CASE WHEN T1.transaction_type_code = 'PUR' THEN T1.amount_of_transaction ELSE 0 END) AS total_purchases FROM Transactions AS T1 GROUP BY T1.investor_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each investor's ID along with the total sum of their sales and the total sum of their purchases.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Transactions AS T1 JOIN Transactions_Lots AS T2 ON T1.transaction_id = T2.transaction_id JOIN Lots AS T3 ON T2.lot_id = T3.lot_id WHERE T3.investor_id = 13;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing all transaction details for any transactions associated with lots owned by the investor with ID 13.

sql: SELECT * FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'SALE' AND amount_of_transaction > (SELECT AVG(amount_of_transaction) FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'PUR');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all transaction details for sales where the transaction amount exceeds the average amount of all purchase transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, T2.Investor_details, T1.amount_of_transaction, T3.purchase_details FROM Transactions AS T1 JOIN Investors AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id JOIN Purchases AS T3 ON T1.transaction_id = T3.purchase_transaction_id WHERE T1.transaction_type_code = 'PUR';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows detailing all purchase transactions, providing the investor's ID and details, the transaction amount, and the corresponding purchase details.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, T1.transaction_id, T1.share_count FROM Transactions AS T1 WHERE T1.share_count = (SELECT MAX(T2.share_count) FROM Transactions AS T2 WHERE T2.investor_id = T1.investor_id);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows identifying each investor's largest transaction(s) by share count, listing the investor ID, the transaction ID, and the number of shares for that transaction.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, T2.Investor_details, COUNT(T1.transaction_id) AS transaction_count FROM Transactions AS T1 JOIN Investors AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id WHERE T2.Investor_details = 'z' GROUP BY T1.investor_id;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying an investor with the detail 'z' and providing a total count of their transactions.

sql: SELECT T2.lot_id FROM Transactions AS T1 JOIN Transactions_Lots AS T2 ON T1.transaction_id = T2.transaction_id GROUP BY T2.lot_id HAVING MIN(T1.transaction_type_code) = 'SALE' AND MAX(T1.transaction_type_code) = 'SALE';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the IDs for lots where all associated transactions are exclusively of the 'SALE' type.

sql: SELECT (SELECT SUM(amount_of_transaction) FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'SALE') - (SELECT SUM(amount_of_transaction) FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'PUR');
result_summary: Returns a single value representing the net transaction amount, calculated by subtracting the total value of all purchase ('PUR') transactions from the total value of all sales ('SALE') transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, T1.Investor_details FROM Investors AS T1 WHERE T1.investor_id IN (SELECT investor_id FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'SALE') AND T1.investor_id NOT IN (SELECT investor_id FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'PUR');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the ID and details for investors who have made sales transactions but have never made any purchase transactions.

sql: SELECT investor_id FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'PUR' EXCEPT SELECT investor_id FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'SALE';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the IDs of investors who have made at least one purchase but have never made a sale.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, T2.Investor_details, COUNT(T1.lot_id) AS lot_count FROM Lots AS T1 JOIN Investors AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id GROUP BY T1.investor_id ORDER BY lot_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single investor with the most lots, displaying their ID, details, and the total count of lots they are associated with.

result_summary: Returns 7 rows, providing a complete list of all missions and their corresponding details, including the launch year, location, speed, and eventual fate.

sql: SELECT Name, Type FROM ship WHERE Nationality = 'United Kingdom';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and type for each ship with United Kingdom nationality.

sql: SELECT Name, Tonnage FROM ship WHERE Type = 'Cargo ship';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and tonnage for each ship classified as a 'Cargo ship'.

sql: SELECT Code, Location, Fate FROM mission WHERE Launched_Year = 1930;

sql: SELECT Code, Speed_knots FROM mission WHERE Location = 'Germany';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the mission code and speed in knots for all missions that took place in Germany.

sql: SELECT Name, Tonnage FROM ship ORDER BY Tonnage DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each ship's name and tonnage, ordered from the heaviest to the lightest ship.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Nationality FROM ship;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each unique nationality of the ships in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Mission_ID, Code, Launched_Year FROM mission WHERE Fate = 'Decommissioned 1950';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing missions that were decommissioned in 1950, showing each mission's ID, code, and launch year.

sql: SELECT Name, Tonnage FROM ship WHERE Tonnage > 3500;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type, T2.Code, T2.Launched_Year FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and type of ships from the United States, along with their associated mission code and the year the mission was launched.

sql: SELECT T1.Type, COUNT(T2.Mission_ID) FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T1.Type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each ship type along with the total count of missions associated with that type.

sql: SELECT Type, AVG(Tonnage) FROM ship GROUP BY Type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each ship type along with the average tonnage for all ships of that type.

sql: SELECT Nationality, COUNT(Ship_ID) FROM ship GROUP BY Nationality HAVING COUNT(Ship_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing nationalities with more than one ship, along with the total ship count for each.

sql: SELECT Location, COUNT(Mission_ID) FROM mission GROUP BY Location HAVING COUNT(Mission_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing locations that have hosted more than one mission, along with the total count of missions for each of those locations.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Launched_Year, T2.Location FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T1.Type = 'Cargo ship';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name of each cargo ship along with the launch year and location for its associated mission.

sql: SELECT Nationality, SUM(Tonnage) FROM ship GROUP BY Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each nationality and the corresponding total tonnage of their ships.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Tonnage FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Launched_Year = 1930;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and tonnage of ships involved in missions that were launched in the year 1930.

sql: SELECT T2.Code, T1.Nationality FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Location = 'Germany';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of ships that have undertaken a mission located in Germany.

sql: SELECT Launched_Year, AVG(Speed_knots) FROM mission GROUP BY Launched_Year;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows detailing the average mission speed in knots for each launch year.

sql: SELECT Nationality, MAX(Tonnage) FROM ship GROUP BY Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each nationality alongside the maximum tonnage of a ship from that nation.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Fate FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T1.Type = 'Battle ship';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and fate for all ships that are classified as 'Battle ship'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type, T2.Code, T2.Launched_Year, T2.Location FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, listing each mission's code, launch year, and location, along with the name and type of the ship that performed it.

sql: SELECT T1.Nationality, COUNT(T2.Mission_ID) FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T1.Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each ship's nationality along with the total count of missions undertaken by ships from that nation.

sql: SELECT Name, Tonnage FROM ship WHERE Tonnage > (SELECT AVG(Tonnage) FROM ship);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of ships whose tonnage is greater than the average tonnage across all ships.

sql: SELECT Code, Speed_knots FROM mission WHERE Speed_knots > (SELECT AVG(Speed_knots) FROM mission);

sql: SELECT Name, Type FROM ship WHERE Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM mission WHERE Location = 'Germany');

sql: SELECT Name, Tonnage FROM ship AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM mission AS T2 WHERE T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID);

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Speed_knots > (SELECT AVG(Speed_knots) FROM mission);

sql: SELECT T1.Type FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T1.Type HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Location) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the types of ships that have been on missions in more than one distinct location.

sql: SELECT Code, Launched_Year, Speed_knots FROM mission AS T1 WHERE Speed_knots > (SELECT AVG(Speed_knots) FROM mission AS T2 WHERE T2.Launched_Year = T1.Launched_Year);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying missions whose speed was faster than the average speed of all missions launched in that same year, listing the mission's code, launch year, and speed in knots.

sql: SELECT Nationality FROM ship GROUP BY Nationality HAVING AVG(Tonnage) > (SELECT AVG(Tonnage) FROM ship);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the nationality whose ships have an average tonnage greater than the overall average tonnage of all ships.

sql: SELECT Launched_Year FROM mission GROUP BY Launched_Year HAVING AVG(Speed_knots) > 23;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the launch year in which the average speed of all missions launched that year was greater than 23 knots.

sql: SELECT Name, Type, Tonnage FROM ship WHERE Tonnage = (SELECT MAX(Tonnage) FROM ship);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name, type, and tonnage for the ship with the maximum tonnage.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Code, T2.Launched_Year FROM ship AS T1 LEFT JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United Kingdom';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing all ships with 'United Kingdom' nationality, showing their name along with their mission code and launch year, including ships that do not have an assigned mission.

sql: SELECT Type FROM ship GROUP BY Type ORDER BY AVG(Tonnage) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the single ship type that has the highest average tonnage across all ships.

sql: SELECT Name FROM ship UNION SELECT Code FROM mission;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows showing a combined and unique list of all ship names and mission codes.

sql: SELECT Name FROM ship EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Launched_Year = 1930;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of ships that did not have a mission launched in the year 1930.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM ship AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM mission WHERE Location = 'Germany') AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Code, T2.Speed_knots FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID ORDER BY T2.Speed_knots DESC LIMIT 2;

result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all cargo ships, excluding any that were recorded as 'Lost (burned) 1931'.

sql: SELECT T1.Nationality, COUNT(T2.Mission_ID) FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Launched_Year = 1930 GROUP BY T1.Nationality HAVING COUNT(T2.Mission_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the nationality that launched more than one mission in the year 1930, along with the total count of those missions.

sql: WITH AverageSpeed AS (SELECT AVG(Speed_knots) AS AvgSpeed FROM mission) SELECT T1.Name, T2.Speed_knots FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID, AverageSpeed WHERE T2.Speed_knots > AverageSpeed.AvgSpeed;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying the ship name and speed for each mission that was conducted at a speed faster than the average speed of all missions.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T2.Document_Type_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code WHERE T1.Document_Type_Code IN ('BK', 'PT');

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_with_Expenses AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Ref_Budget_Codes AS T3 ON T2.Budget_Type_Code = T3.Budget_Type_Code WHERE T3.Budget_Type_Description = 'Government';

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T3.Budget_Type_Description FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_with_Expenses AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Ref_Budget_Codes AS T3 ON T2.Budget_Type_Code = T3.Budget_Type_Code;

sql: SELECT Document_ID, Document_Name FROM Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code != 'CV';

sql: SELECT T2.Document_Type_Name, count(*) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code GROUP BY T2.Document_Type_Name;

sql: SELECT Document_Name, Document_Date FROM Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'CV' UNION SELECT Document_Name, Document_Date FROM Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'BK';

sql: SELECT T2.Project_Details, COUNT(T1.Document_ID) AS DocumentCount FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Projects AS T2 ON T1.Project_ID = T2.Project_ID GROUP BY T2.Project_ID, T2.Project_Details;

sql: SELECT T1.Document_ID, T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 WHERE T1.Document_ID IN (SELECT T2.Document_ID FROM Documents_with_Expenses AS T2) AND T1.Project_ID != (SELECT T3.Project_ID FROM Projects AS T3 WHERE T3.Project_Details = 'Society Research project');

sql: SELECT T2.Project_Details, count(T1.Document_ID) AS DocCount FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Projects AS T2 ON T1.Project_ID = T2.Project_ID GROUP BY T2.Project_Details ORDER BY DocCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the project with the most associated documents, showing the project's details and the total document count.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_ID FROM Documents_with_Expenses AS T1 WHERE T1.Budget_Type_Code = 'GV' EXCEPT SELECT T2.Document_ID FROM Documents AS T2 WHERE T2.Document_Type_Code = 'BK';

sql: SELECT T3.Budget_Type_Description, count(T1.Document_ID) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_with_Expenses AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Ref_Budget_Codes AS T3 ON T2.Budget_Type_Code = T3.Budget_Type_Code GROUP BY T3.Budget_Type_Description;

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T2.Document_Type_Name, T3.Project_Details, T5.Budget_Type_Description FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T1.Project_ID = T3.Project_ID JOIN Documents_with_Expenses AS T4 ON T1.Document_ID = T4.Document_ID JOIN Ref_Budget_Codes AS T5 ON T4.Budget_Type_Code = T5.Budget_Type_Code;

sql: SELECT T1.Project_Details FROM Projects AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Documents AS T2 WHERE T2.Project_ID = T1.Project_ID AND T2.Document_Type_Code = 'CV') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Documents AS T3 WHERE T3.Project_ID = T1.Project_ID AND T3.Document_Type_Code != 'CV');
