sql: SELECT T2.name, T1.move_in_year FROM Office_locations AS T1 JOIN Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each company's name alongside the year they moved into their office location.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.move_in_year FROM buildings AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.building_id;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing the building name and the move-in year for each office location.

sql: SELECT Industry, COUNT(*) FROM Companies GROUP BY Industry;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each industry along with the total count of companies within that industry.

sql: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM buildings GROUP BY City;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each city and the total count of buildings located within it.

sql: SELECT City, AVG(Height) FROM buildings GROUP BY City;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each city along with the average height of the buildings in that city.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN buildings AS T3 ON T2.building_id = T3.id WHERE T3.City = 'Mexico City';
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the names of companies that have offices located in Mexico City.

sql: SELECT T1.name, SUM(T3.Stories) FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN buildings AS T3 ON T2.building_id = T3.id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each company's name alongside the total number of building stories they occupy across all their office locations.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.move_in_year > 2021;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the names of companies that have moved into a new office location after the year 2021.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM buildings AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.building_id JOIN Companies AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Industry = 'Banking';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of buildings that house companies operating in the 'Banking' industry.

sql: SELECT AVG(Stories) FROM buildings WHERE Status = 'under construction';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average number of stories for all buildings currently under construction.

sql: SELECT move_in_year, COUNT(company_id) FROM Office_locations GROUP BY move_in_year;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each move-in year and the total number of companies that moved into an office during that year.

sql: SELECT T3.Industry, COUNT(T2.building_id) FROM Companies AS T3 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T3.id = T2.company_id GROUP BY T3.Industry;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each industry and the total count of office locations associated with that industry.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.City FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN buildings AS T3 ON T2.building_id = T3.id WHERE T1.Headquarters = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique cities where companies headquartered in the USA have office locations.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.move_in_year FROM buildings AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.building_id JOIN Companies AS T2 ON T3.company_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Headquarters = 'UK';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the building name and move-in year for offices belonging to companies that are headquartered in the UK.

sql: SELECT City FROM buildings ORDER BY Height DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city that contains the single tallest building in the dataset.

sql: SELECT name, Profits_billion FROM Companies WHERE Sales_billion > 150;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names and profits for companies with sales exceeding 150 billion.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM buildings AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.building_id JOIN Companies AS T2 ON T3.company_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Stories > 60;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the names of companies that have offices located in buildings taller than 60 stories.

sql: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM buildings GROUP BY City HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing cities that contain more than one building, along with the total count of buildings in each of those cities.

sql: SELECT name FROM buildings WHERE Height = (SELECT MAX(Height) FROM buildings);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the tallest building.

sql: SELECT Industry FROM Companies GROUP BY Industry HAVING AVG(Market_Value_billion) > (SELECT AVG(Market_Value_billion) FROM Companies);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the industries where the average company market value is greater than the overall average market value across all companies.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.building_id) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of companies that have at least one registered office location.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM buildings AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.building_id FROM Office_locations AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of buildings that have at least one office location within them.

sql: SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT T1.company_id FROM Office_locations AS T1 JOIN buildings AS T2 ON T1.building_id = T2.id WHERE T2.City = 'Monterrey');
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the names of companies that do not have an office location in the city of Monterrey.

sql: SELECT name, Profits_billion FROM Companies WHERE Industry = 'Banking' ORDER BY Profits_billion DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most profitable company within the 'Banking' industry, showing its name and profit in billions.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM buildings AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.building_id JOIN Companies AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Headquarters = 'USA' AND T1.Height > (SELECT AVG(T4.Height) FROM buildings AS T4 WHERE T4.City = 'Mexico City');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of buildings that house USA-headquartered companies and are taller than the average building height in Mexico City.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Office_locations AS T1 JOIN Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id WHERE T1.building_id IN (SELECT id FROM buildings WHERE City = 'Monterrey');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of companies that have an office located in the city of Monterrey.

sql: SELECT name FROM Companies UNION SELECT name FROM buildings;
result_summary: Returns 31 rows containing a combined list of unique names from both the Companies and buildings tables.

sql: SELECT T3.City FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN buildings AS T3 ON T2.building_id = T3.id GROUP BY T3.City ORDER BY COUNT(T1.id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city that contains the highest number of company offices.

sql: SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Headquarters = 'USA' EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of companies headquartered in the USA that do not have any recorded office locations.

sql: SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Industry = 'Banking' INTERSECT SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Headquarters = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of banking companies that are headquartered in the USA.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Height, T1.City FROM buildings AS T1 WHERE T1.Height = (SELECT MAX(T2.Height) FROM buildings AS T2 WHERE T2.City = T1.City);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the name, height, and city for the tallest building in each respective city.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Profits_billion FROM Companies AS T1 WHERE T1.Profits_billion > (SELECT AVG(T2.Profits_billion) FROM Companies AS T2 WHERE T2.Industry = T1.Industry);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names and profits of companies whose profits are greater than the average profit for their respective industry.

sql: SELECT Headquarters, SUM(Market_Value_billion) AS TotalMarketValue FROM Companies GROUP BY Headquarters ORDER BY TotalMarketValue DESC LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the top two headquarters locations with the highest total company market value, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM buildings AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.building_id WHERE T2.move_in_year IN (SELECT move_in_year FROM Office_locations GROUP BY move_in_year HAVING COUNT(*) = 1);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of buildings that were the sole office location to be moved into during a specific year.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Stories) FROM buildings AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.building_id JOIN Companies AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Sales_billion > 100;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of stories for buildings occupied by companies with sales greater than $100 billion.

sql: SELECT T3.name FROM buildings AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.building_id JOIN Companies AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T2.move_in_year < 2023 AND T1.Status = 'under construction';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of companies that planned to move into an office before 2023, but whose designated building is still under construction.

sql: SELECT name, Sales_billion / Assets_billion AS SalesToAssetRatio FROM Companies WHERE Assets_billion > 0 ORDER BY SalesToAssetRatio ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row for the company with the lowest sales-to-asset ratio, showing its name and that calculated ratio.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Industry FROM buildings AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.building_id JOIN Companies AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T1.Height > 200;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique industries of companies that have offices in buildings taller than 200.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id WHERE T1.Industry != 'Banking';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of non-banking companies that have a registered office location.

sql: SELECT T_Bank.name, T2.name FROM (SELECT id, name FROM Companies WHERE Industry = 'Banking') AS T_Bank JOIN Office_locations AS T1 ON T_Bank.id = T1.company_id JOIN buildings AS T2 ON T1.building_id = T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of banking companies and the names of the buildings where their offices are located.

sql: SELECT City FROM buildings WHERE Status = 'under construction' INTERSECT SELECT City FROM buildings WHERE Status = 'proposed';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the city that has buildings both currently under construction and proposed for future development.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Industry, T3.name, T3.City FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN buildings AS T3 ON T2.building_id = T3.id WHERE T2.move_in_year > 2020;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing companies that moved into an office after 2020, showing the company name, its industry, the name of the building, and the city of that new office location.

sql: SELECT color_code, color_description FROM Ref_Colors;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing all available colors, showing the specific code and text description for each.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_description FROM Products WHERE product_category_code = 'Spices';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the product name and description for all products categorized as 'Spices'.

sql: SELECT characteristic_name, characteristic_data_type FROM Characteristics ORDER BY characteristic_name;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each characteristic's name and its corresponding data type, sorted alphabetically by the characteristic's name.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Products;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total number of products.

sql: SELECT characteristic_type_description FROM Ref_Characteristic_Types WHERE characteristic_type_code = 'Grade';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the full description for the specific characteristic type code 'Grade'.

sql: SELECT product_category_description, unit_of_measure FROM Ref_Product_Categories;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each product category and its corresponding unit of measure.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.product_category_description FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Product_Categories AS T2 ON T1.product_category_code = T2.product_category_code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each product's name alongside its corresponding category description, retrieved by joining the products and product categories tables.

sql: SELECT characteristic_name FROM Characteristics WHERE characteristic_type_code = 'Purity';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all characteristics that are classified under the 'Purity' type.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE color_code = '9';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all products that have a color code of '9'.

sql: SELECT product_name, typical_selling_price FROM Products ORDER BY typical_selling_price DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each product's name and its typical selling price, ordered from the most expensive to the least expensive.

sql: SELECT T2.color_description, count(T1.product_id) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Colors AS T2 ON T1.color_code = T2.color_code GROUP BY T2.color_description;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each color description and the total count of products available in that color.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, count(T2.characteristic_id) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each product by name along with a count of its associated characteristics.

sql: SELECT product_category_code FROM Products GROUP BY product_category_code HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the product categories that contain more than one distinct product.

sql: SELECT T1.characteristic_name FROM Characteristics AS T1 JOIN Ref_Characteristic_Types AS T2 ON T1.characteristic_type_code = T2.characteristic_type_code WHERE T2.characteristic_type_description = 'Grade';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of all characteristics that are classified as a 'Grade'.

sql: SELECT T2.product_category_description, AVG(T1.typical_selling_price) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Product_Categories AS T2 ON T1.product_category_code = T2.product_category_code GROUP BY T2.product_category_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each product category description along with the average typical selling price for products within that category.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM Product_Characteristics WHERE product_characteristic_value = 'low');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all products that have a characteristic value of 'low'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.characteristic_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id WHERE T1.product_category_code = 'Herbs';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique characteristic names for all products that belong to the 'Herbs' category.

sql: SELECT T2.product_category_description, count(T1.product_id) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Product_Categories AS T2 ON T1.product_category_code = T2.product_category_code GROUP BY T2.product_category_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each product category description and the total count of products within that category.

sql: SELECT T1.characteristic_id FROM Product_Characteristics AS T1 GROUP BY T1.characteristic_id HAVING count(T1.product_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the IDs of product characteristics that are associated with more than one product.

sql: SELECT T3.characteristic_name, T4.characteristic_type_description FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id JOIN Ref_Characteristic_Types AS T4 ON T3.characteristic_type_code = T4.characteristic_type_code WHERE T1.product_name = 'peper';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.product_characteristic_value FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id WHERE T3.characteristic_name = 'fast';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing products with a 'fast' characteristic, showing each product's name and its specific value for that characteristic (e.g., 'low', 'medium').

sql: SELECT product_name, typical_buying_price FROM Products WHERE typical_buying_price IS NOT NULL AND typical_buying_price > 0;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing products that have a recorded, positive buying price, showing each product's name and its corresponding price.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT product_id FROM Product_Characteristics);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of all products that have at least one associated characteristic defined.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_category_code = 'Spices' AND color_code = '2';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the product classified as a 'Spice' with a color code of '2'.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.color_description FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Colors AS T2 ON T1.color_code = T2.color_code WHERE T2.color_description = 'yellow';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the product name and color description for all products that are 'yellow'.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 LEFT JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.characteristic_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of products that have no associated characteristics defined.

sql: SELECT characteristic_name FROM Characteristics WHERE characteristic_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT characteristic_id FROM Product_Characteristics);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of characteristics that are not currently assigned to any product.

sql: SELECT T1.product_id FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id WHERE T3.characteristic_type_code = 'Grade' EXCEPT SELECT T1.product_id FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id WHERE T3.characteristic_type_code = 'Purity';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the product IDs for all products that have a 'Grade' characteristic but do not have a 'Purity' characteristic.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.typical_selling_price FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.typical_selling_price > (SELECT AVG(T2.typical_selling_price) FROM Products AS T2 WHERE T2.product_category_code = T1.product_category_code);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and selling price for products that are priced higher than the average price within their own product category.

sql: SELECT T1.product_category_code, T1.avg_price FROM (SELECT product_category_code, AVG(typical_selling_price) AS avg_price FROM Products GROUP BY product_category_code) AS T1 ORDER BY T1.avg_price DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single product category with the highest average selling price.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_category_code = 'Spices' UNION SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Colors AS T2 ON T1.color_code = T2.color_code WHERE T2.color_description = 'yellow';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of all products that are either in the 'Spices' category or are yellow in color.

sql: SELECT T2.color_description FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Colors AS T2 ON T1.color_code = T2.color_code GROUP BY T2.color_description ORDER BY count(T1.product_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most common product color, determined by counting the number of products for each color and selecting the highest count.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name HAVING count(T2.characteristic_id) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of products that have at least two associated characteristics.

sql: SELECT T1.product_category_description FROM Ref_Product_Categories AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Products AS T2 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T2.product_category_code = T1.product_category_code AND T3.product_characteristic_value = 'low');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the descriptions for product categories that contain at least one product with a characteristic value of 'low'.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT T2.product_id FROM Characteristics AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.characteristic_id = T2.characteristic_id WHERE T1.characteristic_name = 'slow') AND product_id NOT IN (SELECT T2.product_id FROM Characteristics AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.characteristic_id = T2.characteristic_id WHERE T1.characteristic_name = 'fast');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of products that have the 'slow' characteristic but not the 'fast' characteristic.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.product_id, T2.product_id FROM Product_Characteristics AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.characteristic_id = T2.characteristic_id AND T1.product_id < T2.product_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing unique pairs of product IDs that share at least one common characteristic.

sql: SELECT product_characteristic_value, count(*) AS value_count FROM Product_Characteristics GROUP BY product_characteristic_value ORDER BY value_count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top 3 most common product characteristic values and their frequency, ordered from most frequent to least frequent.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id WHERE T3.characteristic_type_code IN ('Grade', 'Purity') GROUP BY T1.product_name HAVING count(DISTINCT T3.characteristic_type_code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the product that is characterized by both 'Grade' and 'Purity'.

sql: SELECT T4.characteristic_type_description, count(DISTINCT T1.product_id) AS product_count FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id JOIN Ref_Characteristic_Types AS T4 ON T3.characteristic_type_code = T4.characteristic_type_code GROUP BY T4.characteristic_type_description;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each product characteristic type description alongside a count of the unique products associated with that type.

sql: SELECT T3.characteristic_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id GROUP BY T3.characteristic_name HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.product_category_code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of product characteristics that are associated with products across more than one product category.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_category_code = 'Herbs' INTERSECT SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id JOIN Ref_Characteristic_Types AS T4 ON T3.characteristic_type_code = T4.characteristic_type_code WHERE T4.characteristic_type_description = 'Purity';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of products that are both categorized as 'Herbs' and have the 'Purity' characteristic.

sql: SELECT product_name, (typical_selling_price - typical_buying_price) AS profit_margin FROM Products WHERE product_category_code = 'Spices' AND typical_buying_price > 0 AND typical_selling_price > 0 ORDER BY profit_margin DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the most profitable products in the 'Spices' category, showing each product's name and calculated profit margin, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T3.characteristic_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id WHERE T1.product_category_code = 'Spices' GROUP BY T3.characteristic_name ORDER BY count(T1.product_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most common characteristic found among products in the 'Spices' category.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T4.characteristic_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Colors AS T2 ON T1.color_code = T2.color_code JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T3 ON T1.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T4 ON T3.characteristic_id = T4.characteristic_id WHERE T2.color_description = 'red';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and characteristic for each product that is described as 'red'.

sql: SELECT T2.product_characteristic_value FROM Characteristics AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.characteristic_id = T2.characteristic_id WHERE T1.characteristic_type_code = 'Grade' GROUP BY T2.product_characteristic_value ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most common 'Grade' value assigned to products, determined by frequency.

sql: SELECT T1.product_category_description, COUNT(DISTINCT T5.characteristic_type_code) AS distinct_char_types FROM Ref_Product_Categories AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_category_code = T2.product_category_code JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T4 ON T3.characteristic_id = T4.characteristic_id JOIN Ref_Characteristic_Types AS T5 ON T4.characteristic_type_code = T5.characteristic_type_code GROUP BY T1.product_category_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each product category and the corresponding count of unique characteristic types for the products within that category.

sql: SELECT T1.product_category_code FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.typical_buying_price > 0 AND T1.typical_selling_price > 0 GROUP BY T1.product_category_code HAVING AVG(T1.typical_buying_price) < AVG(T1.typical_selling_price);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the product category code for categories where, on average, the selling price is greater than the buying price.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name HAVING count(T2.characteristic_id) > (SELECT avg(char_count) FROM (SELECT count(characteristic_id) AS char_count FROM Product_Characteristics GROUP BY product_id) AS T3);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of products that have a higher-than-average number of associated characteristics.

sql: SELECT T1.product_category_code, T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_name IN (SELECT MAX(T2.product_name) FROM Products AS T2 GROUP BY T2.product_category_code);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each product category code alongside the product name that comes last alphabetically within that category.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Product_Categories AS T2 ON T1.product_category_code = T2.product_category_code JOIN Ref_Colors AS T3 ON T1.color_code = T3.color_code WHERE T2.product_category_description = 'Herbs' AND T3.color_description = 'red';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all red-colored products that are categorized as 'Herbs'.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_details FROM Product;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name and details for every product in the database.

sql: SELECT * FROM Staff WHERE staff_first_name = 'Lacey';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details for the staff member whose first name is 'Lacey'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT problem_category_description FROM Problem_Category_Codes;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique problem category descriptions available.

sql: SELECT * FROM Problem_Log WHERE problem_status_code = 'Solved';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows containing the complete log details for all problems that have been marked as 'Solved'.

sql: SELECT problem_id, problem_description FROM Problems WHERE date_problem_reported = '1995-05-14 08:32:56';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the problem ID and description for the specific problem reported at 08:32:56 on May 14, 1995.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT reported_by_staff_id FROM Problems;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, providing a unique list of the staff member IDs for every staff person who has reported a problem.

sql: SELECT * FROM Product ORDER BY product_name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing all available products with their corresponding ID and details, sorted alphabetically by product name.

sql: SELECT * FROM Problem_Log WHERE assigned_to_staff_id = 11;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the complete log entries for all problems assigned to the staff member with an ID of 11.

sql: SELECT problem_status_description FROM Problem_Status_Codes;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the descriptions for all available problem statuses.

sql: SELECT * FROM Problems WHERE problem_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete record and all associated details for the specific problem with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, COUNT(T1.problem_id) FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T2.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each product name along with the total count of problems reported for that product.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name, T2.problem_description FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.reported_by_staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each reported problem alongside the first and last name of the staff member who reported it.

sql: SELECT T2.problem_status_description, COUNT(T1.problem_log_id) FROM Problem_Log AS T1 JOIN Problem_Status_Codes AS T2 ON T1.problem_status_code = T2.problem_status_code GROUP BY T2.problem_status_description;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each problem status description along with the total number of problems recorded for that status.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Problem_Log AS T1 JOIN Problem_Category_Codes AS T2 ON T1.problem_category_code = T2.problem_category_code WHERE T2.problem_category_description = 'User Interface.';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows containing all details from the problem log for issues that are categorized as 'User Interface'.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.reported_by_staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id HAVING COUNT(T2.problem_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who have reported more than one problem.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Product WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM Problems);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of all products that have associated recorded problems.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id WHERE T2.problem_category_code = 'GUI';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique first and last names of staff members who have been assigned to handle problems in the 'GUI' category.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Problem_Log AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.problem_id = T2.problem_id JOIN Product AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_name = 'rose';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing all fields from the problem log table for issues specifically related to the 'rose' product.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.reported_by_staff_id UNION SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.closure_authorised_by_staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the first and last names of all unique staff members who have either reported a problem or authorized a problem's closure.

sql: SELECT * FROM Problems WHERE problem_id IN (SELECT problem_id FROM Problem_Log WHERE problem_status_code != 'Solved');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows containing all details for problems that are not yet marked as 'Solved' in the problem log.

sql: SELECT T2.problem_category_description, count(T1.problem_log_id) FROM Problem_Log AS T1 JOIN Problem_Category_Codes AS T2 ON T1.problem_category_code = T2.problem_category_code GROUP BY T2.problem_category_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each problem category description along with the total count of problems logged for that category.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.closure_authorised_by_staff_id WHERE T2.problem_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last name of the staff member who authorized the closure for the problem with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_description FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.problem_id = T2.problem_id WHERE T2.problem_status_code = 'Reported';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the descriptions for all problems that have a 'Reported' status.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_description, T2.staff_first_name, T2.staff_last_name FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.reported_by_staff_id = T2.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, each showing a problem description and the full name of the staff member who reported that problem.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_description, T3.problem_category_description FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.problem_id = T2.problem_id JOIN Problem_Category_Codes AS T3 ON T2.problem_category_code = T3.problem_category_code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the description of each logged problem alongside its associated category description.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.reported_by_staff_id EXCEPT SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who have reported a problem but have not been assigned to resolve any problems.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Product AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Problem_Log AS T3 ON T2.problem_id = T3.problem_id WHERE T3.problem_category_code = 'GUI' AND T3.problem_status_code = 'Solved' GROUP BY T1.product_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.problem_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of products that have had at least one solved problem related to their Graphical User Interface (GUI).

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.problem_category_code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who have been assigned problems from more than one distinct category.

sql: SELECT problem_id, julianday(date_problem_closed) - julianday(date_problem_reported) AS resolution_days FROM Problems WHERE date_problem_closed IS NOT NULL ORDER BY resolution_days DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the problem with the longest resolution time, showing its ID and the number of days from when it was reported to when it was closed.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.reported_by_staff_id INTERSECT SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.closure_authorised_by_staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who have both reported a problem and also authorized a problem for closure.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id WHERE T2.problem_category_code = 'Middleware' GROUP BY T1.staff_id ORDER BY COUNT(T2.problem_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last name of the staff member assigned to the most problems in the 'Middleware' category.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Product AS T1 WHERE T1.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM Problems) AND T1.product_id NOT IN (SELECT T2.product_id FROM Problems AS T2 JOIN Problem_Log AS T3 ON T2.problem_id = T3.problem_id JOIN Staff AS T4 ON T3.assigned_to_staff_id = T4.staff_id WHERE T4.staff_first_name = 'Lacey' AND T4.staff_last_name = 'Bosco');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of products that have reported problems, but none of which are assigned to staff member 'Lacey Bosco'.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.problem_id), COUNT(DISTINCT T3.assigned_to_staff_id) FROM Product AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Problem_Log AS T3 ON T2.problem_id = T3.problem_id GROUP BY T1.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, with each row corresponding to a product, detailing the total number of unique problems reported and the number of distinct staff members assigned to those problems.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.problem_status_code != 'Solved' THEN 1 END) = 0 AND COUNT(*) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who have successfully resolved all problems assigned to them.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_description FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.problem_id = T2.problem_id GROUP BY T1.problem_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.assigned_to_staff_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the descriptions of problems that have been assigned to more than one staff member.

sql: SELECT AVG(log_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(problem_log_id) AS log_count FROM Problem_Log GROUP BY problem_id) AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the average number of log entries per problem.

sql: SELECT staff_first_name, staff_last_name FROM Staff EXCEPT SELECT T3.staff_first_name, T3.staff_last_name FROM (SELECT T1.staff_id FROM Problems AS T1 UNION SELECT T2.assigned_to_staff_id FROM Problem_Log AS T2) AS T0 JOIN Staff AS T3 ON T0.staff_id = T3.staff_id;

sql: SELECT T2.problem_description FROM Problem_Log AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.problem_id = T2.problem_id GROUP BY T1.problem_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.problem_status_code) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Problem_Status_Codes);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the descriptions for problems that have experienced every possible status code defined in the system.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.reported_by_staff_id WHERE T2.problem_id = (SELECT problem_id FROM Problem_Log GROUP BY problem_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the staff member who reported the single most frequently logged problem.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.closure_authorised_by_staff_id) AS authorizer_count FROM Product AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.closure_authorised_by_staff_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY T1.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each product name along with a count of the unique staff members who have authorized problem closures for that product.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id WHERE T2.problem_category_code = 'GUI' INTERSECT SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id WHERE T2.problem_category_code = 'Middleware';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of the staff member who has been assigned to problems in both the 'GUI' and 'Middleware' categories.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name, COUNT(T2.problem_id) FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.reported_by_staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id HAVING COUNT(T2.problem_id) > (SELECT AVG(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(problem_id) as c FROM Problems GROUP BY reported_by_staff_id));
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying staff members who have reported a higher-than-average number of problems, listing their name and the total count of problems each has reported.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_description, T2.staff_first_name AS reporter_first, T2.staff_last_name AS reporter_last, T3.staff_first_name AS authorizer_first, T3.staff_last_name AS authorizer_last FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.reported_by_staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Staff AS T3 ON T1.closure_authorised_by_staff_id = T3.staff_id WHERE T1.product_id = 4;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing problems associated with product ID 4, including the problem description, the full name of the staff member who reported it, and the staff member who authorized its closure.

sql: SELECT T3.problem_category_description FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.problem_id = T2.problem_id JOIN Problem_Category_Codes AS T3 ON T2.problem_category_code = T3.problem_category_code GROUP BY T3.problem_category_description ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T1.product_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single problem category description that affects the highest number of unique products.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.problem_description FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.problem_id = T2.problem_id WHERE T1.closure_authorised_by_staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the unique description of a problem that was closed and authorized by the same staff member to whom it was assigned.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.reported_by_staff_id INTERSECT SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id INTERSECT SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.closure_authorised_by_staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who have performed all three of the following actions: reported a problem, been assigned to a problem, and authorized a problem's closure.

sql: SELECT problem_description FROM Problems WHERE problem_id IN (SELECT problem_id FROM Problem_Log) AND problem_id NOT IN (SELECT problem_id FROM Problem_Log WHERE problem_status_code = 'Solved');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the descriptions of all problems that have been logged but are not yet marked as 'Solved'.

sql: SELECT T3.problem_category_description, T4.staff_first_name, T4.staff_last_name, T2.max_cnt FROM (SELECT problem_category_code, assigned_to_staff_id, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM Problem_Log GROUP BY problem_category_code, assigned_to_staff_id) AS T1 JOIN (SELECT problem_category_code, MAX(cnt) as max_cnt FROM (SELECT problem_category_code, assigned_to_staff_id, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM Problem_Log GROUP BY problem_category_code, assigned_to_staff_id) GROUP BY problem_category_code) AS T2 ON T1.problem_category_code = T2.problem_category_code AND T1.cnt = T2.max_cnt JOIN Problem_Category_Codes AS T3 ON T1.problem_category_code = T3.problem_category_code JOIN Staff AS T4 ON T1.assigned_to_staff_id = T4.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying the staff member(s) who handled the most problems for each problem category, along with their name and the total count of problems they resolved.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id JOIN Problems AS T3 ON T2.problem_id = T3.problem_id JOIN Staff AS T4 ON T3.reported_by_staff_id = T4.staff_id WHERE T1.staff_last_name != T4.staff_last_name;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who were assigned to solve a problem reported by a staff member with a different last name.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_description, T4.staff_first_name, T4.staff_last_name FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Problem_Log AS T3 ON T1.problem_id = T3.problem_id JOIN Staff AS T4 ON T3.assigned_to_staff_id = T4.staff_id WHERE T2.product_name = 'chat' AND T3.problem_status_code = 'Solved';

sql: SELECT T1.Membership_card, AVG(T2.Total_amount) FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Membership_card;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each membership card type alongside the average happy hour spending for members holding that card.

sql: SELECT T1.Address, T1.Score FROM shop AS T1 WHERE T1.Shop_ID IN (SELECT T2.Shop_ID FROM happy_hour AS T2 WHERE T2.Month = 'May');

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Total_amount) FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT Month FROM happy_hour GROUP BY Month HAVING SUM(Num_of_shaff_in_charge) > 12;

sql: SELECT T1.Address FROM shop AS T1 JOIN happy_hour AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T1.Open_Year = '2008';

sql: SELECT Level_of_membership, AVG(Age) FROM member GROUP BY Level_of_membership;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID;

sql: SELECT T1.Shop_ID, T1.Num_of_staff FROM shop AS T1 JOIN happy_hour AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T1.Score >= 36.0;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the shop ID and number of staff for high-scoring shops (score of 36 or more) that offer a happy hour.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN happy_hour AS T3 ON T2.HH_ID = T3.HH_ID JOIN shop AS T4 ON T3.Shop_ID = T4.Shop_ID WHERE T4.Open_Year = '2010';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and age of members who attended a happy hour event held at a shop that opened in 2010.

sql: SELECT Address, Num_of_staff FROM shop WHERE Num_of_staff = (SELECT MAX(Num_of_staff) FROM shop);

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Total_amount > (SELECT AVG(Total_amount) FROM happy_hour_member);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of members for each happy hour transaction that exceeded the average transaction amount.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN happy_hour AS T3 ON T2.HH_ID = T3.HH_ID JOIN shop AS T4 ON T3.Shop_ID = T4.Shop_ID WHERE T4.Score = 42.0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of members who have attended happy hours at shops with a specific score of 42.0.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM happy_hour AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.HH_ID = T2.HH_ID JOIN member AS T3 ON T2.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID WHERE T1.Month = 'May' EXCEPT SELECT T3.Name FROM happy_hour AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.HH_ID = T2.HH_ID JOIN member AS T3 ON T2.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID WHERE T1.Month = 'April';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of a member who attended a happy hour event in May but did not attend one in April.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Total_amount) AS Total FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY Total DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of the top 3 members by their total amount spent during happy hours, ordered from the highest total amount to the lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Shop_ID, T1.Address FROM shop AS T1 JOIN happy_hour AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T2.Num_of_shaff_in_charge < T1.Num_of_staff;
