sql: SELECT 'Hartford' AS City, SUM(T2.Total_amount) FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Address = 'Hartford' UNION ALL SELECT 'Waterbury' AS City, SUM(T2.Total_amount) FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Address = 'Waterbury';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows calculating the total happy hour amount spent by members, aggregated separately for the cities of Hartford and Waterbury.

sql: SELECT T1.Level_of_membership, T5.Address, SUM(T3.Total_amount) FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T3 ON T1.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID JOIN happy_hour AS T4 ON T3.HH_ID = T4.HH_ID JOIN shop AS T5 ON T4.Shop_ID = T5.Shop_ID GROUP BY T1.Level_of_membership, T5.Address;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows detailing the total happy hour spending for each combination of shop address and customer membership level.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T1.Address FROM member AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.Address = T2.Address WHERE T1.Member_ID < T2.Member_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing distinct pairs of members who share the same address, showing the names of both members and their shared address.

sql: SELECT T1.Address FROM shop AS T1 JOIN happy_hour AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID GROUP BY T1.Shop_ID, T1.Address HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Month) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the addresses of shops whose happy hour events all occurred within a single, distinct month.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Level_of_membership = (SELECT MAX(Level_of_membership) FROM member);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.HH_ID, T1.Month, T3.Address, SUM(T2.Total_amount) FROM happy_hour AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.HH_ID = T2.HH_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T1.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID GROUP BY T1.HH_ID, T1.Month, T3.Address;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each happy hour event by its ID, month, and shop address, along with the total amount spent by members during that event.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Total_amount) FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Membership_card IN ('Black', 'White') GROUP BY T1.Member_ID, T1.Name HAVING SUM(T2.Total_amount) > 20.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and total spending amounts for members with 'Black' or 'White' cards who have spent more than $20.

sql: SELECT T1.Address FROM shop AS T1 JOIN happy_hour AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T1.Open_Year = '2010' AND T2.Num_of_shaff_in_charge > 10;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the addresses of shops that opened in 2010 and whose happy hours are managed by more than 10 staff members.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, providing a complete list of all customers with their full details including name, address, and contact information.

sql: SELECT * FROM Accounts WHERE other_account_details = 'VIP';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows containing all account details for accounts that are designated as 'VIP'.

sql: SELECT customer_first_name, customer_last_name FROM Customers ORDER BY customer_last_name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first and last name of each customer, ordered alphabetically by last name.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers_Cards WHERE card_type_code = 'Credit';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, providing the complete record for each customer card identified as a 'Credit' card.

sql: SELECT transaction_id, transaction_amount FROM Financial_Transactions ORDER BY transaction_amount DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the top 5 largest financial transactions, ordered from highest to lowest amount.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT transaction_type FROM Financial_Transactions;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique types of financial transactions recorded.

sql: SELECT * FROM Financial_Transactions WHERE transaction_type = 'Refund';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, providing all available details for every financial transaction that is classified as a 'Refund'.

sql: SELECT customer_id, customer_email FROM Customers WHERE customer_first_name = 'Aniyah';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the customer ID and email for any customer with the first name 'Aniyah'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Accounts WHERE customer_id = 6;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all account details for the customer with ID 6.

sql: SELECT card_number, card_type_code FROM Customers_Cards;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each customer's card number and its associated card type code.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, T2.account_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows matching each customer's first and last name with their associated account name.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, COUNT(T2.card_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows, listing each customer by their first and last name along with the total count of cards they have.

sql: SELECT transaction_type, SUM(transaction_amount) FROM Financial_Transactions GROUP BY transaction_type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each transaction type (e.g., 'Payment', 'Refund') and the total sum of all transaction amounts for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.card_type_code = 'Debit';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of all customers who have a debit card.

sql: SELECT other_account_details, COUNT(account_id) FROM Accounts GROUP BY other_account_details;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing each type of account detail along with the total count of accounts for that specific type.

sql: SELECT T1.account_name, SUM(T2.transaction_amount) FROM Accounts AS T1 JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id GROUP BY T1.account_name;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, listing each account name along with the total sum of its associated transaction amounts.

sql: SELECT T1.card_number, T2.customer_first_name, T2.customer_last_name FROM Customers_Cards AS T1 JOIN Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows that associate each card number with the first and last name of the customer who owns the card.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Financial_Transactions AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.card_id = T2.card_id WHERE T2.card_type_code = 'Credit';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing all details for financial transactions that were made using a credit card.

sql: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(account_id) FROM Accounts GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(account_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying customers who hold more than one account, along with the total number of accounts for each of those customers.

sql: SELECT T1.card_number, AVG(T2.transaction_amount) FROM Customers_Cards AS T1 JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T2 ON T1.card_id = T2.card_id GROUP BY T1.card_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each unique card number alongside the average transaction amount associated with that card.

sql: SELECT * FROM Financial_Transactions WHERE transaction_amount > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows containing complete details for all high-value financial transactions where the transaction amount exceeds 1000.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.other_account_details = 'VIP';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of all customers who have a 'VIP' account status.

sql: SELECT T3.* FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T1.customer_email = 'idickinson@example.com';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all financial transaction details for the customer with the email 'idickinson@example.com'.

sql: SELECT T1.card_type_code, COUNT(T2.transaction_id) FROM Customers_Cards AS T1 JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T2 ON T1.card_id = T2.card_id GROUP BY T1.card_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each card type (e.g., Credit, Debit) and the total number of financial transactions made with it.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE customer_last_name = 'Brekke';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details for customers with the last name 'Brekke'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.card_type_code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the ID, first name, and last name of the customer who holds more than one distinct type of card.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, SUM(T3.transaction_amount) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T2.other_account_details = 'VIP' GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last name of each VIP customer along with the total sum of their financial transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.transaction_type = 'Payment' EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.transaction_type = 'Refund';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the customer ID and first name of customers who have made a payment but have never received a refund.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, AVG(T3.transaction_amount) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING AVG(T3.transaction_amount) > (SELECT AVG(transaction_amount) FROM Financial_Transactions);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first names of customers whose average transaction amount is greater than the overall transaction average, along with their specific average transaction amount.

sql: SELECT T3.* FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T1.customer_id = (SELECT customer_id FROM Accounts GROUP BY customer_id ORDER BY COUNT(account_id) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing all financial transaction details for the single customer who holds the most bank accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T2.customer_id FROM Accounts AS T2 WHERE T2.account_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT account_id FROM Financial_Transactions));
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the full details for customers who have never made any financial transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, SUM(T3.transaction_amount) AS total_spent, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(T3.transaction_amount) DESC) AS customer_rank FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each customer by ID and first name, along with their total transaction amount and a corresponding spending rank based on who spent the most.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T3.transaction_id, T3.transaction_amount, T3.transaction_date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T2.other_account_details = 'VIP' AND T3.transaction_type = 'Payment';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing payment transactions made by VIP customers, showing the customer's first name, transaction ID, amount, and date for each payment.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.card_id = T3.card_id UNION SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the ID and first name of each unique customer who has made a financial transaction, combining results from both card and account transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, T3.transaction_amount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.transaction_amount = (SELECT MAX(transaction_amount) FROM Financial_Transactions);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the customer (by first and last name) who made the largest single financial transaction, along with the amount of that transaction.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.card_id) AS card_count FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.card_id = T3.card_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.card_id) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing customers who have used two or more distinct payment cards for transactions, showing their first name, last name, and a count of their unique cards.

sql: SELECT T1.card_type_code, COUNT(T2.transaction_id) AS number_of_transactions, SUM(T2.transaction_amount) AS total_amount FROM Customers_Cards AS T1 JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T2 ON T1.card_id = T2.card_id GROUP BY T1.card_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each card type along with its total number of transactions and the total sum of all transaction amounts.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, SUM(T3.transaction_amount) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING SUM(T3.transaction_amount) > 2000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying customers with a total transaction amount greater than $2000, listing each customer's first and last name along with their total transaction sum.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.other_account_details = 'Regular' EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_id FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.other_account_details = 'VIP';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows with the customer IDs for customers who have a 'Regular' account but do not have a 'VIP' account.

sql: SELECT T1.card_number, T2.customer_first_name, COUNT(T3.transaction_id) FROM Customers_Cards AS T1 JOIN Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T1.card_id = T3.card_id GROUP BY T1.card_id HAVING COUNT(T3.transaction_id) > 5;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.other_account_details = 'VIP' INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.card_type_code = 'Credit';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the customer ID and first name for customers who are both VIP account holders and also have a credit card.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, MAX(T3.transaction_date) AS latest_transaction FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each customer's first name and last name, along with the date of their most recent financial transaction.

sql: SELECT T1.account_id, T1.account_name FROM Accounts AS T1 JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id GROUP BY T1.account_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.transaction_type) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and name of accounts that have engaged in exactly two distinct types of financial transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Financial_Transactions AS T1 JOIN (SELECT account_id, MIN(transaction_date) AS min_date FROM Financial_Transactions GROUP BY account_id) AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id AND T1.transaction_date = T2.min_date;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, showing the full details for the first financial transaction recorded for each unique account.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, COUNT(T3.transaction_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.transaction_type = 'Payment' GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each customer's first and last name along with the total count of their 'Payment' transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T2.account_name, T3.transaction_id, T3.transaction_amount, T3.transaction_date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE date(T3.transaction_date) = '2018-03-24';
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing all financial transactions that occurred on March 24, 2018, providing the customer's first name, account name, transaction ID, amount, and the specific transaction date.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T2.card_type_code, SUM(T3.transaction_amount) AS total_amount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.card_id = T3.card_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T2.card_type_code ORDER BY T1.customer_first_name, T2.card_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the total transaction amount for each customer, broken down by card type, ordered alphabetically by the customer's first name and then by the card type code.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, SUM(T3.transaction_amount) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T1.customer_phone LIKE '%(673)%' GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, COUNT(T3.transaction_id) AS transaction_count FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id ORDER BY transaction_count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the ID and full name of the top three customers with the most financial transactions, ordered from highest to lowest transaction count.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, SUM(T3.transaction_amount) AS customer_total, (SUM(T3.transaction_amount) * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(transaction_amount) FROM Financial_Transactions)) AS percentage_of_total FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each customer's ID, their total transaction amount, and their contribution as a percentage of the total transaction volume across all customers.

sql: SELECT Player_name, Sponsor_name, Occupation FROM player WHERE Residence = 'Winnipeg';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name, sponsor, and occupation for all players who reside in 'Winnipeg'.

sql: SELECT Club_name, Start_year FROM club WHERE Region = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and start year for all clubs located in the 'USA' region.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM player;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of players.

sql: SELECT T1.Coach_name, T2.Club_name FROM coach AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T1.Club_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all coaches for the club with ID 1, along with the corresponding club name.

sql: SELECT Player_name, Votes FROM player ORDER BY Votes DESC;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing player names and their corresponding votes, ordered from the highest number of votes to the lowest.

sql: SELECT * FROM player WHERE Player_name = 'Jean Luc Bouché';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete profile information for the player named 'Jean Luc Bouché'.

sql: SELECT Club_ID, Points FROM match_result WHERE Gold = 20;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the Club ID and its points for the club that has achieved exactly 20 gold medals.

sql: SELECT Coach_name, Gender FROM coach WHERE Rank = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and gender of the top-ranked coach (Rank 1).

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name, T2.Starting_year FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID WHERE T2.Starting_year = 2010;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of players who began their coaching careers in the year 2010, along with that starting year.

sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Club_name) FROM club;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total count of distinct clubs.

sql: SELECT Occupation, COUNT(Player_ID) FROM player GROUP BY Occupation;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each occupation along with the total number of players who have that occupation.

sql: SELECT T1.Coach_name, T2.Club_name, T2.Region FROM coach AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each coach's name, along with the name and region of the club they are associated with.

sql: SELECT T1.Region, SUM(T2.Points) FROM club AS T1 JOIN match_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.Region;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each region along with the total points accumulated by all clubs from that region.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID JOIN club AS T4 ON T3.Club_ID = T4.Club_ID WHERE T4.Start_year = 2009;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of players associated with clubs that were founded in the year 2009.

sql: SELECT T1.Coach_name, COUNT(T2.Player_ID) FROM coach AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Coach_ID = T2.Coach_ID GROUP BY T1.Coach_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each coach's name along with the total number of players they coach.

sql: SELECT T2.Club_name, T1.Gold + T1.Big_Silver + T1.Small_Silver + T1.Bronze AS Total_Medals FROM match_result AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID ORDER BY Total_Medals DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each club's name alongside their total medal count (sum of gold, silver, and bronze medals), ordered from the highest total to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID WHERE T2.Coach_ID IN (SELECT Coach_ID FROM coach WHERE Club_ID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all players whose coaches belong to the club with ID 1.

sql: SELECT Player_name, 'Player' FROM player WHERE Gender = 'M' UNION SELECT Coach_name, 'Coach' FROM coach WHERE Gender = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows combining the names of all male players and female coaches, with a second column identifying their role as either 'Player' or 'Coach'.

sql: SELECT T1.Club_name, COUNT(T2.Coach_ID) FROM club AS T1 LEFT JOIN coach AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.Club_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each club's name along with the total count of coaches associated with that club.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name, T3.Coach_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID WHERE T1.Rank = '1st';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the player with a '1st' rank and the name of their coach.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name, T2.Starting_year FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID WHERE T2.Starting_year >= 2011;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of players who began coaching in or after 2011, along with their specific starting year.

sql: SELECT Residence, AVG(Votes) FROM player GROUP BY Residence;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each player residence along with the average number of votes received by players from that residence.

sql: SELECT T1.Club_name, T2.Points FROM club AS T1 JOIN match_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Points > 100;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and points for all clubs that have accumulated over 100 points.

sql: SELECT T1.Sponsor_name, T3.Coach_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID WHERE T1.Rank = '2nd';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the sponsor's name and coach's name for each player who has a rank of '2nd'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Gender, T3.Gender FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique gender combinations that exist between players and their coaches, showing the player's gender and the coach's gender for each pairing.

sql: SELECT T1.Coach_name, COUNT(T2.Player_ID) AS Player_Count FROM coach AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Coach_ID = T2.Coach_ID GROUP BY T1.Coach_name ORDER BY Player_Count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the coach who manages the most players, along with the total count of players they coach.

sql: SELECT Player_name, Votes FROM player WHERE Votes > (SELECT AVG(Votes) FROM player);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names and vote counts of players who received an above-average number of votes.

sql: SELECT T2.Club_name FROM coach AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.Club_ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Gender) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any club that employs coaches of different genders.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID WHERE T3.Club_ID IN (SELECT Club_ID FROM match_result WHERE Points > 150);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of players belonging to clubs that have earned more than 150 points.

sql: SELECT T1.Coach_name FROM coach AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Coach_ID = T2.Coach_ID JOIN player AS T3 ON T2.Player_ID = T3.Player_ID GROUP BY T1.Coach_ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Residence) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of coaches who manage players residing in at least two different locations.

sql: SELECT Region, Total_Points FROM (SELECT T1.Region, SUM(T2.Points) AS Total_Points FROM club AS T1 JOIN match_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.Region) ORDER BY Total_Points DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the region with the highest total points, calculated by summing the points from all clubs within that region.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID JOIN club AS T4 ON T3.Club_ID = T4.Club_ID WHERE T1.Occupation = 'Researcher' AND T4.Region = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the names of all players, excluding any player who is a 'Researcher' and coached by someone at a club in the 'USA' region.

sql: SELECT T1.Coach_name, T2.Coach_name, T3.Club_name FROM coach AS T1 JOIN coach AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID AND T1.Coach_ID < T2.Coach_ID JOIN club AS T3 ON T1.Club_ID = T3.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying all unique pairs of coaches who work for the same club, listing the two coach names and their shared club name.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name, T1.Gender AS Player_Gender, T3.Coach_name, T3.Gender AS Coach_Gender FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID WHERE T1.Gender <> T3.Gender;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying players who are coached by someone of a different gender, showing the name and gender for both the player and the coach.

sql: SELECT Player_name, Votes FROM player WHERE Votes > (SELECT MAX(Votes) FROM player WHERE Residence = 'Brandon');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows showing the names and vote counts for all players who received more votes than the top vote-getter from the 'Brandon' residence.

sql: WITH ClubPoints AS (SELECT Club_ID, Points FROM match_result WHERE Points > 100) SELECT T1.Player_name, T4.Club_name, T5.Points FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID JOIN club AS T4 ON T3.Club_ID = T4.Club_ID JOIN ClubPoints AS T5 ON T4.Club_ID = T5.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the player name, club name, and point total for all players belonging to clubs that have accumulated more than 100 points.

sql: SELECT T2.Club_name FROM coach AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.Club_ID HAVING AVG(T1.Rank) < (SELECT AVG(Rank) FROM coach);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the club where the average rank of its coaches is lower than the overall average rank across all coaches.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID WHERE T2.Coach_ID = (SELECT T4.Coach_ID FROM player AS T3 JOIN player_coach AS T4 ON T3.Player_ID = T4.Player_ID WHERE T3.Player_name = 'Fiona Shiells') AND T1.Player_name <> 'Fiona Shiells';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all players who share the same coach as 'Fiona Shiells', excluding Fiona herself from the list.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name, T1.Residence, T1.Votes FROM player AS T1 WHERE T1.Votes = (SELECT MAX(T2.Votes) FROM player AS T2 WHERE T2.Residence = T1.Residence);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying the player who received the most votes for each distinct residence, showing the player's name, their residence, and their vote count.

sql: SELECT T2.Club_name, CAST(T1.Points AS REAL) / T1.Gold AS Efficiency FROM match_result AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T1.Gold > 0 ORDER BY Efficiency DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows ranking clubs by their point-to-gold efficiency, from highest to lowest, for all clubs that have won at least one 'Gold'.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name, T1.Occupation, T2.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.Occupation = T2.Occupation AND T1.Player_ID < T2.Player_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying unique pairs of players who share the same occupation, showing both player names alongside their common occupation.

sql: SELECT T3.Club_name, T1.Coach_name, T1.Rank FROM coach AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Club_ID, MIN(Rank) AS MinRank FROM coach GROUP BY Club_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID AND T1.Rank = T2.MinRank JOIN club AS T3 ON T1.Club_ID = T3.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the highest-ranked coach for each club, listing the club's name, the coach's name, and their corresponding rank.

sql: SELECT Player_name FROM player WHERE Residence = 'Thompson' UNION SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID WHERE T3.Club_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all players who either reside in 'Thompson' or are coached by someone from Club ID 3.

sql: SELECT Coach_name FROM coach WHERE Coach_ID NOT IN (SELECT T1.Coach_ID FROM player_coach AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID WHERE T2.Gender = 'M');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of coaches who do not coach any male players.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID EXCEPT SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID JOIN club AS T4 ON T3.Club_ID = T4.Club_ID WHERE T4.Start_year = 1998;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of players who have a coach, but whose coach does not belong to a club that started in 1998.

sql: SELECT T1.Club_name, T2.Points, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY T2.Points DESC) as Rank_by_Points FROM club AS T1 JOIN match_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each club's name and total points, along with a calculated rank based on those points from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Club_name, T2.Points FROM club AS T1 JOIN match_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Points > (SELECT AVG(Points) FROM match_result);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the club name and points for match results that have a point total greater than the overall average.

sql: SELECT Player_name FROM player WHERE Occupation = 'Researcher' INTERSECT SELECT Player_name FROM player WHERE Rank = '1st';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the player who is both a 'Researcher' by occupation and holds the '1st' rank.

sql: SELECT T4.Region, SUM(T1.Votes) AS Total_Votes FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID JOIN club AS T4 ON T3.Club_ID = T4.Club_ID GROUP BY T4.Region;
result_summary: Returns 1 row that aggregates the total votes for all players, grouped by the region of their associated club.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name, T4.Club_name, T4.Start_year AS Club_Start, T2.Starting_year AS Player_Start FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID JOIN club AS T4 ON T3.Club_ID = T4.Club_ID WHERE T2.Starting_year = T4.Start_year + 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing players who began their coaching relationship exactly one year after their coach's club was founded, showing the player's name, the club's name, and the respective start years for the club and player.

sql: SELECT Name, County_ID FROM city WHERE Hispanic = 99.6;

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS County_Name, T2.Name AS City_Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.City_ID) FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID WHERE T1.Police_officers > 100;

sql: SELECT Name, Population FROM county_public_safety WHERE Population = (SELECT MAX(Population) FROM county_public_safety);

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Abbotsford';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, AVG(T2.White) FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT Location, MAX(Crime_rate) FROM county_public_safety GROUP BY Location;

sql: SELECT Police_force, SUM(Population) FROM county_public_safety GROUP BY Police_force;

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Population FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID;

sql: SELECT Name, Residents_per_officer FROM county_public_safety WHERE Residents_per_officer = (SELECT MIN(Residents_per_officer) FROM county_public_safety);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the county with the best police staffing (the lowest number of residents per officer), showing the county's name and that minimum ratio.

sql: SELECT Police_force, COUNT(County_ID) FROM county_public_safety GROUP BY Police_force;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each police force and the total number of counties they are responsible for.

sql: SELECT AVG(Hispanic) FROM city;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID WHERE T1.Case_burden >= 100;

sql: SELECT Name, Crime_rate FROM county_public_safety WHERE Crime_rate > (SELECT AVG(Crime_rate) FROM county_public_safety);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and crime rates of counties where the crime rate is higher than the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.City_ID) FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID GROUP BY T1.County_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.City_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each county that contains more than one city, along with the total count of cities within it.

sql: SELECT Name FROM county_public_safety WHERE County_ID IN (SELECT County_ID FROM city WHERE Hispanic = 99.6);

sql: SELECT Name FROM county_public_safety WHERE Police_force = 'RCMP' EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM county_public_safety WHERE Location = 'East';

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID WHERE T1.Population > (SELECT AVG(Population) FROM county_public_safety);

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.avg_asian_pop FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN (SELECT County_ID, AVG(Asian) AS avg_asian_pop FROM city GROUP BY County_ID) AS T3 ON T1.County_ID = T3.County_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each county's name alongside the average Asian population calculated from the cities within that county.

sql: SELECT Name, Case_burden FROM county_public_safety WHERE Case_burden > (SELECT Case_burden FROM county_public_safety WHERE Name = 'Abbotsford');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and case burden for all counties whose case burden is higher than that of 'Abbotsford'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING AVG(T2.Multiracial) > 7.0;

sql: SELECT Name FROM county_public_safety WHERE Location = 'West' UNION SELECT Name FROM county_public_safety WHERE Crime_rate > 120;

sql: SELECT Name FROM county_public_safety WHERE Location = 'West' INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM county_public_safety WHERE Police_officers > 35;

sql: SELECT Name, Population, Crime_rate FROM county_public_safety WHERE Population < (SELECT AVG(Population) FROM county_public_safety) AND Crime_rate > (SELECT AVG(Crime_rate) FROM county_public_safety);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying counties with below-average populations but above-average crime rates, listing each county's name, population, and crime rate.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 WHERE T1.Police_force = 'Abbotsford Police Department' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM city AS T2 WHERE T2.County_ID = T1.County_ID AND T2.Name = 'Aguada');
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the county public safety organization that uses the 'Abbotsford Police Department' and is located in the same county as the city 'Aguada'.

sql: SELECT Location, SUM(Population) AS Total_Population, SUM(Police_officers) AS Total_Officers, CAST(SUM(Police_officers) AS REAL) / SUM(Population) AS Officer_Ratio FROM county_public_safety GROUP BY Location;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the total population, total police officers, and the calculated officer-to-population ratio for each location.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM city AS T2 JOIN county_public_safety AS T1 ON T2.County_ID = T1.County_ID WHERE T1.County_ID NOT IN (SELECT County_ID FROM county_public_safety WHERE Police_force = 'RCMP');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of cities located in counties that are not policed by the 'RCMP'.

sql: SELECT Location, AVG(Police_officers) FROM county_public_safety GROUP BY Location HAVING AVG(Police_officers) > 50;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM city AS T2 JOIN (SELECT Name, County_ID FROM county_public_safety ORDER BY Police_officers DESC LIMIT 2) AS T1 ON T2.County_ID = T1.County_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the cities that belong to the two counties with the most police officers, showing the county name and the corresponding city name for each.

sql: SELECT Name, Residents_per_officer FROM county_public_safety WHERE Residents_per_officer > (SELECT AVG(Residents_per_officer) FROM county_public_safety);

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T2.Black FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID WHERE T2.Black BETWEEN 5 AND 8;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the county name, city name, and Black population percentage for cities where this demographic represents between 5% and 8% of the population.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, SUM(T1.Population * T2.Hispanic / 100) AS Estimated_Hispanic_Population FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID GROUP BY T1.Location;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the total estimated Hispanic population for each geographic location, calculated by aggregating county and city demographic data.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID WHERE T1.Population > 100000 AND T1.Case_burden > 90;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of cities that are in large-population counties (over 100,000 people) with a high public safety case burden (greater than 90).

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T2.Hispanic FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID WHERE T2.Hispanic = (SELECT MAX(T3.Hispanic) FROM city AS T3 WHERE T3.County_ID = T1.County_ID);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, each identifying the city with the highest Hispanic population percentage for a given county, displaying the county name, the city name, and that maximum percentage.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM city AS T2 WHERE T2.County_ID = T1.County_ID AND T2.Black > 5) AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM city AS T3 WHERE T3.County_ID = T1.County_ID AND T3.Asian > 0.1);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of county public safety organizations for counties that contain at least one city with a Black population greater than 5 and at least one city with an Asian population metric greater than 0.1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, MAX(T2.White) - MIN(T2.White) AS White_Pop_Range FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.City_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing counties that have more than one city, showing the range in the white population across the cities within each county.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Role FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID WHERE T3.Attendance = 165;

sql: SELECT T1.Host, T1.Date FROM performance AS T1 WHERE T1.Attendance > (SELECT AVG(T3.Attendance) FROM performance AS T3) AND T1.Performance_ID IN (SELECT T2.Performance_ID FROM member_attendance AS T2 GROUP BY T2.Performance_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Member_ID) >= 1);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the host and date for performances that had above-average attendance and were attended by at least one member.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Nationality FROM member AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM member_attendance AS T2 JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID WHERE T2.Member_ID = T1.Member_ID AND T3.Location = 'TD Garden');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and nationality of each member who has never attended a performance at 'TD Garden'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Location FROM member AS T1, member_attendance AS T2, performance AS T3 WHERE T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID AND T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID AND T2.Num_of_Pieces = (SELECT MAX(Num_of_Pieces) FROM member_attendance);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members who played the maximum number of pieces in a single performance, along with the location of that performance.

sql: SELECT T1.Nationality, AVG(T3.Attendance) AS Avg_Attendance_Per_Nationality FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID GROUP BY T1.Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each nationality alongside the average attendance of performances attended by members from that country.

sql: SELECT Performance_ID, Date, Host FROM (SELECT * FROM performance WHERE Attendance > 100) AS T1 WHERE T1.Host LIKE '%Rangers';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the performance ID, date, and host for performances with an attendance over 100 that were hosted by a team with 'Rangers' in its name.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Location) FROM member_attendance AS T2 JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID WHERE T2.Member_ID = T1.Member_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the member who has attended performances in more than one distinct location.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Performance_ID IN (SELECT Performance_ID FROM performance WHERE Location = 'Verizon Center') AND T1.Role = 'Violin';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the member whose role is 'Violin' and who attended a performance at the 'Verizon Center'.

sql: SELECT T1.Host FROM performance AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Performance_ID = T2.Performance_ID GROUP BY T1.Host HAVING SUM(T2.Num_of_Pieces) > (SELECT AVG(TotalPieces) FROM (SELECT SUM(Num_of_Pieces) AS TotalPieces FROM member_attendance GROUP BY Performance_ID) AS T_sub);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying hosts where the total number of musical pieces played across all their hosted performances exceeds the average number of pieces played per single performance.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.AvgPieces FROM member AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Member_ID, AVG(Num_of_Pieces) AS AvgPieces FROM member_attendance GROUP BY Member_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.AvgPieces > 2.5;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying members who average more than 2.5 pieces per attendance, showing each member's name and their calculated average.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Role FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM member_attendance WHERE Num_of_Pieces > 2) AND T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM member_attendance WHERE Performance_ID IN (SELECT Performance_ID FROM performance WHERE Attendance < 200));
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T3.Location, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Nationality) FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID GROUP BY T3.Location HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Nationality) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing performance locations that had attendees from more than one country, showing the location and the total count of distinct nationalities represented.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM member_attendance WHERE Performance_ID = 3) UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM member_attendance WHERE Performance_ID = 2);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of unique members who attended either performance 2 or performance 3.

sql: SELECT T1.Host, T1.Location FROM performance AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM member_attendance AS T2 WHERE T2.Performance_ID = T1.Performance_ID) = 0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the host and location for any performance that had zero member attendance.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Performance_ID) FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID WHERE T3.Attendance > 1000 GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing each member's name and a count of the highly-attended performances (over 1000 attendees) they were present at.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name, T1.Role FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Num_of_Pieces > (SELECT AVG(Num_of_Pieces) FROM member_attendance);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique name and role of members who have performed in a number of pieces greater than the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Role FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'Argentina' AND T3.Location = 'Verizon Center';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and role of any member with Argentinian nationality who attended a performance at the 'Verizon Center'.

sql: WITH MemberPieces AS (SELECT Member_ID, SUM(Num_of_Pieces) AS TotalPieces FROM member_attendance GROUP BY Member_ID) SELECT T1.Name, T2.TotalPieces FROM member AS T1 JOIN MemberPieces AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.TotalPieces > 3 ORDER BY T2.TotalPieces DESC;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members who have contributed more than 3 total pieces, ordered from the highest number of pieces to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT T2.Member_ID FROM member_attendance AS T2 GROUP BY T2.Member_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Performance_ID) = (SELECT MAX(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Performance_ID) AS c FROM member_attendance GROUP BY Member_ID) AS t));
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the member who has attended the most performances.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, T1.Host FROM performance AS T1 WHERE (SELECT SUM(T2.Num_of_Pieces) FROM member_attendance AS T2 WHERE T2.Performance_ID = T1.Performance_ID) > 5;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Location FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'Uruguay';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a member from Uruguay and the location of a performance they attended.

sql: SELECT T1.ClubName, T2.Position FROM Club AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.ClubID = T2.ClubID;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Position = 'Vice-President';

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T3.ClubName, T2.Position FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID, T1.Fname, T1.LName HAVING COUNT(T2.ClubID) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.ClubName FROM Club AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.ClubID = T2.ClubID GROUP BY T1.ClubName HAVING COUNT(T2.StuID) > 1;

sql: SELECT T3.ClubName, AVG(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID GROUP BY T3.ClubName;

sql: SELECT T1.ClubName FROM Club AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.ClubID = T2.ClubID GROUP BY T1.ClubName ORDER BY COUNT(T2.StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID WHERE T1.city_code = 'BAL' AND T3.ClubLocation = 'AKW';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students from city code 'BAL' who are members of a club located in 'AKW'.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.ClubID = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.StuID FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.ClubID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the student IDs for members of Club 1 who are not also members of Club 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Age FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID WHERE T3.ClubName = 'Bootup Baltimore' ORDER BY T1.Age DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the oldest student in the 'Bootup Baltimore' club, showing their first name and age.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student) GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of students who are members of a club and whose age is greater than the average age of all students.

sql: SELECT T3.ClubName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID GROUP BY T3.ClubName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.city_code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any club that has members from more than one city.

sql: SELECT T1.ClubName FROM Club AS T1 LEFT JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.ClubID = T2.ClubID WHERE T2.StuID IS NULL;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, COUNT(T2.ClubID) FROM Student AS T1 LEFT JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID, T1.Fname, T1.LName;

sql: SELECT T_Derived.ClubName FROM (SELECT T3.ClubName, AVG(T1.Age) AS avg_age FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID GROUP BY T3.ClubName) AS T_Derived WHERE T_Derived.avg_age > 19;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the club where the average age of its student members is greater than 19.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.Major = (SELECT Major FROM Student WHERE StuID = 1001) AND T1.StuID <> 1001;

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, T2.StuID, T1.ClubID FROM Member_of_club AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.ClubID = T2.ClubID AND T1.StuID < T2.StuID;

sql: SELECT T1.Major FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.Major ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T1.StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the major with the highest number of students who are members of a club.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID WHERE T3.ClubName = 'Hopkins Student Enterprises' AND T1.city_code <> 'HKG';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last name of a student who is a member of the 'Hopkins Student Enterprises' club and is not from the city with the code 'HKG'.

sql: SELECT T2.Position, AVG(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Position IN ('President', 'Vice-President', 'CTO') GROUP BY T2.Position;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the average student age for each key club leadership position ('President', 'Vice-President', 'CTO').

sql: SELECT T3.ClubName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID GROUP BY T3.ClubID, T3.ClubName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.city_code) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of clubs where all student members are from the same city.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.ClubID) AS NumberOfClubs FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.city_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each student city code along with a count of the unique clubs that students from that city belong to.

sql: SELECT T3.Fname, T3.LName, T1.ClubName FROM Club AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.ClubID = T2.ClubID JOIN Student AS T3 ON T2.StuID = T3.StuID WHERE T2.Position = 'President';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the student who is a club president by their first and last name, along with the name of the club they preside over.

sql: SELECT T1.ClubName, CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Member_of_club AS T2 WHERE T2.ClubID = T1.ClubID AND T2.StuID = 1002) THEN 'Is a Member' ELSE 'Not a Member' END AS MembershipStatus FROM Club AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing every club and a status indicating whether a specific student (ID 1002) is a member of that club.

sql: SELECT T1.ClubName, COUNT(T2.StuID) FROM Club AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.ClubID = T2.ClubID WHERE T1.ClubLocation = 'AKW' GROUP BY T1.ClubName;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each club located at 'AKW' along with its total number of members.

sql: SELECT Citizenship, COUNT(*) FROM singer GROUP BY Citizenship;

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM song AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID GROUP BY T2.Name ORDER BY COUNT(T1.Song_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM song AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID ORDER BY T1.Sales ASC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Sales) FROM song AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;

sql: SELECT T2.Name, SUM(T1.Sales) FROM song AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID WHERE T1.Highest_Position = 1.0 GROUP BY T2.Name;

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Song_ID) FROM song AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID WHERE T2.Net_Worth_Millions > 25.0 GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing singers with a net worth greater than 25 million and the total number of songs each singer has recorded.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM singer AS T2 JOIN song AS T1 ON T2.Singer_ID = T1.Singer_ID WHERE T2.Birth_Year < (SELECT MAX(Birth_Year) FROM singer) GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all singers, excluding any who were born in the most recent year found in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM song AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID WHERE T2.Citizenship = 'United States' GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING SUM(T1.Sales) > 300000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a singer from the United States whose total sales across all their songs exceed 300,000.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM song AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID WHERE T1.Sales = (SELECT MAX(Sales) FROM song);
