sql: SELECT T3.Location, AVG(T3.TotalQuantity) AS AvgStockPerShop FROM (SELECT T1.Location, T1.Shop_Name, SUM(T2.Quantity) AS TotalQuantity FROM shop AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID GROUP BY T1.Location, T1.Shop_Name) AS T3 GROUP BY T3.Location ORDER BY AvgStockPerShop DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the location that has the highest average total stock quantity per shop.

sql: SELECT ID, Name, District, Population FROM city WHERE CountryCode = 'AFG';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID, name, district, and population for each city located in Afghanistan.

sql: SELECT Name, Continent, Region FROM country WHERE Code = 'ABW';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name, continent, and region for the country identified by the code 'ABW'.

sql: SELECT Language, IsOfficial, Percentage FROM countrylanguage WHERE CountryCode = 'ABW';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the languages spoken in the country with code 'ABW', showing whether each is an official language and the percentage of the population that speaks it.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM country;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all countries in the database.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Continent FROM country ORDER BY Continent;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each unique continent, sorted in alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT Name, Population FROM city WHERE Population > 1000000;
result_summary: Returns 237 rows listing the name and population for all cities with more than one million inhabitants.

sql: SELECT Name FROM country WHERE Region = 'Caribbean';
result_summary: Returns 24 rows listing the names of all countries located in the Caribbean region.

sql: SELECT Name, Population FROM country ORDER BY Population DESC LIMIT 10;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names and populations of the 10 most populous countries, ordered from highest to lowest population.

sql: SELECT CountryCode, Language FROM countrylanguage WHERE IsOfficial = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 238 rows listing the official language for each country, identified by the country's code.

sql: SELECT ID, CountryCode, District FROM city WHERE Name = 'Herat';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID, country code, and district for the city named 'Herat'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.Name = 'Afghanistan';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the country name ('Afghanistan') alongside the names of its corresponding cities.

sql: SELECT CountryCode, COUNT(ID) FROM city GROUP BY CountryCode ORDER BY COUNT(ID) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 232 rows listing each country code and the total count of its cities, ordered from the country with the most cities to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Language FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.IsOfficial = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 238 rows listing each country's name alongside its officially recognized language.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Population) FROM city AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 232 rows listing each country's name along with the average population of its cities.

sql: SELECT Name, LifeExpectancy FROM country WHERE LifeExpectancy < (SELECT AVG(LifeExpectancy) FROM country WHERE LifeExpectancy IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 82 rows listing countries with a life expectancy below the global average, showing each country's name and its corresponding life expectancy.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Capital = T2.ID;
result_summary: Returns 232 rows listing each country's name alongside the name of its corresponding capital city.

sql: SELECT Language, COUNT(CountryCode) FROM countrylanguage GROUP BY Language HAVING COUNT(CountryCode) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 26 rows listing languages that are spoken in more than 5 countries, along with the total count of countries for each language.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.Continent = 'Asia';
result_summary: Returns 1766 rows listing each city in the continent of Asia along with its corresponding country name.

sql: SELECT Name, IndepYear FROM country WHERE IndepYear > 1970;
result_summary: Returns 54 rows listing the name and independence year for countries that became independent after 1970.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Population) FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.Continent = 'Africa' GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 57 rows listing each country in Africa along with the total population calculated by summing all of its respective cities.

sql: SELECT Name, Region FROM country WHERE GovernmentForm = 'Republic';
result_summary: Returns 122 rows listing the name and region of all countries that have a 'Republic' form of government.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language = 'English';
result_summary: Returns 60 rows listing the names of all countries where English is a spoken language.

sql: SELECT Name, GNP FROM country WHERE GNP > 6000;
result_summary: Returns 109 rows listing the name and Gross National Product (GNP) for each country with a GNP exceeding 6000.

sql: SELECT Language, Percentage FROM countrylanguage WHERE CountryCode = 'ABW' AND IsOfficial = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unofficial languages spoken in the country 'ABW' and the percentage of the population that speaks each one.

sql: SELECT Name FROM city WHERE CountryCode = 'AFG' UNION ALL SELECT Name FROM city WHERE CountryCode = 'NLD';
result_summary: Returns 32 rows listing the names of all cities located in either Afghanistan (AFG) or the Netherlands (NLD).

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Capital = T2.ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Angola';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the capital city for the country of 'Angola'.

sql: SELECT T1.Continent, T1.Name, T1.LifeExpectancy FROM country AS T1 WHERE T1.LifeExpectancy = (SELECT MAX(T2.LifeExpectancy) FROM country AS T2 WHERE T2.Continent = T1.Continent AND T2.LifeExpectancy IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY T1.Continent;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying the country with the highest life expectancy for each continent, listing the continent, country name, and that life expectancy, ordered alphabetically by continent.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Language) AS LanguageCount FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Language) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 69 rows listing countries where more than five languages are spoken, showing each country's name and its total language count.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Population FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language = 'English' AND T2.IsOfficial = 'T' AND T1.Population > 10000000;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names and populations of large countries (over 10 million people) where English is an official language.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, (SELECT T2.Name FROM city AS T2 WHERE T2.CountryCode = T1.Code ORDER BY T2.Population DESC LIMIT 1) AS MostPopulousCity FROM country AS T1 WHERE T1.Code = 'AFG';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the country with code 'AFG' (Afghanistan) and showing the name of its single most populous city.

sql: SELECT Name FROM country AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM city AS T2 WHERE T2.CountryCode = T1.Code);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of countries that do not have any cities recorded in the database.

sql: SELECT T2.Continent, SUM(T1.Population) AS TotalCityPopulation FROM city AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Continent;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each continent along with the total population of all its recorded cities.

sql: SELECT T2.Language FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.Continent = 'Africa' AND T2.IsOfficial = 'T' EXCEPT SELECT T4.Language FROM country AS T3 JOIN countrylanguage AS T4 ON T3.Code = T4.CountryCode WHERE T3.Continent = 'Europe';
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing the official languages spoken in African countries that are not spoken in any European country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Population AS CountryPop, T2.Name AS Capital, T2.Population AS CapitalPop FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Capital = T2.ID WHERE T2.Population > T1.Population * 0.2 AND T1.Population > 0;
result_summary: Returns 62 rows identifying countries where the capital city's population is more than 20% of the total country population, listing each country's name, population, capital name, and capital population.

sql: SELECT T1.GovernmentForm, COUNT(*) FROM country AS T1 WHERE T1.Continent IN (SELECT T2.Continent FROM country AS T2 WHERE T2.LifeExpectancy IS NOT NULL GROUP BY T2.Continent HAVING AVG(T2.LifeExpectancy) > 75) GROUP BY T1.GovernmentForm;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows showing a count of countries for each form of government, limited to countries on continents where the average life expectancy is greater than 75 years.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Continent FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language = 'Dutch' AND T1.Continent <> 'Europe';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and continent of countries outside of Europe where Dutch is a spoken language.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name, T4.NumOfficialLanguages FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T3 ON T1.Capital = T3.ID JOIN (SELECT CountryCode, COUNT(*) AS NumOfficialLanguages FROM countrylanguage WHERE IsOfficial = 'T' GROUP BY CountryCode) AS T4 ON T1.Code = T4.CountryCode;
result_summary: Returns 189 rows listing each country's name, its corresponding capital city name, and the total count of official languages spoken in that country.

sql: SELECT Language, COUNT(CountryCode) AS CountryCount FROM countrylanguage GROUP BY Language HAVING Language <> 'English' ORDER BY CountryCount DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the top 5 most widespread languages, excluding English, along with a count of the countries where each is spoken, ordered from most to least common.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T2.Population FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.Continent = 'Asia' AND T2.Population > 5000000;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the country name, city name, and population for each city in Asia with a population greater than 5 million.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language = 'Papiamento' INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM country WHERE Region = 'Caribbean';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of countries that are located in the Caribbean region and where Papiamento is a spoken language.

sql: SELECT T2.Continent, AVG(T1.Population) FROM city AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Continent HAVING COUNT(T1.ID) > 10;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows showing the average city population for each continent, filtered to include only those continents that have more than 10 cities in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.GovernmentForm = 'Republic' AND T1.Continent = 'Africa' AND T2.Language = 'English' AND T2.IsOfficial = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of African countries that are republics and have English as an official language.

sql: SELECT Name, Population, SurfaceArea, Population / SurfaceArea AS PopulationDensity FROM country WHERE SurfaceArea > 0 AND Population / SurfaceArea > 1000 ORDER BY PopulationDensity DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing countries with a population density greater than 1000, showing each country's name, population, surface area, and calculated density, ordered from most to least dense.

sql: SELECT Language, Percentage FROM countrylanguage WHERE CountryCode = 'ABW' AND Percentage = (SELECT MAX(Percentage) FROM countrylanguage WHERE CountryCode = 'ABW');
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most spoken language and its percentage of speakers for the country with code 'ABW'.

sql: SELECT Name, GNP, GNPOld FROM country WHERE GNPOld IS NOT NULL AND GNPOld > GNP;
result_summary: Returns 63 rows listing countries whose Gross National Product (GNP) has decreased, showing the country's name along with its current and previous GNP values.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Population, T2.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Capital = T2.ID WHERE T1.Continent = 'North America' ORDER BY T1.Population DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name, population, and capital city for the three most populous countries in North America, ordered from highest to lowest population.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Capital = T2.ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Kabul';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the country for which 'Kabul' serves as the capital city.

sql: WITH AsiaOfficialLangs AS (SELECT DISTINCT T2.Language FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.Continent = 'Asia' AND T2.IsOfficial = 'T') SELECT T1.Language FROM countrylanguage AS T1 WHERE T1.Language IN (SELECT Language FROM AsiaOfficialLangs) GROUP BY T1.Language HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.CountryCode) > 3;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the official languages of Asian countries that are also spoken in more than three countries globally.

sql: SELECT Name, GovernmentForm FROM country WHERE HeadOfState = 'Beatrix' AND GovernmentForm <> 'Constitutional Monarchy';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and government form for countries led by 'Beatrix' that are not constitutional monarchies.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T3.Language FROM country AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Continent = T2.Continent AND T1.Code < T2.Code JOIN countrylanguage AS T3 ON T1.Code = T3.CountryCode JOIN countrylanguage AS T4 ON T2.Code = T4.CountryCode AND T3.Language = T4.Language LIMIT 10;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows identifying pairs of distinct countries on the same continent that share a common language, listing the two country names and their shared language.

sql: SELECT grant_id, grant_amount FROM Grants ORDER BY grant_amount DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each grant's ID and its corresponding amount, ordered from the highest grant amount to the lowest.

sql: SELECT organisation_details FROM Organisations WHERE organisation_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the details for the specific organization with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT * FROM Document_Types;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all available document types, showing the document type code and its corresponding description.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Projects;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all projects.

sql: SELECT staff_id, employer_organisation_id FROM Research_Staff;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, listing each research staff member's ID and the corresponding ID of their employer organization.

sql: SELECT T1.project_details, T2.organisation_details FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Organisations AS T2 ON T1.organisation_id = T2.organisation_id WHERE T1.organisation_id = 15;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the project details and organization details for the specific organization with an ID of 15.

sql: SELECT * FROM Documents WHERE document_type_code = 'APP';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows containing all details for documents classified as applications (document type 'APP').

sql: SELECT role_code, role_description FROM Staff_Roles;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each available staff role's code and its corresponding description.

sql: SELECT grant_id, grant_amount FROM Grants WHERE grant_amount > 4000;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the ID and amount for all grants with a value greater than $4,000.

sql: SELECT T1.project_id, T2.outcome_description FROM Project_Outcomes AS T1 JOIN Research_Outcomes AS T2 ON T1.outcome_code = T2.outcome_code WHERE T1.outcome_code = 'Paper';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the project IDs and outcome descriptions for all projects that resulted in a 'Paper'.

sql: SELECT T2.organisation_details, COUNT(T1.project_id) FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Organisations AS T2 ON T1.organisation_id = T2.organisation_id GROUP BY T2.organisation_details;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each organization's details along with the total count of projects associated with that organization.

sql: SELECT T2.organisation_details, SUM(T1.grant_amount) FROM Grants AS T1 JOIN Organisations AS T2 ON T1.organisation_id = T2.organisation_id GROUP BY T2.organisation_details;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each organisation's details along with the total grant amount it has received.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_id, T2.project_details FROM Project_Staff AS T1 JOIN Projects AS T2 ON T1.project_id = T2.project_id WHERE T1.role_code = 'leader';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the staff ID of each project leader alongside the details of the project they are assigned to.

sql: SELECT T1.outcome_code, COUNT(T1.project_id) FROM Project_Outcomes AS T1 GROUP BY T1.outcome_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total count of projects for each distinct outcome code.

sql: SELECT T1.grant_id FROM Grants AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.grant_id = T2.grant_id WHERE T2.document_type_code = 'APP';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the grant IDs for grants that have an associated document of type 'APP' (application).

sql: SELECT T1.project_details FROM Projects AS T1 WHERE T1.project_id IN (SELECT project_id FROM Project_Outcomes WHERE outcome_code = 'Patent');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the project details for all projects that resulted in a patent.

sql: SELECT T1.grant_id, COUNT(T2.document_id) FROM Grants AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.grant_id = T2.grant_id GROUP BY T1.grant_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, listing each grant ID along with the total count of documents associated with that grant.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_details FROM Research_Staff AS T1 JOIN Project_Staff AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.project_id = 2;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.project_details, T2.role_description FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Project_Staff AS T3 ON T1.project_id = T3.project_id JOIN Staff_Roles AS T2 ON T3.role_code = T2.role_code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the details for each project and the corresponding description of every staff role assigned to it.

sql: SELECT T2.organisation_details FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Organisations AS T2 ON T1.organisation_id = T2.organisation_id WHERE T2.organisation_type = 'RES';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the details for all organizations of type 'RES' that are associated with a project.

sql: SELECT AVG(grant_amount) FROM Grants;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average grant amount calculated across all grants in the table.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_id FROM Project_Staff AS T1 WHERE T1.role_code = 'leader' INTERSECT SELECT T2.staff_id FROM Project_Staff AS T2 WHERE T2.role_code = 'researcher';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.project_id, T1.project_details FROM Projects AS T1 WHERE T1.organisation_id IN (SELECT organisation_id FROM Organisations WHERE organisation_type = 'RES');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the project ID and project details for all projects that belong to a research ('RES') type organization.

sql: SELECT T1.grant_id, T1.grant_amount FROM Grants AS T1 WHERE T1.organisation_id = 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and amount for each grant belonging to the organization with an ID of 10.

sql: SELECT T1.project_id, T2.task_details FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Tasks AS T2 ON T1.project_id = T2.project_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, listing the project ID and the details for each task associated with that project.

sql: SELECT T1.grant_id FROM Grants AS T1 WHERE T1.grant_amount > (SELECT AVG(grant_amount) FROM Grants);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the IDs for grants with an amount greater than the overall average grant amount.

sql: SELECT T1.organisation_id FROM Organisations AS T1 WHERE T1.organisation_id IN (SELECT organisation_id FROM Projects) AND T1.organisation_id IN (SELECT organisation_id FROM Grants);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the IDs of organizations that are involved in both projects and grants.

sql: SELECT T1.project_id FROM Project_Staff AS T1 GROUP BY T1.project_id HAVING COUNT(T1.staff_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the project IDs for all projects that are staffed by more than one person.

sql: SELECT T3.staff_details FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Project_Staff AS T2 ON T1.project_id = T2.project_id JOIN Research_Staff AS T3 ON T2.staff_id = T3.staff_id WHERE T1.organisation_id != T3.employer_organisation_id;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the details for a research staff member who is working on a project for an organization that is not their own employer.

sql: SELECT T1.organisation_id FROM Organisations AS T1 WHERE T1.organisation_id IN (SELECT organisation_id FROM Projects) EXCEPT SELECT T2.organisation_id FROM Organisations AS T2 WHERE T2.organisation_id IN (SELECT organisation_id FROM Grants);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the IDs for organizations that have one or more projects but do not have any grants.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_id, T3.project_details FROM Project_Staff AS T1 JOIN Staff_Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T1.project_id = T3.project_id WHERE T2.role_code = 'leader' AND T3.project_id IN (SELECT project_id FROM Project_Outcomes WHERE outcome_code = 'Paper');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.organisation_type, AVG(T2.grant_amount) FROM Organisation_Types AS T1 JOIN Organisations AS T3 ON T1.organisation_type = T3.organisation_type JOIN Grants AS T2 ON T3.organisation_id = T2.organisation_id GROUP BY T1.organisation_type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each organization type alongside the average grant amount awarded to organizations of that type.

sql: SELECT T1.project_id FROM Project_Outcomes AS T1 WHERE T1.outcome_code = 'Paper' INTERSECT SELECT T2.project_id FROM Project_Outcomes AS T2 WHERE T2.outcome_code = 'Patent';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the project IDs for projects that have resulted in both a 'Paper' and a 'Patent' as outcomes.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_id FROM Project_Staff AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Project_Outcomes AS T2 WHERE T2.project_id = T1.project_id AND T2.outcome_code = 'Patent');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the staff IDs for all personnel who have worked on projects that resulted in a patent.

sql: SELECT T1.organisation_id, T1.organisation_details FROM Organisations AS T1 JOIN (SELECT organisation_id, COUNT(*) AS grant_count FROM Grants GROUP BY organisation_id) AS T2 ON T1.organisation_id = T2.organisation_id ORDER BY T2.grant_count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the ID and details for the top three organizations, ranked by the total number of grants they have received.

sql: SELECT T1.project_id FROM Projects AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Project_Staff WHERE project_id = T1.project_id AND role_code = 'leader') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Project_Staff WHERE project_id = T1.project_id AND role_code = 'researcher');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the project IDs for all projects that are managed by a 'leader' but have no assigned 'researcher'.

sql: SELECT T3.organisation_details FROM Grants AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.grant_id = T2.grant_id JOIN Organisations AS T3 ON T1.organisation_id = T3.organisation_id GROUP BY T3.organisation_id HAVING COUNT(T2.document_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the details for organisations that have submitted more than one document across all their grants.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_id, T1.staff_details FROM Research_Staff AS T1 JOIN Project_Staff AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id, T1.staff_details HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.project_id) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.organisation_id, T1.organisation_details FROM Organisations AS T1 WHERE T1.organisation_id NOT IN (SELECT organisation_id FROM Projects);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the ID and details for organizations that have no associated projects.

sql: SELECT T2.role_description, COUNT(T1.project_id) FROM Project_Staff AS T1 JOIN Staff_Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code GROUP BY T2.role_description HAVING COUNT(T1.project_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing staff roles that are assigned to more than one project, along with a count of the total projects for each of those roles.

sql: SELECT T1.organisation_id, AVG(T2.grant_amount) AS avg_grant FROM Organisations AS T1 JOIN Grants AS T2 ON T1.organisation_id = T2.organisation_id GROUP BY T1.organisation_id HAVING AVG(T2.grant_amount) > (SELECT AVG(grant_amount) FROM Grants);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying organizations whose average grant amount is greater than the overall average across all grants, listing each qualifying organization's ID and their specific average grant.

sql: SELECT T1.grant_id FROM Grants AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.grant_id = T2.grant_id WHERE T2.sent_date > T2.response_received_date;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the grant IDs for which a response to a document was received before the document was officially sent.

sql: SELECT T3.staff_id FROM Organisations AS T1 JOIN Projects AS T2 ON T1.organisation_id = T2.organisation_id JOIN Project_Staff AS T3 ON T2.project_id = T3.project_id WHERE T1.organisation_type = 'RES' AND T3.role_code = 'leader';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the staff ID for a project leader working at a research-type ('RES') organization.

sql: SELECT T1.project_id FROM Tasks AS T1 GROUP BY T1.project_id HAVING COUNT(T1.task_id) > 0 UNION SELECT T2.project_id FROM Project_Outcomes AS T2 GROUP BY T2.project_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the unique IDs for all projects that either have at least one task or have at least one recorded outcome.

sql: SELECT staff_id FROM Project_Staff WHERE role_code = 'leader' EXCEPT SELECT staff_id FROM Project_Staff WHERE role_code = 'researcher';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows containing the staff IDs for individuals who are project leaders but are not also designated as researchers.

sql: SELECT T1.project_id, T1.project_details FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN (SELECT project_id, MAX(date_to) as latest_date FROM Project_Staff GROUP BY project_id) AS T2 ON T1.project_id = T2.project_id WHERE T2.latest_date < '2000-01-01';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows containing the project ID and details for all projects where staff assignments concluded before the year 2000.

sql: SELECT T1.organisation_id, T3.document_type_code, COUNT(T1.grant_id) FROM Grants AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.grant_id = T2.grant_id JOIN Document_Types AS T3 ON T2.document_type_code = T3.document_type_code GROUP BY T1.organisation_id, T3.document_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the total count of grants for each organization, broken down by document type code.

sql: SELECT T3.staff_id FROM Grants AS T1 JOIN Projects AS T2 ON T1.organisation_id = T2.organisation_id JOIN Project_Staff AS T3 ON T2.project_id = T3.project_id WHERE T1.grant_amount > 4000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the staff ID of a staff member who is working on a project for an organization that has received a grant amount greater than 4000.

sql: SELECT T1.project_id, T1.project_details FROM Projects AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Project_Outcomes AS T2 WHERE T2.project_id = T1.project_id) = 0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the project ID and details for all projects that have no recorded outcomes.

sql: SELECT T1.organisation_id FROM Organisations AS T1 JOIN Research_Staff AS T2 ON T1.organisation_id = T2.employer_organisation_id INTERSECT SELECT T3.organisation_id FROM Organisations AS T3 JOIN Projects AS T4 ON T3.organisation_id = T4.organisation_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the IDs for organisations that both employ research staff and are associated with projects.

sql: SELECT * FROM employee;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing all employees and their corresponding ID, name, age, and city.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM employee WHERE City = 'Bristol';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age for all employees from Bristol.

sql: SELECT Name, Location FROM shop;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, providing a complete list of all shops and their corresponding locations.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM employee ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and age of each employee, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT * FROM hiring WHERE Is_full_time = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing all hiring details (Shop ID, Employee ID, start date, and full-time status) for employees who were hired on a full-time basis.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM shop;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all shops.

sql: SELECT Employee_ID, Bonus FROM evaluation WHERE Year_awarded = '2011';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the employee ID and bonus amount for awards given in the year 2011.

sql: SELECT Manager_name FROM shop WHERE Shop_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the manager for the specific shop with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT City FROM employee;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique cities where employees are located.

sql: SELECT AVG(Bonus) FROM evaluation;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average bonus calculated across all evaluations.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Start_from = '2003';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the employee who started working in the year 2003.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID JOIN employee AS T3 ON T2.Employee_ID = T3.Employee_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each employee's name and the name of the shop where they are employed.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Employee_ID) FROM hiring AS T1 JOIN shop AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each shop by name and the total count of employees hired for that shop.

sql: SELECT Employee_ID, SUM(Bonus) FROM evaluation GROUP BY Employee_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows showing each employee's ID and the total sum of their bonuses.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN evaluation AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of employees who have received an evaluation.

sql: SELECT City, AVG(Age) FROM employee GROUP BY City;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each city along with the average age of employees for that city.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID WHERE T1.Age > 30 AND T3.Location = 'Helsinki';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM evaluation AS T1 JOIN employee AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Employee_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any employee who has received more than one performance evaluation.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T2.Is_full_time = 'T' ORDER BY T1.Number_products DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of shops that hire full-time employees, ordered in descending order by the number of products each shop has.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Start_from FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T3 ON T1.Employee_ID = T3.Employee_ID JOIN shop AS T2 ON T3.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'FC Haka';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the name and start date of each employee working for the 'FC Haka' shop.

sql: SELECT Name FROM employee WHERE Employee_ID NOT IN (SELECT Employee_ID FROM evaluation);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of employees who have not yet received an evaluation.

sql: SELECT Year_awarded, MAX(Bonus) FROM evaluation GROUP BY Year_awarded;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each award year along with the maximum bonus paid out in that year.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Manager_name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T2.Start_from >= '2009';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows containing the unique names of managers whose shops have hired personnel on or after the year 2009.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Is_full_time = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all employees who are marked as full-time.

sql: SELECT Name FROM shop WHERE Number_products > 5000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of large-scale shops that carry more than 5,000 products.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN evaluation AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Bonus > (SELECT AVG(Bonus) FROM evaluation);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the employee who received a bonus greater than the overall average bonus across all evaluations.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Shop_ID, COUNT(Employee_ID) AS Emp_Count FROM hiring GROUP BY Shop_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID ORDER BY T2.Emp_Count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the shop that has the most employees.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID WHERE T3.Manager_name = 'Antti Muurinen' AND T1.Employee_ID IN (SELECT Employee_ID FROM evaluation);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age, T1.City FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN evaluation AS T3 ON T1.Employee_ID = T3.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Start_from < '2010' AND T3.Year_awarded > '2010';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name, age, and city of employees who were hired before 2010 and subsequently received an award after 2010.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID WHERE T3.Number_products < 10000 AND T1.Employee_ID IN (SELECT Employee_ID FROM evaluation);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of evaluated employees who work at shops with fewer than 10,000 products.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID WHERE T3.Location = 'Valkeakoski' UNION SELECT Name FROM employee WHERE Age > 40;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all employees who either work at a shop in 'Valkeakoski' or are older than 40.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID WHERE T1.City = 'Bristol' AND T3.Name != 'FC Haka';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of employees from Bristol who do not work at the 'FC Haka' shop.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 WHERE T1.City = 'Bristol' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Name FROM employee AS T2 JOIN evaluation AS T3 ON T2.Employee_ID = T3.Employee_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of employees who are based in Bristol and have also received an evaluation.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Start_from = '2003' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN evaluation AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Year_awarded = '2011';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an employee who was hired in 2003 but did not receive an award in 2011.

sql: SELECT T3.Manager_name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID GROUP BY T3.Manager_name HAVING AVG(T1.Age) > 25;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of managers for whom the average age of their employees is greater than 25.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN evaluation AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY SUM(T2.Bonus) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the employee who has received the highest total bonus amount across all their evaluations.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN evaluation AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING MAX(T2.Bonus) > 3000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of employees whose highest recorded bonus from any single evaluation is greater than 3000.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM hiring AS T1 JOIN shop AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T1.Employee_ID = (SELECT Employee_ID FROM employee ORDER BY Age DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the shop where the oldest employee works.

sql: SELECT T3.Manager_name, COUNT(T4.Employee_ID) FROM shop AS T3 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T3.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID JOIN employee AS T1 ON T2.Employee_ID = T1.Employee_ID LEFT JOIN evaluation AS T4 ON T1.Employee_ID = T4.Employee_ID GROUP BY T3.Manager_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each shop manager and the total number of performance evaluations completed for the employees under their supervision.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM evaluation AS T3 WHERE T3.Employee_ID = T1.Employee_ID);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of employees who have not yet received an evaluation.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Number_products) FROM shop AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID JOIN employee AS T3 ON T2.Employee_ID = T3.Employee_ID WHERE T3.City = 'Bristol';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average number of products for shops that employ people from the city of Bristol.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name FROM employee AS T1 LEFT JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID LEFT JOIN shop AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each employee's name and the corresponding name of the shop they are assigned to.

sql: SELECT SUM(T3.Bonus) FROM shop AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID JOIN evaluation AS T3 ON T2.Employee_ID = T3.Employee_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'Helsinki';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the total bonus amount for all employees hired for shops located in Helsinki.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Employee_ID) > (SELECT AVG(Emp_Count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Employee_ID) AS Emp_Count FROM hiring GROUP BY Shop_ID));
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the shop that has more employees than the average number of employees across all shops.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM employee AS T1 WHERE T1.Age > (SELECT AVG(T2.Age) FROM employee AS T2 WHERE T2.City = T1.City);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age of employees who are older than the average age for their respective city.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN evaluation AS T3 ON T1.Employee_ID = T3.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Is_full_time = 'T' AND T3.Bonus = 3000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a full-time employee who received a bonus of 3000.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN evaluation AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID ORDER BY T2.Year_awarded ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the employee who received the earliest recorded evaluation award.

sql: SELECT SUM(T3.Number_products) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID WHERE T1.City = 'Bath';
result_summary: Returns a single value representing the total number of products across all shops that have hired employees from the city of Bath.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Start_from != (SELECT MIN(Year_awarded) FROM evaluation AS T3 WHERE T3.Employee_ID = T1.Employee_ID);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of employees whose first award was not received in the same year they started their job.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Employee_ID), COALESCE(SUM(T4.Bonus), 0) FROM shop AS T1 LEFT JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID LEFT JOIN evaluation AS T4 ON T2.Employee_ID = T4.Employee_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each shop's name alongside a count of its unique employees and the total sum of bonuses paid to those employees.

sql: SELECT * FROM Movie;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, providing a complete list of all movies with their corresponding ID, title, release year, and director.

sql: SELECT name FROM Reviewer;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of all reviewers in the database.

sql: SELECT title, year FROM Movie ORDER BY year ASC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the title and release year of each movie, ordered chronologically from the oldest to the newest.

sql: SELECT title FROM Movie WHERE director = 'George Lucas';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the title of the movie directed by 'George Lucas'.

sql: SELECT name FROM Reviewer WHERE rID = 201;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the reviewer with the specific ID of 201.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT stars FROM Rating;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each unique star rating value that exists in the ratings data.

sql: SELECT mID FROM Rating WHERE rID = 201;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the movie IDs (`mID`) for all ratings submitted by the reviewer with ID 201.

sql: SELECT title, director FROM Movie WHERE year < 1970;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the title and director for all movies released before the year 1970.

sql: SELECT * FROM Rating WHERE ratingDate IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, listing all available details (reviewer ID, movie ID, stars, and date) for every movie rating that has a recorded date.

sql: SELECT name FROM Reviewer ORDER BY name DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of all reviewers, ordered in reverse alphabetical order (Z-A).

sql: SELECT T1.title, T2.stars FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows that pair each movie title with a corresponding star rating it has received.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.stars FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing each individual rating event, showing the reviewer's name and the number of stars they awarded.

sql: SELECT T1.title, COUNT(T2.mID) FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.title;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each movie title along with the total number of ratings it has received.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.rID) FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID GROUP BY T1.rID;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each reviewer by name and the total number of ratings they have submitted.

sql: SELECT T1.title, AVG(T2.stars) FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.title;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each movie title along with its average star rating based on all submitted ratings.

sql: SELECT T1.name, AVG(T2.stars) FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID GROUP BY T1.rID;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name of each reviewer along with the average star rating they have given across all their ratings.

sql: SELECT T1.title, MAX(T2.ratingDate) FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.title;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each movie title along with the date of its most recent rating.

sql: SELECT T2.title FROM Rating AS T1 JOIN Movie AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID JOIN Reviewer AS T3 ON T1.rID = T3.rID WHERE T3.name = 'Sarah Martinez';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of movies that have been rated by the reviewer named 'Sarah Martinez'.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Rating AS T1 JOIN Reviewer AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID JOIN Movie AS T3 ON T1.mID = T3.mID WHERE T3.title = 'Gone with the Wind';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of the reviewers who have rated the movie 'Gone with the Wind'.

sql: SELECT T1.title, T2.name, T3.stars, T3.ratingDate FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T3 ON T1.mID = T3.mID JOIN Reviewer AS T2 ON T3.rID = T2.rID;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing every individual movie rating, including the movie's title, the reviewer's name, the star rating, and the date of the rating.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID WHERE T1.year > 1960;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the unique titles of movies that were released after 1960 and have received at least one rating.

sql: SELECT T1.title, MIN(T2.stars), MAX(T2.stars) FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.title;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows showing each movie's title along with the lowest and highest rating it has received from reviewers.

sql: SELECT T1.director, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.mID) FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.director;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each movie director and the total count of their unique movies that have received a rating.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID WHERE T2.ratingDate = '2011-01-22';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of reviewers who submitted a rating on January 22, 2011.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID WHERE T2.stars = 4;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique titles of all movies that have received a 4-star rating.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.mID HAVING COUNT(T2.mID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the titles of movies that have received two or more ratings.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID GROUP BY T1.rID HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of reviewers who have submitted two or more ratings.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID WHERE T1.rID IN (SELECT rID FROM Rating WHERE mID = 101) GROUP BY T1.name HAVING AVG(T2.stars) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of reviewers who have rated a specific movie (ID 101) and whose overall average rating across all movies is greater than 2 stars.

sql: SELECT T2.title FROM Rating AS T1 JOIN Movie AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID WHERE T1.stars = 4 EXCEPT SELECT T2.title FROM Rating AS T1 JOIN Movie AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID WHERE T1.stars = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the title of a movie that has received a 4-star rating but has not received a 2-star rating.

sql: SELECT T2.title FROM Rating AS T1 JOIN Movie AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID WHERE T1.stars = 2 INTERSECT SELECT T2.title FROM Rating AS T1 JOIN Movie AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID WHERE T1.stars = 4;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of movies that have received both a 2-star rating and a 4-star rating from viewers.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.title ORDER BY COUNT(T2.mID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the title of the single movie that has received the most ratings.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.title HAVING MAX(T2.stars) = 4;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of movies for which the highest rating received from any reviewer was exactly 4 stars.

sql: SELECT T1.title, COUNT(T2.mID) FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.title HAVING AVG(T2.stars) > 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing highly-rated movies (average rating > 3 stars) along with the total number of ratings each movie has received.

sql: SELECT MAX(T1.year) - MIN(T1.year) FROM Movie AS T1 WHERE T1.mID IN (SELECT DISTINCT mID FROM Rating);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value: the time span in years between the oldest and newest movies that have received at least one rating.

sql: SELECT name FROM Reviewer EXCEPT SELECT T2.name FROM Rating AS T1 JOIN Reviewer AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID WHERE T1.mID = 101;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of reviewers who have not submitted a rating for the movie with ID 101.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.title FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID JOIN Movie AS T3 ON T2.mID = T3.mID WHERE T2.stars > (SELECT AVG(stars) FROM Rating WHERE mID = T2.mID);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of reviewers and the titles of movies for which the reviewer's rating was higher than that movie's average rating.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID GROUP BY T1.rID, T1.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.mID) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of reviewers who have rated exactly one movie.

sql: SELECT T1.title, COUNT(T2.mID) FROM Movie AS T1 LEFT JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID WHERE T1.director = 'Victor Fleming' GROUP BY T1.title;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each movie directed by 'Victor Fleming' along with the total number of ratings received for that movie.

sql: SELECT name FROM Reviewer EXCEPT SELECT T2.name FROM Rating AS T1 JOIN Reviewer AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID WHERE T1.stars = 2;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of reviewers who have never given a 2-star rating.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID WHERE T1.year < 1970 GROUP BY T1.title HAVING AVG(T2.stars) >= 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of movies released before 1970 that have an average rating of 3 stars or higher.

sql: SELECT T1.director, AVG(T2.stars) FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.director;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each movie director along with the average star rating for their movies.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Rating AS T1 JOIN Reviewer AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID WHERE T1.stars = 2 INTERSECT SELECT T2.name FROM Rating AS T1 JOIN Reviewer AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID WHERE T1.stars = 4;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of reviewers who have submitted both a 2-star rating and a 4-star rating.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.mID, T1.title HAVING COUNT(T2.ratingDate) = COUNT(T2.mID);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the titles of movies for which every single rating recorded has an associated rating date.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID WHERE T2.ratingDate = (SELECT MIN(ratingDate) FROM Rating);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the reviewer who submitted the very first rating on record, based on the earliest rating date.

sql: SELECT T1.title, T3.name, T2.stars FROM Movie AS T1 LEFT JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID LEFT JOIN Reviewer AS T3 ON T2.rID = T3.rID;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each movie rating, showing the movie's title, the name of the reviewer, and the number of stars they awarded.

sql: SELECT T1.year FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.mID, T1.year ORDER BY AVG(T2.stars) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the release year of the single movie with the highest average rating.

sql: SELECT title FROM Movie EXCEPT SELECT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of movies that have not received any ratings.

sql: SELECT name FROM Reviewer EXCEPT SELECT T2.name FROM Reviewer AS T2 JOIN Rating AS T1 ON T2.rID = T1.rID;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID WHERE T2.mID IN (SELECT mID FROM Movie WHERE year = 1939);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of reviewers who have rated at least one movie released in the year 1939.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.rID) FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID GROUP BY T1.rID, T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.rID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing reviewers who have submitted more than one rating, along with the total number of ratings each has provided.

sql: SELECT * FROM Highschooler WHERE grade = 9;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the ID, name, and grade for all high schoolers in the 9th grade.

sql: SELECT name, grade FROM Highschooler WHERE ID = 1510;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and grade of the high school student with an ID of 1510.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Highschooler;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total count of all high schoolers.

sql: SELECT student_id, friend_id FROM Friend;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing all recorded friendship pairings, with each row containing the ID of a student and the ID of one of their friends.

sql: SELECT student_id, liked_id FROM Likes;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each "like" relationship between students, showing the ID of the student who performed the like and the ID of the student who was liked.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Likes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id WHERE T2.liked_id = 1689;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the high schooler who has 'liked' the student identified by the ID 1689.

sql: SELECT name, grade FROM Highschooler ORDER BY grade DESC, name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing all high schoolers and their grades, ordered from the highest grade to the lowest, with students in the same grade listed alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id WHERE T2.friend_id = 1381;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the high schooler who is friends with the student identified by ID 1381.

sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT grade) FROM Highschooler;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total count of unique grade levels present among all high schoolers.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT student_id FROM Likes;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows containing a unique list of IDs for every student who has expressed a 'like'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.friend_id) FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, listing each high schooler by name and the total number of friends they have.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.student_id) FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Likes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.liked_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each high schooler's name and the total number of 'likes' they have received from other students.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.ID HAVING COUNT(T2.friend_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of high schoolers who have more than one friend.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Likes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.liked_id GROUP BY T1.ID HAVING COUNT(T2.student_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of high schoolers who are liked by more than one other student.

sql: SELECT name FROM Highschooler WHERE ID IN (SELECT student_id FROM Friend WHERE friend_id = 1709);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the high schooler who is a friend of the student with ID 1709.

sql: SELECT name FROM Highschooler WHERE ID IN (SELECT liked_id FROM Likes WHERE student_id = 1689);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the high schooler liked by the student with ID 1689.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.grade) FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the overall average grade of all high schoolers who are listed as having friends.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Friend AS T2 WHERE T2.student_id = T1.ID);
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the names of all highschoolers who have at least one friend recorded in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM Highschooler AS T1, Highschooler AS T2, Friend AS T3 WHERE T1.ID = T3.student_id AND T2.ID = T3.friend_id AND T1.grade = T2.grade;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, each containing a pair of high school student names who are friends and are in the same grade.

sql: SELECT name FROM Highschooler WHERE ID NOT IN (SELECT student_id FROM Likes);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of high schoolers who have not liked any other student.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T3.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.liked_id = T3.ID WHERE T2.grade = 9;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of 9th-grade students alongside the names of the students they like.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Friend WHERE student_id IN (SELECT ID FROM Highschooler WHERE grade = 9);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value that is the total count of friendships for all students in the 9th grade.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Friend AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID WHERE T1.friend_id = (SELECT ID FROM Highschooler WHERE name = 'Gabriel');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the highschooler who is a friend of the student named 'Gabriel'.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T3.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.liked_id = T3.ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, where each row is a pair of student names indicating that the first student likes the second student.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Likes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T2.liked_id = T3.ID WHERE T1.grade = 9 AND T3.name = 'Tiffany';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T3.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.liked_id = T3.ID WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Likes AS T4 WHERE T4.student_id = T1.liked_id AND T4.liked_id = T1.student_id) AND T1.student_id < T1.liked_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, with each row identifying a unique pair of high school students who have a mutual 'like' for one another.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T3.name FROM Friend AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.friend_id = T3.ID EXCEPT SELECT T2.name, T3.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.liked_id = T3.ID;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing pairs of high schoolers who are friends, but where the first student in the pair does not also "like" the second student.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T3.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.liked_id = T3.ID WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Likes AS T4 WHERE T4.student_id = T1.liked_id AND T4.liked_id = T1.student_id);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing pairs of high school students where the first student likes the second, but the affection is not reciprocated.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.grade FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.ID HAVING COUNT(T2.friend_id) = (SELECT COUNT(friend_id) FROM Friend GROUP BY student_id ORDER BY COUNT(friend_id) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and grade of the high schooler who has the most friends.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.grade FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Likes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.liked_id GROUP BY T1.ID HAVING COUNT(T2.student_id) = (SELECT COUNT(student_id) FROM Likes GROUP BY liked_id ORDER BY COUNT(student_id) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and grade of the highschooler(s) who received the most 'likes' from other students, representing the most popular individuals.

sql: SELECT name FROM Highschooler WHERE ID NOT IN (SELECT student_id FROM Friend UNION SELECT friend_id FROM Friend) AND ID IN (SELECT liked_id FROM Likes);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T3 ON T1.ID = T3.student_id JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T3.friend_id = T2.ID WHERE T1.grade <> T2.grade;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing pairs of high school friends who are in different grades, showing the name of the first student and their corresponding friend.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Friend AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID WHERE T1.friend_id = (SELECT ID FROM Highschooler WHERE name = 'Gabriel') INTERSECT SELECT T2.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID WHERE T1.liked_id = (SELECT ID FROM Highschooler WHERE name = 'Gabriel');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.grade FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Likes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.liked_id GROUP BY T1.grade ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single high school grade level that collectively receives the most 'likes', determined by counting the number of likes per grade.

sql: SELECT T1.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Friend AS T2 WHERE T2.student_id = T1.ID), (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Likes AS T3 WHERE T3.student_id = T1.ID) FROM Highschooler AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows, listing each highschooler by name along with a count of their total friends and the total number of other students they like.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Likes AS T2 WHERE T2.student_id = T1.ID) > (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Friend AS T3 WHERE T3.student_id = T1.ID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of high schoolers who have received more "likes" than they have "friends".

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Likes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T2.liked_id = T3.ID WHERE T3.grade > T1.grade;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of high schoolers who have 'liked' a student in a higher grade than their own.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Friend AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.friend_id = T2.ID WHERE T1.student_id = 1510 UNION SELECT T2.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.liked_id = T2.ID WHERE T1.student_id = 1510;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of highschoolers who are either friends with or liked by the student with ID 1510.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name, T3.name FROM Likes AS L1 JOIN Likes AS L2 ON L1.liked_id = L2.student_id JOIN Highschooler AS T1 ON L1.student_id = T1.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON L1.liked_id = T2.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON L2.liked_id = T3.ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows that identify three-person "chains of affection," listing the names of three high schoolers where the first student likes the second, and the second student in turn likes the third.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T3.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.liked_id = T3.ID WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Friend AS T4 WHERE T4.student_id = T1.student_id AND T4.friend_id = T1.liked_id);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing pairs of high school students who are also friends, showing the name of the student who likes someone and the name of the friend they like.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T4.name FROM Friend AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.friend_id = T2.friend_id AND T1.student_id < T2.student_id JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.student_id = T3.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T4 ON T2.student_id = T4.ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing pairs of high schoolers' names who share at least one friend in common.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 WHERE T1.ID IN (SELECT student_id FROM Friend) EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T2.friend_id = T3.ID WHERE T1.grade = T3.grade;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of highschoolers whose friends are all in different grades than themselves.

sql: SELECT T1.grade, AVG(T1.friend_count) FROM (SELECT T2.ID, T2.grade, COUNT(T3.friend_id) AS friend_count FROM Highschooler AS T2 JOIN Friend AS T3 ON T2.ID = T3.student_id GROUP BY T2.ID) AS T1 GROUP BY T1.grade;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each grade level and the average number of friends for students within that grade.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM Highschooler AS T1, Highschooler AS T2, Likes AS T3 WHERE T1.ID = T3.student_id AND T2.ID = T3.liked_id AND (SELECT count(*) FROM Friend WHERE student_id = T1.ID) > (SELECT count(*) FROM Friend WHERE student_id = T2.ID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing pairs of high schooler names where the first student likes the second, and the first student has more friends than the second.

sql: SELECT name FROM Highschooler WHERE ID != 1510 AND ID NOT IN (SELECT friend_id FROM Friend WHERE student_id = 1510) AND ID NOT IN (SELECT liked_id FROM Likes WHERE student_id = 1510);
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the names of high schoolers who are not friends with student ID 1510 and are not liked by student ID 1510.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM (SELECT T2.name, ((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Friend WHERE student_id = T2.ID) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Likes WHERE student_id = T2.ID)) AS connections FROM Highschooler AS T2 WHERE T2.grade = 9) AS T1 ORDER BY T1.connections DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the 9th-grade student with the most social connections, where connections are calculated as the sum of their friends and likes.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T4.name FROM Friend AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.friend_id = T2.student_id JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.student_id = T3.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T4 ON T2.friend_id = T4.ID WHERE T3.name = 'Jordan' AND T4.name <> 'Jordan' AND T2.friend_id NOT IN (SELECT friend_id FROM Friend WHERE student_id = T1.student_id);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of highschoolers who are second-degree connections (friends of friends) to the student 'Jordan', excluding any of Jordan's direct friends.
