sql: SELECT T3.ResTypeName, SUM(T2.Spent) AS TotalSpending FROM Restaurant_Type AS T3 JOIN Type_Of_Restaurant AS T4 ON T3.ResTypeID = T4.ResTypeID JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T4.ResID = T2.ResID GROUP BY T3.ResTypeName HAVING SUM(T2.Spent) > 10;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing restaurant types where total customer spending exceeds 10, showing the specific restaurant type name and its aggregated total spending.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Restaurant AS T3 ON T2.ResID = T3.ResID WHERE T2.Spent > (SELECT AVG(Spent) FROM Visits_Restaurant) AND T3.Rating < 4;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.ResName, COUNT(T2.StuID) FROM Restaurant AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.ResID = T2.ResID WHERE T1.Address = '3233 St Paul St, Baltimore, MD 21218' GROUP BY T1.ResName;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the restaurant at '3233 St Paul St, Baltimore, MD 21218' and the total number of student visits it has received.

sql: SELECT customer_id, customer_first_name, customer_last_name, town_city FROM Customers WHERE country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the ID, first name, last name, and city for all customers located in the USA.

sql: SELECT product_name, unit_price FROM Products WHERE product_color = 'Red';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and unit price for all products that are red.

sql: SELECT account_id, customer_id, date_account_opened FROM Accounts WHERE account_name = '900';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the account ID, customer ID, and the date it was opened for the specific account named '900'.

sql: SELECT transaction_id, account_id, transaction_amount FROM Financial_Transactions WHERE transaction_type = 'Payment';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows detailing all financial transactions classified as a 'Payment', providing the transaction ID, the associated account ID, and the amount for each.

sql: SELECT production_type_code, product_type_description, vat_rating FROM Product_Categories ORDER BY vat_rating DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing product categories with their corresponding code, description, and VAT rating, ordered from the highest VAT rating to the lowest.

sql: SELECT customer_id, email_address, phone_number FROM Customers WHERE customer_first_name = 'Dee';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the customer ID, email address, and phone number for any customer with the first name 'Dee'.

sql: SELECT product_name, unit_price FROM Products WHERE unit_price > 600;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the product name and unit price for high-priced products with a unit price greater than 600.

sql: SELECT order_id, date_order_placed FROM Orders WHERE customer_id = 12;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the order ID and placement date for all orders placed by the customer with ID 12.

sql: SELECT invoice_number, invoice_date FROM Invoices WHERE order_id = 9;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the invoice number and date for all invoices associated with order number 9.

sql: SELECT product_id, product_name, unit_price FROM Products WHERE production_type_code = 'Food';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the product ID, name, and unit price for all products classified as 'Food'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, T2.date_order_placed FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each order, showing the first and last name of the customer who placed it and the date it was placed.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each customer's first and last name along with the total number of orders they have placed.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, SUM(T2.product_quantity) AS total_quantity FROM Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id GROUP BY T1.order_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows showing the total quantity of items for each distinct order ID.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.product_type_description FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Categories AS T2 ON T1.production_type_code = T2.production_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, listing each product's name along with its associated category description as found by joining the products and product categories tables.

sql: SELECT transaction_type, SUM(transaction_amount) AS total_amount FROM Financial_Transactions GROUP BY transaction_type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each transaction type alongside the total sum of all transaction amounts for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T2.order_id, T3.invoice_date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Invoices AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first name of the customer who placed an order, the corresponding order ID, and the date each order was invoiced.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique first and last names of all customers who have an account.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, SUM(T1.product_quantity) AS total_quantity_ordered FROM Order_Items AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T2.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each product name and the total quantity ordered for that product across all orders.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, COUNT(T2.account_id) AS number_of_accounts FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each customer's first name and the total number of accounts they hold.

sql: SELECT T2.product_type_description, AVG(T1.unit_price) AS average_price FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Categories AS T2 ON T1.production_type_code = T2.production_type_code GROUP BY T2.product_type_description;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each product category description along with the average unit price for all products within that category.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T2.order_id, T2.date_order_placed FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.state_county_province = 'WestVirginia';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first name, order ID, and placement date for an order placed by a customer from West Virginia.

sql: SELECT product_name, unit_price FROM Products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT product_id FROM Order_Items);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the product name and unit price for every product that has been sold at least once.

sql: SELECT T1.invoice_number, T1.product_title, T1.product_price FROM Invoice_Line_Items AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.product_color = 'Red';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the invoice number, product title, and price for all sales transactions involving a red product.

sql: SELECT product_size, COUNT(product_id) AS number_of_products FROM Products GROUP BY product_size;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the total count of products for each available product size.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 LEFT JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of all customers who have never placed an order.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, SUM(T3.product_quantity) AS total_items_ordered FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING SUM(T3.product_quantity) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first name, last name, and total items ordered for each customer who has purchased more than 5 items in total across all their orders.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, SUM(T3.transaction_amount) AS total_payments FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.transaction_type = 'Payment' GROUP BY T1.customer_id ORDER BY total_payments DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the top 3 customers by their total payment amount, listing each customer's full name and their total payments from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T4.production_type_code) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have purchased products from at least two different production types.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, AVG(julianday(T3.invoice_date) - julianday(T2.date_order_placed)) AS avg_days_to_invoice FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Invoices AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer's first and last name, along with the average number of days between placing an order and the order being invoiced.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T_derived.customer_total FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.customer_id, SUM(T4.derived_total_cost) AS customer_total FROM Orders AS T2 JOIN Invoices AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Invoice_Line_Items AS T4 ON T3.invoice_number = T4.invoice_number GROUP BY T2.customer_id) AS T_derived ON T1.customer_id = T_derived.customer_id WHERE T_derived.customer_total > (SELECT AVG(derived_total_cost) FROM Invoice_Line_Items);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first name and total purchase amount for customers whose total spending exceeds the average cost of a single invoice line item.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.other_account_details IN ('Regular', 'VIP') GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.other_account_details) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of customers who hold both a 'Regular' and a 'VIP' account.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T4.product_name, T4.unit_price FROM Financial_Transactions AS T1 JOIN Invoices AS T2 ON T1.invoice_number = T2.invoice_number JOIN Invoice_Line_Items AS T3 ON T2.invoice_number = T3.invoice_number JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T1.transaction_type = 'Refund';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique products and their unit prices that have been associated with a 'Refund' transaction.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T1.country = 'USA' AND T4.product_name = 'Coffee Bean';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T3.product_type_description, SUM(T1.derived_total_cost) AS total_revenue FROM Invoice_Line_Items AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Product_Categories AS T3 ON T2.production_type_code = T3.production_type_code GROUP BY T3.product_type_description;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each product category description and the total revenue generated for that category.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, T3.invoice_number FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Invoices AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id LEFT JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T4 ON T3.invoice_number = T4.invoice_number WHERE T4.transaction_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying customers by their first and last name, along with the invoice numbers for any invoices that do not have an associated financial transaction.

sql: SELECT T2.product_type_description, T1.product_name, T1.unit_price FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Categories AS T2 ON T1.production_type_code = T2.production_type_code WHERE T1.unit_price = (SELECT MAX(unit_price) FROM Products AS T_inner WHERE T_inner.production_type_code = T1.production_type_code);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the most expensive product within each product category, showing the category's description, the product's name, and its maximum unit price.

sql: SELECT customer_id, customer_first_name, customer_last_name FROM Customers EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.production_type_code = 'Books';
result_summary: Returns 14 rows identifying all customers (ID, first name, and last name) who have never purchased a product of type 'Books'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, SUM(T4.transaction_amount) AS total_paid_in_march FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T4 ON T2.account_id = T4.account_id WHERE T4.transaction_type = 'Payment' AND T4.transaction_date LIKE '2018-03-%' GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each customer's first name and the total amount they paid in transactions during March 2018.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, AVG(T_derived.item_count) AS avg_items_per_order FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.order_id, T2.customer_id, SUM(T3.product_quantity) as item_count FROM Orders AS T2 JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T2.order_id) AS T_derived ON T1.customer_id = T_derived.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each customer by first name and showing the average number of items they purchase per order.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.production_type_code IN ('Food', 'Books') GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T4.production_type_code) = 2;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.product_type_description, COUNT(T1.product_id) AS num_products FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Categories AS T2 ON T1.production_type_code = T2.production_type_code GROUP BY T2.product_type_description HAVING COUNT(T1.product_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing product categories that contain more than one product, along with the total number of products within each of those categories.

sql: SELECT SUM(T2.derived_total_cost) AS unpaid_invoices_value FROM Invoices AS T1 JOIN Invoice_Line_Items AS T2 ON T1.invoice_number = T2.invoice_number WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Financial_Transactions AS T3 WHERE T3.invoice_number = T1.invoice_number AND T3.transaction_type = 'Payment');
result_summary: Returns a single row with the total value of all unpaid invoices, calculated by summing the costs of their constituent line items.

sql: SELECT customer_first_name, customer_last_name, country FROM Customers WHERE country = 'USA' UNION SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, T1.country FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.other_account_details = 'VIP';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first name, last name, and country of customers who are either located in the USA or are designated as VIPs.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.town_city = 'New Nikolas' INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_size = 'Medium';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT STRFTIME('%Y-%m', date_order_placed) AS order_month, COUNT(order_id) AS monthly_order_count FROM Orders GROUP BY order_month ORDER BY monthly_order_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single month with the highest number of placed orders and the total order count for that month.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T2.date_order_placed) = '2012' GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID, first name, and last name of the unique customer who placed an order in the year 2012.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, T3.transaction_amount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id ORDER BY T3.transaction_amount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the customer (first and last name) who made the single largest financial transaction, along with the amount of that transaction.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, MAX(T2.date_order_placed) AS latest_order_date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each customer's full name and the date of their most recent order.

sql: SELECT product_name, unit_price FROM Products WHERE unit_price > (SELECT AVG(unit_price) FROM Products WHERE production_type_code = 'Books');
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the product name and unit price for all products that are more expensive than the average price of products in the 'Books' category.

sql: SELECT Customer_ID, Customer_Details FROM Customers;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the unique ID and details for every customer in the database.

sql: SELECT Customer_ID, Customer_Details FROM Customers WHERE Customer_Details = 'America Jaskolski';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the Customer ID and details for the specific customer named 'America Jaskolski'.

sql: SELECT Policy_ID, Customer_ID, Start_Date FROM Policies WHERE Policy_Type_Code = 'Jurisdiction';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the policy ID, customer ID, and start date for all policies identified as the 'Jurisdiction' type.

sql: SELECT Claim_Header_ID, Policy_ID, Amount_Claimed FROM Claim_Headers WHERE Claim_Status_Code = 'Settled';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the claim ID, policy ID, and amount claimed for all claims that have a 'Settled' status.

sql: SELECT Staff_ID, Staff_Details FROM Staff ORDER BY Staff_Details;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the ID and details for each staff member, sorted alphabetically by the staff details.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Claim_Headers;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of claim headers.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Claim_Type_Code FROM Claim_Headers;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, providing a unique list of all available claim type codes.

sql: SELECT Claim_ID, Created_by_Staff_ID FROM Claims_Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'Medical';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the claim ID and the ID of the staff member who created the document, specifically for all documents classified as 'Medical'.

sql: SELECT Claim_Processing_ID, Claim_ID, Staff_ID FROM Claims_Processing WHERE Claim_Outcome_Code = 'In progress';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows for all insurance claims currently in progress, listing their processing ID, original claim ID, and the ID of the staff member assigned.

sql: SELECT * FROM Claims_Processing_Stages WHERE Claim_Status_Name = 'Open';
result_summary: Returns 1 row providing the full details for the specific claims processing stage that has an 'Open' status.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details, T2.Policy_Type_Code, T2.Start_Date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the customer's details, their policy type code, and the policy's start date for each policy on record.

sql: SELECT Policy_Type_Code, COUNT(*) FROM Policies GROUP BY Policy_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each policy type code and the total count of policies associated with each type.

sql: SELECT Claim_Status_Code, SUM(Amount_Claimed) FROM Claim_Headers GROUP BY Claim_Status_Code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the total amount claimed for each distinct claim status.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details, T2.Claim_ID, T2.Document_Type_Code FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Documents AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Created_by_Staff_ID;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows associating staff members with the claim documents they created, listing the staff's details, the claim ID, and the document type for each entry.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details, COUNT(T2.Claim_ID) FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Documents AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Created_by_Staff_ID GROUP BY T1.Staff_ID;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each staff member's details along with the total count of claim documents they have created.

sql: SELECT T1.Claim_Header_ID, T1.Claim_Type_Code, T1.Amount_Claimed FROM Claim_Headers AS T1 JOIN Claims_Processing AS T2 ON T1.Claim_Header_ID = T2.Claim_ID WHERE T2.Claim_Outcome_Code = 'In progress';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing claims that are currently 'In progress', showing each claim's ID, type, and the total amount claimed.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details, T2.Claim_ID FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Processing AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Staff_ID JOIN Claims_Processing_Stages AS T3 ON T2.Claim_Stage_ID = T3.Claim_Stage_ID WHERE T3.Claim_Status_Name = 'Open';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the staff details and the corresponding claim ID for all claims that are currently in an 'Open' status.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID WHERE T2.Policy_Type_Code = 'Jurisdiction';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the details of customers who hold a policy of the 'Jurisdiction' type.

sql: SELECT Claim_Type_Code, AVG(Amount_Piad) FROM Claim_Headers GROUP BY Claim_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the average amount paid for each distinct claim type code.

sql: SELECT Claim_Header_ID, Claim_Status_Code, Date_of_Claim FROM Claim_Headers WHERE Claim_Header_ID IN (SELECT Claim_ID FROM Claims_Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'Medical');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the ID, status code, and date for claims that have an associated medical document.

sql: SELECT T1.Claim_Header_ID, T1.Claim_Type_Code, T2.Document_Type_Code, T2.Created_Date FROM Claim_Headers AS T1 JOIN Claims_Documents AS T2 ON T1.Claim_Header_ID = T2.Claim_ID WHERE T1.Claim_Status_Code = 'Disputed';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing documents associated with disputed claims, including the claim ID, claim type, document type, and the document's creation date.

sql: SELECT Policy_ID, Policy_Type_Code FROM Policies AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Claim_Headers AS T2 WHERE T1.Policy_ID = T2.Policy_ID);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing policies that have an associated claim, showing each policy's ID and its corresponding type code.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details, T2.Document_Type_Code FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Documents AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Created_by_Staff_ID WHERE T2.Claim_ID = 27;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the staff members who created documents for claim #27, along with the type of each document.

sql: SELECT T1.Policy_ID, SUM(T2.Amount_Claimed), SUM(T2.Amount_Piad) FROM Policies AS T1 JOIN Claim_Headers AS T2 ON T1.Policy_ID = T2.Policy_ID GROUP BY T1.Policy_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each policy ID along with the total sum of amounts claimed and the total sum of amounts paid for all claims under that policy.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details, T2.Claim_ID, T2.Claim_Outcome_Code FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Processing AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Staff_ID WHERE T2.Claim_ID = 27;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the staff details and claim outcome for each staff member who processed claim ID 27.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details, COUNT(T2.Policy_ID) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Policy_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing customers who hold more than one policy, along with the total count of their policies.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Claim_ID) FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Processing AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Staff_ID GROUP BY T1.Staff_ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Claim_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the details of staff members who have processed more than one unique claim, along with the total count of distinct claims they handled.

sql: SELECT Claim_ID FROM Claims_Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code IN ('Medical', 'Document') GROUP BY Claim_ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT Document_Type_Code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the IDs of claims that have both 'Medical' and 'Document' type documents on file.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details, T2.Policy_ID FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID LEFT JOIN Claim_Headers AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID WHERE T3.Claim_Header_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows identifying policies that have never had a claim, listing the associated customer's details and the specific policy ID.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details, SUM(T3.Amount_Claimed - T3.Amount_Piad) AS Total_Discrepancy FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claim_Headers AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each customer's details alongside the total discrepancy between the amount claimed and the amount paid, summed across all of that customer's claims.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details, SUM(T3.Amount_Claimed) AS Total_Claimed FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Processing AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Staff_ID JOIN Claim_Headers AS T3 ON T2.Claim_ID = T3.Claim_Header_ID GROUP BY T1.Staff_ID ORDER BY Total_Claimed DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the staff member with the highest total value of processed claims, showing their details and the summed claim amount.

sql: SELECT Claim_Header_ID, Date_of_Claim, Date_of_Settlement FROM Claim_Headers WHERE julianday(Date_of_Settlement) - julianday(Date_of_Claim) > 365;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing claims that took more than a year to settle, showing the claim ID along with the original claim date and the final settlement date.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details, SUM(T3.Amount_Piad) AS Total_Paid FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claim_Headers AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_ID ORDER BY Total_Paid DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the customer who has received the highest total amount in paid claims, displaying their details and this total sum.

sql: SELECT Claim_Header_ID FROM Claim_Headers WHERE Claim_Status_Code = 'Disputed' INTERSECT SELECT Claim_ID FROM Claims_Processing WHERE Staff_ID = 589;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the IDs for claims that are both marked as 'Disputed' and are being processed by staff member 589.

sql: SELECT Created_by_Staff_ID FROM Claims_Documents EXCEPT SELECT Staff_ID FROM Claims_Processing WHERE Claim_ID = 27;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the IDs of staff members who have created claims documents but were not involved in processing claim #27.

sql: SELECT Claim_Type_Code FROM Claim_Headers WHERE Claim_Status_Code = 'Disputed' GROUP BY Claim_Type_Code ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most common claim type among all claims with a 'Disputed' status.

sql: SELECT T1.Policy_Type_Code, AVG(julianday(T2.Date_of_Settlement) - julianday(T2.Date_of_Claim)) AS Avg_Processing_Days FROM Policies AS T1 JOIN Claim_Headers AS T2 ON T1.Policy_ID = T2.Policy_ID WHERE T2.Date_of_Settlement IS NOT NULL GROUP BY T1.Policy_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each policy type alongside the average number of days from the initial claim date to the final settlement date.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details, SUM(T3.Amount_Claimed) AS Customer_Total_Claimed FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claim_Headers AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_ID HAVING SUM(T3.Amount_Claimed) > (SELECT AVG(Amount_Claimed) FROM Claim_Headers);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing customers whose total claimed amount exceeds the average of all claims, showing each customer's details and their total amount claimed.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Processing AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Staff_ID GROUP BY T1.Staff_ID HAVING MIN(T2.Claim_Outcome_Code) = 'In progress' AND MAX(T2.Claim_Outcome_Code) = 'In progress';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the details of staff members whose entire workload consists only of claims currently marked as 'In progress'.

sql: SELECT T1.Policy_Type_Code, CAST(SUM(T2.Amount_Piad) AS REAL) / SUM(T2.Amount_Claimed) AS Paid_to_Claimed_Ratio FROM Policies AS T1 JOIN Claim_Headers AS T2 ON T1.Policy_ID = T2.Policy_ID WHERE T2.Amount_Claimed > 0 GROUP BY T1.Policy_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows calculating the ratio of the total amount paid to the total amount claimed for each distinct policy type code.

sql: SELECT T1.Claim_Header_ID, T1.Claim_Type_Code, T1.Date_of_Claim FROM Claim_Headers AS T1 LEFT JOIN Claims_Documents AS T2 ON T1.Claim_Header_ID = T2.Claim_ID WHERE T2.Claim_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying claims that are missing associated documents, showing each claim's ID, type code, and date of claim.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details, COUNT(T2.Claim_ID) AS Medical_Doc_Count FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Documents AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Created_by_Staff_ID WHERE T2.Document_Type_Code = 'Medical' GROUP BY T1.Staff_ID ORDER BY Medical_Doc_Count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the top 3 staff members who have created the most 'Medical' type claim documents, ordered from the highest count to the lowest.

sql: SELECT Claim_Header_ID, Amount_Claimed, Amount_Piad, ABS(Amount_Claimed - Amount_Piad) AS Discrepancy FROM Claim_Headers ORDER BY Discrepancy DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single claim with the largest financial discrepancy, which is the absolute difference between the amount claimed and the amount paid.

sql: SELECT T1.Policy_ID, T1.Customer_ID, T1.Start_Date FROM Policies AS T1 WHERE strftime('%Y', T1.Start_Date) = '2018' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Claim_Headers AS T2 WHERE T1.Policy_ID = T2.Policy_ID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the policy ID, customer ID, and start date for all policies that started in 2018 and have had at least one claim filed against them.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Customer_Details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claim_Headers AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID WHERE T3.Claim_Status_Code = 'Settled';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique details for customers who have had at least one claim settled.

sql: SELECT T1.Claim_Header_ID, T1.Claim_Type_Code, T1.Amount_Claimed FROM Claim_Headers AS T1 JOIN Claims_Processing AS T2 ON T1.Claim_Header_ID = T2.Claim_ID JOIN Staff AS T3 ON T2.Staff_ID = T3.Staff_ID WHERE T3.Staff_Details = 'Cathryn' AND T1.Claim_Status_Code = 'Disputed';

sql: SELECT T1.Claim_Header_ID, T4.Staff_Details FROM Claim_Headers AS T1 JOIN Claims_Processing AS T2 ON T1.Claim_Header_ID = T2.Claim_ID JOIN Claims_Processing_Stages AS T3 ON T2.Claim_Stage_ID = T3.Claim_Stage_ID JOIN Staff AS T4 ON T2.Staff_ID = T4.Staff_ID WHERE T3.Claim_Status_Name = 'Open' AND T1.Claim_Type_Code = 'Handphone Subsidy';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the staff members currently assigned to open 'Handphone Subsidy' claims, showing the specific claim ID for each assignment.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_ID, T1.Staff_Details, 'Document Creator' AS Role FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Documents AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Created_by_Staff_ID UNION SELECT T1.Staff_ID, T1.Staff_Details, 'Claim Processor' AS Role FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Processing AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Staff_ID;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows identifying staff members by their ID and details, assigning them a 'Role' of either 'Document Creator' or 'Claim Processor' based on their involvement in the claims lifecycle.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details, T3.Claim_Header_ID, T3.Date_of_Claim FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claim_Headers AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID WHERE T2.Policy_Type_Code = 'Jurisdiction' AND T3.Claim_Type_Code = 'Child Birth';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the customer's details, claim ID, and the date of the claim for any customer who filed a 'Child Birth' claim under a 'Jurisdiction' type policy.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details, SUM(T3.Amount_Claimed) AS Total_Claimed, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(T3.Amount_Claimed) DESC) AS Customer_Rank FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claim_Headers AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows for each customer, showing their details, the total amount they have claimed across all policies, and their corresponding rank based on this total from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT STU_FNAME, STU_LNAME, STU_GPA FROM STUDENT WHERE DEPT_CODE = 'BIOL';

sql: SELECT T2.EMP_LNAME, AVG(T1.STU_GPA) FROM STUDENT AS T1 JOIN EMPLOYEE AS T2 ON T1.PROF_NUM = T2.EMP_NUM GROUP BY T2.EMP_LNAME;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.STU_FNAME, T1.STU_LNAME FROM STUDENT AS T1 JOIN ENROLL AS T2 ON T1.STU_NUM = T2.STU_NUM JOIN CLASS AS T3 ON T2.CLASS_CODE = T3.CLASS_CODE JOIN COURSE AS T4 ON T3.CRS_CODE = T4.CRS_CODE WHERE T1.DEPT_CODE <> T4.DEPT_CODE;

sql: SELECT T_derived.Publisher, T_derived.avg_price FROM (SELECT Publisher, AVG(Price) as avg_price FROM publication GROUP BY Publisher) AS T_derived WHERE T_derived.avg_price > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM publication);

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T1.Writer FROM book AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM publication AS T2 WHERE T1.Book_ID = T2.Book_ID AND T2.Price > 10000000);

sql: SELECT T1.Writer FROM book AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Writer HAVING COUNT(T1.Book_ID) = (SELECT MAX(book_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Book_ID) as book_count FROM book GROUP BY Writer) AS T2);

sql: SELECT T1.Writer, SUM(T2.Price) AS TotalValue FROM book AS T1 JOIN publication AS T2 ON T1.Book_ID = T2.Book_ID GROUP BY T1.Writer ORDER BY TotalValue DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T2.Publisher, SUM(T2.Price) AS TotalPrice FROM publication AS T2 WHERE T2.Book_ID IN (SELECT T1.Book_ID FROM book AS T1 WHERE T1.Writer = 'Garth Ennis') GROUP BY T2.Publisher;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM customer AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T2.customer_id FROM rental AS T2 JOIN inventory AS T3 ON T2.inventory_id = T3.inventory_id JOIN film AS T4 ON T3.film_id = T4.film_id WHERE T4.special_features LIKE '%Behind the Scenes%') AND T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T5.customer_id FROM payment AS T5 WHERE T5.amount > 5.99);
result_summary: Returns 589 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have both rented a film with 'Behind the Scenes' special features and made a payment greater than $5.99.

sql: SELECT T3.name, AVG(T1.rental_duration) AS avg_duration FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_category AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id JOIN category AS T3 ON T2.category_id = T3.category_id GROUP BY T3.name;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows, listing each film category name along with the average rental duration for all films within that category.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN address AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id WHERE T2.city_id = 300 AND T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T3.customer_id FROM rental AS T3 JOIN inventory AS T4 ON T3.inventory_id = T4.inventory_id JOIN film AS T5 ON T4.film_id = T5.film_id WHERE T5.rental_duration > 7);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.title, T2.actor_count FROM film AS T1 JOIN (SELECT film_id, COUNT(actor_id) AS actor_count FROM film_actor GROUP BY film_id) AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id ORDER BY T2.actor_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns the single film title that has the largest cast, along with the total count of actors in that film.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN rental AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN inventory AS T3 ON T2.inventory_id = T3.inventory_id JOIN film_actor AS T4 ON T3.film_id = T4.film_id JOIN actor AS T5 ON T4.actor_id = T5.actor_id WHERE T5.first_name = 'NICK' AND T5.last_name = 'WAHLBERG';
result_summary: Returns 288 rows listing the unique first and last names of customers who have rented a film starring the actor Nick Wahlberg.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.total FROM customer AS T1 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount) AS total FROM payment GROUP BY customer_id) AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.first_name = 'MARY';
result_summary: Returns 1 row for the customer named 'MARY', showing their full name and the total amount they have paid across all transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM film AS T1 JOIN inventory AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id WHERE T2.inventory_id NOT IN (SELECT inventory_id FROM rental WHERE inventory_id IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the title of a film that has an available inventory copy which has never been rented.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM actor AS T1 WHERE T1.actor_id IN (SELECT actor_id FROM film_actor WHERE film_id = (SELECT film_id FROM film WHERE title = 'ACADEMY DINOSAUR')) AND T1.actor_id IN (SELECT actor_id FROM film_actor GROUP BY actor_id HAVING COUNT(film_id) > 10);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the first and last names of prolific actors (those with more than 10 film credits) who also starred in the movie 'ACADEMY DINOSAUR'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN payment AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.store_id = 1 GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name HAVING SUM(T2.amount) > (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM payment);
result_summary: Returns 326 rows with the first and last names of customers from store 1 whose total spending exceeds the overall average payment amount.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN rental AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN inventory AS T3 ON T2.inventory_id = T3.inventory_id JOIN film_category AS T4 ON T3.film_id = T4.film_id JOIN category AS T5 ON T4.category_id = T5.category_id WHERE T5.name = 'Animation' UNION SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN rental AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN inventory AS T3 ON T2.inventory_id = T3.inventory_id JOIN film_category AS T4 ON T3.film_id = T4.film_id JOIN category AS T5 ON T4.category_id = T5.category_id WHERE T5.name = 'Children';
result_summary: Returns 579 rows with the IDs, first names, and last names of all unique customers who have rented a film from either the 'Animation' or 'Children' categories.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM actor AS T1 JOIN film_actor AS T2 ON T1.actor_id = T2.actor_id JOIN film AS T3 ON T2.film_id = T3.film_id GROUP BY T1.actor_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name HAVING MIN(T3.rating) = 'PG' AND MAX(T3.rating) = 'PG';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN rental AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN inventory AS T3 ON T2.inventory_id = T3.inventory_id JOIN film AS T4 ON T3.film_id = T4.film_id JOIN film_category AS T5 ON T4.film_id = T5.film_id JOIN category AS T6 ON T5.category_id = T6.category_id WHERE T6.name = 'Action' GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T4.film_id) > 5;

sql: SELECT property_name, room_count FROM Properties WHERE room_count > 5;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names and room counts for properties that have more than 5 rooms.

sql: SELECT property_name, date_on_market FROM Properties ORDER BY date_on_market DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing property names and the date they were put on the market, ordered from most recent to least recent.

sql: SELECT property_name, vendor_requested_price FROM Properties ORDER BY vendor_requested_price DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the five most expensive properties by their vendor-requested price, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT * FROM Other_Available_Features WHERE feature_type_code = 'Amenity';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all details for available features that are classified as an 'Amenity'.

sql: SELECT property_id, property_name, date_sold FROM Properties WHERE date_sold IS NOT NULL;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT property_type_code FROM Properties;

sql: SELECT T2.property_type_description, COUNT(T1.property_id) FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Ref_Property_Types AS T2 ON T1.property_type_code = T2.property_type_code GROUP BY T2.property_type_description;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each property type description along with the total count of properties belonging to that type.

sql: SELECT property_type_code, AVG(agreed_selling_price) FROM Properties GROUP BY property_type_code;

sql: SELECT T1.property_name FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id WHERE T2.feature_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of properties that have the specific characteristic identified by feature ID 3.

sql: SELECT property_name, vendor_requested_price, agreed_selling_price FROM Properties WHERE agreed_selling_price < vendor_requested_price;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing properties that sold for less than the vendor's asking price, showing the property name, the requested price, and the final agreed selling price.

sql: SELECT T1.feature_name, T2.feature_type_name FROM Other_Available_Features AS T1 JOIN Ref_Feature_Types AS T2 ON T1.feature_type_code = T2.feature_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each available feature along with its corresponding feature type name, effectively translating feature codes into human-readable categories.

sql: SELECT T2.feature_name, T2.feature_description FROM Other_Property_Features AS T1 JOIN Other_Available_Features AS T2 ON T1.feature_id = T2.feature_id WHERE T1.property_id = 15;
result_summary: Returns 1 row detailing the name and description of the feature associated with the property identified by ID 15.

sql: SELECT T2.feature_name, COUNT(T1.property_id) FROM Other_Property_Features AS T1 JOIN Other_Available_Features AS T2 ON T1.feature_id = T2.feature_id GROUP BY T2.feature_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each available property feature by name, along with a count of the number of properties that have that feature.

sql: SELECT property_name, agreed_selling_price FROM Properties WHERE agreed_selling_price = (SELECT MAX(agreed_selling_price) FROM Properties);

sql: SELECT AVG(room_count) FROM Properties WHERE date_sold IS NOT NULL;

sql: SELECT T1.property_name FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Other_Available_Features AS T3 ON T2.feature_id = T3.feature_id WHERE T3.feature_name = 'Pool';

sql: SELECT T1.property_name, COUNT(T2.feature_id) AS NumberOfFeatures FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id GROUP BY T1.property_id, T1.property_name HAVING COUNT(T2.feature_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.property_type_code, T3.feature_name, COUNT(T3.feature_id) AS FeatureCount FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Other_Available_Features AS T3 ON T2.feature_id = T3.feature_id GROUP BY T1.property_type_code, T3.feature_name ORDER BY T1.property_type_code, FeatureCount DESC;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.property_type_code FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Other_Available_Features AS T3 ON T2.feature_id = T3.feature_id WHERE T3.feature_name = 'Pool';

sql: SELECT T1.property_name FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Other_Available_Features AS T3 ON T2.feature_id = T3.feature_id WHERE T3.feature_description = 'Swimming Pool.';

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Enrolled_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID ORDER BY count(T2.CID) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Enrolled_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Course AS T3 ON T2.CID = T3.CID JOIN Faculty AS T4 ON T3.Instructor = T4.FacID WHERE T4.Rank = 'Instructor';

sql: SELECT DNO FROM Course INTERSECT SELECT DNO FROM Minor_in;

sql: SELECT T1.StuID FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.Major = 600 EXCEPT SELECT T2.StuID FROM Enrolled_in AS T2 JOIN Course AS T3 ON T2.CID = T3.CID WHERE T3.DNO = 600;

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname FROM Student UNION ALL SELECT Fname, Lname FROM Faculty;

sql: SELECT Major, count(StuID) FROM Student GROUP BY Major ORDER BY count(StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Fname, T2.LName, T1.Major, T1.city_code FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.Major = T2.Major AND T1.city_code = T2.city_code WHERE T1.StuID < T2.StuID;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Enrolled_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Grade = 'A-' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Enrolled_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Grade = 'B';

sql: SELECT T1.CName, T1.Credits FROM Course AS T1 JOIN Faculty AS T2 ON T1.Instructor = T2.FacID WHERE T2.Rank = 'Professor' ORDER BY T1.Credits DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T3.Major, avg(T3.CourseCount) FROM (SELECT T1.StuID, T1.Major, count(T2.CID) AS CourseCount FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Enrolled_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID, T1.Major) AS T3 GROUP BY T3.Major;

sql: SELECT T1.IHSAA_Class, SUM(T2.amount) / SUM(T1.Enrollment) AS EndowmentPerStudent FROM School AS T1 JOIN endowment AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id GROUP BY T1.IHSAA_Class;

sql: SELECT T3.School_name FROM budget AS T1 JOIN budget AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id JOIN School AS T3 ON T1.School_id = T3.School_id WHERE T1.Year = 1999 AND T2.Year = 2000 AND T2.Invested > T1.Budgeted;

sql: SELECT T2.School_name, T1.Year, MAX(CAST(T1.Budget_invested_percent AS REAL)) FROM budget AS T1 JOIN School AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id GROUP BY T2.School_name, T1.Year ORDER BY MAX(CAST(T1.Budget_invested_percent AS REAL)) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.School_name, T2.Year, T2.Budgeted FROM School AS T1 JOIN budget AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id WHERE T2.Budgeted = (SELECT MAX(Budgeted) FROM budget AS T3 WHERE T3.School_id = T1.School_id);

sql: SELECT T1.School_name, T2.School_name, T1.County FROM School AS T1 JOIN School AS T2 ON T1.County = T2.County WHERE T1.School_id < T2.School_id;

sql: SELECT T1.School_name FROM School AS T1 JOIN budget AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id WHERE T2.Year = 2001 AND T1.Enrollment > (SELECT Enrollment FROM School WHERE School_name = 'Bremen');

sql: SELECT T2.donator_name FROM endowment AS T2 WHERE T2.School_id = (SELECT School_id FROM School WHERE School_name = 'Culver Community') EXCEPT SELECT T2.donator_name FROM endowment AS T2 WHERE T2.School_id = (SELECT School_id FROM School WHERE School_name = 'Bremen');

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T2.School_name, T1.Budgeted * 100.0 / T3.TotalYearlyBudget AS PercentageOfTotal FROM budget AS T1 JOIN School AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id JOIN (SELECT Year, SUM(Budgeted) AS TotalYearlyBudget FROM budget GROUP BY Year) AS T3 ON T1.Year = T3.Year;

sql: SELECT donator_name, SUM(amount) AS total_donated, COUNT(DISTINCT School_id) AS schools_donated_to FROM endowment GROUP BY donator_name ORDER BY total_donated DESC LIMIT 3;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Competition FROM game;

sql: SELECT Player, Injury FROM injury_accident WHERE Number_of_matches = '12';

sql: SELECT Home_team, Away_team, Score FROM game WHERE Competition = 'League';

sql: SELECT name, Total_Attendance FROM stadium WHERE Home_Games = 8;

sql: SELECT Player, Injury FROM injury_accident WHERE Source = 'inter.it';

sql: SELECT Date, Home_team, Away_team, Competition FROM game WHERE Season = 2007;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Home_team FROM game;

sql: SELECT T1.Date, T1.Home_team, T2.name FROM game AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.stadium_id = T2.id;

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T1.Injury, T2.Date FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id;

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) FROM game AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.stadium_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name;

sql: SELECT T1.game_id, COUNT(T1.id) AS NumberOfInjuries FROM injury_accident AS T1 GROUP BY T1.game_id;

sql: SELECT Player, COUNT(*) FROM injury_accident GROUP BY Player;

sql: SELECT Injury, AVG(Number_of_matches) FROM injury_accident GROUP BY Injury;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name FROM game AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.stadium_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Competition = 'League';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the unique names of stadiums that have hosted a game in the 'League' competition.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Player FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Score = '0–0';

sql: SELECT Home_team, COUNT(*) FROM game GROUP BY Home_team;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Player FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id JOIN stadium AS T3 ON T2.stadium_id = T3.id WHERE T3.name = 'Cowboys Stadium';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the distinct player who had an injury accident in a game played at 'Cowboys Stadium'.

sql: SELECT Source, COUNT(*) FROM injury_accident GROUP BY Source;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the source of the injury accident data and the total count of accidents from that source.

sql: SELECT T2.Date, T2.Home_team, T2.Away_team FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Number_of_matches > '2';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the date, home team, and away team for games where a player sustained an injury that caused them to miss more than two matches.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.Average_Attendance) FROM game AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.stadium_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Away_team = 'Pakhtakor';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Competition FROM game WHERE Home_team = 'Quruvchi';

sql: SELECT T1.Date, T1.Home_team, T1.Away_team FROM game AS T1 JOIN injury_accident AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.game_id;

sql: SELECT T3.name, SUM(T1.Number_of_matches) FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id JOIN stadium AS T3 ON T2.stadium_id = T3.id GROUP BY T3.name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each stadium by name along with the total count of matches associated with all injury accidents that occurred at that stadium.

sql: SELECT name, Total_Attendance FROM stadium WHERE Total_Attendance > (SELECT AVG(Total_Attendance) FROM stadium);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names and total attendance for stadiums with attendance figures greater than the overall average.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Player FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id WHERE T2.stadium_id IN (SELECT id FROM stadium WHERE Capacity_Percentage > 95);

sql: SELECT Player, SUM(Number_of_matches) FROM injury_accident GROUP BY Player HAVING SUM(Number_of_matches) > 5;

sql: SELECT T3.name FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id JOIN stadium AS T3 ON T2.stadium_id = T3.id GROUP BY T3.name HAVING COUNT(T1.id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM stadium AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM game AS T2 WHERE T2.stadium_id = T1.id AND T2.Competition = 'Cup');

sql: SELECT T1.Season, AVG(T1.injury_count) FROM (SELECT T2.Season, T2.id, COUNT(T3.id) AS injury_count FROM game AS T2 JOIN injury_accident AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.game_id GROUP BY T2.id, T2.Season) AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Season;

sql: SELECT T2.Date, T2.Home_team, T2.Away_team FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Number_of_matches > (SELECT AVG(Number_of_matches) FROM injury_accident);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the date, home team, and away team for games where an injury occurred that caused a player to miss more than the average number of matches.

sql: SELECT T2.Competition, AVG(T1.Number_of_matches) AS avg_missed FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.Competition ORDER BY avg_missed DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT name FROM stadium WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT stadium_id FROM game WHERE Competition = 'Cup');

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T1.Injury, T1.Number_of_matches FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id WHERE T2.stadium_id = (SELECT id FROM stadium ORDER BY Average_Attendance DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row detailing the player, their injury, and the number of matches missed for an injury that occurred at the stadium with the highest average attendance.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.Player, T1.game_id FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN injury_accident AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.game_id AND T1.id < T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying pairs of players who were injured in the same game, listing both players' names and the shared game ID for each incident.

sql: SELECT T3.name FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id JOIN stadium AS T3 ON T2.stadium_id = T3.id GROUP BY T3.name ORDER BY COUNT(T1.id) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Player FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Score = '1–1' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Player FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Score = '0–0';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the players who were injured in a game with a '1-1' score, but were not injured in a game with a '0-0' score.

sql: SELECT T3.name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.id), COUNT(T1.id) FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id JOIN stadium AS T3 ON T2.stadium_id = T3.id GROUP BY T3.name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each stadium's name, along with a count of distinct games that had an injury and the total number of injuries that occurred in that stadium.

sql: SELECT T2.Season FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.Season ORDER BY SUM(T1.Number_of_matches) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single season with the highest total number of matches where injuries occurred.

sql: SELECT T1.Date, T1.Home_team, T1.Away_team FROM game AS T1 WHERE T1.stadium_id IN (SELECT id FROM stadium WHERE Capacity_Percentage > (SELECT AVG(Capacity_Percentage) FROM stadium));
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the date, home team, and away team for games held in stadiums that have an above-average attendance percentage.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Player FROM injury_accident AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.game_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Competition = 'Cup';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique players who were injured during a game in the 'Cup' competition.

sql: SELECT name FROM stadium WHERE Average_Attendance < Total_Attendance / Home_Games;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of stadiums where the recorded average attendance is less than the calculated average attendance (total attendance divided by home games).

sql: SELECT Source, AVG(Number_of_matches) FROM injury_accident GROUP BY Source HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the average number of matches for each injury report source that appears more than once in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Player, Injury, Number_of_matches FROM injury_accident WHERE Number_of_matches = (SELECT MIN(Number_of_matches) FROM injury_accident);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the players, their specific injuries, and the number of matches missed for those injuries that resulted in the minimum number of missed matches across the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Home_team FROM game AS T1 WHERE T1.Competition IN ('League', 'Cup') GROUP BY T1.Home_team HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Competition) = 2;

sql: SELECT contestant_name, contestant_number FROM CONTESTANTS ORDER BY contestant_name DESC;

sql: SELECT phone_number FROM VOTES WHERE contestant_number = 3;

sql: SELECT vote_id, created FROM VOTES ORDER BY created ASC LIMIT 5;

sql: SELECT MAX(contestant_number) FROM CONTESTANTS;

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name, COUNT(T2.vote_id) FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number GROUP BY T1.contestant_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each contestant by name along with the total number of votes they received.

sql: SELECT state, COUNT(*) FROM VOTES GROUP BY state ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each state along with its total vote count, ordered from the state with the most votes to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 WHERE T1.contestant_number IN (SELECT contestant_number FROM VOTES WHERE state = 'CA');

sql: SELECT contestant_number, COUNT(*) FROM VOTES GROUP BY contestant_number HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
