sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T2.state = 'NY';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of contestants who received votes from the state of New York.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM VOTES AS T2 WHERE T2.contestant_number = T1.contestant_number AND T2.state = 'CA');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the contestant who received at least one vote from the state of California.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 LEFT JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T2.vote_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of all contestants who have not received any votes.

sql: SELECT state, COUNT(DISTINCT contestant_number) FROM VOTES GROUP BY state;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each state alongside the total count of unique contestants who received votes in that state.

sql: SELECT AVG(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS c FROM VOTES GROUP BY contestant_number);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of votes received per contestant.

sql: SELECT T1.state FROM AREA_CODE_STATE AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.state = T2.state GROUP BY T1.state;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique states that have both area code information and recorded votes.

sql: SELECT state FROM VOTES GROUP BY state UNION SELECT state FROM AREA_CODE_STATE WHERE state = 'NJ';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing a unique list of states, combining all states that have recorded votes with the state 'NJ' from an area code reference table.

sql: SELECT T2.state FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T1.contestant_name = 'Tabatha Gehling';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the state from which a vote was cast for the contestant named 'Tabatha Gehling'.

sql: SELECT CAST(substr(phone_number, 1, 3) AS INTEGER) AS derived_area_code, state FROM VOTES WHERE state = 'NY';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the derived phone area code and state for voters located in New York ('NY').

sql: SELECT state FROM VOTES GROUP BY state HAVING MAX(created) > '2018-03-09 19:03:30';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the states where the most recent vote was cast after the '2018-03-09 19:03:30' timestamp.

sql: SELECT contestant_number, COUNT(*) FROM VOTES WHERE state IN ('NY', 'CA') GROUP BY contestant_number;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, tallying the total number of votes each contestant received from the states of New York and California.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM VOTES AS T2 WHERE T2.contestant_number = T1.contestant_number AND T2.state = 'NY') > 0 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM VOTES AS T2 WHERE T2.contestant_number = T1.contestant_number AND T2.state = 'CA') > 0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the contestant who received votes from both New York and California.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN (SELECT contestant_number, COUNT(*) AS num_votes FROM VOTES GROUP BY contestant_number) AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number ORDER BY T2.num_votes DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the contestant who received the most votes.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T2.created = (SELECT MIN(created) FROM VOTES);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the contestant who received the very first vote cast, identified by the earliest vote timestamp.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T2.state = 'NY' EXCEPT SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T2.state = 'CA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the contestant who received votes from New York but did not receive any votes from California.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM VOTES AS T2 WHERE T2.contestant_number = T1.contestant_number) AS total_votes FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 ORDER BY T1.contestant_number;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each contestant's name and their total number of votes, ordered by the contestant's number.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 WHERE T1.contestant_number = (SELECT T2.contestant_number FROM VOTES AS T2 GROUP BY T2.contestant_number ORDER BY count(T2.vote_id) ASC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the contestant who received the fewest votes.

sql: WITH VoteCounts AS (SELECT contestant_number, COUNT(*) as v_count FROM VOTES GROUP BY contestant_number) SELECT T1.contestant_name, T2.v_count FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VoteCounts AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T2.v_count > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each contestant who received more than one vote, along with their total vote count.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM VOTES AS T2 WHERE T2.contestant_number = T1.contestant_number);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of contestants who have not received any votes.

sql: SELECT state FROM VOTES WHERE contestant_number = 2 UNION SELECT state FROM VOTES WHERE contestant_number = 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique states where votes were cast for either contestant number 2 or contestant number 3.

sql: SELECT T1.state, count(*) FROM VOTES AS T1 WHERE T1.contestant_number IN (SELECT contestant_number FROM CONTESTANTS WHERE contestant_name LIKE 'T%') GROUP BY T1.state;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the total vote count, grouped by state, for all contestants whose names begin with the letter 'T'.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name, T2.LatestVote FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN (SELECT contestant_number, MAX(created) as LatestVote FROM VOTES GROUP BY contestant_number) AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, providing each contestant's name and the timestamp of the last vote they received.

sql: SELECT state FROM VOTES WHERE contestant_number = 2 INTERSECT SELECT state FROM VOTES WHERE contestant_number = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the state where both contestant number 2 and contestant number 3 received votes.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 WHERE T1.contestant_number IN (SELECT contestant_number FROM VOTES WHERE state = 'NY') AND T1.contestant_number NOT IN (SELECT contestant_number FROM VOTES WHERE state = 'CA');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the contestant who received votes from New York but did not receive any votes from California.

sql: SELECT T1.state, COUNT(T1.vote_id) FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN AREA_CODE_STATE AS T2 ON T1.state = T2.state WHERE T2.area_code > 200 GROUP BY T1.state HAVING COUNT(T1.vote_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each state alongside the total count of votes, but only for states that have an area code greater than 200.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number GROUP BY T1.contestant_name HAVING COUNT(T2.vote_id) > (SELECT AVG(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS c FROM VOTES GROUP BY contestant_number));
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the contestant who received a total vote count greater than the average number of votes per contestant.

sql: SELECT T1.state, COALESCE(T2.v_count, 0) FROM AREA_CODE_STATE AS T1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT state, COUNT(*) as v_count FROM VOTES GROUP BY state) AS T2 ON T1.state = T2.state WHERE T1.state IN ('NJ', 'DC', 'CT');
result_summary: Returns 14 rows showing the total vote count for the specified states ('NJ', 'DC', 'CT'), displaying zero for any of these states that have no recorded votes.

sql: SELECT state, contestant_number FROM VOTES GROUP BY state, contestant_number EXCEPT SELECT state, contestant_number FROM VOTES WHERE contestant_number = 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique state and contestant number combinations that received votes, excluding any votes cast for contestant number 2.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T2.phone_number IN (SELECT phone_number FROM VOTES WHERE state = 'NY');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of contestants who received at least one vote from the state of New York.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.state, T3.area_code FROM VOTES AS T2 JOIN AREA_CODE_STATE AS T3 ON T2.state = T3.state WHERE T2.contestant_number = (SELECT contestant_number FROM VOTES GROUP BY contestant_number ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 50 rows listing the unique states and area codes from which the single contestant with the most votes received their support.

sql: SELECT state FROM VOTES WHERE contestant_number > 1 GROUP BY state HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT contestant_number) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the state that had votes for exactly two distinct contestants, excluding the first contestant (contestant_number 1).

sql: SELECT state FROM VOTES WHERE contestant_number = 3 GROUP BY state ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the state that cast the most votes for contestant number 3.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name, T3.state FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number JOIN AREA_CODE_STATE AS T3 ON CAST(substr(T2.phone_number, 1, 3) AS INTEGER) = T3.area_code WHERE T3.area_code = 201;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.contestant_name, T1.state, COUNT(T1.vote_id) AS vote_count FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number GROUP BY T2.contestant_name, T1.state ORDER BY T1.state, vote_count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the total vote count for each contestant by state, ordered alphabetically by state and then by the highest vote count within that state.

sql: SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T2.state = 'NY' UNION SELECT T1.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T1 JOIN VOTES AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T2.state = 'CT';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of contestants who received votes from either New York ('NY') or Connecticut ('CT').

sql: SELECT T1.state, COUNT(T1.vote_id) FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T2.contestant_name = 'Kelly Clauss' GROUP BY T1.state;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each state and the total number of votes cast from that state for the contestant 'Kelly Clauss'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE payment_method = 'Visa';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows containing all customer details for customers who use 'Visa' as their payment method.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products ORDER BY product_price DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and price of all products, ordered from highest to lowest price.

sql: SELECT customer_name, customer_email FROM Customers WHERE customer_name = 'Ron Emard';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and email address for the specific customer named 'Ron Emard'.

sql: SELECT truck_licence_number, truck_details FROM Trucks WHERE truck_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the license number and details for the specific truck with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT employee_name, employee_phone FROM Employees;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name and phone number for every employee in the directory.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Products;
result_summary: Returns a single value representing the total number of products.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT order_status_code FROM Actual_Orders;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each distinct order status code found in the orders.

sql: SELECT actual_order_id, actual_order_date FROM Actual_Orders WHERE actual_order_date > '2018-03-01';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the ID and date for all actual orders placed after March 1, 2018.

sql: SELECT address_details, city FROM Addresses WHERE country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the address details and corresponding city for all addresses located in the USA.

sql: SELECT route_name FROM Delivery_Routes WHERE route_id > 2;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the names of delivery routes for routes with an ID greater than 2.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T2.address_type, T2.date_from FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.address_type = 'Flat';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of customers with a 'Flat' address type, along with the date from which that address has been active.

sql: SELECT T1.actual_order_id, count(T2.product_id) FROM Actual_Orders AS T1 JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T2 ON T1.actual_order_id = T2.actual_order_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'Success' GROUP BY T1.actual_order_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing the ID for each successful order alongside a count of the total number of products within that order.

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name, T2.actual_order_id, T2.delivery_date FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T2 ON T1.employee_id = T2.driver_employee_id WHERE T1.employee_name = 'Kacie';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the order ID and delivery date for all deliveries handled by the employee named 'Kacie'.

sql: SELECT T1.truck_licence_number, count(T2.actual_order_id) FROM Trucks AS T1 JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T2 ON T1.truck_id = T2.truck_id GROUP BY T1.truck_licence_number;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, listing each truck by its license number along with the total count of orders it has delivered.

sql: SELECT T1.route_name, count(T2.location_code) FROM Delivery_Routes AS T1 JOIN Delivery_Route_Locations AS T2 ON T1.route_id = T2.route_id GROUP BY T1.route_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each delivery route name along with the total number of locations associated with that route.

sql: SELECT T1.actual_order_id, sum(T3.product_price) FROM Actual_Orders AS T1 JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T2 ON T1.actual_order_id = T2.actual_order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'Cancel' GROUP BY T1.actual_order_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing the total price of all products for each individual order that was canceled.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_price > (SELECT avg(product_price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and price for all products that are more expensive than the average product price.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, count(T1.regular_order_id) FROM Regular_Order_Products AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T2.product_name HAVING count(T1.regular_order_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing products that appear in more than one regular order, showing each product's name and its corresponding total order count.

sql: SELECT T1.regular_order_id, T3.product_name, T3.product_price FROM Regular_Orders AS T1 JOIN Regular_Order_Products AS T2 ON T1.regular_order_id = T2.regular_order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T1.regular_order_id = 5;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows detailing the products, including their names and prices, that are part of the regular order with an ID of 5.

sql: SELECT T1.actual_order_id, T2.employee_name, T3.truck_licence_number FROM Order_Deliveries AS T1 JOIN Employees AS T2 ON T1.driver_employee_id = T2.employee_id JOIN Trucks AS T3 ON T1.truck_id = T3.truck_id WHERE T1.delivery_status_code = 'On Road';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the order ID, assigned driver's name, and truck license number for all deliveries currently on the road.

sql: SELECT payment_method, count(customer_id) FROM Customers GROUP BY payment_method;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each payment method along with the total number of customers who use that method.

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name, T2.address_details, T2.city FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.employee_address_id = T2.address_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each employee's name along with their corresponding street address and city, by joining employee records to their associated addresses.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.product_name FROM Actual_Orders AS T1 JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T3 ON T1.actual_order_id = T3.actual_order_id JOIN Products AS T2 ON T3.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'Success';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of all products that have been part of a successfully completed order.

sql: SELECT city, COUNT(address_id) FROM Addresses GROUP BY city ORDER BY COUNT(address_id) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each city and its total address count, ordered from the cities with the most addresses to the least.

sql: SELECT customer_name, date_became_customer FROM Customers WHERE date_became_customer > (SELECT MIN(date_became_customer) FROM Customers);
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the name and sign-up date for every customer, excluding the original customer(s) who joined on the earliest date.

sql: SELECT T2.employee_name, count(DISTINCT T1.location_code) FROM Order_Deliveries AS T1 JOIN Employees AS T2 ON T1.driver_employee_id = T2.employee_id GROUP BY T2.employee_name HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.location_code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each driver who has delivered to more than one distinct location, along with the total count of unique locations they served.

sql: SELECT avg(product_count) FROM (SELECT count(T2.product_id) AS product_count FROM Actual_Orders AS T1 JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T2 ON T1.actual_order_id = T2.actual_order_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'Success' GROUP BY T1.actual_order_id) AS T;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average number of products included in each successful order.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id JOIN Delivery_Route_Locations AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.location_address_id JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T5 ON T4.location_code = T5.location_code JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T6 ON T5.actual_order_id = T6.actual_order_id WHERE T6.product_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of all customers who have purchased the product with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T2.employee_name FROM Order_Deliveries AS T1 JOIN Employees AS T2 ON T1.driver_employee_id = T2.employee_id WHERE T1.location_code = '27 City Rd' UNION SELECT employee_name FROM Employees WHERE employee_name = 'Kacie';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of employees who have delivered orders to '27 City Rd', in addition to the employee named 'Kacie'.

sql: SELECT T1.product_id FROM Regular_Order_Products AS T1 INTERSECT SELECT T3.product_id FROM Actual_Orders AS T2 JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T3 ON T2.actual_order_id = T3.actual_order_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'Cancel';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the product IDs that are part of regular orders and have also been included in orders that were cancelled.

sql: SELECT T3.product_id FROM Order_Deliveries AS T1 JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T2 ON T1.actual_order_id = T2.actual_order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T1.delivery_status_code = 'On Road' EXCEPT SELECT T3.product_id FROM Order_Deliveries AS T1 JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T2 ON T1.actual_order_id = T2.actual_order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T1.driver_employee_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the product ID for any product currently 'On Road' for delivery, excluding those being delivered by the driver with employee ID 1.

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T2 ON T1.employee_id = T2.driver_employee_id GROUP BY T1.employee_name HAVING count(T2.actual_order_id) > (SELECT avg(delivery_count) FROM (SELECT count(actual_order_id) AS delivery_count FROM Order_Deliveries GROUP BY driver_employee_id) AS T);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of drivers whose total number of deliveries is greater than the average number of deliveries across all drivers.

sql: SELECT sum(T4.product_price) FROM Order_Deliveries AS T1 JOIN Actual_Orders AS T2 ON T1.actual_order_id = T2.actual_order_id JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T3 ON T2.actual_order_id = T3.actual_order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T1.location_code = '27 City Rd' AND T2.order_status_code = 'Success';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the total value of all products from successful orders that were delivered to the '27 City Rd' location.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Customer_Addresses AS T2 JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id AND T3.country = 'USA');
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of customers who have at least one address located in the USA.

sql: SELECT T3.product_name, count(T2.product_id) FROM Actual_Orders AS T1 JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T2 ON T1.actual_order_id = T2.actual_order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'Success' GROUP BY T3.product_name ORDER BY count(T2.product_id) DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the top 3 most sold products from successful orders, listing each product name and its total sales count, ordered from most to least popular.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, sum(T8.product_price) AS total_spent FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id JOIN Delivery_Route_Locations AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.location_address_id JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T5 ON T4.location_code = T5.location_code JOIN Actual_Orders AS T6 ON T5.actual_order_id = T6.actual_order_id JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T7 ON T6.actual_order_id = T7.actual_order_id JOIN Products AS T8 ON T7.product_id = T8.product_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name HAVING sum(T8.product_price) > 5000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing high-value customers who have spent more than 5000, showing each customer's name and their total expenditure.

sql: SELECT T2.route_name FROM Delivery_Route_Locations AS T1 JOIN Delivery_Routes AS T2 ON T1.route_id = T2.route_id JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T3 ON T1.location_code = T3.location_code GROUP BY T2.route_name ORDER BY count(T3.actual_order_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the single delivery route with the most orders.

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.employee_id NOT IN (SELECT driver_employee_id FROM Order_Deliveries WHERE delivery_status_code = 'On Road');
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of employees who are not currently on the road making a delivery.

sql: SELECT T1.payment_method, sum(T8.product_price) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id JOIN Delivery_Route_Locations AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.location_address_id JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T5 ON T4.location_code = T5.location_code JOIN Actual_Orders AS T6 ON T5.actual_order_id = T6.actual_order_id JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T7 ON T6.actual_order_id = T7.actual_order_id JOIN Products AS T8 ON T7.product_id = T8.product_id GROUP BY T1.payment_method;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows that list each payment method alongside the total sales value from all products purchased using that method.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id JOIN Delivery_Route_Locations AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.location_address_id JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T5 ON T4.location_code = T5.location_code JOIN Actual_Orders AS T6 ON T5.actual_order_id = T6.actual_order_id WHERE T3.state_province_county = 'Colorado' AND T6.order_status_code = 'Cancel';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT avg(julianday(T2.delivery_date) - julianday(T1.actual_order_date)) FROM Actual_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T2 ON T1.actual_order_id = T2.actual_order_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'Success';
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average delivery time in days for all successful orders, calculated from the order date to the delivery date.

sql: SELECT T1.regular_order_id FROM Regular_Orders AS T1 LEFT JOIN Actual_Orders AS T2 ON T1.regular_order_id = T2.regular_order_id WHERE T2.actual_order_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the IDs of regular orders that do not have a corresponding record in the actual orders table, effectively identifying unfulfilled or missing orders.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, sum(CASE WHEN T3.order_status_code = 'Success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS successful_orders, sum(CASE WHEN T3.order_status_code = 'Cancel' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS cancelled_orders FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Actual_Orders AS T3 ON T2.actual_order_id = T3.actual_order_id GROUP BY T1.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each product alongside the total count of its successful and cancelled orders.

sql: SELECT T1.distributer_id FROM Regular_Orders AS T1 JOIN Regular_Order_Products AS T2 ON T1.regular_order_id = T2.regular_order_id GROUP BY T1.distributer_id ORDER BY count(DISTINCT T2.product_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the ID of the distributor that has ordered the greatest variety of distinct products.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name, T5.employee_name, T4.city FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.city = T4.city JOIN Employees AS T5 ON T4.address_id = T5.employee_address_id;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing unique customer-employee pairs who live in the same city, providing the customer name, employee name, and their shared city.

sql: SELECT T1.actual_order_id FROM Order_Deliveries AS T1 JOIN Employees AS T2 ON T1.driver_employee_id = T2.employee_id WHERE T2.employee_name = 'Kacie' AND T1.actual_order_id IN (SELECT actual_order_id FROM Actual_Order_Products GROUP BY actual_order_id HAVING count(product_id) > 2);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the order ID for an order delivered by the employee 'Kacie' which also contained more than two products.

sql: SELECT CAST(sum(CASE WHEN order_status_code = 'Cancel' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / count(*) FROM Actual_Orders;
result_summary: Returns a single row and column calculating the overall percentage of orders from the `Actual_Orders` table that have been cancelled.

sql: SELECT T1.truck_licence_number, T3.employee_name FROM Trucks AS T1 JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T2 ON T1.truck_id = T2.truck_id JOIN Employees AS T3 ON T2.driver_employee_id = T3.employee_id WHERE T2.delivery_date = (SELECT max(delivery_date) FROM Order_Deliveries WHERE truck_id = T1.truck_id);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each truck's license number along with the name of the employee who drove the truck for its most recent delivery.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE strftime('%Y', T1.date_became_customer) = '2011' AND T2.address_type = 'House' INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id JOIN Delivery_Route_Locations AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.location_address_id JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T5 ON T4.location_code = T5.location_code JOIN Actual_Orders AS T6 ON T5.actual_order_id = T6.actual_order_id WHERE T6.order_status_code = 'Success';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.route_name, max(T5.product_price) FROM Delivery_Routes AS T1 JOIN Delivery_Route_Locations AS T2 ON T1.route_id = T2.route_id JOIN Order_Deliveries AS T3 ON T2.location_code = T3.location_code JOIN Actual_Order_Products AS T4 ON T3.actual_order_id = T4.actual_order_id JOIN Products AS T5 ON T4.product_id = T5.product_id GROUP BY T1.route_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each delivery route and the price of the most expensive product delivered on that route.

sql: SELECT * FROM ACCOUNTS;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing all customer accounts, including their customer ID and name.

sql: SELECT * FROM SAVINGS;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all customer savings accounts with their corresponding customer ID and balance.

sql: SELECT * FROM CHECKING;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each customer's ID and their corresponding checking account balance.

sql: SELECT custid, name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE name = 'Brown';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the customer ID and name for any account holder named 'Brown'.

sql: SELECT custid, balance FROM SAVINGS WHERE balance = 230000.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the customer ID and balance for any customer with an exact savings balance of $230,000.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each customer's name along with their corresponding savings account balance.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name of each customer alongside the balance in their corresponding checking account.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance = 200000.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any customer who has a savings account with a balance of exactly $200,000.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance = 3000.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer who has an exact checking account balance of $3,000.

sql: SELECT custid, balance FROM CHECKING WHERE balance > 2500;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the customer ID and balance for all checking accounts with a balance over $2,500.

sql: SELECT SUM(balance) FROM SAVINGS;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single value representing the total balance summed across all savings accounts.

sql: SELECT AVG(balance) FROM CHECKING;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average balance across all checking accounts.

sql: SELECT MAX(balance) FROM SAVINGS;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the highest balance found across all savings accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance + T3.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each customer's name along with their total combined balance from both their savings and checking accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance = (SELECT MIN(balance) FROM CHECKING);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer who has the lowest balance in their checking account.

sql: SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE name LIKE 'W%';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all accounts that start with the letter 'W'.

sql: SELECT SUM(T2.balance) FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T1.name IN ('Brown', 'Wang');
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total combined savings account balance for all customers named 'Brown' or 'Wang'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, 'SAVINGS' AS account_type, T2.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid UNION ALL SELECT T1.name, 'CHECKING' AS account_type, T3.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows combining all customer savings and checking accounts, listing the customer name, the type of account ('SAVINGS' or 'CHECKING'), and its corresponding balance.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance + T3.balance AS total_assets FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid ORDER BY total_assets DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each customer's name and the sum of their savings and checking account balances, ordered from the highest total assets to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance < (SELECT T4.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T3 JOIN CHECKING AS T4 ON T3.custid = T4.custid WHERE T3.name = 'Brown');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of customers whose checking account balance is less than the balance of the customer named 'Brown'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid WHERE T2.balance > 200000 AND T3.balance > 2500;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a customer who has a savings balance greater than $200,000 and a checking balance greater than $2,500.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM SAVINGS AS T2 WHERE T1.custid = T2.custid);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all customers who have a savings account.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance = (SELECT MAX(balance) FROM SAVINGS);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer with the highest savings account balance.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT custid FROM SAVINGS UNION ALL SELECT custid FROM CHECKING);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single count of the total number of savings and checking accounts.

sql: SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE name <> 'Brown';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all accounts except for the account named 'Brown'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid WHERE T2.balance > T3.balance * 20;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of customers whose savings account balance is more than 20 times their checking account balance.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance < (SELECT AVG(balance) FROM CHECKING);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers whose checking account balance is less than the overall average balance across all checking accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance > (SELECT AVG(balance) FROM SAVINGS);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the customer whose savings account balance exceeds the average balance of all savings accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name, CASE WHEN T2.balance + T3.balance > 200000 THEN 'High-Value' ELSE 'Standard' END AS customer_tier FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each customer's name and a calculated tier, labeling them 'High-Value' if their combined savings and checking account balance exceeds 200,000.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance + T3.balance AS total_assets FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid WHERE T2.balance + T3.balance = (SELECT MAX(S.balance + C.balance) FROM SAVINGS AS S JOIN CHECKING AS C ON S.custid = C.custid);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the customer with the highest total assets, calculated by summing their checking and savings account balances.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance < (SELECT T4.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T3 JOIN SAVINGS AS T4 ON T3.custid = T4.custid WHERE T3.name = 'Wang');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of customers whose savings account balance is less than the balance of the customer named 'Wang'.

sql: WITH TotalAssets AS (SELECT T1.custid, T2.balance + T3.balance AS total FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid) SELECT T4.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T4 JOIN TotalAssets ON T4.custid = TotalAssets.custid WHERE TotalAssets.total > 220000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of customers whose combined balance across their savings and checking accounts exceeds $220,000.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance / T3.balance AS savings_to_checking_ratio FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each customer's name alongside the calculated ratio of their savings account balance to their checking account balance.

sql: SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS EXCEPT SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE name = 'Brown';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all accounts, excluding the account named 'Brown'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance > 200000 INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid WHERE T3.balance < 5000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of customers who have a savings account balance over 200,000 and also have a checking account balance under 5,000.

sql: SELECT T1.name, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY T2.balance + T3.balance DESC) AS asset_rank FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each customer's name and their corresponding asset rank, which is determined by ordering customers from highest to lowest based on their combined savings and checking account balances.

sql: SELECT T1.name, (T2.balance * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(balance) FROM SAVINGS)) AS percentage_of_total_savings FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each customer's name and their savings balance as a percentage of the total savings across all customers.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 WHERE T1.custid IN (SELECT custid FROM CHECKING WHERE balance < (SELECT AVG(balance) FROM CHECKING)) OR T1.custid IN (SELECT custid FROM SAVINGS WHERE balance > (SELECT AVG(balance) FROM SAVINGS));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers who either have a checking account balance below the average or a savings account balance above the average.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid ORDER BY T2.balance LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer with the second-lowest savings account balance.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance = 2000.0 UNION SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid WHERE T3.balance = 230000.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of customers who have either a checking account balance of exactly $2,000 or a savings account balance of exactly $230,000.

sql: SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE custid NOT IN (SELECT custid FROM CHECKING WHERE balance = 10000.0);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of account holders who do not have a checking account with a balance of exactly $10,000.

sql: SELECT T3.name FROM (SELECT T1.custid, T1.balance + T2.balance AS total_assets FROM SAVINGS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid) AS T_ASSETS JOIN ACCOUNTS AS T3 ON T_ASSETS.custid = T3.custid WHERE T_ASSETS.total_assets > (SELECT AVG(T1.balance + T2.balance) FROM SAVINGS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the customer whose combined savings and checking account balance is greater than the average combined balance for all customers.

sql: SELECT T2.balance + T3.balance AS total_assets FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid WHERE T1.name = 'O''mahony';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total assets for the customer named 'O'mahony', calculated by summing their savings and checking account balances.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM SAVINGS AS S JOIN CHECKING AS C ON S.custid = C.custid WHERE S.custid = T1.custid AND S.balance > 3 * C.balance);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of customers whose savings account balance is more than three times their checking account balance.

sql: SELECT (SELECT SUM(balance) FROM CHECKING) AS total_checking, (SELECT SUM(balance) FROM SAVINGS) AS total_savings;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total balance from all checking accounts and the total balance from all savings accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid ORDER BY T2.balance DESC LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the two customers with the highest savings account balances.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM CHECKING AS T2 WHERE T1.custid = T2.custid AND T2.balance = 2000.0);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of account holders who do not have a checking account with a balance of exactly $2000.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T4.name FROM (SELECT T1.name, T2.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid) AS T3, (SELECT T1.name, T2.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid) AS T4 WHERE T3.balance > T4.balance;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing every pair of customers where the first customer's savings account balance is greater than the second customer's balance.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid ORDER BY ABS(T2.balance - (SELECT AVG(balance) FROM CHECKING)) LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer whose checking account balance is closest to the overall average balance across all checking accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid WHERE T2.balance + T3.balance = (SELECT MIN(S.balance + C.balance) FROM SAVINGS AS S JOIN CHECKING AS C ON S.custid = C.custid);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer with the smallest total balance across their savings and checking accounts.

sql: SELECT Country_name, Capital FROM country ORDER BY Country_name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each country's name and its capital, sorted alphabetically by country name.

sql: SELECT Name FROM team;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name of every team.

sql: SELECT Player, College FROM match_season WHERE Draft_Class = '1996 MLS College Draft';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the player and their college for anyone selected in the '1996 MLS College Draft'.

sql: SELECT Player, Season FROM match_season WHERE College = 'Florida International';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the player's name and the season they played, for players who attended 'Florida International' college.

sql: SELECT Player, Team FROM match_season WHERE Position = 'Midfielder';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the player and their corresponding team for all players whose position is 'Midfielder'.

sql: SELECT Player, Total_WL FROM player WHERE Team = 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names and total win-loss records for all players belonging to Team 1.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT College FROM match_season;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, providing a unique list of all colleges that have participated in a match season.

sql: SELECT Player, Position, College FROM match_season WHERE Season = 1996.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the player's name, position, and college for the 1996 season.

sql: SELECT Player_ID, Years_Played, Singles_WL FROM player WHERE Player = 'Im Kyu-tae (492)';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the Player ID, years played, and singles win-loss record for the specific player named 'Im Kyu-tae (492)'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM team WHERE Team_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the team with a specific Team ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.Name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each player from the match season alongside the name of their corresponding team.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.Country_name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country = T2.Country_id;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each player's name from the match season data alongside their corresponding country's name.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Player) FROM team AS T2 JOIN match_season AS T1 ON T2.Team_id = T1.Team GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each team's name along with the total number of matches played by that team in the season.

sql: SELECT Position, AVG(Draft_Pick_Number) FROM match_season GROUP BY Position;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each player position and the corresponding average draft pick number for players in that position.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.Name FROM player AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each player and the name of the team they belong to.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.Name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id WHERE T1.Position = 'Defender';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of players who are defenders and the name of the team they play for.

sql: SELECT College, COUNT(*) FROM match_season GROUP BY College;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, providing a count of matches or season entries for each college.

sql: SELECT Season, COUNT(*) FROM match_season GROUP BY Season;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each season and the total count of matches played during that season.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.Name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id WHERE T1.Draft_Pick_Number = 18;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the player's name and their corresponding team's name for players who were the 18th draft pick.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.Name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id WHERE T1.College = 'UNLV';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the player and their corresponding team name for any player who attended 'UNLV' college.

sql: SELECT T1.Player FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id WHERE T2.Name = 'Columbus Crew';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a player on the 'Columbus Crew' team.

sql: SELECT Season, COUNT(*) FROM match_season WHERE College = 'Florida International' GROUP BY Season;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the number of matches played per season for the 'Florida International' college.

sql: SELECT Draft_Class, COUNT(*) FROM match_season GROUP BY Draft_Class;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each draft class and providing a count of how many match seasons are associated with it.

sql: SELECT Player FROM match_season WHERE Season = 1996.0 UNION SELECT Player FROM match_season WHERE Season = 1998.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique players who participated in either the 1996 or 1998 seasons.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM team AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.Team_id = T2.Team GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of teams that have at least one associated player.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Player) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of teams that had at least one player participate in a match during the season.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id GROUP BY T2.Name ORDER BY AVG(T1.Draft_Pick_Number) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single team with the highest average draft pick number.

sql: SELECT Player FROM match_season WHERE Team IN (SELECT Team FROM player);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of players from the match season, filtered to include only those whose team is also present in the player table.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM team AS T1 JOIN match_season AS T2 ON T1.Team_id = T2.Team JOIN player AS T3 ON T1.Team_id = T3.Team;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of teams that have both participated in a match season and have associated players.

sql: SELECT Draft_Class FROM match_season GROUP BY Draft_Class ORDER BY AVG(Draft_Pick_Number) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the draft class with the highest average draft pick number.

sql: SELECT Player FROM match_season WHERE Player NOT IN (SELECT Player FROM match_season WHERE College = 'Virginia');
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the names of all players who did not attend 'Virginia' college.

sql: SELECT College FROM match_season WHERE Draft_Pick_Number < 20 GROUP BY College ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single college that has produced the most draft picks selected within the top 19 positions.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.Name, T3.Country_name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id JOIN country AS T3 ON T1.Country = T3.Country_id WHERE T1.Position = 'Midfielder' OR T1.Position = 'Defender';
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the player, their team name, and their country for all players whose position is either 'Midfielder' or 'Defender'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM team AS T1 WHERE T1.Team_id IN (SELECT Team FROM player INTERSECT SELECT Team FROM match_season);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of teams that have both registered players and have participated in a match season.

sql: SELECT T1.Player FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id WHERE T1.College = 'Virginia' AND T2.Name = 'Anais VonRueden';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of players from 'Virginia' college who played for the team named 'Anais VonRueden'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Draft_Pick_Number) FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id WHERE T1.Country = 6 GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows showing the average draft pick number for each team within a specific country (ID 6).

sql: SELECT Player FROM player AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM match_season AS T2 WHERE T2.Team = T1.Team AND T2.Draft_Class = '1996 MLS College Draft');

sql: SELECT Name FROM team WHERE Team_id IN (SELECT Team FROM match_season) AND Team_id IN (SELECT Team FROM player);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of teams that have both participated in a match season and have registered players.

sql: SELECT T3.Country_name, COUNT(T1.Player) FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id JOIN country AS T3 ON T1.Country = T3.Country_id WHERE T1.Position = 'Midfielder' GROUP BY T3.Country_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each country and the total number of players from that country who play the 'Midfielder' position.

sql: SELECT Player, Draft_Pick_Number FROM match_season WHERE Draft_Pick_Number < (SELECT Draft_Pick_Number FROM match_season WHERE Player = 'Mike Duhaney Category:Articles with hCards');
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the player and draft pick number for all players who were drafted with a lower pick number than 'Mike Duhaney'.

sql: SELECT Player FROM match_season WHERE Team = (SELECT Team FROM match_season WHERE Player = 'Steve Ralston Category:Articles with hCards');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the player(s) on the same team as 'Steve Ralston Category:Articles with hCards'.

sql: SELECT Season FROM match_season GROUP BY Season ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single season with the most matches played, determined by counting the number of matches per season.

sql: SELECT Player, Draft_Pick_Number FROM match_season WHERE Draft_Pick_Number > (SELECT AVG(Draft_Pick_Number) FROM match_season);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing players whose draft pick number was higher than the average draft pick number across all players.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id EXCEPT SELECT T4.Name FROM player AS T3 JOIN team AS T4 ON T3.Team = T4.Team_id;

sql: SELECT T2.Country_name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country = T2.Country_id GROUP BY T2.Country_name HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of countries that have had at least one recorded match season.

sql: SELECT T1.Player FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country = T2.Country_id WHERE T2.Country_name IN ('Indonesia', 'Iran', 'Iraq');

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Player) FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id WHERE T1.College = 'Florida International' OR T1.College = 'UNLV' GROUP BY T2.Name;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Player, T2.Name, T3.Country_name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id JOIN country AS T3 ON T1.Country = T3.Country_id WHERE T1.Draft_Class = 'Project-40';

sql: SELECT tourney_level, AVG(winner_rank) FROM matches WHERE winner_rank IS NOT NULL GROUP BY tourney_level;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each tournament level and the corresponding average rank of the winner for matches at that level.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.ranking FROM players AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T2.ranking <= 10 AND T2.ranking_date = 20000101;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first name, last name, and ranking for players who were ranked in the top 10 on January 1st, 2000.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, COUNT(T1.winner_id) AS hard_court_wins FROM matches AS T1 JOIN players AS T2 ON T1.winner_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.surface = 'Hard' GROUP BY T1.winner_id HAVING COUNT(T1.winner_id) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 21 rows identifying players with more than two wins on a hard court, showing their first name, last name, and the total count of these wins.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM players WHERE player_id IN (SELECT winner_id FROM matches WHERE loser_name = 'Agnieszka Radwanska');
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the first and last names of every player who has defeated 'Agnieszka Radwanska'.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM matches AS T1 JOIN players AS T2 ON T1.winner_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.surface = 'Hard' GROUP BY T1.winner_id ORDER BY COUNT(T1.winner_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the player with the most wins in matches played on a 'Hard' surface.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM matches AS T1 JOIN players AS T2 ON T1.winner_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.tourney_name = 'WTA Championships' INTERSECT SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM matches AS T1 JOIN players AS T2 ON T1.loser_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.tourney_name = 'WTA Championships';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of players who have both won and lost at least one match in the 'WTA Championships' tournament.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM players WHERE country_code = 'SUI' EXCEPT SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM matches AS T1 JOIN players AS T2 ON T1.loser_id = T2.player_id WHERE T2.country_code = 'SUI';
result_summary: Returns 242 rows listing the first and last names of all Swiss players who have never lost a match.

sql: SELECT winner_name, loser_name, winner_rank, loser_rank, (winner_rank - loser_rank) AS rank_upset FROM matches WHERE winner_rank > loser_rank ORDER BY rank_upset DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single biggest ranking upset, showing the winner and loser, their ranks, and the calculated rank difference by which the match was won.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, T1.winner_rank, T1.loser_rank FROM matches AS T1 JOIN players AS T2 ON T1.winner_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.winner_rank > T1.loser_rank;
result_summary: Returns 110 rows for all upset match victories where a lower-ranked player defeated a higher-ranked one, listing the winner's full name alongside both the winner's and loser's rank.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM matches AS T1 JOIN players AS T2 ON T1.winner_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.winner_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.surface) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 21 rows listing the first and last names of players who have demonstrated versatility by winning matches on at least two different court surfaces.

sql: SELECT tourney_name FROM matches GROUP BY tourney_name HAVING AVG(winner_age) < 30;
result_summary: Returns 47 rows listing the names of tournaments where the average age of the winners is less than 30.

sql: SELECT T1.country_code, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.winner_id) AS distinct_winners FROM players AS T1 JOIN matches AS T2 ON T1.country_code = T2.winner_ioc GROUP BY T1.country_code HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.winner_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each country that has more than one unique match winner, along with the total count of distinct winners for each of those countries.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM players AS T1 WHERE T1.player_id IN (SELECT player_id FROM rankings WHERE ranking = 1) AND T1.player_id IN (SELECT winner_id FROM matches);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of players who have achieved a number 1 ranking and have also won at least one match.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM players AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM matches WHERE winner_id = T1.player_id AND loser_ioc = 'POL') > (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM matches WHERE loser_id = T1.player_id AND winner_ioc = 'POL');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of players who have won more matches against opponents from Poland than they have lost.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM matches AS T1 JOIN players AS T2 ON T1.winner_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.tourney_name = 'WTA Championships' AND T1.round = 'RR' UNION SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM matches AS T1 JOIN players AS T2 ON T1.loser_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.tourney_name = 'WTA Championships' AND T1.round = 'RR';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of all unique players who participated in the Round Robin ('RR') round of the 'WTA Championships' tournament.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, T1.ranking FROM rankings AS T1 JOIN players AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.ranking_date = 20000101 AND T1.ranking < (SELECT ranking FROM rankings WHERE player_id = 200748 AND ranking_date = 20000101);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.country_code, T3.first_name, T3.last_name, T1.best_rank FROM (SELECT P.country_code, MIN(R.ranking) AS best_rank FROM players AS P JOIN rankings AS R ON P.player_id = R.player_id GROUP BY P.country_code) AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T2 ON T1.best_rank = T2.ranking JOIN players AS T3 ON T2.player_id = T3.player_id AND T1.country_code = T3.country_code GROUP BY T1.country_code;
result_summary: Returns 95 rows, one for each country, identifying the player who achieved that nation's all-time best ranking, along with their name and that specific rank.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM players AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM matches WHERE winner_id = T1.player_id AND year = 2013) AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM rankings WHERE player_id = T1.player_id AND ranking <= 10 AND ranking_date = 20000101);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last name of any player who was ranked in the top 10 on January 1st, 2000, and also won a match during the year 2013.

sql: SELECT winner_name FROM matches GROUP BY winner_name HAVING COUNT(winner_id) > (SELECT AVG(win_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(winner_id) AS win_count FROM matches GROUP BY winner_id));
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the names of match winners whose total number of wins is greater than the average number of wins across all players.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, T3.first_name, T3.last_name, COUNT(*) AS matches_played FROM matches AS T1 JOIN players AS T2 ON T1.winner_id = T2.player_id JOIN players AS T3 ON T1.loser_id = T3.player_id GROUP BY T2.player_id, T3.player_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 39 rows identifying head-to-head matchups where one player has beaten another more than once, showing the full names of the winner and loser alongside the total number of victories.

sql: SELECT T3.first_name, T3.last_name FROM (SELECT winner_id AS player_id, surface FROM matches UNION ALL SELECT loser_id, surface FROM matches) AS T1 JOIN players AS T3 ON T1.player_id = T3.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.surface) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the first and last names of players who have competed exclusively on a single, unique type of court surface.

sql: SELECT winner_name, minutes FROM matches WHERE minutes = (SELECT MAX(minutes) FROM matches WHERE surface = 'Hard');
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the winner's name and the duration in minutes for the longest match played on a 'Hard' surface.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.winner_name, T3.ranking FROM matches AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T3 ON T1.winner_id = T3.player_id WHERE T1.tourney_name = 'WTA Championships' AND SUBSTR(CAST(T3.ranking_date AS TEXT), 1, 4) = CAST(T1.year AS TEXT);
result_summary: Returns 40 rows listing the unique names and rankings of players who won the 'WTA Championships' tournament, with the ranking taken from the same year as their victory.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, CAST((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM matches WHERE winner_id = T1.player_id) AS REAL) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM matches WHERE loser_id = T1.player_id) AS win_loss_ratio FROM players AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM matches WHERE loser_id = T1.player_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 49 rows for players with at least one recorded loss, listing each player's first name, last name, and their calculated win/loss ratio.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.ranking_points FROM players AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.country_code = 'SUI' AND T2.ranking_date = 20000101 AND T2.ranking_points > (SELECT AVG(T4.ranking_points) FROM rankings AS T4 JOIN players AS T3 ON T4.player_id = T3.player_id WHERE T3.country_code = 'SUI' AND T4.ranking_date = 20000101);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.best_rank, T2.worst_rank FROM players AS T1 JOIN (SELECT player_id, MIN(ranking) AS best_rank, MAX(ranking) AS worst_rank FROM rankings GROUP BY player_id) AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id;
result_summary: Returns 2775 rows, listing each player's full name along with their calculated career-best (minimum) and career-worst (maximum) historical ranking.

sql: SELECT tourney_name FROM matches WHERE winner_rank IS NOT NULL GROUP BY tourney_name HAVING MIN(winner_rank) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of tournaments where a player ranked number one has won at least one match.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM players AS T1 LEFT JOIN matches AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.winner_id WHERE T2.winner_id IS NULL AND T1.player_id IN (SELECT player_id FROM rankings);
result_summary: Returns 2737 rows listing the first and last names of ranked players who have never won a match.

sql: SELECT * FROM list;
result_summary: Returns 60 rows representing a complete student roster, listing each student's last name, first name, grade, and assigned classroom.

sql: SELECT * FROM teachers;

result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the last and first names of all students in the 2nd grade.

result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the last name, first name, grade, and classroom number for all students assigned to classroom 101.

sql: SELECT T1.LastName, T1.FirstName, T2.LastName AS TeacherLastName FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom;

sql: SELECT Grade, COUNT(*) FROM list GROUP BY Grade;

sql: SELECT Classroom, COUNT(*) FROM list GROUP BY Classroom;

sql: SELECT T1.LastName, T1.FirstName FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom WHERE T2.LastName = 'MACROSTIE';

sql: SELECT LastName, FirstName, Classroom FROM list WHERE Classroom = 101 OR Classroom = 107;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.LastName, T2.FirstName FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom WHERE T1.Grade = 2;

sql: SELECT LastName, FirstName, COUNT(*) FROM teachers GROUP BY LastName, FirstName;

sql: SELECT MIN(Grade), MAX(Grade) FROM list;
result_summary: Returns a single row identifying the minimum and maximum grade values from the entire list.

sql: SELECT T1.LastName, T1.FirstName, T2.LastName AS TeacherLastName FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom ORDER BY T2.LastName;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.LastName, T2.FirstName FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom WHERE T1.Grade = 6;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the unique teacher(s) assigned to teach 6th grade by their last and first names.

sql: SELECT T1.LastName, T1.FirstName FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom WHERE T2.FirstName = ' OTHA' AND T2.LastName = 'MOYER';

sql: SELECT T1.LastName, T1.FirstName, T2.LastName AS TeacherName FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom WHERE T1.Classroom = 101;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of all students in classroom 101, along with the last name of their assigned teacher.

sql: SELECT T2.FirstName, T2.LastName, T1.Classroom, COUNT(T1.LastName) FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom GROUP BY T1.Classroom HAVING COUNT(T1.LastName) > 1;

sql: SELECT FirstName, LastName FROM list WHERE Classroom IN (SELECT Classroom FROM teachers WHERE LastName = 'MACROSTIE');

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName FROM teachers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM list AS T2 WHERE T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom);

result_summary: Returns 71 rows, providing a unique list of all first names by combining records from both the `list` and `teachers` tables.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, (SELECT T2.LastName FROM teachers AS T2 WHERE T2.Classroom = T1.Classroom) AS TeacherLastName FROM list AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 60 rows listing each student's first and last name, along with the last name of the teacher assigned to that student's classroom.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, T2.NumStudents FROM list AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Classroom, COUNT(*) AS NumStudents FROM list GROUP BY Classroom) AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom;
result_summary: Returns 60 rows listing each student's first and last name, along with the total number of students in their respective classroom.

sql: SELECT T2.LastName, T2.FirstName, AVG(T1.Grade) FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom GROUP BY T2.LastName, T2.FirstName;

sql: SELECT T2.LastName FROM teachers AS T2 JOIN list AS T1 ON T2.Classroom = T1.Classroom GROUP BY T2.LastName, T1.Classroom HAVING AVG(T1.Grade) > (SELECT AVG(Grade) FROM list);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the last names of teachers whose classrooms have an average student grade that is higher than the overall average grade across all students.

sql: SELECT T1.LastName, T1.FirstName, T2.LastName, T2.FirstName, T1.Classroom FROM list AS T1 JOIN list AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom WHERE T1.LastName > T2.LastName;

sql: SELECT FirstName, COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT FirstName FROM list UNION ALL SELECT FirstName FROM teachers) AS T GROUP BY FirstName ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC;

sql: SELECT FirstName, LastName, Grade FROM list WHERE Grade > (SELECT AVG(Grade) FROM list WHERE Classroom = 101);

sql: SELECT * FROM manufacturer;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows providing a complete list of all manufacturers, detailing their ID, opening year, name, and the number of factories and shops they operate.

sql: SELECT Name, Market_Rate FROM furniture;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and market rate for each piece of furniture.

sql: SELECT * FROM manufacturer WHERE Name = 'IKEA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with all details for the manufacturer named 'IKEA', including its opening year and the number of factories and shops it operates.

sql: SELECT Name, Num_of_Component FROM furniture WHERE Market_Rate = 40.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and number of components for furniture with a market rate of exactly 40.0.

sql: SELECT Name, Num_of_Factories FROM manufacturer ORDER BY Num_of_Factories DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each manufacturer and their number of factories, ordered from the manufacturer with the most factories to the fewest.

sql: SELECT Name, Num_of_Component FROM furniture ORDER BY Num_of_Component ASC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each furniture piece's name and its number of components, ordered from the fewest components to the most.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM manufacturer;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all manufacturers.

sql: SELECT avg(Market_Rate) FROM furniture;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average market rate calculated across all furniture items.

sql: SELECT Name, Num_of_Shops FROM manufacturer WHERE Open_Year = 1980;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and number of shops for any manufacturer that opened in the year 1980.

sql: SELECT max(Price_in_Dollar) FROM furniture_manufacte;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the maximum price in dollars for any piece of manufactured furniture.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID JOIN furniture AS T3 ON T2.Furniture_ID = T3.Furniture_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name of each manufacturer alongside the name of a piece of furniture they produce.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, count(T2.Furniture_ID) FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each manufacturer's name along with the total number of furniture items they produce.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, count(T2.Manufacturer_ID) FROM furniture AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Furniture_ID = T2.Furniture_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each type of furniture along with the total number of manufacturers that produce it.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, avg(T2.Price_in_Dollar) FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each manufacturer's name along with the average dollar price of the furniture they produce.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING count(T2.Furniture_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a manufacturer who produces more than one type of furniture.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID JOIN furniture AS T3 ON T2.Furniture_ID = T3.Furniture_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Chevrolet House';

sql: SELECT Name FROM manufacturer WHERE Manufacturer_ID NOT IN (SELECT Manufacturer_ID FROM furniture_manufacte);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all manufacturers that are not identified as furniture manufacturers.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T3.Market_Rate, T2.Price_in_Dollar, T1.Name FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID JOIN furniture AS T3 ON T2.Furniture_ID = T3.Furniture_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing furniture items along with their market rate, price in dollars, and the name of the manufacturer who produces them.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, sum(T3.Num_of_Component) FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID JOIN furniture AS T3 ON T2.Furniture_ID = T3.Furniture_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each manufacturer's name and the total number of components used across all furniture they produce.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T1.Name FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID JOIN furniture AS T3 ON T2.Furniture_ID = T3.Furniture_ID WHERE T3.Num_of_Component > 5;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing furniture items that have more than 5 components, along with the name of the manufacturer for each item.

result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all car manufacturers that were established after the year 1990.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, avg(T2.Price_in_Dollar) FROM furniture AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Furniture_ID = T2.Furniture_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each furniture name along with its average price in dollars, calculated across different manufacturers.

sql: SELECT * FROM furniture WHERE Name IN ('Bookcase', 'Chabudai');

sql: SELECT Name, Num_of_Shops FROM manufacturer WHERE Num_of_Shops = (SELECT max(Num_of_Shops) FROM manufacturer);
