sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Train_ID) FROM station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each station's name along with the total number of trains that pass through it.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Station_ID) FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, listing each train by name along with a count of the total number of stations on its route.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows, listing each train's name alongside the name of a station it services.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'London Waterloo';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of trains that have a scheduled stop at the 'London Waterloo' station.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Guruvayur Express';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the stations where the 'Guruvayur Express' train stops.

sql: SELECT AVG(Number_of_Platforms) FROM station;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average number of platforms across all stations.

sql: SELECT SUM(Total_Passengers) FROM station WHERE Location = 'London';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of passengers across all stations located in London.

sql: SELECT Name, Main_Services FROM station WHERE Main_Services LIKE '%Main Line%';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the name and main services for all stations whose services include a 'Main Line'.

sql: SELECT Name, Number_of_Platforms FROM station WHERE Number_of_Platforms = (SELECT MAX(Number_of_Platforms) FROM station);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the station or stations that have the maximum number of platforms, showing their name and platform count.

sql: SELECT Service, COUNT(*) FROM train GROUP BY Service;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each train service type and the total number of trains operating under that service.

sql: SELECT Name, Time FROM train WHERE Service = 'Daily' ORDER BY Time;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names and departure times for all trains with daily service, ordered chronologically from the earliest time.

sql: SELECT SUM(Number_of_Platforms) FROM station WHERE Location = 'London';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of platforms across all train stations located in London.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID WHERE T2.Station_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all trains that pass through the station with ID 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID WHERE T2.Train_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the station served by the train with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT AVG(Annual_interchanges) FROM station WHERE Location = 'London';
result_summary: Returns a single value representing the average number of annual interchanges for all stations located in London.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID GROUP BY T1.Station_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Train_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of train stations that are served by more than one train line.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Train_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the single train station that serves the highest number of trains.

sql: SELECT Name, Total_Passengers FROM station WHERE Total_Passengers > (SELECT AVG(Total_Passengers) FROM station);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and total passenger counts for stations that have an above-average number of passengers.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID WHERE T2.Station_ID IN (SELECT Station_ID FROM station WHERE Location = 'London');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of all trains that pass through any station located in London.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID WHERE T2.Train_ID IN (1, 2) GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Train_ID) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the station that is a shared stop for both Train ID 1 and Train ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID WHERE T2.Station_ID = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID WHERE T2.Station_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the train that stops at Station 1 but does not stop at Station 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM station AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM train_station AS T2 JOIN train AS T3 ON T2.Train_ID = T3.Train_ID WHERE T2.Station_ID = T1.Station_ID AND T3.Service = 'Daily');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of all stations that have at least one train with a 'Daily' service.

sql: SELECT Name, Total_Passengers, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY Total_Passengers DESC) FROM station;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each station's name and total passenger count, along with a calculated rank for each station from busiest to least busy.

sql: SELECT Name, 'Train' AS Type FROM train UNION ALL SELECT Name, 'Station' AS Type FROM station;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows showing a combined list of all train and station names, with a 'Type' column to distinguish whether the name refers to a 'Train' or a 'Station'.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, T1.Name, T1.Total_Passengers FROM station AS T1 WHERE T1.Total_Passengers = (SELECT MAX(T2.Total_Passengers) FROM station AS T2 WHERE T2.Location = T1.Location);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing for each location the single station with the highest total number of passengers, along with its name and passenger count.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T3.Total_Passengers) FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each train's name along with the total number of passengers aggregated from all the stations it services.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID WHERE T2.Station_ID = (SELECT Station_ID FROM station ORDER BY Annual_entry_exit DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the distinct names of trains that pass through the single busiest station, as determined by the highest annual passenger entry and exit.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Service FROM station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID JOIN train AS T3 ON T2.Train_ID = T3.Train_ID WHERE T1.Station_ID = (SELECT Station_ID FROM station ORDER BY Total_Passengers DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the distinct service type offered by trains that stop at the station with the highest total passenger count.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Name, T4.Name FROM train_station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID AND T1.Train_ID < T2.Train_ID JOIN train AS T3 ON T1.Train_ID = T3.Train_ID JOIN train AS T4 ON T2.Train_ID = T4.Train_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing unique pairs of train names that share at least one common station.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Number_of_Platforms) FROM station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID JOIN train AS T3 ON T2.Train_ID = T3.Train_ID WHERE T3.Service = 'Daily';
result_summary: Returns a single value representing the average number of platforms for all stations that are serviced by daily trains.

sql: SELECT Name FROM train WHERE Train_ID NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT T1.Train_ID FROM train_station AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID WHERE T2.Location != 'London');
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of trains that exclusively stop at stations located in London.

sql: SELECT Name FROM station WHERE Number_of_Platforms > (SELECT AVG(Number_of_Platforms) FROM station) AND Station_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Station_ID FROM train_station);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of stations that have an above-average number of platforms and are actively served by trains.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T3.Train_ID) FROM station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID JOIN train AS T3 ON T2.Train_ID = T3.Train_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'London' AND T3.Service = 'Daily' GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each train station in London along with the total count of trains with 'Daily' service that pass through it.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID JOIN train AS T3 ON T2.Train_ID = T3.Train_ID WHERE T3.Service = 'Daily' GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY COUNT(T3.Train_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single station served by the most daily trains, ordered by the total count of trains per station.

sql: SELECT T1.Station_ID FROM train_station AS T1 WHERE T1.Train_ID = 2 INTERSECT SELECT T2.Station_ID FROM train_station AS T2 WHERE T2.Train_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the ID of the station that is served by both Train 2 and Train 3.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T1.Time > '12:00' AND T3.Number_of_Platforms > 15;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of trains that run after 12:00 PM and make stops at large stations with more than 15 platforms.

sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Main_Services) FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Guruvayur Express';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a count of the unique main services offered across all stations where the 'Guruvayur Express' train stops.

sql: SELECT Location FROM station GROUP BY Location ORDER BY AVG(Total_Passengers) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single location that has the highest average number of total passengers across all of its stations.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID GROUP BY T1.Train_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Station_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of trains that are scheduled to stop at one or more stations.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Number_of_Platforms FROM station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID JOIN train AS T3 ON T2.Train_ID = T3.Train_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Ananthapuri Express' ORDER BY T1.Number_of_Platforms DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and platform count of the station with the most platforms that serves the 'Ananthapuri Express' train.

sql: SELECT COUNT(document_code) FROM Documents;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total number of documents.

sql: SELECT document_code, functional_area_code FROM Document_Functional_Areas LIMIT 5;

sql: SELECT T2.role_description, COUNT(T1.user_id) FROM Users AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code GROUP BY T2.role_description;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows that list each role description along with the total number of users assigned to that role.

sql: SELECT AVG(access_count) FROM Documents WHERE document_type_code = 'Book';
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average access count for all documents classified as a 'Book'.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code WHERE T2.functional_area_code = '1';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all documents associated with the functional area identified by code '1'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the unique names of all documents that have at least one associated document section.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name, COUNT(T2.section_id) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code GROUP BY T1.document_name;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each document name along with the total number of sections it contains.

sql: SELECT document_name, document_type_code FROM Documents WHERE access_count > 4000;

sql: SELECT T2.role_description FROM Users AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code WHERE T1.user_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the role description for the user with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Structures AS T2 ON T1.document_structure_code = T2.document_structure_code WHERE T2.document_structure_description = 'Header';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all documents that are classified with a 'Header' document structure.

sql: SELECT functional_area_code, COUNT(document_code) FROM Document_Functional_Areas GROUP BY functional_area_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each functional area code and the total count of documents associated with it.

sql: SELECT user_name FROM Users WHERE role_code IN ('DBA', 'PROJ-MGR');
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the usernames for all users identified as either a Database Administrator ('DBA') or a Project Manager ('PROJ-MGR').

sql: SELECT T1.document_code FROM Document_Sections AS T1 GROUP BY T1.document_code HAVING count(T1.section_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the document codes for all documents that contain more than one section.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.image_name FROM Document_Sections AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections_Images AS T2 ON T1.section_id = T2.section_id JOIN Images AS T3 ON T2.image_id = T3.image_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the unique names of all images that are used in any document section.

sql: SELECT T2.functional_area_description, AVG(T1.access_count) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T3 ON T1.document_code = T3.document_code JOIN Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T3.functional_area_code = T2.functional_area_code GROUP BY T2.functional_area_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows calculating the average document access count for each distinct functional area.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name, T2.document_structure_description FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Structures AS T2 ON T1.document_structure_code = T2.document_structure_code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each document name alongside the description of its corresponding document structure.

sql: SELECT document_name, access_count FROM Documents ORDER BY access_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most frequently accessed document and its total access count.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code JOIN Functional_Areas AS T3 ON T2.functional_area_code = T3.functional_area_code JOIN Document_Structures AS T4 ON T1.document_structure_code = T4.document_structure_code WHERE T3.functional_area_description = 'Acknowledgement' AND T4.document_structure_description = 'Bib';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of documents that have a functional area of 'Acknowledgement' and a document structure of 'Bib'.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 WHERE T1.document_code IN (SELECT T2.document_code FROM Document_Sections AS T2 WHERE T2.section_id IN (SELECT T3.section_id FROM Document_Sections_Images AS T3));
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of all documents that contain at least one image within any of their sections.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code JOIN Functional_Areas AS T3 ON T2.functional_area_code = T3.functional_area_code WHERE T3.functional_area_description = 'Signature' EXCEPT SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code JOIN Functional_Areas AS T3 ON T2.functional_area_code = T3.functional_area_code WHERE T3.functional_area_description = 'Acknowledgement';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of documents that are associated with the 'Signature' functional area but not the 'Acknowledgement' area.

sql: SELECT T1.document_structure_description FROM Document_Structures AS T1 WHERE T1.document_structure_code IN (SELECT DISTINCT T2.parent_document_structure_code FROM Document_Structures AS T2 WHERE T2.parent_document_structure_code IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the descriptions for all document structures that serve as parents to other structures.

sql: SELECT T1.document_type_code, AVG(T3.section_count) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN (SELECT document_code, COUNT(*) AS section_count FROM Document_Sections GROUP BY document_code) AS T3 ON T1.document_code = T3.document_code GROUP BY T1.document_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each document type and the average number of sections per document for that type.

sql: SELECT document_name, access_count FROM Documents WHERE access_count > (SELECT AVG(access_count) FROM Documents);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing documents with an access count greater than the overall average, showing the name and specific access count for each of these more popular documents.

sql: SELECT T3.image_name, COUNT(T1.section_id) AS usage_count FROM Document_Sections AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections_Images AS T2 ON T1.section_id = T2.section_id JOIN Images AS T3 ON T2.image_id = T3.image_id GROUP BY T3.image_name ORDER BY usage_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most frequently used image, providing its name and a count of the document sections it appears in.

sql: SELECT T1.functional_area_description FROM Functional_Areas AS T1 LEFT JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.functional_area_code = T2.functional_area_code WHERE T2.document_code IS NULL;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name, T3.document_structure_description AS parent_structure_description FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Structures AS T2 ON T1.document_structure_code = T2.document_structure_code JOIN Document_Structures AS T3 ON T2.parent_document_structure_code = T3.document_structure_code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each document's name alongside the description of its parent structural element.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code WHERE T1.document_type_code = 'Book' AND T2.section_title = 'after';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of books that contain a section with the title 'after'.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name, T1.document_type_code, T3.functional_area_description FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code JOIN Functional_Areas AS T3 ON T2.functional_area_code = T3.functional_area_code WHERE T3.functional_area_code IN ('1', '9');
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the name, type code, and functional area description for documents belonging to the 'Keep blank' and 'Acknowledgement' functional areas.

sql: SELECT document_code FROM Document_Sections GROUP BY document_code HAVING COUNT(section_id) > (SELECT COUNT(section_id) FROM Document_Sections WHERE document_code = '217');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the codes for documents that have a higher number of sections than the document with code '217'.

sql: SELECT T2.role_description, T1.user_name FROM Users AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code WHERE T1.role_code IN (SELECT role_code FROM Users GROUP BY role_code HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the user name and role description for all users who belong to a role shared by more than one person.

sql: SELECT image_id, image_name FROM Images WHERE image_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT image_id FROM Document_Sections_Images);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID and name of all images that are not currently used or referenced within any document section.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name, T2.section_title FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code JOIN Document_Sections_Images AS T3 ON T2.section_id = T3.section_id JOIN Images AS T4 ON T3.image_id = T4.image_id WHERE T4.image_alt_text = 'Arden';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the document names and section titles for all sections that contain the image with the alt text 'Arden'.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name, T2.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.document_structure_code = T2.document_structure_code AND T1.document_code < T2.document_code;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows, creating unique pairs of documents that share the same document structure or template.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code WHERE T2.functional_area_code IN ('1', '9') GROUP BY T1.document_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.functional_area_code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of documents that belong to both functional area '1' and functional area '9'.

sql: SELECT T2.functional_area_description AS ParentArea FROM Functional_Areas AS T1 JOIN Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.parent_functional_area_code = T2.functional_area_code WHERE T1.functional_area_description = 'Acknowledgement';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the parent functional area for the area described as 'Acknowledgement'.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Structures AS T2 ON T1.document_structure_code = T2.document_structure_code WHERE T2.document_structure_code = T2.parent_document_structure_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of documents that are classified under a top-level or root document structure.

sql: SELECT T1.user_name, T2.role_description FROM Users AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code WHERE T2.role_description = 'Project Manager' UNION SELECT T1.user_name, T2.role_description FROM Users AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code WHERE T2.role_description = 'Database Administrator';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the username and role description for every user identified as either a 'Project Manager' or a 'Database Administrator'.

sql: SELECT T2.document_structure_description, SUM(T1.access_count) AS total_access FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Structures AS T2 ON T1.document_structure_code = T2.document_structure_code GROUP BY T2.document_structure_description ORDER BY total_access DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows showing the total access count for each type of document structure, ordered from the most accessed to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 WHERE T2.document_code = T1.document_code AND T2.functional_area_code = '1') AND T1.access_count > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of highly-accessed documents (over 1000 times) that belong to functional area '1'.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name, COUNT(T4.image_id) AS image_count FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code JOIN Document_Sections_Images AS T3 ON T2.section_id = T3.section_id JOIN Images AS T4 ON T3.image_id = T4.image_id GROUP BY T1.document_name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each document by name and the total count of images it contains.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 WHERE T1.document_type_code = 'Paper' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Document_Sections AS T2 WHERE T2.document_code = T1.document_code);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of documents that are classified as 'Paper' and contain at least one document section.

sql: SELECT T1.role_description, COUNT(T2.user_id) AS user_count FROM Roles AS T1 JOIN Users AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code GROUP BY T1.role_description HAVING COUNT(T2.user_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the role descriptions that have more than one user, along with the total count of users for each of those roles.

sql: SELECT forename, surname, nationality FROM drivers ORDER BY surname;
result_summary: Returns 842 rows listing the forename, surname, and nationality for each driver, ordered alphabetically by their surname.

sql: SELECT name, location FROM circuits WHERE country = 'Australia';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location of racing circuits in Australia.

sql: SELECT name, date FROM races WHERE year = 2009 ORDER BY date;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the name and date of all races from the 2009 season, ordered chronologically.

sql: SELECT name FROM constructors WHERE nationality = 'British';
result_summary: Returns 85 rows listing the names of all constructors with a 'British' nationality.

sql: SELECT forename, surname, dob FROM drivers WHERE surname = 'Hamilton';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first name, last name, and date of birth for all drivers with the surname 'Hamilton'.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM drivers;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all drivers.

sql: SELECT status FROM status ORDER BY statusId;
result_summary: Returns 134 rows listing all possible statuses, ordered by their internal status ID.

sql: SELECT forename, surname FROM drivers WHERE driverId = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first name and surname for the specific driver with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT year FROM seasons ORDER BY year DESC;
result_summary: Returns 69 rows listing each season's year, ordered from the most recent to the oldest.

sql: SELECT name FROM races WHERE raceId = 18;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the specific race identified by race ID 18.

sql: SELECT T2.country, count(T1.raceId) FROM races AS T1 JOIN circuits AS T2 ON T1.circuitId = T2.circuitId GROUP BY T2.country ORDER BY count(T1.raceId) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 32 rows listing each country along with the total number of races held there, ordered from the country with the most races to the least.

sql: SELECT T2.name, SUM(T1.points) FROM results AS T1 JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId GROUP BY T2.name ORDER BY SUM(T1.points) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 207 rows listing each constructor's name and their total accumulated points, ordered from the constructor with the most points to the least.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN status AS T3 ON T1.statusId = T3.statusId WHERE T3.status = 'Accident';
result_summary: Returns 345 rows providing a unique list of the first and last names of all drivers who have been involved in a race that ended due to an 'Accident'.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, SUM(T1.wins) AS total_wins FROM driverStandings AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.wins > 0 GROUP BY T1.driverId ORDER BY total_wins DESC;
result_summary: Returns 107 rows listing drivers who have won at least one race, showing their full name and their total career wins, ordered from the highest number of wins to the lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T1.constructorId = T3.constructorId WHERE T3.name = 'McLaren';
result_summary: Returns 51 rows listing the unique first and last names of all drivers who have driven for the McLaren team.

sql: SELECT nationality, count(*) FROM drivers GROUP BY nationality ORDER BY count(*) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 41 rows listing each driver nationality and the total count of drivers for that nationality, ordered from the most common to the least common nationality.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T2.date FROM qualifying AS T1 JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId WHERE T1.driverId = 1 AND T1.position = 1;
result_summary: Returns 72 rows listing the name and date of each race where a specific driver (ID 1) qualified in the first position.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, SUM(T1.laps) AS total_laps FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.driverId ORDER BY total_laps DESC;
result_summary: Returns 842 rows listing each driver's full name and the total number of laps they have completed, ordered from the driver with the most laps to the least.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, SUM(T1.points) AS total_points FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.driverId ORDER BY total_points DESC LIMIT 10;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the top 10 drivers by their total career points, ordered from the highest number of points to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.qualifyId) AS pole_positions FROM qualifying AS T1 JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId WHERE T1.position = 1 GROUP BY T2.name ORDER BY pole_positions DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each constructor's name and their total count of pole positions, ordered from the constructor with the most poles to the one with the fewest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name, T2.country FROM races AS T1 JOIN circuits AS T2 ON T1.circuitId = T2.circuitId WHERE T1.year = 2009;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the unique names and countries of every circuit that hosted a race during the 2009 season.

sql: SELECT T2.name, SUM(T1.wins) AS total_wins FROM constructorStandings AS T1 JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId GROUP BY T2.name HAVING SUM(T1.wins) > 0 ORDER BY total_wins DESC;
result_summary: Returns 38 rows listing each winning constructor and their total number of race wins, ordered from the highest number of wins to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T2.year, T3.forename, T3.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T1.driverId = T3.driverId WHERE T1.positionOrder = 1 AND T1.rank = '1';
result_summary: Returns 85 rows detailing the race winners, providing the race name, year, and the full name of the driver who finished in first place.

sql: SELECT T3.name, SUM(T1.points) AS total_points FROM constructorResults AS T1 JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T1.constructorId = T3.constructorId WHERE T2.year = 2009 GROUP BY T3.name ORDER BY total_points DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each constructor's name and their total points scored during the 2009 season, ordered from the highest-scoring constructor to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, AVG(T1.grid) FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T1.constructorId = T3.constructorId WHERE T3.name = 'Williams' AND T1.grid > 0 GROUP BY T1.driverId;
result_summary: Returns 49 rows, listing the first name, surname, and average starting grid position for each driver who has raced for the 'Williams' team, considering only races where they had a valid grid start.

sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname FROM drivers AS T1 WHERE T1.driverId IN (SELECT driverId FROM results WHERE positionOrder = 1) AND T1.driverId NOT IN (SELECT T2.driverId FROM results AS T2 JOIN status AS T3 ON T2.statusId = T3.statusId WHERE T3.status = 'Disqualified');
result_summary: Returns 67 rows listing the first and last names of all drivers who have won at least one race and have never been disqualified.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, count(DISTINCT T1.constructorId) AS constructor_count FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.driverId HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.constructorId) > 2 ORDER BY constructor_count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 287 rows listing drivers who have competed for more than two distinct constructors, showing their name and the total constructor count, ordered from the highest count to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T3.year, T4.forename, T4.surname FROM qualifying AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId AND T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId JOIN drivers AS T4 ON T1.driverId = T4.driverId WHERE T1.position = 1 AND T2.positionOrder > 1;
result_summary: Returns 174 rows listing the race name, year, and driver's name for each instance where a driver achieved pole position in qualifying but did not win the final race.

sql: SELECT forename, surname, nationality FROM drivers WHERE nationality = 'British' UNION SELECT forename, surname, nationality FROM drivers WHERE nationality = 'German';
result_summary: Returns 211 rows listing the first name, last name, and nationality for all drivers who are either British or German.

sql: SELECT T2.name, count(T1.raceId) AS race_count FROM races AS T1 JOIN circuits AS T2 ON T1.circuitId = T2.circuitId GROUP BY T1.circuitId ORDER BY race_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the circuit that has hosted the most races, showing its name and the total race count.

sql: WITH DriverWins AS (SELECT driverId, COUNT(*) AS Wins FROM results WHERE positionOrder = 1 GROUP BY driverId) SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, T1.Wins, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY T1.Wins DESC) AS rank FROM DriverWins AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId;
result_summary: Returns 107 rows, ranking all drivers by their total career wins from most to least, showing each driver's name, total win count, and corresponding rank.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T1.points FROM constructorStandings AS T1 JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId WHERE T1.raceId = (SELECT raceId FROM races WHERE year = 2008 ORDER BY round DESC LIMIT 1) ORDER BY T1.points DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and final points total for the winning constructor from the last race of the 2008 season.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId JOIN circuits AS T4 ON T3.circuitId = T4.circuitId WHERE T4.country = 'Australia' AND T1.positionOrder IN (1, 2, 3);
result_summary: Returns 40 rows listing the unique drivers, by first and last name, who have secured a podium finish (1st, 2nd, or 3rd place) in a race held in Australia.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId JOIN circuits AS T4 ON T3.circuitId = T4.circuitId WHERE T1.positionOrder = 1 GROUP BY T1.driverId HAVING count(DISTINCT T4.country) > 3;
result_summary: Returns 53 rows listing the first and last names of drivers who have won races in more than three different countries.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T3.year, count(*) AS accident_count FROM results AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.statusId = T2.statusId JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId WHERE T2.status = 'Accident' GROUP BY T1.raceId ORDER BY accident_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the race name, year, and total accident count for the single event with the most recorded accidents.

sql: SELECT forename, surname FROM drivers WHERE driverId NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT driverId FROM results WHERE points > 0);
result_summary: Returns 507 rows listing the first and last names of all drivers who have never scored any points.

sql: SELECT T3.forename, T3.surname, T2.grid, T1.positionOrder, (T2.grid - T1.positionOrder) AS position_gain FROM results AS T1 JOIN qualifying AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId AND T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T1.driverId = T3.driverId WHERE T1.grid > 0 AND T1.positionOrder IS NOT NULL ORDER BY position_gain DESC LIMIT 1;
error_message: Discarded: SQL Runtime Error - no such column: T2.grid

sql: SELECT T1.year, T4.forename, T4.surname FROM races AS T1 JOIN (SELECT year, MAX(round) AS max_round FROM races GROUP BY year) AS T2 ON T1.year = T2.year AND T1.round = T2.max_round JOIN driverStandings AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId JOIN drivers AS T4 ON T3.driverId = T4.driverId WHERE T3.position = 1 ORDER BY T1.year;
result_summary: Returns 68 rows listing the name of the world champion driver for each year, ordered chronologically.

sql: SELECT T2.name, count(DISTINCT T1.driverId) AS driver_count FROM results AS T1 JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId GROUP BY T2.name ORDER BY driver_count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 207 rows listing each constructor's name alongside the total count of unique drivers who have raced for them, ordered from the constructor with the most drivers to the fewest.

sql: SELECT T2.name, AVG(T1.points) AS avg_points FROM constructorResults AS T1 JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId GROUP BY T2.name ORDER BY avg_points DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top 3 constructors with the highest average points scored, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T3.forename, T3.surname, T4.name AS race_name FROM results AS T1 JOIN driverStandings AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId AND T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T1.driverId = T3.driverId JOIN races AS T4 ON T1.raceId = T4.raceId WHERE T1.positionOrder = 1 AND T2.wins = 0;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T3.name FROM results AS T1 JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN circuits AS T3 ON T2.circuitId = T3.circuitId WHERE T1.milliseconds IS NOT NULL GROUP BY T2.circuitId HAVING AVG(T1.milliseconds) < (SELECT AVG(milliseconds) FROM results WHERE milliseconds IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 48 rows listing the names of circuits where the average race completion time is faster than the overall average time across all circuits.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.positionOrder BETWEEN 1 AND 10 GROUP BY T1.driverId HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.positionOrder) = 10;
result_summary: Returns 66 rows listing the first and last names of drivers who have achieved a finish in each of the top ten positions (1st through 10th) at least once during their careers.

sql: SELECT T3.name AS race, T4.name AS constructor, T5.forename, T5.surname, T6.forename, T6.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId AND T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId AND T1.driverId < T2.driverId JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId JOIN constructors AS T4 ON T1.constructorId = T4.constructorId JOIN drivers AS T5 ON T1.driverId = T5.driverId JOIN drivers AS T6 ON T2.driverId = T6.driverId WHERE T1.positionOrder IN (1, 2, 3) AND T2.positionOrder IN (1, 2, 3);
result_summary: Returns 636 rows identifying pairs of teammate drivers who both achieved a podium finish (top 3) in the same race, listing the race, constructor, and the full names of the two drivers for each instance.

sql: SELECT T3.forename, T3.surname, T4.name, T4.year FROM results AS T1 JOIN qualifying AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId AND T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T1.driverId = T3.driverId JOIN races AS T4 ON T1.raceId = T4.raceId WHERE T1.positionOrder = 1 AND T1.rank = '1' AND T2.position = 1;
result_summary: Returns 46 rows listing the driver's name, the race name, and the year for every instance where a driver won the race from pole position while also setting the fastest lap.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.positionOrder = 1 UNION SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM qualifying AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.position = 1;
result_summary: Returns 109 rows listing the first and last names of all unique drivers who have ever won a race or secured pole position in a qualifying session.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.positionOrder = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T1.constructorId = T3.constructorId WHERE T3.name = 'McLaren';
result_summary: Returns 79 rows listing the first and last names of all drivers who have won a race, excluding any driver who has ever driven for the McLaren team.

sql: SELECT T2.year, AVG(finishers.finish_count) AS avg_finishers FROM (SELECT raceId, count(*) AS finish_count FROM results WHERE statusId = 1 GROUP BY raceId) AS finishers JOIN races AS T2 ON finishers.raceId = T2.raceId GROUP BY T2.year ORDER BY avg_finishers DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the year with the highest average number of finishers per race.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, T3.name AS constructor_name FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T1.constructorId = T3.constructorId GROUP BY T1.driverId, T3.name HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.constructorId) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2095 rows listing each unique pairing of a driver (by first and last name) with a constructor team they have recorded a race result for.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.name AS constructor, T4.name AS race, T4.year FROM results AS T1 JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T1.constructorId = T3.constructorId JOIN races AS T4 ON T1.raceId = T4.raceId WHERE T1.positionOrder = 1 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM results AS T2 WHERE T2.raceId = T1.raceId AND T2.constructorId = T1.constructorId AND T2.positionOrder = 2);
result_summary: Returns 279 rows listing the constructor, race, and year for each race where a constructor's cars achieved both a first and second place finish.

sql: SELECT * FROM customer WHERE state = 'Utah';
result_summary: Returns 1 row providing the complete profile, including account and loan details, for any customers located in Utah.

sql: SELECT bname, no_of_customers FROM bank WHERE city = 'New York City';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the branch name and the number of customers for all banks located in New York City.

sql: SELECT * FROM loan WHERE loan_type = 'Auto';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for loans identified as 'Auto' loans.

sql: SELECT cust_name, acc_bal FROM customer ORDER BY acc_bal DESC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each customer's name and account balance, ordered from the highest balance to the lowest.

sql: SELECT bname, no_of_customers FROM bank ORDER BY no_of_customers;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each bank's name and its number of customers, ordered from the bank with the fewest customers to the one with the most.

sql: SELECT loan_ID, amount FROM loan WHERE amount > 3000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the loan ID and amount for all loans valued over $3000.

sql: SELECT cust_name, credit_score FROM customer WHERE credit_score > 100;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and credit score for each customer with a credit score greater than 100.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT acc_type FROM customer;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the distinct types of customer accounts available.

sql: SELECT * FROM customer WHERE cust_name = 'Mary';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with all available customer details for the customer named 'Mary'.

sql: SELECT SUM(amount) FROM loan;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total monetary amount of all loans in the table.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, T2.bname FROM customer AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each customer's name and the name of their associated bank branch.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, T2.loan_type, T2.amount FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each customer's name along with the type and amount for every loan they hold.

sql: SELECT T2.bname, COUNT(T1.cust_ID) AS NumberOfCustomers FROM customer AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID GROUP BY T2.bname;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each bank branch name and the total count of customers associated with that branch.

sql: SELECT loan_type, SUM(amount) FROM loan GROUP BY loan_type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each loan type along with the total amount loaned for that type.

sql: SELECT acc_type, AVG(acc_bal) FROM customer GROUP BY acc_type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing each customer account type along with the average account balance for that specific type.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID GROUP BY T1.cust_name HAVING COUNT(T2.loan_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a customer who has taken out more than one loan.

sql: SELECT T2.bname, SUM(T1.amount) AS TotalLoanAmount FROM loan AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID GROUP BY T2.bname;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each bank branch name along with the total amount of all loans associated with that branch.

sql: SELECT AVG(credit_score) FROM customer WHERE state = 'New York';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average credit score for all customers located in New York.

sql: SELECT cust_name, state FROM customer WHERE cust_ID IN (SELECT cust_ID FROM loan WHERE loan_type = 'Mortgages');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and state of each customer who has a 'Mortgages' loan.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of customers who have an associated loan.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.bname FROM loan AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID WHERE T1.loan_type = 'Business';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique bank name that is associated with a 'Business' type loan.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, COUNT(T2.loan_ID) AS NumberOfLoans FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID GROUP BY T1.cust_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each customer's name along with a count of the total number of loans they have.

sql: SELECT T2.bname, SUM(T1.acc_bal) AS TotalBalance FROM customer AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID GROUP BY T2.bname;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each bank branch name and the corresponding total balance of all associated customer accounts.

sql: SELECT cust_name, acc_bal FROM customer WHERE acc_bal = (SELECT MAX(acc_bal) FROM customer);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and account balance of the customer with the highest account balance.

sql: SELECT T2.loan_type, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.cust_ID) AS NumberOfCustomers FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID GROUP BY T2.loan_type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each loan type along with the total number of unique customers who have that type of loan.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, T3.loan_type, T2.city FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T3 ON T1.cust_ID = T3.cust_ID JOIN bank AS T2 ON T3.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each customer's name, their type of loan, and the city of the associated bank branch.

sql: SELECT T2.bname FROM bank AS T2 JOIN (SELECT branch_ID, AVG(acc_bal) as average_balance FROM customer GROUP BY branch_ID) AS T1 ON T2.branch_ID = T1.branch_ID WHERE T1.average_balance > (SELECT AVG(acc_bal) FROM customer);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the bank branch whose customers have an average account balance greater than the overall average account balance across all customers.

sql: SELECT bname FROM bank AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM customer AS T2 WHERE T2.branch_ID = T1.branch_ID AND T2.credit_score > 200);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any bank branch that has at least one customer with a credit score greater than 200.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, SUM(T2.amount) AS TotalLoanAmount FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID GROUP BY T1.cust_name HAVING SUM(T2.amount) > 4000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing customers whose total combined loan amount is greater than 4000, showing each customer's name and their calculated total loan amount.

sql: SELECT cust_name FROM customer UNION SELECT bname FROM bank;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows containing a unique, combined list of all customer names and bank names.

sql: SELECT state FROM customer INTERSECT SELECT state FROM bank;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the states where both customers and banks are located.

sql: SELECT cust_name FROM customer WHERE cust_ID NOT IN (SELECT cust_ID FROM loan);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the customer who does not currently have a loan.

sql: SELECT cust_name FROM customer WHERE cust_ID = (SELECT cust_ID FROM loan ORDER BY amount DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer who has taken out the single largest loan by amount.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, T1.acc_bal FROM customer AS T1 WHERE T1.acc_bal > (SELECT AVG(T2.acc_bal) FROM customer AS T2 WHERE T2.branch_ID = T1.branch_ID);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.city, SUM(T3.amount) FROM customer AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID JOIN loan AS T3 ON T1.cust_ID = T3.cust_ID GROUP BY T2.city;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total loan amount for all customers, grouped by the city of their bank branch.

sql: SELECT T1.bname FROM bank AS T1 JOIN customer AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID JOIN loan AS T3 ON T1.branch_ID = T3.branch_ID GROUP BY T1.bname HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.cust_ID) >= 1 AND SUM(CASE WHEN T3.loan_type = 'Business' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the bank branch that has at least one customer and has also issued one or more 'Business' type loans.

sql: SELECT * FROM loan WHERE amount > (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM loan);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the full details of any loan with an amount greater than the overall average loan amount.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID WHERE T2.loan_type = 'Mortgages' UNION SELECT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 WHERE T1.acc_type = 'saving';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing a unique list of customer names for those who either have a mortgage or a savings account.

sql: SELECT AVG(amount) FROM loan WHERE cust_ID IN (SELECT cust_ID FROM loan GROUP BY cust_ID HAVING count(loan_ID) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average loan amount calculated across all loans belonging to customers who have more than one loan.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID JOIN bank AS T3 ON T2.branch_ID = T3.branch_ID WHERE T1.state = 'New York' AND T1.acc_type = 'saving' AND T2.loan_type = 'Business' AND T3.bname = 'broadway';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a customer from New York with a saving account who has a business loan from the 'broadway' bank branch.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID WHERE T2.loan_type = 'Mortgages' INTERSECT SELECT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID WHERE T2.loan_type = 'Auto';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the customer who has both a mortgage loan and an auto loan.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, T1.credit_score, T2.TotalLoan FROM customer AS T1 JOIN (SELECT cust_ID, SUM(amount) AS TotalLoan FROM loan GROUP BY cust_ID) AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID WHERE T2.TotalLoan > 3000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying customers with a total loan balance exceeding 3000, showing each customer's name, credit score, and their calculated total loan amount.

sql: SELECT T2.bname FROM customer AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID WHERE T1.acc_type = 'saving' EXCEPT SELECT T2.bname FROM customer AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID WHERE T1.acc_type = 'checking';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of bank branches that have customers with a saving account but no customers with a checking account.

sql: SELECT T1.bname, CAST(SUM(T3.amount) AS REAL) / SUM(T2.acc_bal) AS loan_to_balance_ratio FROM bank AS T1 JOIN customer AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID JOIN loan AS T3 ON T2.cust_ID = T3.cust_ID GROUP BY T1.bname;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each bank branch name and its corresponding loan-to-balance ratio, calculated by dividing the total loan amounts by the total customer account balances for that branch.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, T1.acc_bal, (SELECT bname FROM bank AS T2 WHERE T2.branch_ID = T1.branch_ID) AS branch_name FROM customer AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each customer's name and account balance, along with the name of their associated bank branch.

sql: SELECT T2.bname, SUM(CASE WHEN T1.acc_type = 'saving' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS saving_accounts, SUM(CASE WHEN T1.acc_type = 'checking' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS checking_accounts FROM customer AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID GROUP BY T2.bname;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each bank branch name along with the total number of savings accounts and checking accounts associated with that branch.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID EXCEPT SELECT cust_name FROM customer WHERE state = 'Utah';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a customer who has a loan and does not reside in the state of Utah.

sql: SELECT loan_ID, amount FROM loan WHERE amount > (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM loan WHERE loan_type = 'Auto');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the loan ID and amount for loans whose value is greater than the average amount of all 'Auto' loans.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID JOIN bank AS T3 ON T2.branch_ID = T3.branch_ID WHERE T1.acc_bal > 1000 AND T1.credit_score > 20 AND T3.city = 'New York City';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of customers who have an account balance over 1000, a credit score above 20, and have taken out a loan from a branch in New York City.

sql: SELECT T1.bname FROM bank AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID GROUP BY T1.bname HAVING COUNT(T2.loan_ID) >= (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customer AS T3 WHERE T3.branch_ID = T1.branch_ID);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of bank branches where the number of loans issued is greater than or equal to the number of customers at that branch.

sql: SELECT * FROM station WHERE city = 'San Jose';
result_summary: Returns 16 rows providing all station details for every station located in the city of San Jose.

sql: SELECT id, duration, start_date, end_date FROM trip WHERE duration > 500;
result_summary: Returns 5375 rows listing the ID, duration, start date, and end date for all trips with a duration greater than 500 seconds.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT subscription_type FROM trip;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the distinct subscription types recorded for trips.

sql: SELECT * FROM weather WHERE date = '8/29/2013';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, providing a full weather breakdown for five distinct locations (identified by zip code) on the specific date of August 29, 2013.

sql: SELECT name, dock_count FROM station ORDER BY dock_count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 70 rows listing each station's name and its corresponding dock count, ordered from the station with the most docks to the least.

sql: SELECT id, duration FROM trip ORDER BY duration DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the ID and duration of the 5 longest trips, ordered from highest to lowest duration.

sql: SELECT * FROM trip WHERE start_station_id = 63;
result_summary: Returns 213 rows containing all trip details for bike trips that originated from the 'Howard at 2nd' station (station ID 63).

sql: SELECT * FROM status WHERE station_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 123 rows showing the historical status for bike station ID 3, detailing the number of bikes and docks available at different points in time.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT city FROM station;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique cities in which a station is located.

sql: SELECT date, max_temperature_f, mean_temperature_f, min_temperature_f FROM weather WHERE zip_code = 94107;
result_summary: Returns 733 rows listing the daily maximum, mean, and minimum temperatures in Fahrenheit for the zip code 94107.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) FROM trip AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.start_station_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 69 rows, listing each station's name alongside the total number of trips that departed from that station.

sql: SELECT subscription_type, AVG(duration) FROM trip GROUP BY subscription_type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows that list each subscription type ('Customer' and 'Subscriber') along with the corresponding average trip duration for that type.

sql: SELECT SUM(T2.bikes_available) FROM station AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T1.city = 'San Jose';
result_summary: Returns a single row calculating the total number of bikes currently available across all stations in the city of San Jose.

sql: SELECT zip_code, AVG(duration) FROM trip GROUP BY zip_code;
result_summary: Returns 475 rows, with each row showing a zip code and the corresponding average trip duration for that area.

sql: SELECT bike_id, COUNT(*) AS trip_count FROM trip GROUP BY bike_id ORDER BY trip_count DESC LIMIT 10;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows identifying the 10 most frequently used bikes by their ID and total trip count, ordered from highest to lowest count.

sql: SELECT start_station_name, end_station_name, duration FROM trip WHERE duration BETWEEN 300 AND 400;
result_summary: Returns 1380 rows listing the start station, end station, and duration for trips that lasted between 300 and 400 seconds.

sql: SELECT AVG(duration), MAX(duration), MIN(duration) FROM trip WHERE start_station_name = 'Howard at 2nd';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average, maximum, and minimum trip durations for all trips that started at the 'Howard at 2nd' station.

sql: SELECT T1.name, MAX(T2.time) FROM station AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 69 rows listing each station's name alongside the timestamp of its most recent status update.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.bikes_available) FROM station AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T1.installation_date = '8/6/2013';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average number of bikes available across all stations that were installed on August 6, 2013.

sql: SELECT zip_code, COUNT(*) FROM trip GROUP BY zip_code;
result_summary: Returns 475 rows, listing each zip code and the total number of trips associated with it.

sql: SELECT events, AVG(mean_humidity), AVG(mean_temperature_f) FROM weather GROUP BY events;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each type of weather event along with the corresponding average humidity and average temperature in Fahrenheit.

sql: SELECT start_station_name, duration FROM trip WHERE end_station_name = 'San Francisco Caltrain 2 (330 Townsend)';
result_summary: Returns 777 rows listing the starting station and trip duration for all trips that ended at the 'San Francisco Caltrain 2 (330 Townsend)' station.

sql: SELECT name, dock_count FROM station WHERE city = 'San Jose' ORDER BY dock_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and dock count of the San Jose station that has the most docks.

sql: SELECT start_station_name, COUNT(*) FROM trip WHERE subscription_type = 'Subscriber' GROUP BY start_station_name;
result_summary: Returns 68 rows, listing each starting station alongside the total number of trips initiated from that station by customers with a 'Subscriber' subscription.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.duration) FROM trip AS T1 JOIN weather AS T2 ON T1.zip_code = T2.zip_code WHERE T2.max_temperature_f > 75;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average trip duration for all trips that occurred on warm days with a maximum temperature over 75 degrees Fahrenheit.

sql: SELECT id, duration FROM trip WHERE duration > (SELECT AVG(duration) FROM trip);
result_summary: Returns 1413 rows listing the ID and duration for each trip whose duration is longer than the average duration across all trips.

sql: SELECT T1.id, T1.duration, T2.dock_count AS start_station_dock_count, T3.dock_count AS end_station_dock_count FROM trip AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.start_station_id = T2.id JOIN station AS T3 ON T1.end_station_id = T3.id;
result_summary: Returns 9959 rows detailing each trip with its ID, duration, and the number of docks available at both the start and end stations.

sql: SELECT T1.name, AVG(T2.bikes_available) FROM station AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING AVG(T2.bikes_available) < 20;
result_summary: Returns 69 rows identifying bike stations with low availability, listing the name and calculated average number of bikes for stations that have fewer than 20 bikes available on average.

sql: SELECT id, duration, start_station_name FROM trip AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM station AS T2 WHERE T2.id = T1.start_station_id AND T2.city = 'San Jose');
result_summary: Returns 446 rows listing the ID, duration, and start station name for all trips that originated in the city of San Jose.

sql: SELECT id FROM trip WHERE subscription_type = 'Subscriber' EXCEPT SELECT id FROM trip WHERE start_station_id = 69 OR end_station_id = 69;
result_summary: Returns 7226 rows containing the IDs of trips made by subscribers, excluding any trip that either started or ended at station 69.

sql: SELECT start_station_name, AVG(duration) AS avg_duration FROM trip GROUP BY start_station_name ORDER BY avg_duration DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single starting station with the highest average trip duration, showing the station's name and that calculated average.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM station AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM trip AS T2 WHERE T2.start_station_id = T1.id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any station that has never been used as the starting point for a trip.

sql: SELECT T1.subscription_type, COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT * FROM trip WHERE duration > 500) AS T1 GROUP BY T1.subscription_type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total number of trips with a duration over 500, broken down by subscription type.

sql: SELECT start_station_name, COUNT(*) FROM trip GROUP BY start_station_name HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 68 rows listing each trip starting station that was used more than once, along with the total count of trips that began there.

sql: SELECT start_station_name FROM trip WHERE zip_code = 94041 UNION SELECT end_station_name FROM trip WHERE zip_code = 94041;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows providing a unique list of all start and end station names for trips associated with the 94041 zip code.

sql: SELECT T2.city, AVG(T1.duration) FROM trip AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.start_station_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.city;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each city and the average duration of trips that started in that city.

sql: SELECT name, dock_count FROM station WHERE dock_count = (SELECT MAX(dock_count) FROM station);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying all stations that have the maximum number of docks, listing each station's name and its dock count.

sql: SELECT subscription_type, duration, start_date FROM (SELECT subscription_type, duration, start_date, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY subscription_type ORDER BY duration DESC) as rn FROM trip) AS T1 WHERE T1.rn <= 3;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the top 3 longest trips for each subscription type, showing the trip's duration and its start date.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.docks_available) FROM station AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT start_station_id FROM trip GROUP BY start_station_id HAVING AVG(duration) > 500);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of docks available at stations where the average trip duration originating from them exceeds 500.

sql: SELECT id FROM station WHERE city = 'San Jose' INTERSECT SELECT start_station_id FROM trip WHERE subscription_type = 'Subscriber';
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing the IDs for stations in San Jose that have also been used as a starting point for trips by subscribers.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.city, T1.dock_count FROM station AS T1 WHERE T1.dock_count > (SELECT AVG(T2.dock_count) FROM station AS T2 WHERE T2.city = T1.city);
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing the name, city, and dock count for each bike station that has more docks than the average for its specific city.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.duration) FROM trip AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.start_station_id = T2.id JOIN weather AS T3 ON T1.zip_code = T3.zip_code WHERE T2.city = 'San Jose' AND T3.events = '';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the average duration for all trips that started in San Jose on days with no recorded weather events.

sql: SELECT T1.id, T2.id, T1.start_station_name FROM trip AS T1 JOIN trip AS T2 ON T1.start_station_id = T2.start_station_id AND T1.start_date = T2.start_date WHERE T1.id < T2.id AND T1.end_station_id <> T2.end_station_id;
result_summary: Returns 698 rows, identifying pairs of trips that started at the same station on the same day but ended at different destinations, listing the IDs for each trip in the pair and their common start station name.

sql: WITH StationTripCounts AS (SELECT start_station_id, COUNT(*) AS trip_count FROM trip GROUP BY start_station_id) SELECT T2.name, T1.trip_count FROM StationTripCounts AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.start_station_id = T2.id WHERE T1.trip_count > 5;
result_summary: Returns 66 rows listing the names of starting stations that have been used for more than five trips, along with the total trip count for each of those popular stations.

sql: SELECT id, duration, start_date, end_date FROM trip WHERE start_station_id IN (SELECT id FROM station WHERE city = 'San Jose');
result_summary: Returns 446 rows, providing the trip ID, duration, start date, and end date for all trips that started in the city of 'San Jose'.

sql: SELECT start_station_name FROM trip GROUP BY start_station_name HAVING (MAX(duration) - MIN(duration)) > 3600;
result_summary: Returns 43 rows listing the names of start stations where the difference between the longest and shortest trip duration is greater than one hour.
