sql: SELECT DISTINCT city FROM Addresses;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing each unique city present in the addresses data.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Students;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single column containing the total number of students.

sql: SELECT incident_id, student_id, date_incident_start FROM Behavior_Incident WHERE incident_type_code = 'VIOLENCE';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the incident ID, the involved student's ID, and the start date for all recorded incidents of violence.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.city, T2.country FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first and last name of each student along with their corresponding city and country.

sql: SELECT address_id, date_address_from, date_address_to, monthly_rental FROM Student_Addresses WHERE student_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the address details for student ID 3, including the dates of residence and the associated monthly rental amount.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, email_address FROM Students ORDER BY last_name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each student's first name, last name, and email address, sorted alphabetically by last name.

sql: SELECT T1.detention_id, T1.datetime_detention_start, T2.detention_type_description FROM Detention AS T1 JOIN Ref_Detention_Type AS T2 ON T1.detention_type_code = T2.detention_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each detention record, including its unique ID, start date and time, and the corresponding detention type description.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, count(T2.incident_id) FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each student's first and last name along with the total number of behavior incidents they have.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, count(T2.detention_id) AS number_of_detentions FROM Teachers AS T1 JOIN Detention AS T2 ON T1.teacher_id = T2.teacher_id GROUP BY T1.teacher_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, with each row listing a teacher's first and last name and the total number of detentions they have assigned.

sql: SELECT student_id, first_name, last_name FROM Students WHERE student_id IN (SELECT student_id FROM Behavior_Incident WHERE incident_type_code = 'VIOLENCE');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the student ID, first name, and last name for all students who have been involved in a violent incident.

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING count(T2.address_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the student ID, first name, and last name for students who have more than one address on file.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Teachers AS T1 JOIN Detention AS T2 ON T1.teacher_id = T2.teacher_id JOIN Ref_Detention_Type AS T3 ON T2.detention_type_code = T3.detention_type_code WHERE T3.detention_type_code = 'AFTER';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique first and last names of teachers who have assigned an after-school detention.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, 'Student' FROM Students UNION SELECT first_name, last_name, 'Teacher' FROM Teachers;
result_summary: Returns 30 rows containing a consolidated list of all students and teachers, showing each person's first name, last name, and a column identifying their role.

sql: SELECT T1.date_of_notes, T2.first_name, T2.last_name, T3.first_name, T3.last_name FROM Assessment_Notes AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Teachers AS T3 ON T1.teacher_id = T3.teacher_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows detailing each assessment note, including the date it was recorded, the full name of the student being assessed, and the full name of the teacher who wrote it.

sql: SELECT T2.incident_type_description, count(T1.incident_id) AS number_of_incidents FROM Behavior_Incident AS T1 JOIN Ref_Incident_Type AS T2 ON T1.incident_type_code = T2.incident_type_code GROUP BY T2.incident_type_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each type of incident description along with the total number of incidents recorded for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.incident_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the student ID, first name, and last name of students who were involved in detention incident number 1.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the student ID, first name, and last name for all unique students who have an address in the USA.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Assessment_Notes AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.teacher_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of students who have an assessment note written by the teacher with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.monthly_rental FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.monthly_rental > 800;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the first name, last name, and monthly rental cost for students who pay more than $800 in rent.

sql: SELECT T2.city, AVG(T1.monthly_rental) FROM Student_Addresses AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id GROUP BY T2.city;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each city alongside the average monthly rental cost for students in that city.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.incident_summary FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.recommendations = 'Transfer schools';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first name, last name, and incident summary for students whose behavior incident resulted in a recommendation to transfer schools.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.detention_id) AS break_detentions FROM Teachers AS T1 JOIN Detention AS T2 ON T1.teacher_id = T2.teacher_id WHERE T2.detention_type_code = 'BREAK ' GROUP BY T1.teacher_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each teacher's first and last name along with the total number of 'break' detentions they have assigned.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T3 ON T2.incident_id = T3.incident_id WHERE T3.incident_type_code = 'VIOLENCE';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of students who were placed in detention for a violence-related incident.

sql: SELECT teacher_id, first_name, last_name FROM Teachers WHERE teacher_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT teacher_id FROM Detention) AND teacher_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT teacher_id FROM Assessment_Notes);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows with the ID, first name, and last name of teachers who have recorded a detention but have not entered any assessment notes.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.incident_id) AS incident_count FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id ORDER BY incident_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the student with the most behavior incidents, showing their full name and the total incident count.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T4.incident_type_description, T5.detention_type_description FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T3 ON T2.incident_id = T3.incident_id JOIN Ref_Incident_Type AS T4 ON T3.incident_type_code = T4.incident_type_code JOIN Detention AS T6 ON T2.detention_id = T6.detention_id JOIN Ref_Detention_Type AS T5 ON T6.detention_type_code = T5.detention_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing students who have received detention, showing each student's name, the description of the behavioral incident, and the type of detention assigned.

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.incident_type_code = 'VIOLENCE' EXCEPT SELECT T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Detention AS T3 ON T2.detention_id = T3.detention_id WHERE T3.detention_type_code = 'AFTER';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID, first name, and last name of students who have been involved in a violent incident but have not been assigned after-school detention.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.detention_id) FROM Teachers AS T1 JOIN Detention AS T2 ON T1.teacher_id = T2.teacher_id GROUP BY T1.teacher_id HAVING COUNT(T2.detention_id) > (SELECT AVG(detention_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(detention_id) AS detention_count FROM Detention GROUP BY teacher_id));
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first name, last name, and total detention count for teachers who have assigned more detentions than the average across all teachers.

sql: SELECT T2.incident_type_description, COUNT(*) AS detention_count FROM Behavior_Incident AS T1 JOIN Ref_Incident_Type AS T2 ON T1.incident_type_code = T2.incident_type_code JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T3 ON T1.incident_id = T3.incident_id GROUP BY T2.incident_type_description ORDER BY detention_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most common type of incident resulting in a detention, along with the total count for that incident type.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Detention AS T3 ON T2.detention_id = T3.detention_id GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.teacher_id) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of students who have been assigned detention by at least two different teachers.

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.incident_type_code = 'NOISE' INTERSECT SELECT T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.incident_type_code = 'DISTURB';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying students who have been cited for both a 'NOISE' and a 'DISTURB' behavior incident.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T1.student_id NOT IN (SELECT student_id FROM Students_in_Detention);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique IDs, first names, and last names of students who have had a behavior incident but are not currently assigned to detention.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T4.incident_type_code) AS unique_incident_types FROM Teachers AS T1 JOIN Detention AS T2 ON T1.teacher_id = T2.teacher_id JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T3 ON T2.detention_id = T3.detention_id JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T4 ON T3.incident_id = T4.incident_id GROUP BY T1.teacher_id ORDER BY unique_incident_types DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the teacher who has dealt with the widest variety of behavior incidents leading to detention, showing their name and the count of unique incident types handled.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T1.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.country = 'USA' AND T2.recommendations = 'Transfer schools';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of students from the USA whose behavior incident recommendation was to 'Transfer schools'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.first_name, T3.last_name, T2.city FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id JOIN Students AS T3 ON T3.address_id = T2.address_id WHERE T1.student_id < T3.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing unique pairs of students who live at the same address, showing the first and last names of both students along with their shared city.

sql: WITH Noise_Incident_Students AS (SELECT T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.incident_id FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.incident_type_code = 'NOISE') SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Noise_Incident_Students AS T1 JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T2 ON T1.incident_id = T2.incident_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of students who received detention as a result of a noise-related behavior incident.

sql: SELECT T1.incident_id, T1.student_id FROM Behavior_Incident AS T1 WHERE strftime('%s', T1.date_incident_end) - strftime('%s', T1.date_incident_start) > (SELECT AVG(strftime('%s', date_incident_end) - strftime('%s', date_incident_start)) FROM Behavior_Incident);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the incident and student IDs for behavior incidents that lasted longer than the average incident duration.

sql: SELECT T2.city FROM Student_Addresses AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id GROUP BY T2.city ORDER BY AVG(T1.monthly_rental) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city with the highest average monthly rental paid by students.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Assessment_Notes AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Teachers AS T3 ON T2.teacher_id = T3.teacher_id WHERE T3.first_name = 'Lyla' AND T3.last_name = 'Medhurst';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the student taught by the teacher 'Lyla Medhurst'.

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.incident_type_code) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the ID, first name, and last name for students who have only ever been involved in a single, unique type of behavioral incident.

sql: SELECT T3.country, CAST(COUNT(T2.incident_id) AS REAL) / COUNT(DISTINCT T1.student_id) AS avg_incidents_per_student FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T1.address_id = T3.address_id GROUP BY T3.country;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average number of behavior incidents per student, broken down by each student's country of residence.

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.incident_id), COUNT(DISTINCT T3.detention_id), COUNT(DISTINCT T4.notes_id) FROM Students AS T1 LEFT JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id LEFT JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T3 ON T1.student_id = T3.student_id LEFT JOIN Assessment_Notes AS T4 ON T1.student_id = T4.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, providing a summary for each student that includes their ID and name, along with the total number of unique behavior incidents, detentions, and assessment notes on their record.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T3 ON T1.student_id = T3.student_id WHERE T2.other_details = 'house' AND T3.incident_type_code = 'NOISE';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of students who live in a house and have had a 'NOISE' related behavior incident.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T1.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.city = 'North Loyceville';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of students who are currently in detention and live in the city of 'North Loyceville'.

sql: SELECT T1.teacher_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Teachers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Assessment_Notes AS T2 WHERE T2.teacher_id = T1.teacher_id);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the ID, first name, and last name for each teacher who has created at least one assessment note.

sql: SELECT T1.detention_id, T2.first_name, T2.last_name, (strftime('%s', T1.datetime_detention_end) - strftime('%s', T1.datetime_detention_start)) / 60 AS duration_minutes FROM Detention AS T1 JOIN Teachers AS T2 ON T1.teacher_id = T2.teacher_id ORDER BY duration_minutes DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the top 5 longest detentions, including the detention ID, the name of the assigning teacher, and the calculated duration in minutes, ordered from longest to shortest.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Detention AS T3 ON T2.detention_id = T3.detention_id WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Assessment_Notes AS T4 WHERE T4.student_id = T1.student_id AND T4.teacher_id = T3.teacher_id);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Employee_Name, Date_of_Birth FROM Employees;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each employee's name and their corresponding date of birth.

sql: SELECT Document_ID, Document_Name FROM All_Documents;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the document ID and name for every document in the system.

sql: SELECT Document_Name, Date_Stored FROM All_Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'CV';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and storage date for all documents classified as a CV.

sql: SELECT * FROM Employees WHERE Employee_Name = 'Ebba';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete employee record for the employee with the name 'Ebba'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Document_Type_Code FROM All_Documents;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each of the unique document type codes available in the document collection.

sql: SELECT Location_Name FROM Ref_Locations;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all available reference locations.

sql: SELECT Role_Name FROM Roles;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, providing a complete list of all available role names within the system.

sql: SELECT * FROM All_Documents WHERE Document_ID = 25;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with all recorded information for the document with the specific ID of 25.

sql: SELECT Document_Name, Document_Type_Code FROM All_Documents ORDER BY Document_Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name and type code for every document, ordered alphabetically by document name.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T2.Document_Type_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each document's name alongside its corresponding, human-readable document type name.

sql: SELECT T2.Document_Type_Name, COUNT(T1.Document_ID) FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code GROUP BY T2.Document_Type_Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each document type name and the total count of documents associated with that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name, T2.Role_Name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.Role_Code = T2.Role_Code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each employee by name along with their corresponding assigned role name.

sql: SELECT T2.Role_Name, COUNT(T1.Employee_ID) FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.Role_Code = T2.Role_Code GROUP BY T2.Role_Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each role name and the total number of employees assigned to that role.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Locations AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID WHERE T2.Location_Code = 'c';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all documents found at the location with the code 'c'.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID WHERE T2.Destroyed_by_Employee_ID = 156;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a document that was destroyed by the employee with ID 156.

sql: SELECT T2.Role_Name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.Role_Code = T2.Role_Code GROUP BY T2.Role_Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Employee_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of job roles that are assigned to more than one employee.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T3.Employee_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Employees AS T3 ON T2.Destruction_Authorised_by_Employee_ID = T3.Employee_ID;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each document scheduled for destruction along with the name of the employee who authorized it.

sql: SELECT T2.Document_Type_Name FROM Ref_Document_Types AS T2 JOIN All_Documents AS T1 ON T2.Document_Type_Code = T1.Document_Type_Code JOIN Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T3 ON T1.Document_ID = T3.Document_ID GROUP BY T2.Document_Type_Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique types of documents that contain at least one item scheduled for destruction.

sql: SELECT T2.Location_Name, COUNT(T1.Document_ID) FROM Document_Locations AS T1 JOIN Ref_Locations AS T2 ON T1.Location_Code = T2.Location_Code GROUP BY T2.Location_Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each location name along with the total count of documents associated with that location.

sql: SELECT Document_Name FROM All_Documents WHERE Date_Stored = (SELECT MAX(Date_Stored) FROM All_Documents);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the documents that were most recently stored.

sql: SELECT Document_Name FROM All_Documents WHERE Date_Stored = '1976-06-15 03:40:06';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of documents that were stored at the exact time '1976-06-15 03:40:06'.

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.Role_Code = T2.Role_Code WHERE T2.Role_Name = 'Manager';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the employee whose role is 'Manager'.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T3.Location_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Locations AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Ref_Locations AS T3 ON T2.Location_Code = T3.Location_Code WHERE T1.Document_Type_Code = 'CV';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of documents that are CVs, along with the corresponding name of the location where each CV is stored.

sql: SELECT strftime('%Y', Date_Stored) AS StorageYear, COUNT(Document_ID) FROM All_Documents GROUP BY StorageYear;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, listing each storage year alongside the total count of documents stored during that year.

sql: SELECT T2.Employee_Name FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T1 JOIN Employees AS T2 ON T1.Destroyed_by_Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE T1.Document_ID = 7;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the employee responsible for destroying the document with an ID of 7.

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.Role_Code IS NOT NULL AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T2 WHERE T2.Destroyed_by_Employee_ID = T1.Employee_ID);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of employees with an assigned role who have not destroyed any documents.

sql: SELECT T2.Location_Name FROM Document_Locations AS T1 JOIN Ref_Locations AS T2 ON T1.Location_Code = T2.Location_Code GROUP BY T1.Location_Code ORDER BY count(T1.Document_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the single location that contains the most documents.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 WHERE T1.Document_ID IN (SELECT Document_ID FROM Document_Locations) EXCEPT SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 WHERE T1.Document_ID IN (SELECT Document_ID FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all documents that have a known location and are not scheduled for destruction.

sql: SELECT T3.Employee_Name, T4.Role_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Employees AS T3 ON T2.Destroyed_by_Employee_ID = T3.Employee_ID JOIN Roles AS T4 ON T3.Role_Code = T4.Role_Code WHERE T1.Document_Type_Code = 'BK';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and roles of employees who were responsible for destroying documents of type 'BK'.

sql: SELECT T2.Document_Type_Name, T1.Document_Name, T1.Date_Stored FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code WHERE T1.Date_Stored = (SELECT MAX(T3.Date_Stored) FROM All_Documents AS T3 WHERE T3.Document_Type_Code = T1.Document_Type_Code);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the most recently stored document for each document type, showing its type, name, and storage date.

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.Employee_ID IN (SELECT T2.Destruction_Authorised_by_Employee_ID FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T2 WHERE T2.Document_ID IN (SELECT T3.Document_ID FROM All_Documents AS T3 WHERE T3.Date_Stored < '1990-01-01 00:00:00'));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of employees who have authorized the destruction of documents that were stored prior to 1990.

sql: SELECT T2.Role_Name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.Role_Code = T2.Role_Code GROUP BY T2.Role_Name HAVING MIN(T1.Date_of_Birth) > '1980-01-01 00:00:00';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the names of roles where every employee in that role was born after January 1st, 1980.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_ID FROM All_Documents AS T1 WHERE T1.Document_Type_Code = 'CV' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Document_ID FROM Document_Locations AS T2 WHERE T2.Location_Code = 'e';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the document IDs for all documents that are both of type 'CV' and are stored in location 'e'.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T3.Document_Type_Name, T2.Day_Number FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Calendar AS T2 ON T1.Date_Stored = T2.Calendar_Date JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T3 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T3.Document_Type_Code WHERE T2.Day_Number = 7;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and type for all documents associated with day number 7 from the calendar.

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'ED' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Employee_Name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'MG';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of employees who have the role code 'ED' but do not have the role code 'MG'.

sql: SELECT T1.Role_Name FROM Roles AS T1 WHERE T1.Role_Code NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT T2.Role_Code FROM Employees AS T2 WHERE T2.Role_Code IS NOT NULL);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T3.Role_Name, COUNT(T1.Document_ID) AS DocumentsDestroyed FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T1 JOIN Employees AS T2 ON T1.Destroyed_by_Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN Roles AS T3 ON T2.Role_Code = T3.Role_Code GROUP BY T3.Role_Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T3.Employee_Name AS Authorizer, T4.Employee_Name AS Destroyer FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Employees AS T3 ON T2.Destruction_Authorised_by_Employee_ID = T3.Employee_ID JOIN Employees AS T4 ON T2.Destroyed_by_Employee_ID = T4.Employee_ID;

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID WHERE T1.Document_Type_Code = 'CV';

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, (SELECT T3.Location_Name FROM Ref_Locations AS T3 WHERE T3.Location_Code = T2.Location_Code) AS Location FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Locations AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID;

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'ED' UNION ALL SELECT T1.Employee_Name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'MG';

sql: SELECT T3.Role_Name, COUNT(T1.Document_ID) AS Authorizations FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T1 JOIN Employees AS T2 ON T1.Destruction_Authorised_by_Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN Roles AS T3 ON T2.Role_Code = T3.Role_Code GROUP BY T3.Role_Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Locations AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID WHERE T2.Location_Code = 'e' AND T1.Date_Stored < '2000-01-01 00:00:00';

result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the employee role that has authorized the destruction of the most documents.

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.Role_Code = T2.Role_Code WHERE T2.Role_Name = 'Manager' AND T1.Date_of_Birth BETWEEN '1970-01-01 00:00:00' AND '1979-12-31 23:59:59';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of an employee who is a manager and was born in the 1970s.

sql: SELECT Document_Type_Name, Document_Type_Description FROM Ref_Document_Types WHERE Document_Type_Code IN (SELECT DISTINCT T1.Document_Type_Code FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID);

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T6.Document_Type_Name, T4.Location_Name, T5.Employee_Name AS Destroyer FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Locations AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Ref_Locations AS T4 ON T2.Location_Code = T4.Location_Code JOIN Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T3 ON T1.Document_ID = T3.Document_ID JOIN Employees AS T5 ON T3.Destroyed_by_Employee_ID = T5.Employee_ID JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T6 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T6.Document_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows identifying documents slated for destruction, showing each document's name, type, location, and the employee responsible for its destruction.

sql: SELECT T4.Employee_Name AS Destroyer, T2.Employee_Name AS Authorizer FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T1 JOIN Employees AS T2 ON T1.Destruction_Authorised_by_Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN Employees AS T4 ON T1.Destroyed_by_Employee_ID = T4.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Role_Code <> T4.Role_Code;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the employee who destroyed a document and the employee who authorized it, for all instances where the two employees have different job roles.

sql: SELECT pName FROM Player;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name of every player in the database.

sql: SELECT cName, enr FROM College;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each college's name and its enrollment number.

sql: SELECT pName, HS FROM Player WHERE HS > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing the name and high score for all players whose high score exceeds 1000.

sql: SELECT pID, cName FROM Tryout WHERE pPos = 'goalie';

sql: SELECT cName FROM College WHERE state = 'LA';

sql: SELECT pName, HS FROM Player ORDER BY HS DESC;

sql: SELECT pName FROM Player WHERE pID = 10001;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the player with player ID 10001.

sql: SELECT T1.pName, T2.pPos FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID;

sql: SELECT MAX(enr) FROM College;

sql: SELECT cName, COUNT(*) FROM Tryout GROUP BY cName;

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.pPos = 'goalie';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all players who have tried out for the 'goalie' position.

sql: SELECT pID, COUNT(*) FROM Tryout GROUP BY pID HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying any player who participated in more than one tryout, along with the total count of their tryouts.

sql: SELECT AVG(HS) FROM Player;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average high score across all players.

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.cName = 'ASU';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of players who have tried out for the college 'ASU'.

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.decision = 'yes';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of players who successfully passed their tryouts.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.cName, T1.state FROM College AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.cName = T2.cName;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique colleges, by name and state, that have held a tryout.

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID JOIN College AS T3 ON T2.cName = T3.cName WHERE T3.state = 'AZ';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the names of players who had a tryout at a college located in Arizona.

sql: SELECT pPos, COUNT(*) FROM Tryout GROUP BY pPos;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each distinct player position (`pPos`) from the tryouts along with a count of how many players are trying out for that position.

sql: SELECT pName, HS FROM Player WHERE HS > (SELECT AVG(HS) FROM Player);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and high scores of all players whose high score is greater than the overall average.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT pPos FROM Tryout;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique player positions recorded in the tryouts.

sql: SELECT T2.cName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T1.pName = 'Andrew';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the colleges where the player named 'Andrew' had a tryout.

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T1.yCard = 'no' AND T2.pPos = 'striker';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the player who tried out as a striker and has not received a yellow card.

sql: SELECT T1.pName, T2.cName FROM Player AS T1 LEFT JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing every player's name and the name of each college they have tried out for, including players who have not yet had a tryout.

sql: SELECT T1.pName, T3.enr FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID JOIN College AS T3 ON T2.cName = T3.cName;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name of each player and the student enrollment of the college where they had a tryout.

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID GROUP BY T1.pID, T1.pName HAVING count(T2.cName) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of any player who has participated in more than one tryout.

sql: SELECT cName FROM College WHERE cName IN (SELECT DISTINCT cName FROM Tryout) ORDER BY enr DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the college with the highest enrollment among all colleges that have held a tryout.

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID JOIN College AS T3 ON T2.cName = T3.cName WHERE T3.enr > 15000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of players who have tried out for large colleges, specifically those with an enrollment greater than 15,000 students.

sql: SELECT T2.state, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.pID) FROM Tryout AS T1 JOIN College AS T2 ON T1.cName = T2.cName GROUP BY T2.state;

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.pPos = 'goalie' AND T2.decision = 'yes' UNION SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.pPos = 'striker' AND T2.decision = 'yes';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all players who were successfully selected during tryouts for either the 'goalie' or 'striker' position.

sql: SELECT pPos FROM Tryout GROUP BY pPos ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most common player position from the tryouts based on the number of applicants for each position.

sql: SELECT pName, HS FROM Player ORDER BY HS DESC LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and high scores of the top 2 players, ordered from highest to lowest score.

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID JOIN College AS T3 ON T2.cName = T3.cName WHERE T2.decision = 'yes' AND T3.enr < 20000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of players who successfully passed a tryout for a college with an enrollment of less than 20,000 students.

sql: SELECT T1.pName, T3.enr FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID JOIN College AS T3 ON T2.cName = T3.cName WHERE T2.decision = 'no';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the name of each player rejected from a tryout and the student enrollment of the college that rejected them.

sql: SELECT pID FROM Tryout WHERE cName = 'LSU' INTERSECT SELECT pID FROM Tryout WHERE cName = 'ASU';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the player ID (`pID`) of the player who participated in tryouts for both 'LSU' and 'ASU'.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.HS) FROM Player AS T1 WHERE T1.pID IN (SELECT pID FROM Tryout WHERE decision = 'yes');
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the average HS score for all players who passed their tryouts.

sql: SELECT cName FROM Tryout GROUP BY cName HAVING MIN(decision) = 'yes';

sql: SELECT T2.cName, AVG(T1.HS) FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID GROUP BY T2.cName;

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.decision = 'yes' ORDER BY T1.HS ASC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.pName, T3.cName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID JOIN College AS T3 ON T2.cName = T3.cName WHERE T1.HS * 10 >= T3.enr;

sql: SELECT pName FROM Player WHERE pID NOT IN (SELECT pID FROM Tryout WHERE decision = 'no');

sql: SELECT state FROM College GROUP BY state ORDER BY SUM(enr) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT MAX(enr) - MIN(enr) FROM College;

sql: SELECT T3.cName, T3.state, COUNT(T1.pID) FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID JOIN College AS T3 ON T2.cName = T3.cName GROUP BY T3.cName, T3.state;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.actid FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname, T2.actid FROM Faculty AS T1 JOIN Faculty_Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.FacID = T2.FacID;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, COUNT(T2.actid) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid GROUP BY T1.StuID;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname, T3.activity_name FROM Faculty AS T1 JOIN Faculty_Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.FacID = T2.FacID JOIN Activity AS T3 ON T2.actid = T3.actid WHERE T1.Rank = 'Instructor';

sql: SELECT city_code, COUNT(StuID) FROM Student GROUP BY city_code;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Fname, T2.Lname FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Faculty AS T2 ON T1.Advisor = T2.FacID;

sql: SELECT T2.activity_name, COUNT(T1.stuid) FROM Participates_in AS T1 JOIN Activity AS T2 ON T1.actid = T2.actid GROUP BY T2.activity_name;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each activity name along with the total number of students participating in that activity.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Faculty AS T2 ON T1.Advisor = T2.FacID WHERE T2.Rank = 'Professor';
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the first and last names of all students who are advised by a faculty member with the rank of 'Professor'.

sql: SELECT Rank, COUNT(FacID) FROM Faculty GROUP BY Rank;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM Faculty AS T1 JOIN Faculty_Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.FacID = T2.FacID GROUP BY T1.FacID HAVING COUNT(T2.actid) > 2;

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student);

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID NOT IN (SELECT T1.stuid FROM Participates_in AS T1 JOIN Activity AS T2 ON T1.actid = T2.actid WHERE T2.activity_name = 'Canoeing');

sql: SELECT T2.Fname, T2.Lname FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Faculty AS T2 ON T1.Advisor = T2.FacID GROUP BY T1.Advisor ORDER BY COUNT(T1.StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, 'Student' AS Role FROM Student UNION SELECT Fname, Lname, 'Faculty' AS Role FROM Faculty;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Fname, T2.LName, T1.city_code FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.city_code = T2.city_code WHERE T1.StuID < T2.StuID;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Faculty_Participates_in AS T2 WHERE T1.Advisor = T2.FacID);

sql: SELECT Major, Sex, COUNT(*) FROM Student GROUP BY Major, Sex;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM Faculty AS T1 LEFT JOIN Faculty_Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.FacID = T2.FacID WHERE T2.FacID IS NULL AND T1.FacID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Advisor FROM Student);

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T4.Rank FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid JOIN Activity AS T3 ON T2.actid = T3.actid JOIN Faculty AS T4 ON T1.Advisor = T4.FacID WHERE T3.activity_name = 'Mountain Climbing';

sql: SELECT T2.activity_name FROM Participates_in AS T1 JOIN Activity AS T2 ON T1.actid = T2.actid GROUP BY T1.actid ORDER BY COUNT(T1.stuid) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the most popular activity, determined by finding the activity with the highest count of student participants.

sql: SELECT * FROM Student WHERE Age = 18;

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T3.Venue FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Herbert Swindells';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and venue of the event reported on by the journalist 'Herbert Swindells'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Event_ID) FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID WHERE T1.Years_working > 5 GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM event AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.journalist_ID) > 1;

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T1.Age > 30;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID GROUP BY T1.journalist_ID, T1.Name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Event_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Nationality, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Event_ID) FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Nationality;

result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing pairs of journalists' names who have both reported on the same news event.

sql: SELECT T1.Nationality FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID GROUP BY T1.Nationality ORDER BY AVG(T1.Years_working) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Name FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Event_Attendance > (T1.Years_working * 10);

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, providing a complete list of all customers and their details, including payment method, name, and the date they became a customer.

sql: SELECT * FROM Products WHERE product_details = 'Latte';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details for the product identified as 'Latte'.

sql: SELECT address_content, city, state_province_county FROM Addresses WHERE country = 'USA' ORDER BY state_province_county;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the street address, city, and state for all addresses in the USA, sorted alphabetically by state.

sql: SELECT order_status, COUNT(*) FROM Customer_Orders GROUP BY order_status;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each distinct order status along with the total number of orders for that status.

sql: SELECT customer_name FROM Customers WHERE payment_method = 'Cash';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT payment_method FROM Customers;

sql: SELECT product_id, product_details FROM Products ORDER BY product_details DESC;

sql: SELECT product_id, SUM(order_quantity) FROM Order_Items GROUP BY product_id;

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T2.order_date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each customer's name alongside the date for each of their corresponding orders.

sql: SELECT channel_code, contact_number FROM Customer_Contact_Channels WHERE customer_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the available contact channels and their corresponding contact numbers for a single, specific customer (customer ID 2).

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_status = 'Cancelled';

sql: SELECT T1.product_details, SUM(T2.order_quantity) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_details;

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.address_type = 'Billing';

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name HAVING count(*) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T3.city FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id;

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, count(T2.order_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.payment_method = 'Credit Card' GROUP BY T1.customer_name;

sql: SELECT product_details FROM Products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM Order_Items WHERE order_quantity >= 5);

sql: SELECT T1.product_details, AVG(T2.order_quantity) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_details;

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Contact_Channels AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.channel_code = 'Email';

sql: SELECT customer_name FROM Customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status = 'Cancelled');

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.address_type IN ('Billing', 'Residential') GROUP BY T1.customer_name HAVING count(DISTINCT T2.address_type) = 2;

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, T1.order_date, T1.order_status FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_details = 'Americano';

sql: SELECT T4.city, SUM(T1.order_quantity) FROM Order_Items AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.address_id GROUP BY T4.city;

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name ORDER BY count(T2.order_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the single customer who has placed the most orders.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_details = 'Dove Chocolate' EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_details = 'Americano';

sql: SELECT T1.payment_method, SUM(T3.order_quantity) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.payment_method;

sql: SELECT T2.product_details FROM Order_Items AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_id ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T1.order_id) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T5.city, T4.product_details, T3.order_quantity FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN (SELECT T6.customer_id, T7.city FROM Customer_Addresses AS T6 JOIN Addresses AS T7 ON T6.address_id = T7.address_id) AS T5 ON T1.customer_id = T5.customer_id WHERE T2.order_status = 'Delivered';

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MIN(order_date) AS first_order_date FROM Customer_Orders GROUP BY customer_id) AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id AND T2.order_date = T3.first_order_date WHERE T2.order_status = 'Cancelled';

sql: SELECT T4.product_details, SUM(T3.order_quantity) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T1.payment_method = 'Cheque' GROUP BY T4.product_details;

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Contact_Channels AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.channel_code = 'Email' GROUP BY T1.customer_name HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T3.order_quantity) AS total_quantity FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name ORDER BY total_quantity DESC;

sql: SELECT T1.protein_name, T2.Institution FROM protein AS T1 JOIN Institution AS T2 ON T1.Institution_id = T2.Institution_id;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Pixels FROM phone AS T1 JOIN chip_model AS T2 ON T1.chip_model = T2.Model_name JOIN screen_mode AS T3 ON T1.screen_mode = T3.Graphics_mode WHERE T2.RAM_MiB >= 32.0;

result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of TV channels that broadcast both regular TV series and cartoons.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Directed_by FROM Cartoon AS T1 WHERE T1.Channel IN (SELECT Channel FROM TV_series WHERE Rating > 5);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique directors of cartoons that air on channels featuring TV series with a rating greater than 5.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN TV_series AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel GROUP BY T1.series_name HAVING AVG(T2.Viewers_m) > 8;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the TV channel(s) where the average viewership across all of its associated series is greater than 8 million.

sql: SELECT Title FROM Cartoon WHERE Channel NOT IN (SELECT Channel FROM TV_series WHERE Episode = 'A Love of a Lifetime');
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the titles of cartoons that do not air on the same channel as the TV series episode 'A Love of a Lifetime'.

sql: SELECT T1.Directed_by, count(DISTINCT T1.Channel) FROM Cartoon AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Directed_by ORDER BY count(DISTINCT T1.Channel) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each director alongside the count of distinct channels they have directed for, ordered from the director with the most unique channels to the fewest.

sql: SELECT T3.series_name, T1.Episode, T1.Rating FROM TV_series AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Channel, MAX(Rating) AS max_rating FROM TV_series GROUP BY Channel) AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.Channel AND T1.Rating = T2.max_rating JOIN TV_Channel AS T3 ON T1.Channel = T3.id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows identifying the highest-rated episode for each TV channel, listing the channel's name, the episode's title, and its top rating.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name FROM TV_Channel AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT Channel FROM Cartoon EXCEPT SELECT Channel FROM TV_series);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of TV channels that are classified as cartoon channels but do not have any associated TV series.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name, T2.c_count + T3.s_count FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Channel, count(*) AS c_count FROM Cartoon GROUP BY Channel) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel JOIN (SELECT Channel, count(*) AS s_count FROM TV_series GROUP BY Channel) AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.Channel WHERE T1.Country = 'Italy';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each Italian TV channel's name alongside the total combined count of its cartoons and TV series programs.

sql: SELECT Written_by, COUNT(*) FROM Cartoon GROUP BY Written_by HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying cartoon writers who have written more than one cartoon, along with the total count of cartoons for each of those writers.

sql: SELECT Episode, Weekly_Rank FROM TV_series WHERE Weekly_Rank = (SELECT MIN(Weekly_Rank) FROM TV_series WHERE Channel IN (SELECT id FROM TV_Channel WHERE Content = 'music'));
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the episode that achieved the best (lowest) weekly rank among all shows on music-content channels, displaying its title and rank.

sql: SELECT series_name, Language FROM TV_Channel WHERE id IN (SELECT Channel FROM TV_series) AND id NOT IN (SELECT Channel FROM Cartoon);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and language of TV channels that broadcast TV series but do not broadcast cartoons.

sql: SELECT T1.Country, AVG(T2.Rating) FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN TV_series AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel GROUP BY T1.Country;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each country along with the average rating for all TV series broadcast by channels from that country.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name, T2.Title FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Channel, Title FROM Cartoon UNION ALL SELECT Channel, Episode FROM TV_series) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows that match TV channel names with the corresponding titles of the cartoons and TV series episodes they broadcast.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Directed_by FROM Cartoon AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TV_Channel AS T2 WHERE T2.id = T1.Channel AND T2.Pay_per_view_PPV = 'no');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique directors who have directed at least one cartoon airing on a free (non-Pay-per-view) TV channel.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN TV_series AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel WHERE T2.Viewers_m = (SELECT MAX(Viewers_m) FROM TV_series);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the TV channel that broadcasts the single TV series with the highest viewership.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name, SUM(T2.show_count) AS total_shows FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Channel, COUNT(*) AS show_count FROM TV_series GROUP BY Channel UNION ALL SELECT Channel, COUNT(*) AS show_count FROM Cartoon GROUP BY Channel) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel GROUP BY T1.series_name ORDER BY total_shows DESC;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each TV channel by name along with the total count of shows (combining both TV series and cartoons), ordered from the channel with the most shows to the least.

sql: SELECT Episode, Rating FROM TV_series WHERE Rating > (SELECT AVG(Rating) FROM TV_series);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the episode and rating for all TV series episodes that have a rating higher than the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name, T2.Title, (SELECT count(*) FROM TV_series AS T3 WHERE T3.Channel = T1.id) AS series_count FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN Cartoon AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each cartoon's title, the name of its TV channel, and a count of the total number of series broadcast by that channel.

sql: SELECT T1.Written_by, count(T1.id) AS cartoon_count FROM Cartoon AS T1 JOIN TV_Channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.id WHERE T2.Country = 'Italy' GROUP BY T1.Written_by ORDER BY cartoon_count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top 3 writers with the most cartoons on Italian TV channels, ordered from the highest cartoon count to the lowest.

sql: SELECT series_name FROM TV_Channel AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TV_series AS T2 WHERE T2.Channel = T1.id AND T2.Share = 9) AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Cartoon AS T3 WHERE T3.Channel = T1.id AND T3.Written_by = 'Steven Melching');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the TV channel that airs both a TV series with a share of 9 and a cartoon written by 'Steven Melching'.

sql: SELECT Directed_by, MIN(Original_air_date) FROM Cartoon GROUP BY Directed_by;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each cartoon director along with the original air date of their earliest-aired cartoon.

sql: SELECT Episode, Weekly_Rank FROM TV_series WHERE Weekly_Rank < (SELECT Weekly_Rank FROM TV_series WHERE Episode = 'Game Three');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the TV series episodes and their corresponding weekly rank for all episodes that ranked better (i.e., had a rank number less than) the 'Game Three' episode.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name, T2.Title, T2.Type FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Channel, Episode AS Title, 'TV Series' AS Type FROM TV_series UNION ALL SELECT Channel, Title, 'Cartoon' AS Type FROM Cartoon) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel WHERE T1.Country = 'Italy';
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing all TV series and cartoons broadcast on Italian channels, showing the channel name, the program's title, and its content type.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Channel, COUNT(DISTINCT Directed_by) AS dir_count FROM Cartoon GROUP BY Channel) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel ORDER BY T2.dir_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the TV channel that has aired cartoons from the largest number of distinct directors.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name, T2.Title, T2.Directed_by FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN (SELECT * FROM Cartoon WHERE Directed_by <> 'Ben Jones') AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the TV channel's series name, the cartoon's title, and its director for all cartoons that were not directed by 'Ben Jones'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Staff LIMIT 10;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows representing a sample of staff members, showing all their available profile details including ID, gender, name, and contact information.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_price > 100;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and price of products that cost more than $100.

sql: SELECT * FROM Complaints WHERE staff_id = 114;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing all details for every complaint handled by the staff member with ID 114.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE state = 'Hawaii';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for the customer located in Hawaii.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products ORDER BY product_price DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each product and its corresponding price, ordered from most to least expensive.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Customers;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value that represents the total number of customers.

sql: SELECT * FROM Complaints WHERE complaint_status_code = 'New';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all available details for customer complaints that currently have a 'New' status.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM Staff;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last name of each staff member.

sql: SELECT * FROM Products WHERE product_category_code = 'Book';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details for products categorized as 'Book'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Complaints WHERE complaint_outcome_code = 'OK';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows containing all available details for complaints that were resolved with an 'OK' outcome.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, COUNT(T1.complaint_id) FROM Complaints AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T2.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each product name along with the total count of complaints filed for that product.

sql: SELECT product_category_code, AVG(product_price) FROM Products GROUP BY product_category_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each product category code along with the average price of products within that category.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique customer ID and email address for each customer who has filed a complaint.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, COUNT(T1.complaint_id) FROM Complaints AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T2.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each staff member's first and last name along with the total count of complaints they have handled.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM Complaints WHERE complaint_status_code = 'New');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the product name and price for products that currently have a complaint with a 'New' status.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Complaints AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id HAVING COUNT(T1.complaint_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last name of each staff member who has had at least one complaint filed against them.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.complaint_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the full customer details for the specific customer who filed complaint ID 2.

sql: SELECT complaint_type_code, COUNT(*) FROM Complaints WHERE complaint_status_code = 'Closed' GROUP BY complaint_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each complaint type alongside the total number of complaints that have been marked as 'Closed' for that specific type.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT product_id FROM Complaints);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.complaint_type_code FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the first and last name of each staff member alongside the type of complaint they handled.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.customer_type_code = 'Good Credit Rating';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the customer ID and email address for customers with a 'Good Credit Rating' who have filed a complaint.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_price > (SELECT AVG(product_price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and price for products that cost more than the average product price.

sql: SELECT customer_id, town_city, state FROM Customers WHERE state = 'Kansas' UNION SELECT customer_id, town_city, state FROM Customers WHERE state = 'Hawaii';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the customer ID, town/city, and state for all customers located in either Kansas or Hawaii.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.complaint_type_code = 'Product Failure';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of products that have received a complaint categorized as a 'Product Failure'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.staff_id, T1.first_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T3.town_city = 'Maryamport';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the staff ID and first name of staff members who have handled complaints from customers living in 'Maryamport'.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, COUNT(T1.complaint_id) AS complaint_count FROM Complaints AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T1.complaint_status_code = 'Closed' GROUP BY T2.staff_id ORDER BY complaint_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single staff member with the highest count of 'Closed' complaints, providing their first name, last name, and the total complaint count.

sql: SELECT T2.customer_id, T2.email_address, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.product_id) AS distinct_products_complained FROM Complaints AS T1 JOIN Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T2.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.product_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying customers who have complained about more than one unique product, listing each customer's ID, email address, and the total count of distinct products involved in their complaints.

sql: SELECT T2.product_category_code, AVG(julianday(T1.date_complaint_closed) - julianday(T1.date_complaint_raised)) AS avg_resolution_days FROM Complaints AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.complaint_status_code = 'Closed' GROUP BY T2.product_category_code ORDER BY avg_resolution_days DESC;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the average time (in days) to resolve a complaint for each product category, ordered from the category with the longest resolution time to the shortest.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_name = 'Chocolate';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the customer ID and email address for all customers who have filed a complaint related to the 'Chocolate' product.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.complaint_type_code = 'Product Failure' EXCEPT SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.complaint_type_code = 'Product Unusable';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address, T3.product_name, T3.product_price FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id ORDER BY T3.product_price DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the customer (by ID and email) and the product (by name and price) associated with the complaint filed against the single most expensive product.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_id, T1.first_name, SUM(CASE WHEN T2.complaint_status_code = 'New' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS new_complaints, SUM(CASE WHEN T2.complaint_status_code = 'Closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS closed_complaints FROM Staff AS T1 LEFT JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each staff member's ID and first name, along with the total count of their 'New' and 'Closed' complaints.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Complaints AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Complaints AS T3 WHERE T3.customer_id = T1.customer_id AND T3.complaint_outcome_code != 'OK');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the customer ID and email address for customers who have filed at least one complaint, where all of their complaints were resolved successfully with an 'OK' status.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.product_price, T1.product_category_code FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_price > (SELECT AVG(T2.product_price) FROM Products AS T2 WHERE T2.product_category_code = T1.product_category_code);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying products that are priced higher than the average price within their own product category, showing the product's name, price, and category code.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T3.product_price) AS total_product_value FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id ORDER BY total_product_value DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top staff members, ranked from highest to lowest by the total value of products associated with the complaints they handled.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_category_code IN ('Book', 'Hardware') GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.product_category_code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the customer ID and email address for customers who have lodged complaints about products in both the 'Book' and 'Hardware' categories.

sql: SELECT T1.complaint_id, T1.date_complaint_raised, T2.product_name, T2.date_product_discontinued FROM Complaints AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.date_complaint_raised > T2.date_product_discontinued;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying complaints that were raised for products after they had already been discontinued, listing the complaint ID, the date it was raised, the product name, and the product's discontinuation date.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Complaints AS T2 WHERE T2.staff_id = T1.staff_id AND T2.complaint_type_code = 'Product Failure');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the staff ID, first name, and last name for all staff members who have never been associated with a 'Product Failure' complaint.

sql: SELECT AVG(complaint_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(complaint_id) AS complaint_count FROM Complaints GROUP BY customer_id) AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of complaints per customer.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T2.customer_id FROM Complaints AS T2) AND T1.town_city IN (SELECT T3.town_city FROM Customers AS T3 GROUP BY T3.town_city HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.product_id, T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Complaints AS T2 JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T2.product_id = T1.product_id AND T3.customer_type_code = 'Good Credit Rating');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the product ID and name for products that have received at least one complaint from a customer with a 'Good Credit Rating'.

sql: SELECT T3.product_id, T3.product_name, T3.product_description FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T1.last_name = 'Erdman';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the product details (ID, name, and description) for a product involved in a complaint handled by the staff member 'Erdman'.

sql: SELECT T3.product_name, T4.product_name FROM Complaints AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id AND T1.product_id < T2.product_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T1.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T2.product_id = T4.product_id GROUP BY T3.product_name, T4.product_name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the top 5 pairs of products most frequently complained about together by the same customers, ordered from the most common pair to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Complaints) AND T1.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Complaints WHERE complaint_status_code = 'Closed');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the customer ID and email address for customers who have at least one active complaint that has not yet been marked as 'Closed'.

sql: SELECT T2.complaint_type_code FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.product_category_code = 'Food' GROUP BY T2.complaint_type_code ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most common complaint type for products in the 'Food' category, based on the total number of complaints.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.town_city = 'Sauerberg' AND T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T2.customer_id FROM Complaints AS T2 WHERE T2.complaint_status_code = 'New');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the customer ID and email address for customers in 'Sauerberg' who have a complaint with a 'New' status.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_id, T1.first_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id EXCEPT SELECT T1.staff_id, T1.first_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.product_id = 117;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the ID and first name of a staff member who has handled at least one complaint, but has not handled any complaints related to product ID 117.

sql: SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN T1.complaint_status_code = 'Closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(T1.complaint_id) AS percentage_closed FROM Complaints AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.product_category_code = 'Book';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the percentage of complaints marked as 'Closed' for all products within the 'Book' category.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, COUNT(T1.complaint_id) AS num_complaints FROM Complaints AS T1 GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(T1.complaint_id) > (SELECT AVG(complaint_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(complaint_id) AS complaint_count FROM Complaints GROUP BY customer_id) AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying customers with an above-average number of complaints, showing each customer's ID and their total complaint count.

sql: SELECT T2.product_category_code, COUNT(T1.complaint_id) as complaint_count FROM Complaints AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.complaint_type_code = 'Product Unusable' GROUP BY T2.product_category_code ORDER BY complaint_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single product category with the most "Product Unusable" complaints, along with the total count of those complaints.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T2.customer_id FROM Complaints AS T2 JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.date_product_first_available > '2000-01-01');

sql: SELECT team_long_name, team_short_name FROM Team WHERE team_short_name = 'GEN';

sql: SELECT name FROM League WHERE name = 'Belgium Jupiler League';
