sql: SELECT * FROM employee;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows containing the complete record for every employee, including their name, SSN, personal details, salary, and department information.

sql: SELECT Dname, Dnumber FROM department;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and corresponding number for every department.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname FROM employee WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of all female employees.

sql: SELECT Pname, Plocation FROM project WHERE Plocation = 'Bellaire';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and location for the project located in 'Bellaire'.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname, Salary FROM employee ORDER BY Salary DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each employee's first name, last name, and salary, ordered from highest to lowest salary.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM employee;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of employees.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname, Address FROM employee WHERE Dno = 5;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first name, last name, and address for all employees belonging to department number 5.

sql: SELECT Dependent_name, Essn FROM dependent WHERE Relationship = 'Daughter';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name of each dependent identified as a 'Daughter' and the Social Security Number of the employee they are associated with.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Dlocation FROM dept_locations;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows providing a distinct list of all locations where departments are based.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname, T2.Dname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of all employees, along with the name of the department to which they are assigned.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname, T2.Hours FROM employee AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn WHERE T2.Pno = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first name, last name, and hours worked for each employee assigned to project number 1.

sql: SELECT T2.Dname, count(T1.Ssn) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber GROUP BY T2.Dname;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each department name along with the total count of employees in that department.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN dependent AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique first and last names of employees who have at least one dependent.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber WHERE T2.Dname = 'Research';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows with the first and last names of all employees who work in the 'Research' department.

sql: SELECT T2.Dname, AVG(T1.Salary) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber GROUP BY T2.Dname;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each department name and the average salary of the employees within that department.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname FROM employee WHERE Ssn IN (SELECT Mgr_ssn FROM department);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of employees who serve as department managers.

sql: SELECT T1.Pname, SUM(T2.Hours) FROM project AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Pnumber = T2.Pno GROUP BY T1.Pname;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each project name along with the total sum of hours worked on that project.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn GROUP BY T1.Ssn, T1.Fname, T1.Lname HAVING SUM(T2.Hours) > 10;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last names of employees who have worked a cumulative total of more than 10 hours across all their assigned projects.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Mgr_ssn JOIN dept_locations AS T3 ON T2.Dnumber = T3.Dnumber WHERE T3.Dlocation = 'Houston';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of managers for any department located in 'Houston'.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn JOIN project AS T3 ON T2.Pno = T3.Pnumber WHERE T3.Pname = 'ProductX';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of all employees assigned to the project named 'ProductX'.

sql: SELECT T2.Dname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber GROUP BY T2.Dname HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of departments that have more than one employee.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN dependent AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn WHERE T1.Sex = T2.Sex;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of employees who have a dependent of the same gender.

sql: SELECT T1.Pname, T2.Dname FROM project AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dnum = T2.Dnumber;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows containing each project name and the name of its associated department.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname FROM employee WHERE Ssn NOT IN (SELECT Mgr_ssn FROM department WHERE Mgr_ssn IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of employees who are not department managers.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Address FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Mgr_ssn WHERE T2.Dname = 'Research';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first name and address of the employee who is the manager of the 'Research' department.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname AS Employee_Name, T1.Lname AS Employee_Lname, T2.Fname AS Supervisor_Name, T2.Lname AS Supervisor_Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN employee AS T2 ON T1.Super_ssn = T2.Ssn;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each employee's first and last name alongside the first and last name of their direct supervisor.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname FROM employee WHERE Salary = (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM employee);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of the employee with the highest salary.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 WHERE T1.Dno IN (SELECT T2.Dnumber FROM dept_locations AS T2 WHERE T2.Dlocation = 'Stafford');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of all employees who work in departments based at the 'Stafford' location.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname, Salary FROM employee WHERE Salary > (SELECT AVG(Salary) FROM employee);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first name, last name, and salary for each employee who earns more than the company's average salary.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber JOIN works_on AS T3 ON T1.Ssn = T3.Essn JOIN project AS T4 ON T3.Pno = T4.Pnumber WHERE T2.Dname = 'Research' AND T4.Plocation != 'Houston';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of employees from the 'Research' department who are working on projects located anywhere other than 'Houston'.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname, T3.Fname AS ManagerFname, T3.Lname AS ManagerLname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber JOIN employee AS T3 ON T2.Mgr_ssn = T3.Ssn;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each employee's first and last name along with the first and last name of their department's manager.

sql: SELECT T3.Pname, AVG(T1.Salary) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn JOIN project AS T3 ON T2.Pno = T3.Pnumber GROUP BY T3.Pname;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each project name along with the average salary of the employees working on that specific project.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname FROM employee WHERE Ssn NOT IN (SELECT Essn FROM dependent);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of employees who do not have any dependents.

sql: SELECT Fname FROM employee WHERE Sex = 'M' UNION SELECT Dependent_name FROM dependent WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows containing a unique, combined list of the first names of all male employees and the names of all female dependents.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 WHERE T1.Dno = 5 EXCEPT SELECT T2.Fname, T2.Lname FROM employee AS T2 JOIN works_on AS T3 ON T2.Ssn = T3.Essn WHERE T3.Pno = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of employees from department number 5 who are not assigned to project number 1.

sql: SELECT T4.Dlocation, SUM(T2.Hours) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn JOIN department AS T3 ON T1.Dno = T3.Dnumber JOIN dept_locations AS T4 ON T3.Dnumber = T4.Dnumber GROUP BY T4.Dlocation;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each department location along with the total sum of hours worked by employees in that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN employee AS T2 ON T1.Super_ssn = T2.Ssn WHERE T1.Salary < T2.Salary;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last names of employees who earn less than their direct supervisor.

sql: SELECT T1.Dname FROM department AS T1 WHERE T1.Dnumber IN (SELECT T2.Dnum FROM project AS T2 WHERE T2.Plocation = 'Bellaire');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the department that has projects located in 'Bellaire'.

sql: SELECT T1.Pname FROM project AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Pnumber = T2.Pno GROUP BY T1.Pname HAVING SUM(T2.Hours) >= 40;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of projects that have a cumulative total of at least 40 hours worked on them.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname FROM employee WHERE Bdate = (SELECT MIN(Bdate) FROM employee);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the oldest employee in the company.

sql: SELECT T2.Dname, AVG(T1.Salary) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber GROUP BY T2.Dname HAVING COUNT(T1.Ssn) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the department name and the average employee salary for each department that has more than one employee.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 WHERE T1.Ssn IN (SELECT T2.Essn FROM dependent AS T2) INTERSECT SELECT T3.Fname, T3.Lname FROM employee AS T3 WHERE T3.Dno = 5;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of employees who both work in department number 5 and have at least one dependent.

sql: SELECT T3.Fname, T3.Lname, COUNT(T4.Dependent_name) AS NumberOfDependents FROM department AS T1 JOIN employee AS T3 ON T1.Mgr_ssn = T3.Ssn LEFT JOIN dependent AS T4 ON T3.Ssn = T4.Essn GROUP BY T3.Ssn, T3.Fname, T3.Lname;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each department manager by their first and last name, along with the total count of their dependents.

sql: SELECT Plocation FROM project UNION SELECT Dlocation FROM dept_locations;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique locations that are either a project location or a department location.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn JOIN project AS T3 ON T2.Pno = T3.Pnumber WHERE T1.Dno = T3.Dnum;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the first and last names of employees who work on projects that are controlled by their own department.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Mgr_ssn JOIN dependent AS T3 ON T1.Ssn = T3.Essn WHERE T3.Dependent_name = 'Alice';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the manager who has a dependent named 'Alice'.

sql: SELECT T2.Dname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber GROUP BY T2.Dname ORDER BY AVG(T1.Salary) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the department with the highest average employee salary.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 WHERE T1.Ssn IN (SELECT DISTINCT Essn FROM works_on) AND T1.Ssn NOT IN (SELECT Essn FROM dependent);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows with the first and last names of employees who are assigned to projects but do not have any dependents.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 WHERE T1.Super_ssn = (SELECT T2.Ssn FROM employee AS T2 WHERE T2.Fname = 'Franklin' AND T2.Lname = 'Wong');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows with the first and last names of all employees who are supervised by Franklin Wong.

sql: SELECT customer_id, customer_name, customer_email FROM Customers WHERE payment_method_code = 'Credit Card';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the ID, name, and email for all customers whose payment method is 'Credit Card'.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_type_code = 'Hardware';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and price for all products classified as 'Hardware'.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, contact_phone FROM Contacts WHERE gender = 'female';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the first name, last name, and contact phone number for all contacts identified as female.

sql: SELECT line_1_number_building, city, state_province_county FROM Addresses WHERE country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the street address, city, and state for all recorded locations within the USA.

sql: SELECT order_id, customer_id, order_date FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status_code = 'Completed';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows of completed orders, listing the order ID, customer ID, and the date for each order.

sql: SELECT customer_name, customer_email FROM Customers ORDER BY customer_name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each customer's name and email address, sorted alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products ORDER BY product_price DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and price of the five most expensive products, ordered from highest to lowest price.

sql: SELECT COUNT(customer_id) FROM Customers;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total number of customers.

sql: SELECT order_id, product_id FROM Order_Items WHERE order_quantity = '3';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the specific order and product IDs for all line items with an order quantity of exactly three.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT product_type_code FROM Products;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all the unique product type codes available in the product catalog.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Contacts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each customer's name along with the first and last name of their associated contact person.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) AS order_count FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer's name along with a count of the total number of orders they have placed.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, SUM(T2.order_quantity) AS total_quantity_sold FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each product name along with the total quantity sold for that product.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, T2.product_name, T2.product_price, T1.order_quantity FROM Order_Items AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows detailing the items within each order, listing the corresponding order ID, product name, product price, and the quantity ordered.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T2.order_id, T2.order_date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.customer_name = 'Kayley';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the order ID and order date for all orders placed by the customer 'Kayley'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_email FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'Completed';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique names and email addresses for all customers who have at least one completed order.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, COUNT(T2.address_id) AS number_of_addresses FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Address_History AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer's ID along with the total count of their historical addresses.

sql: SELECT T1.product_type_code, AVG(T1.product_price) AS average_price FROM Products AS T1 GROUP BY T1.product_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each product type code and the corresponding average product price for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.customer_name FROM Contacts AS T1 JOIN Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.gender = 'male';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first name, last name, and associated customer name for all contacts identified as male.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, COUNT(T2.order_item_id) AS item_count FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id GROUP BY T1.order_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows, providing a count of the total number of items for each unique customer order.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.order_quantity > 5;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of products that have been purchased in quantities greater than 5.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T2.line_1_number_building, T2.city FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Address_History AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T3.address_id = T2.address_id WHERE T2.city = 'Hertafurt';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and full street address for any customer with a historical or current address in the city of 'Hertafurt'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_phone FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'Part';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows containing the names and phone numbers of customers who have partially fulfilled orders.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows providing a unique list of the names of all customers who have placed at least one order.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, T1.order_date FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_name = 'Apple';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows with the order ID and order date for all customer orders that included the product 'Apple'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) AS order_count FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name HAVING COUNT(T2.order_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing repeat customers who have placed more than one order, along with the total count of their orders.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.product_price FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_id IN (SELECT T2.product_id FROM Order_Items AS T2 GROUP BY T2.product_id HAVING SUM(T2.order_quantity) > 10);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the product name and price for all products with a total order quantity greater than 10.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name, T3.order_id, T3.order_date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Address_History AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T2.address_id = T4.address_id JOIN Customer_Orders AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T4.city = 'Hertafurt';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name, order ID, and order date for a customer who has a historical address in the city of 'Hertafurt'.

sql: SELECT T2.order_id, T1.customer_name, SUM(T3.product_price * T4.order_quantity) AS total_order_cost FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T4 ON T2.order_id = T4.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T4.product_id = T3.product_id GROUP BY T2.order_id, T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows, with each row showing a unique customer order, the corresponding customer's name, and the calculated total cost for that specific order.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T2.customer_id FROM Contacts AS T2) INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T3.customer_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T3);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of customers who have both placed an order and have contact information on file.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_email FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_name = 'Apple';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique customers who have purchased the product 'Apple', providing their name and email address.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T3.product_price * T4.order_quantity) AS total_spent FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T4 ON T2.order_id = T4.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T4.product_id = T3.product_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the top-spending customer, showing their name and the total amount they have spent across all orders.

sql: SELECT customer_name, customer_email FROM Customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM Customer_Orders);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and email of customers who have never placed an order.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT product_id FROM Order_Items);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and price for products that have never been included in an order.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.order_status_code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of customers who have placed orders that have been in more than one distinct status (e.g., 'shipped' and 'returned').

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.customer_name FROM Contacts AS T1 JOIN Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Customer_Address_History AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.address_id WHERE T1.gender = 'female' AND T4.country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows containing a unique list of female contacts (first and last name) and their corresponding customer's name, filtered to include only customers with a business address in the USA.

sql: SELECT T4.customer_name, T4.avg_items_per_order FROM (SELECT T1.customer_name, AVG(T3.item_count) AS avg_items_per_order FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN (SELECT order_id, COUNT(order_item_id) AS item_count FROM Order_Items GROUP BY order_id) AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name) AS T4 WHERE T4.avg_items_per_order > 0;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each customer's name and their average number of items per order, for all customers who have placed at least one order.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T4.product_price * T3.order_quantity) AS total_value FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name HAVING SUM(T4.product_price * T3.order_quantity) > (SELECT AVG(order_total) FROM (SELECT SUM(P.product_price * OI.order_quantity) AS order_total FROM Order_Items AS OI JOIN Products AS P ON OI.product_id = P.product_id GROUP BY OI.order_id));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing high-value customers whose total lifetime spending is greater than the average value of a single order, showing each customer's name and their total calculated spend.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Customer_Orders AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Customers AS T4 ON T3.customer_id = T4.customer_id WHERE T1.product_type_code = 'Hardware' AND T4.payment_method_code = 'Credit Card';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique 'Hardware' product that has been purchased by customers paying with a credit card.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T3.line_1_number_building, T3.city, T2.date_from, T2.date_to FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Address_History AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status_code = 'Completed');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows detailing the address history (street, city, and dates of residence) for every customer who has placed a completed order.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Contacts) AND T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Address_History) AND T1.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Orders);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of customers who have contact and address history on file but have never placed an order.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, 'Male Contact' AS contact_type FROM Contacts WHERE gender = 'male' UNION SELECT first_name, last_name, 'Female Contact' AS contact_type FROM Contacts WHERE gender = 'female';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first and last names of all contacts, with a 'contact_type' column categorizing each person as either 'Male Contact' or 'Female Contact' based on their gender.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Address_History AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.city = 'Hertafurt' EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Address_History AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.city = 'Edgardoberg';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a customer who has lived in 'Hertafurt' but has never lived in 'Edgardoberg'.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.product_price FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM Order_Items) ORDER BY T1.product_price DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and price of the single most expensive product that has ever been ordered.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T2.order_count FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, COUNT(order_id) AS order_count FROM Customer_Orders GROUP BY customer_id) AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id ORDER BY T2.order_count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer's name and their total order count, sorted from the customer with the most orders to the one with the fewest.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_type_code IN ('Hardware', 'Clothes') GROUP BY T1.customer_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T4.product_type_code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of customers who have purchased products from both the 'Hardware' and 'Clothes' categories.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, T1.customer_id, T1.order_date, T1.order_status_code FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 WHERE T1.order_date = (SELECT MAX(T2.order_date) FROM Customer_Orders AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows showing the order ID, customer ID, date, and status for each customer's most recent order.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_email FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Contacts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Customer_Address_History AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.address_id WHERE T4.country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names and email addresses of all customers who have a current or historical address in the USA.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T4.order_quantity) AS total_apple_products FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T4 ON T2.order_id = T4.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T4.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_name = 'Apple' GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each customer who purchased 'Apple' products and the total quantity they ordered.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Address_History AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name, T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(T2.address_id) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of customers who have only ever had a single address recorded in their history.

sql: SELECT * FROM workshop;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows providing a complete list of all workshops, showing the ID, date, venue, and name for each.

sql: SELECT Author, College FROM submission;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the author and college associated with each submission.

sql: SELECT Submission_ID, Author, College FROM submission WHERE Scores = 79.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the submission ID, author, and college for all submissions that received a score of exactly 79.0.

sql: SELECT Name, Date FROM workshop WHERE Venue = 'London UK';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and date for all workshops held at the 'London UK' venue.

sql: SELECT Author, Scores FROM submission WHERE College = 'Florida';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the author and score for the submission from 'Florida' college.

sql: SELECT Submission_ID, Scores FROM submission ORDER BY Scores DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each submission's ID and its corresponding score, ordered from highest to lowest score.

sql: SELECT Name, Venue FROM workshop ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and venue of each workshop, sorted alphabetically by the workshop's name.

sql: SELECT Result FROM Acceptance WHERE Submission_ID = 3 AND Workshop_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the acceptance result for the submission with ID 3 to the workshop with ID 2.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Venue FROM workshop;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each of the unique venues where a workshop is held.

sql: SELECT Submission_ID, Workshop_ID FROM Acceptance WHERE Result = 'Accepted';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the submission and workshop IDs for all submissions that have been accepted.

sql: SELECT T1.Author, T2.Result FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each author alongside the acceptance or rejection result for their corresponding submission.

sql: SELECT avg(Scores) FROM submission;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average score across all submissions.

sql: SELECT Result, count(*) FROM Acceptance GROUP BY Result;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each distinct acceptance result (e.g., 'Accepted', 'Rejected') and provides a total count for each category.

sql: SELECT Author, College FROM submission WHERE Scores > 75;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the authors and their colleges for submissions that achieved a score greater than 75.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Rejected';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique authors who have had at least one of their submissions rejected.

sql: SELECT T1.Workshop_ID, count(T1.Submission_ID) FROM Acceptance AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Workshop_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each workshop's ID along with the total count of submissions accepted into that workshop.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Acceptance AS T1 JOIN workshop AS T2 ON T1.Workshop_ID = T2.Workshop_ID WHERE T1.Result = 'Accepted';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of workshops that have a corresponding 'Accepted' result.

sql: SELECT T1.Author, T1.College FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T1.College = 'Florida';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the authors and college for each accepted submission originating from the 'Florida' college.

sql: SELECT College, avg(Scores) FROM submission GROUP BY College;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each college along with the average submission score for that college.

sql: SELECT Author, College FROM submission WHERE Submission_ID IN (SELECT Submission_ID FROM Acceptance);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the author and associated college for each submission that has been accepted.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Acceptance AS T1 JOIN workshop AS T2 ON T1.Workshop_ID = T2.Workshop_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of workshops that have had at least one acceptance.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T3.Date FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID WHERE T1.Submission_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and date of the workshop where the submission with an ID of 3 was accepted.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Acceptance WHERE Result = 'Rejected';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all acceptance records with a 'Rejected' result.

sql: SELECT max(Scores) FROM submission WHERE College = 'Miami (OH)';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the maximum score achieved from all submissions by the college 'Miami (OH)'.

sql: SELECT Author FROM submission WHERE Scores = 78.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the author of the submission that received a score of exactly 78.0.

sql: SELECT Author, College FROM submission WHERE Submission_ID NOT IN (SELECT Submission_ID FROM Acceptance);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the author and college for each submission that was not accepted.

sql: SELECT Author, Scores FROM submission WHERE Scores > (SELECT avg(Scores) FROM submission);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the authors and scores for all submissions that performed better than the overall average score.

sql: SELECT Author FROM submission WHERE Scores = (SELECT max(Scores) FROM submission);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the author of the submission that received the highest score.

sql: SELECT Author, College FROM submission WHERE College = 'Florida' UNION SELECT Author, College FROM submission WHERE College = 'Notre Dame';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the authors and their associated colleges for all submissions from either 'Florida' or 'Notre Dame'.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Acceptance AS T1 LEFT JOIN workshop AS T2 ON T1.Workshop_ID = T2.Workshop_ID WHERE T2.Workshop_ID IS NULL;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT College FROM submission GROUP BY College ORDER BY avg(Scores) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the college with the highest average score across its submissions.

sql: SELECT T3.Venue, count(*) FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID GROUP BY T3.Venue;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each workshop venue along with the total count of accepted submissions for that location.

sql: SELECT Submission_ID, count(Workshop_ID) FROM Acceptance GROUP BY Submission_ID HAVING count(Workshop_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying any submission accepted into more than one workshop, along with the total count of its workshop acceptances.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, avg(T1.Scores) FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID GROUP BY T3.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each workshop's name alongside the average score of its accepted submissions.

sql: SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID GROUP BY T1.Author, T1.Submission_ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Result) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the author of any submission that has received exactly two distinct review results (e.g., both 'Accept' and 'Reject').

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Acceptance AS T1 JOIN workshop AS T2 ON T1.Workshop_ID = T2.Workshop_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING count(CASE WHEN T1.Result = 'Accepted' THEN 1 END) = 0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of workshops that have had zero accepted submissions.

sql: SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID WHERE T3.Venue = 'London UK' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID WHERE T3.Venue = 'New Jersey USA';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the authors who had a submission accepted at a workshop in London, but did not have a submission accepted at a workshop in New Jersey.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID GROUP BY T3.Name HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.College) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a workshop that has accepted submissions from more than one distinct college.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Conference 2007';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique author who had a submission accepted into the 'Conference 2007' workshop.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Acceptance AS T1 JOIN workshop AS T2 ON T1.Workshop_ID = T2.Workshop_ID GROUP BY T2.Name ORDER BY count(T1.Submission_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the workshop with the most accepted submissions.

sql: SELECT T1.Author, T2.Author, T1.College FROM submission AS T1, submission AS T2 WHERE T1.College = T2.College AND T1.Author <> T2.Author;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Author FROM submission INTERSECT SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Accepted';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the authors who have had at least one submission accepted.

sql: SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Accepted' UNION SELECT Author FROM submission WHERE College = 'Notre Dame';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of authors whose submissions were accepted or who are from 'Notre Dame' college.

sql: SELECT T2.Result, avg(T1.Scores) FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID GROUP BY T2.Result;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows calculating the average submission score for each acceptance result status ('Accepted' and 'Rejected').

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM workshop AS T1 WHERE T1.Workshop_ID = (SELECT T2.Workshop_ID FROM Acceptance AS T2 WHERE T2.Result = 'Accepted' AND T2.Submission_ID = (SELECT T3.Submission_ID FROM submission AS T3 WHERE T3.Scores = (SELECT max(Scores) FROM submission)));
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Author, count(*) FROM submission GROUP BY Author;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each author along with their total count of submissions.

sql: SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Workshop 2007' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Workshop 2007' AND T2.Result = 'Accepted';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of an author whose submission to 'Workshop 2007' was not accepted.

sql: SELECT T1.Author, T1.Scores, T2.Result FROM submission AS T1 LEFT JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Rejected' OR T2.Result IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing authors of submissions that were either rejected or have no acceptance decision, along with their submission scores and the result.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, avg(T1.Scores) AS avg_score FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID GROUP BY T3.Name ORDER BY avg_score ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the workshop with the lowest average score among its accepted submissions.

sql: WITH AcceptedSubmissions AS (SELECT T1.Author, T2.Workshop_ID FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Accepted') SELECT T1.Name, T2.Author FROM workshop AS T1 JOIN AcceptedSubmissions AS T2 ON T1.Workshop_ID = T2.Workshop_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name of each workshop alongside the author of a submission that was accepted into it.

sql: SELECT * FROM useracct;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows containing the user ID and name for all user accounts.

sql: SELECT title FROM item;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the title of every available item.

sql: SELECT * FROM useracct WHERE name = 'Helen';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the user ID and name for the user account named 'Helen'.

sql: SELECT * FROM review WHERE rating = 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing all information for reviews that received a perfect rating of 10.

sql: SELECT * FROM trust WHERE trust = 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the source and target user IDs for all relationships that have the highest possible trust level of 10.

sql: SELECT title FROM item ORDER BY title ASC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the titles of all available items, sorted alphabetically.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM useracct;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total count of all user accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name of the user who authored each submitted review, with one row appearing for each review a user has written.

sql: SELECT title FROM item WHERE i_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the title of the item with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the title of each item that has received a review, with titles repeated for every review submitted.

sql: SELECT T1.name, count(T2.a_id) FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each user's name and the total number of reviews they have submitted.

sql: SELECT T1.title, avg(T2.rating) FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id GROUP BY T1.title;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each item title along with its calculated average review rating.

sql: SELECT T1.name, AVG(T2.rating) FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each user's name along with the average rating they have given across their reviews.

sql: SELECT u_id FROM review GROUP BY u_id HAVING count(a_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the user IDs of users who have submitted more than one review.

sql: SELECT source_u_id, count(target_u_id) FROM trust GROUP BY source_u_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each source user's ID along with a count of the total number of other users they trust.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT target_u_id FROM trust;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique IDs of all users who are trusted by at least one other user.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN trust AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.target_u_id WHERE T2.source_u_id = (SELECT u_id FROM useracct WHERE name = 'Helen');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of users who are trusted by the user 'Helen'.

sql: SELECT i_id FROM review GROUP BY i_id HAVING count(a_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the IDs of items that have received more than one review.

sql: SELECT source_u_id, AVG(trust) FROM trust GROUP BY source_u_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each source user ID along with their average assigned trust rating.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.rating FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T3 ON T1.u_id = T3.u_id JOIN item AS T2 ON T3.i_id = T2.i_id WHERE T2.title = 'orange';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of users and the ratings they provided for the item titled 'orange'.

sql: SELECT name FROM useracct WHERE u_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT u_id FROM review);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all users who have not submitted any reviews.

sql: SELECT T1.title, MIN(T2.rating), MAX(T2.rating) FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id GROUP BY T1.title;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each item's title along with the minimum and maximum review rating it has received.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN trust AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.target_u_id WHERE T2.trust = 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of users who have received the highest possible trust rating (a score of 10).

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.title FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id JOIN item AS T3 ON T2.i_id = T3.i_id ORDER BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each item reviewed by a user, pairing the user's name with the item's title, and sorted alphabetically by user name.

sql: SELECT target_u_id, SUM(trust) FROM trust GROUP BY target_u_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows showing the total accumulated trust score for each target user, grouped by their user ID.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN (SELECT u_id FROM review INTERSECT SELECT source_u_id FROM trust) AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of users who have both written a review and established a trust relationship with another user.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id WHERE T2.i_id IN (SELECT i_id FROM review WHERE u_id = (SELECT u_id FROM useracct WHERE name = 'Helen')) AND T1.name <> 'Helen';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a user who has reviewed at least one of the same items as the user 'Helen'.

sql: SELECT name FROM useracct WHERE u_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT u_id FROM review) AND u_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT target_u_id FROM trust);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a user who has written a review but is not trusted by any other user.

sql: SELECT i_id FROM review GROUP BY i_id HAVING AVG(rating) > (SELECT AVG(rating) FROM review);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the IDs of items whose average rating is greater than the overall average rating across all reviews.

sql: SELECT T1.source_u_id, T1.target_u_id FROM trust AS T1 JOIN trust AS T2 ON T1.source_u_id = T2.target_u_id AND T1.target_u_id = T2.source_u_id;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT name FROM useracct WHERE u_id = (SELECT u_id FROM review GROUP BY u_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the user who has written the most reviews.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING MAX(T2.rating) = 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of users who have given at least one perfect 10-star rating.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM trust AS T2 WHERE T2.target_u_id = T1.u_id) AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM review AS T3 WHERE T3.u_id = T1.u_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any user who has been trusted by at least one other user and has also written a review.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 WHERE T1.u_id IN (SELECT u_id FROM review) EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 WHERE T1.u_id IN (SELECT target_u_id FROM trust);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a user who has written a review but is not trusted by any other user.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id WHERE T1.i_id = (SELECT i_id FROM review GROUP BY i_id ORDER BY avg(rating) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the title of the single item with the highest average review rating, with the title displayed once for each review the item has.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 WHERE T1.u_id IN (SELECT T2.u_id FROM review AS T2 GROUP BY T2.u_id, T2.i_id HAVING AVG(T2.rating) < (SELECT AVG(rating) FROM review WHERE i_id = T2.i_id));
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a user who has rated at least one item lower than that item's overall average rating.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 WHERE T1.u_id IN (SELECT target_u_id FROM trust WHERE source_u_id = 1) AND T1.u_id IN (SELECT u_id FROM review);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a user who is both trusted by the user with ID 1 and has also written a review.

sql: SELECT T1.u_id, T1.i_id, T1.rating - T2.avg_rating AS rating_difference FROM review AS T1 JOIN (SELECT i_id, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating FROM review GROUP BY i_id) AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, showing for each review the user ID, item ID, and the calculated difference between that specific rating and the item's overall average rating.

sql: SELECT T1.source_u_id AS A, T1.target_u_id AS B, T2.target_u_id AS C FROM trust AS T1 JOIN trust AS T2 ON T1.target_u_id = T2.source_u_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying two-step paths of trust between users, where column A represents a user who trusts the user in column B, who in turn trusts the user in column C.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id WHERE T2.i_id IN (SELECT i_id FROM review GROUP BY i_id HAVING MAX(rating) = 10 AND MIN(rating) = 5);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of users who have reviewed items whose rating history includes both a maximum score of 10 and a minimum score of 5.

sql: SELECT name FROM useracct WHERE u_id IN (SELECT T1.u_id FROM review AS T1 JOIN item AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id WHERE T2.title = 'orange' UNION SELECT target_u_id FROM trust WHERE source_u_id = (SELECT u_id FROM useracct WHERE name = 'Helen'));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the names of users who have either reviewed the item 'orange' or are trusted by the user 'Helen'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 WHERE T1.u_id = (SELECT target_u_id FROM trust GROUP BY target_u_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the user who is trusted by the most other users, based on a count of trust relationships.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.trusting_count, T3.trusted_by_count FROM useracct AS T1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT source_u_id, COUNT(*) AS trusting_count FROM trust GROUP BY source_u_id) AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.source_u_id LEFT JOIN (SELECT target_u_id, COUNT(*) AS trusted_by_count FROM trust GROUP BY target_u_id) AS T3 ON T1.u_id = T3.target_u_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows detailing each user's trust network, showing their name alongside a count of users they trust and a count of users who trust them.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 WHERE T1.u_id IN (SELECT target_u_id FROM trust) AND T1.u_id NOT IN (SELECT u_id FROM review);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of users who are trusted by other users but have not yet submitted any reviews themselves.

sql: SELECT avg(T1.rating) FROM review AS T1 JOIN useracct AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id WHERE T2.u_id IN (SELECT target_u_id FROM trust WHERE source_u_id = (SELECT u_id FROM useracct WHERE name = 'Helen'));
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average review rating given by all users that the user 'Helen' trusts.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T4.title FROM trust AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.source_u_id = T2.u_id JOIN review AS T3 ON T1.target_u_id = T3.u_id JOIN item AS T4 ON T2.i_id = T4.i_id WHERE T2.i_id = T3.i_id;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the unique title of an item that has been reviewed by both a user and another user whom they trust.

sql: SELECT T1.rank FROM review AS T1 JOIN useracct AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id JOIN item AS T3 ON T1.i_id = T3.i_id WHERE T2.name = 'Helen' AND T3.title = 'orange';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the rank that the user named 'Helen' gave to the item titled 'orange'.

sql: SELECT name FROM useracct WHERE u_id IN (SELECT source_u_id FROM trust GROUP BY source_u_id HAVING avg(trust) > 7);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of users whose average trust rating is greater than 7.

sql: SELECT T1.u_id AS higher_reviewer, T2.u_id AS lower_reviewer, T1.i_id FROM review AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id AND T1.rating > T2.rating;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing pairs of reviewers for the same item where one reviewer gave a higher rating than the other, listing the user ID for the higher rating, the user ID for the lower rating, and the shared item's ID.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.title FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id JOIN item AS T3 ON T2.i_id = T3.i_id WHERE T2.rank = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the user who wrote a top-ranked review and the title of the item they reviewed.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM host ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and age of each host, ordered alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT * FROM party WHERE Party_Theme = 'Spring';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with all details for the party with the 'Spring' theme.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM host WHERE Nationality = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and age for all hosts whose nationality is the 'United States'.

sql: SELECT Party_Theme, First_year FROM party WHERE Location = 'Hemkade 48 Zaandam';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the party theme and the first year for all parties held at the 'Hemkade 48 Zaandam' location.

sql: SELECT Name, Nationality, Age FROM host;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name, nationality, and age for each host.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Location FROM party;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each unique location where a party has been held.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM host WHERE Age > 35;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and age of hosts who are older than 35.

sql: SELECT Party_ID, Host_ID FROM party_host WHERE Is_Main_in_Charge = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows that identify the main host in charge of each party, providing the corresponding `Party_ID` and `Host_ID`.

sql: SELECT * FROM party ORDER BY Number_of_hosts DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing all details for each party, ordered from the party with the most hosts to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Theme, T2.Name FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T3 ON T1.Party_ID = T3.Party_ID JOIN host AS T2 ON T3.Host_ID = T2.Host_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each party's theme along with the name of its corresponding host.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Party_ID) FROM party_host AS T1 JOIN host AS T2 ON T1.Host_ID = T2.Host_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each host's name along with the total count of parties they are hosting.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Theme, COUNT(T2.Host_ID) FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID GROUP BY T1.Party_Theme;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each party theme along with the total number of hosts for that theme.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM host AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Host_ID = T2.Host_ID WHERE T2.Is_Main_in_Charge = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the names of hosts who are designated as the main person in charge of a party.

sql: SELECT Nationality, AVG(Age) FROM host GROUP BY Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each nationality along with the average age of hosts from that country.

sql: SELECT Party_Theme, Number_of_hosts FROM party WHERE Number_of_hosts > 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the theme and host count for parties that have more than 5 hosts.

sql: SELECT Name FROM host WHERE Host_ID IN (SELECT Host_ID FROM party_host WHERE Party_ID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the host for the party with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Theme, T3.Name FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID WHERE T2.Is_Main_in_Charge = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each party's theme and the name of the host designated as the main person in charge.

sql: SELECT Nationality, COUNT(*) FROM host GROUP BY Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each host's nationality and the corresponding count of hosts from that nationality.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM host ORDER BY Age DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and age of the oldest host.

sql: SELECT Party_Theme, Location FROM party WHERE First_year = '2000';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the theme and location for all parties that first occurred in the year 2000.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, COUNT(T3.Host_ID) FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'Various locations' GROUP BY T1.Location;
result_summary: Returns 1 row counting the total number of hosts for parties that take place at 'Various locations'.

sql: SELECT Party_Theme, First_year FROM party WHERE Number_of_hosts > 3;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the theme and first year for parties that have more than three hosts.

sql: SELECT AVG(Number_of_hosts) FROM party WHERE First_year > '1999';

sql: SELECT T1.parent_entry_id, COUNT(*) FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 WHERE T1.parent_entry_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY T1.parent_entry_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.catalog_entry_id = T2.catalog_entry_id JOIN Attribute_Definitions AS T3 ON T2.attribute_id = T3.attribute_id WHERE T3.attribute_name = 'Yellow' INTERSECT SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_level_number = T2.catalog_level_number JOIN Catalogs AS T3 ON T2.catalog_id = T3.catalog_id WHERE T3.catalog_publisher = 'Murray Coffee shop';

sql: SELECT T2.catalog_level_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_level_number = T2.catalog_level_number GROUP BY T2.catalog_level_name ORDER BY AVG(T1.price_in_dollars) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the catalog level that contains the items with the highest average price.

sql: SELECT T3.catalog_publisher, SUM(T1.price_in_dollars) AS total_value FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_level_number = T2.catalog_level_number JOIN Catalogs AS T3 ON T2.catalog_id = T3.catalog_id GROUP BY T3.catalog_publisher ORDER BY total_value DESC;

result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique attribute names for all items found within the 'Chocolate' catalog.

sql: SELECT Price_Category, COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN price_in_dollars > 500 THEN 'High' WHEN price_in_dollars >= 200 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END AS Price_Category FROM Catalog_Contents) AS T1 GROUP BY Price_Category;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows grouping catalog items into price categories ('High', 'Medium', or 'Low') and providing a count of items for each category.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_level_number = T2.catalog_level_number JOIN Catalogs AS T3 ON T2.catalog_id = T3.catalog_id WHERE T3.catalog_name = 'Coffee Bean' AND T1.price_in_dollars > (SELECT AVG(T4.price_in_dollars) FROM Catalog_Contents AS T4 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T5 ON T4.catalog_level_number = T5.catalog_level_number JOIN Catalogs AS T6 ON T5.catalog_id = T6.catalog_id WHERE T6.catalog_name = 'Chocolate');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of items from the 'Coffee Bean' catalog that are more expensive than the average-priced item in the 'Chocolate' catalog.

sql: SELECT T3.attribute_name FROM Attribute_Definitions AS T3 WHERE T3.attribute_id IN (SELECT T2.attribute_id FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.catalog_entry_id = T2.catalog_entry_id WHERE T1.price_in_dollars > 600);

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_name FROM Catalogs AS T1 WHERE T1.catalog_id IN (SELECT T2.catalog_id FROM Catalog_Structure AS T2 WHERE T2.catalog_level_name = 'Category') EXCEPT SELECT T1.catalog_name FROM Catalogs AS T1 WHERE T1.catalog_id IN (SELECT T2.catalog_id FROM Catalog_Structure AS T2 WHERE T2.catalog_level_name = 'Product');

sql: SELECT T3.catalog_publisher FROM Catalogs AS T3 WHERE T3.catalog_id = (SELECT T2.catalog_id FROM Catalog_Structure AS T2 JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T1 ON T2.catalog_level_number = T1.catalog_level_number ORDER BY T1.price_in_euros DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the publisher whose catalog contains the single most expensive item, determined by the highest price in euros.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name, T1.price_in_dollars FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 WHERE T1.catalog_level_number = 1 UNION ALL SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name, T1.price_in_dollars FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 WHERE T1.price_in_dollars > 500;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing the name and price of all catalog items that are either top-level (level 1) or have a price greater than $500.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name, T1.catalog_level_number, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T2 WHERE T2.catalog_entry_id = T1.catalog_entry_id) AS attribute_count FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each catalog entry's name and level number, along with a count of how many additional attributes each entry has.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T2 WHERE T2.catalog_entry_id = T1.catalog_entry_id) > (SELECT AVG(attr_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(attribute_id) as attr_count FROM Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes GROUP BY catalog_entry_id) AS T3);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of catalog entries that possess a greater number of additional attributes than the average catalog entry.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_level_number = T2.catalog_level_number WHERE T2.catalog_id = 1 AND T1.price_in_dollars = (SELECT MAX(T3.price_in_dollars) FROM Catalog_Contents AS T3 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T4 ON T3.catalog_level_number = T4.catalog_level_number WHERE T4.catalog_id = 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the most expensive catalog item from the catalog with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_name, T2.AvgPrice FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T4.catalog_id, AVG(T3.price_in_dollars) AS AvgPrice FROM Catalog_Contents AS T3 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T4 ON T3.catalog_level_number = T4.catalog_level_number GROUP BY T4.catalog_id) AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each catalog's name along with the calculated average price of the items contained within that catalog.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_name, T3.catalog_entry_name, T3.price_in_dollars FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T3 ON T2.catalog_level_number = T3.catalog_level_number WHERE T3.price_in_dollars = (SELECT MAX(T5.price_in_dollars) FROM Catalog_Contents AS T5 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T4 ON T5.catalog_level_number = T4.catalog_level_number WHERE T4.catalog_id = T1.catalog_id);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, identifying the most expensive item within each product catalog by listing the catalog's name, the item's name, and its maximum price.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name, T2.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T2 ON T1.parent_entry_id = T2.parent_entry_id AND T1.previous_entry_id = T2.previous_entry_id WHERE T1.catalog_entry_id <> T2.catalog_entry_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying pairs of different catalog entries that occupy the same hierarchical position within the catalog structure.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Roller_Coaster_ID) FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each country's name along with the total count of roller coasters located in that country.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Height) FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each country along with the average height of its roller coasters.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Name FROM country AS T2 JOIN roller_coaster AS T1 ON T2.Country_ID = T1.Country_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of countries that have at least one roller coaster.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Park FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Austria';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of roller coasters and the parks they are in, specifically for those located in Austria.

sql: SELECT Status, COUNT(*) FROM roller_coaster GROUP BY Status;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each roller coaster's operational status and the total count of roller coasters with that status.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Speed) FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Roller_Coaster_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing countries that have more than one roller coaster, showing each country's name and the average speed of its coasters.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Name, T2.Population FROM country AS T2 JOIN roller_coaster AS T1 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T1.Speed > '55';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique countries that have at least one roller coaster with a speed greater than 55, showing each country's name and population.

sql: SELECT Park, SUM(Length) FROM roller_coaster GROUP BY Park;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each park along with the total combined length of all its roller coasters.

sql: SELECT Park, COUNT(*) FROM roller_coaster GROUP BY Park HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each park and the total number of roller coasters it contains.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T2.Languages = 'German';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of roller coasters located in countries where the official language is German.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, MAX(T1.Height) FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each country along with the height of its tallest roller coaster.

sql: SELECT Name, Speed FROM roller_coaster WHERE Speed > '51';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and speed of roller coasters with a speed greater than 51.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Length FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Austria' ORDER BY T1.Length DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and length of the longest roller coaster in Austria.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Finland';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all roller coasters located in Finland.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Height) FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING AVG(T1.Height) > (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM roller_coaster);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying countries whose average roller coaster height is greater than the overall global average, showing each qualifying country and its specific average height.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Park FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T2.Country_ID = (SELECT Country_ID FROM country ORDER BY Area DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and park of the roller coaster located in the country with the largest geographical area.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T1.Speed > '51' GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Roller_Coaster_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of each country that has more than one roller coaster with a speed exceeding 51.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Roller_Coaster_ID) AS Coaster_Count FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Name ORDER BY Coaster_Count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top countries by their total number of roller coasters, ordered from most to fewest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Park FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T2.Population > 8000000 AND T2.Area < 100000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and parks of roller coasters located in small but populous countries (population over 8 million and area under 100,000).

sql: SELECT Name, Park, Speed FROM roller_coaster WHERE Country_ID = 1 AND Speed > (SELECT AVG(Speed) FROM roller_coaster WHERE Country_ID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name, park, and speed for any roller coaster in a specific country (ID 1) that has a speed greater than the average speed of all roller coasters in that same country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Speed, T2.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T1.Speed = (SELECT MAX(T3.Speed) FROM roller_coaster AS T3 WHERE T3.Country_ID = T1.Country_ID);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, identifying the name and speed of the fastest roller coaster within each respective country.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM roller_coaster WHERE Height > (SELECT MAX(Height) FROM roller_coaster WHERE Country_ID = 2);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and height of any roller coaster that is taller than the tallest roller coaster from the country with ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM roller_coaster AS T2 WHERE T2.Country_ID = T1.Country_ID) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM roller_coaster AS T3 WHERE T3.Country_ID = T1.Country_ID AND T3.Status != 'Operating');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of countries where all of their roller coasters are currently in 'Operating' status.

sql: SELECT Name FROM roller_coaster UNION SELECT Name FROM country;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows containing a combined, unique list of names from both the roller coaster and country tables.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM country AS T2 JOIN roller_coaster AS T1 ON T2.Country_ID = T1.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Name EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM country AS T2 JOIN roller_coaster AS T1 ON T2.Country_ID = T1.Country_ID WHERE T1.Speed = '51';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of countries that have roller coasters, but specifically excluding any country that has a roller coaster with a speed of '51'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, MAX(T1.Height) - AVG(T1.Height) FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing countries with more than one roller coaster, showing the difference between the height of the tallest coaster and the average coaster height for that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T2.Languages = 'German' AND T1.Height > 100;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and height of roller coasters that are taller than 100 units and located in German-speaking countries.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM country AS T2 JOIN roller_coaster AS T1 ON T2.Country_ID = T1.Country_ID INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM country WHERE Population > 8000000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of countries that have a population greater than 8 million and also contain at least one roller coaster.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 WHERE T1.Country_ID = (SELECT Country_ID FROM roller_coaster WHERE Name = 'Boardwalk Bullet') AND T1.Name != 'Boardwalk Bullet';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of other roller coasters located in the same country as 'Boardwalk Bullet'.

sql: SELECT Park FROM roller_coaster WHERE Height = (SELECT MAX(Height) FROM roller_coaster);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the park that houses the tallest roller coaster.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Name ORDER BY AVG(T1.Speed) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the country with the highest average roller coaster speed across all of its roller coasters.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.Population) FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T1.Speed = '70';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average population for countries that have a roller coaster with a speed of '70'.

sql: SELECT T2.Languages, COUNT(T1.Roller_Coaster_ID) FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Languages HAVING COUNT(T1.Roller_Coaster_ID) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the languages spoken in countries that have more than two roller coasters, along with the total count of roller coasters for each language.

sql: SELECT Name FROM roller_coaster WHERE Length > (SELECT AVG(Length) FROM roller_coaster) AND Height < (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM roller_coaster);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a roller coaster that is longer than the average length but shorter than the average height.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN roller_coaster AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T1.Roller_Coaster_ID < T2.Roller_Coaster_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, with each row containing a unique pair of roller coaster names that are located in the same country.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Dauling Dragon' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM roller_coaster AS T3 WHERE T3.Country_ID = T1.Country_ID AND T3.Speed = '70');
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the country where the 'Dauling Dragon' roller coaster is located, but only if that country also contains at least one roller coaster with a speed of 70.

result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all roller coasters located in either Sweden or Austria.
