sql: SELECT * FROM film;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing a complete catalog of all films, showing the ID, title, studio, director, and gross revenue for each.

sql: SELECT Title, Director FROM film;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing every film's title and its corresponding director from the database.

sql: SELECT * FROM film WHERE Director = 'Steven Spielberg';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all film details for movies directed by 'Steven Spielberg'.

sql: SELECT Country, Number_cities FROM market;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each country from the market data along with its corresponding number of cities.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM film;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value that represents the total count of all films.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Studio FROM film;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each unique film studio present in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Title, Gross_in_dollar FROM film ORDER BY Gross_in_dollar DESC;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each film's title and its gross earnings in dollars, ordered from highest to lowest earnings.

sql: SELECT Title, Gross_in_dollar FROM film WHERE Gross_in_dollar > 150000000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the title and gross revenue for films that earned more than $150 million.

sql: SELECT * FROM market WHERE Country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing all market details, including the Market ID and number of cities, for the market located in the USA.

sql: SELECT * FROM film_market_estimation WHERE Year = 1945;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for the film market estimation from the year 1945.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, count(T2.Estimation_ID) FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Title;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each film title along with the total number of market estimations associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.Country, count(T2.Estimation_ID) FROM market AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.Country;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each country and the total number of film market estimations made for that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T1.Studio, T2.Country FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T3 ON T1.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID JOIN market AS T2 ON T3.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each film's title, its studio, and the country of its associated market.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Title HAVING count(T2.Estimation_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the titles of all films that have at least one associated market estimation.

sql: SELECT Director, avg(Gross_in_dollar) FROM film GROUP BY Director;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each film director along with the average gross revenue in dollars for their films.

sql: SELECT Title FROM film WHERE Film_ID IN (SELECT Film_ID FROM film_market_estimation WHERE High_Estimate > 8000.0);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of films that have a high market estimate greater than 8000.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Low_Estimate = 80000.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of a film that has a low market estimation of exactly 80000.

sql: SELECT T2.Type, T2.Year FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Director = 'Sydney Pollack';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the market estimation type and year for films directed by 'Sydney Pollack'.

sql: SELECT Estimation_ID, High_Estimate - Low_Estimate FROM film_market_estimation;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each film market estimation ID along with the calculated range between its high and low estimates.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T2.Country FROM film AS T1 LEFT JOIN film_market_estimation AS T3 ON T1.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID LEFT JOIN market AS T2 ON T3.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows, listing each film title paired with the countries where it has a market estimation.

sql: SELECT Year, sum(High_Estimate) FROM film_market_estimation GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows showing the total high-end market value estimate for films, aggregated for each year.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID WHERE T3.Country = 'Japan';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the titles of films that have a market estimation associated with the country of Japan.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T2.High_Estimate - T2.Low_Estimate FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each film's title and the calculated difference between its high and low market value estimates.

sql: SELECT Type, avg(High_Estimate) FROM film_market_estimation GROUP BY Type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each film market estimation type along with the average high estimate for that type.

sql: SELECT sum(T1.Number_cities) FROM market AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the total number of cities across all markets that have an associated film market estimation.

sql: SELECT T1.Director, sum(T1.Gross_in_dollar) FROM film AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Director HAVING sum(T1.Gross_in_dollar) > 200000000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a director whose films have a combined gross revenue of over $200 million, showing the director's name and that total gross amount.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID WHERE T3.Country = 'USA' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID WHERE T3.Country = 'China';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of a film that is marketed in the USA but not in China.

sql: SELECT T1.Country FROM market AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.Country ORDER BY count(T2.Estimation_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the country with the highest number of film market estimations.

sql: SELECT T1.Director FROM film AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Director HAVING avg(T1.Gross_in_dollar) > (SELECT avg(Gross_in_dollar) FROM film);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing directors whose average film gross is greater than the overall average gross across all films.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Market_ID IN (SELECT Market_ID FROM market WHERE Number_cities > 600);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of films that have market estimations for very large markets, specifically those covering more than 600 cities.

sql: SELECT T1.Studio, sum(T1.Gross_in_dollar), count(T3.Estimation_ID) FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T3 ON T1.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Studio;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each film studio, the total gross revenue from their films, and the total count of market estimations for those films.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID WHERE T3.Country = 'Japan' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID WHERE T3.Country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of a film that is available in both the Japanese and US markets.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T1.Gross_in_dollar FROM film AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM film_market_estimation AS T2 WHERE T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID AND T1.Gross_in_dollar > T2.High_Estimate);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the titles and gross revenue for films that outperformed their highest market estimation.

sql: SELECT T1.Country, sum(T2.High_Estimate) AS TotalHighEstimate FROM market AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.Country ORDER BY TotalHighEstimate DESC LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the top two countries with the highest total film market high estimate, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Type = 'Mass suicide murder' AND T1.Gross_in_dollar > 150000000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of a film that grossed over $150 million and is classified under the market estimation type 'Mass suicide murder'.

sql: SELECT T1.Studio FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.Studio ORDER BY count(DISTINCT T3.Country) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single film studio that has marketed films in the most unique countries.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, count(T2.Market_ID) AS MarketCount FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Title HAVING MarketCount > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each film title that is estimated for more than one market, along with the total count of those markets.

sql: SELECT T1.Country FROM market AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.Country HAVING avg(T2.Low_Estimate) < (SELECT avg(Low_Estimate) FROM film_market_estimation);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the countries where the average low-end film market estimation falls below the overall average estimation across all markets.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID WHERE T3.Country = 'USA' UNION SELECT Title FROM film WHERE Gross_in_dollar > 400000000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of films that were either marketed in the USA or had a gross revenue exceeding $400 million.

sql: SELECT Country FROM market WHERE Market_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Market_ID FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Director = 'Steven Spielberg');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the countries that are not considered markets for any film directed by Steven Spielberg.

sql: SELECT T1.Director FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Director ORDER BY sum(T2.High_Estimate) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single director whose films have the highest combined high-end market estimate, based on summing the estimates for all their films.

sql: SELECT Year FROM film_market_estimation GROUP BY Year ORDER BY count(DISTINCT Film_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the year that had the highest number of distinct films.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Film_ID, sum(High_Estimate) AS TotalHigh FROM film_market_estimation GROUP BY Film_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Gross_in_dollar > T2.TotalHigh;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the titles of films whose actual gross revenue exceeded the sum of their high-end market estimations.

sql: SELECT T1.Studio FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Studio IN (SELECT Studio FROM film GROUP BY Studio HAVING count(*) = 1);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of film studios that have produced exactly one film.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T1.Gross_in_dollar, T2.avg_gross, (T1.Gross_in_dollar - T2.avg_gross) AS diff FROM film AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Studio, avg(Gross_in_dollar) AS avg_gross FROM film GROUP BY Studio) AS T2 ON T1.Studio = T2.Studio;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each film's title and gross revenue, alongside its studio's average gross revenue and the calculated difference between them.

sql: SELECT T1.Country, T3.Title, T2.High_Estimate FROM market AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID JOIN film AS T3 ON T2.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID JOIN (SELECT Market_ID, max(High_Estimate) AS max_est FROM film_market_estimation GROUP BY Market_ID) AS T4 ON T2.Market_ID = T4.Market_ID AND T2.High_Estimate = T4.max_est;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, identifying the film with the highest market estimate for each country, along with the country name and that film's high estimate value.

sql: SELECT T1.Director, avg(T2.High_Estimate - T2.Low_Estimate) as avg_diff FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Director;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each film director along with the average difference between the high and low market estimations for their films.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T1.Gross_in_dollar FROM film AS T1 WHERE T1.Gross_in_dollar > (SELECT avg(T2.Gross_in_dollar) FROM film AS T2 WHERE T1.Studio = T2.Studio);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the title and gross earnings for films that performed better than the average gross of their respective studio.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_market_estimation AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Type = 'Mass suicide' AND T2.Year < 1945;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of a film that had a 'Mass suicide' market estimation type from before the year 1945.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE good_or_bad_customer = 'good';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows containing all available details for customers who are classified as 'good'.

sql: SELECT product_name, daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE product_type_code = 'Cutlery';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and daily hire cost for all products classified as 'Cutlery'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT payment_type_code FROM Payments;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each unique payment type code available.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Bookings WHERE returned_damaged_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns a single row with the total count of all bookings that were returned damaged.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, date_became_customer FROM Customers ORDER BY date_became_customer DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each customer's first name, last name, and the date they became a customer, ordered from the most recently acquired customer to the oldest.

sql: SELECT * FROM Payments WHERE payment_type_code = 'Cash';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the complete details for every payment transaction made with cash.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.booking_status_code FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id LIMIT 10;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the first name, last name, and booking status for customers associated with a booking.

sql: SELECT * FROM Discount_Coupons WHERE coupon_amount = 500;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the ID, issue date, and amount for any discount coupon valued at exactly 500.

sql: SELECT SUM(amount_payable) FROM Bookings;
result_summary: Returns a single row calculating the total amount payable from all bookings.

sql: SELECT * FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Provisional';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows containing the complete details for all bookings with a 'Provisional' status.

sql: SELECT T1.product_type_code, AVG(T1.daily_hire_cost) FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 GROUP BY T1.product_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each product type code along with the calculated average daily hire cost for that product category.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.booking_id) AS booking_count FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each customer by their first and last name, along with a count of the total number of bookings they have made.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, SUM(T2.booked_count) AS total_booked FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each product by name, along with a calculation of the total number of times that product has been booked.

sql: SELECT booking_id FROM Products_Booked GROUP BY booking_id HAVING COUNT(product_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the booking IDs for all bookings that contain more than one product.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.product_name FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.returned_late_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of products that have been returned late at least once.

sql: SELECT T1.booking_id, T1.amount_payable FROM Bookings AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id WHERE T2.amount_paid_in_full_yn = '0';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the booking ID and total amount payable for all bookings that have not yet been paid in full.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Confirmed');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have at least one confirmed booking.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.coupon_amount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Discount_Coupons AS T3 ON T1.coupon_id = T3.coupon_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each customer's first name, last name, and the value of their assigned discount coupon.

sql: SELECT T1.booking_id, T1.customer_id, T1.amount_outstanding FROM Bookings AS T1 WHERE T1.amount_outstanding > 50;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing bookings with an outstanding balance greater than 50, showing the booking ID, customer ID, and the specific amount owed for each.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN View_Product_Availability AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.available_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all products that are currently available for hire.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.good_or_bad_customer = 'good' INTERSECT SELECT T3.first_name FROM Bookings AS T2 JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T2.booking_status_code = 'Confirmed';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first names of customers who are both classified as 'good' and have at least one 'Confirmed' booking.

sql: SELECT payment_type_code, SUM(amount_paid) FROM Payments GROUP BY payment_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each payment type along with the total amount paid for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.product_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked AS T3b ON T2.booking_id = T3b.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T3 ON T3b.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T1.first_name = 'Rosalee';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the products booked by the customer 'Rosalee Kessler', showing her full name and the name of each product.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.booking_id FROM Bookings AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id WHERE T2.payment_type_code = 'Credit Card';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique booking IDs for all bookings that were paid for, at least in part, by credit card.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.good_or_bad_customer = 'good' UNION SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.gender_mf = '0';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the unique first and last names of customers who are either designated as 'good' or have a gender code of '0'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T2.amount_payable) AS total_spent FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.booking_status_code = 'Confirmed' GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(T2.booking_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first name, last name, and total amount spent for customers who have made more than one confirmed booking.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, SUM(T2.booked_amount) AS total_revenue FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top three products by their total booked revenue, ordered from highest to lowest revenue.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T3.customer_id FROM Bookings AS T3 JOIN Products_Booked AS T4 ON T3.booking_id = T4.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T5 ON T4.product_id = T5.product_id WHERE T5.product_name = 'Book collection A') AND T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T2.customer_id FROM Payments AS T2 WHERE T2.payment_type_code = 'Cash');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have both booked 'Book collection A' and paid using cash.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT product_id FROM Products_Booked WHERE returned_late_yn = '1');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of products for hire that have never been returned late.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Bookings AS T2 WHERE T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Payments AS T3 WHERE T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of a customer who has made a booking but has not yet made a payment.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T3.avg_discount_ratio FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.customer_id, AVG(T2.amount_of_discount / T2.amount_payable) as avg_discount_ratio FROM Bookings AS T2 WHERE T2.amount_payable > 0 AND T2.amount_of_discount > 0 GROUP BY T2.customer_id) AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows showing the ID, first name, and average discount ratio for each customer who has received a discount on a paid booking.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.returned_damaged_yn = '1' EXCEPT SELECT T3.first_name, T3.last_name FROM Customers AS T3 JOIN Payments AS T4 ON T3.customer_id = T4.customer_id WHERE T4.payment_type_code = 'Cash';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have returned a damaged item, excluding any of those customers who have ever paid with cash.

sql: SELECT T1.product_type_code, T1.product_name, T1.daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 WHERE T1.daily_hire_cost = (SELECT MAX(T2.daily_hire_cost) FROM Products_for_Hire AS T2 WHERE T2.product_type_code = T1.product_type_code);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the most expensive hireable product within each product type category, showing its type code, name, and daily hire cost.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount_due) as total_due, SUM(amount_paid) as total_paid FROM Payments GROUP BY customer_id) AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.total_paid < T2.total_due;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the customer ID and first name for all customers whose total amount paid is less than their total amount due.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.amount_outstanding > (SELECT AVG(amount_outstanding) FROM Bookings);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique first and last names of customers whose outstanding booking amount is greater than the average outstanding amount across all bookings.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.product_name FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN View_Product_Availability AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Bookings AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id WHERE T2.available_yn = '0' AND T3.booking_status_code = 'Confirmed';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, SUM(T2.amount_of_refund) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.good_or_bad_customer = 'good' GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING SUM(T2.amount_of_refund) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing 'good' customers who have received a refund, showing each customer's first name and their total refund amount.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.booking_id IN (SELECT booking_id FROM Products_Booked GROUP BY booking_id HAVING COUNT(product_id) > 2);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T3.product_name, T4.payment_type_code FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked AS T3b ON T2.booking_id = T3b.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T3 ON T3b.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Payments AS T4 ON T2.booking_id = T4.booking_id WHERE T4.payment_type_code = 'Check' AND T4.amount_paid_in_full_yn = '1';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Discount_Coupons AS T2 ON T1.coupon_id = T2.coupon_id WHERE T2.coupon_amount > (SELECT AVG(coupon_amount) FROM Discount_Coupons);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of customers whose assigned discount coupon has a value greater than the average coupon amount.

sql: SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN booking_status_code = 'Confirmed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) FROM Bookings;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single value representing the overall percentage of bookings that have a 'Confirmed' status.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.payment_type_code IN ('Cash', 'Credit Card') GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.payment_type_code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of the customer who has paid using both 'Cash' and 'Credit Card' as payment methods.

sql: SELECT T1.product_type_code, SUM(T3.count_hired) FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Bookings AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id GROUP BY T1.product_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each product type code along with the total quantity of items hired for that specific product type.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, AVG(julianday(T2.booking_end_date) - julianday(T2.booking_start_date)) AS avg_booking_days FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(T2.booking_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the customer ID and the average booking duration in days for each customer who has made more than one booking.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked AS T4 ON T3.booking_id = T4.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T2 ON T4.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.good_or_bad_customer = 'good' GROUP BY T2.product_name ORDER BY COUNT(T4.booking_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the single most popular product booked by customers who are classified as 'good', based on the total count of bookings.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM Customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Provisional');
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the first and last names of customers who do not have any bookings with a 'Provisional' status.

sql: SELECT T1.gender_mf FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.gender_mf ORDER BY AVG(T2.amount_payable) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the gender that has the highest average booking amount.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.customer_id, T4.customer_id FROM Products_Booked AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id JOIN Products_Booked AS T3 ON T1.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Bookings AS T4 ON T3.booking_id = T4.booking_id WHERE T2.customer_id < T4.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing unique pairs of customer IDs where both customers have booked at least one of the same products.

sql: SELECT T1.booking_id, T1.amount_payable, T1.amount_of_discount FROM Bookings AS T1 WHERE T1.booking_id IN (SELECT T2.booking_id FROM Payments AS T2 WHERE T2.payment_type_code = 'Credit Card' AND T2.amount_paid_in_full_yn = '0');
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the booking ID, total payable amount, and discount for any booking that has an outstanding balance and was paid for with a 'Credit Card'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id WHERE T3.product_id = (SELECT product_id FROM Products_for_Hire ORDER BY daily_hire_cost DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of all customers who have booked the single most expensive product available for daily hire.

sql: SELECT Title, Original_air_date FROM film WHERE Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the titles and original air dates of all films directed by 'Bill Schreiner'.

sql: SELECT Name, Capacity FROM cinema WHERE Location = 'County Dublin';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and capacity of the cinema located in 'County Dublin'.

sql: SELECT Name, Openning_year FROM cinema ORDER BY Openning_year ASC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing all cinemas and their opening years, ordered chronologically from the oldest to the newest.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM film;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of films.

sql: SELECT Title, Directed_by FROM film WHERE Production_code = '50021–50025';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the title and director of the film identified by the production code '50021–50025'.

sql: SELECT Cinema_ID, Film_ID, Date FROM schedule WHERE Price = 12.99;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the Cinema ID, Film ID, and date for all scheduled showings that have a ticket price of $12.99.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Directed_by FROM film;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique directors of the films in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T2.Cinema_ID FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each film's title and the corresponding cinema ID where it is scheduled to be shown.

sql: SELECT Capacity FROM cinema WHERE Name = 'Codling';
result_summary: Returns 1 row providing the seating capacity for the cinema named 'Codling'.

sql: SELECT Title, Rank_in_series FROM film ORDER BY Rank_in_series DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the title of each film and its rank within its series, ordered from highest to lowest rank.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Codling';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the titles of all films scheduled to be shown at the 'Codling' cinema.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Film_ID) FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each cinema by name along with the total count of films scheduled to be shown at that location.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.Price) FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average ticket price for all film screenings that occurred on May 21st.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Date) FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each cinema by name along with the total count of unique days it has a schedule.

sql: SELECT Name FROM cinema WHERE Cinema_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Cinema_ID FROM schedule);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all cinemas that currently have at least one event in their schedule.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T1.Directed_by FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Show_times_per_day > 2;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the title and director for films that are shown frequently (more than two times per day).

sql: SELECT SUM(Capacity) FROM cinema WHERE Location = 'County Wicklow';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total seating capacity for all cinemas located in County Wicklow.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T3.Name FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T1.Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the titles of films directed by 'Bill Schreiner' and the names of the cinemas where they are scheduled to be shown.

sql: SELECT MAX(Price) FROM schedule;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the maximum price found across the entire schedule.

sql: SELECT Directed_by, COUNT(*) FROM film GROUP BY Directed_by;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each director along with the total number of films they have directed.

sql: SELECT Name, Openning_year FROM cinema WHERE Openning_year > 2010;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and opening year for all cinemas that opened after 2010.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of films scheduled to be shown on '21 May'.

sql: SELECT Name, Capacity FROM cinema WHERE Capacity > 400;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and seating capacities for all cinemas that can hold more than 400 people.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T2.Date FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID ORDER BY T2.Date;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the title and scheduled showing date for each film, ordered by the showing date.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T1.Title = 'The Case of the Smart Dummy';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the cinema showing the film 'The Case of the Smart Dummy'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May' GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Film_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the cinema that showed more than one film on the date '21 May'.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Show_times_per_day = (SELECT MAX(Show_times_per_day) FROM schedule);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the titles of films that have the maximum number of show times per day.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Directed_by FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T3.Capacity > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique director of a film scheduled to be shown in a cinema with a capacity greater than 1000.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Name FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T3.Openning_year < 2012 AND T1.Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of cinemas that opened before 2012 and have screened a film directed by 'Bill Schreiner'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, AVG(T2.Price) FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Film_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and average ticket price for each cinema that is showing more than one film.

sql: SELECT Title FROM film WHERE Film_ID NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Film_ID FROM schedule);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY SUM(T2.Show_times_per_day) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the single cinema with the most showtimes per day.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Cinema_ID) FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Title;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each film title alongside the total count of unique cinemas showing that film.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T3.Location = 'County Wicklow' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T3.Location = 'County Cork';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of films showing in 'County Wicklow' that are not also showing in 'County Cork'.

sql: SELECT T1.Directed_by FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Directed_by ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Cinema_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the director whose films are screened in the highest number of distinct cinemas.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 Jun';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the cinema that has a schedule on both '21 May' and '21 Jun'.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T3.Capacity FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T1.Title = 'The Case of the Mystery Weekend' ORDER BY T3.Capacity DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and capacity of the largest cinema that is showing the film 'The Case of the Mystery Weekend'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Capacity * SUM(T2.Show_times_per_day) AS Potential_Viewers FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Cinema_ID, T1.Name, T1.Capacity;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each cinema's name and its total potential daily viewers, calculated by multiplying the cinema's capacity by its total number of daily showtimes.

sql: SELECT AVG(Price) FROM schedule WHERE Cinema_ID IN (SELECT Cinema_ID FROM cinema WHERE Openning_year > 2009);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average ticket price for schedules at all cinemas that opened after 2009.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Price = (SELECT MIN(Price) FROM schedule);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of the film that is available to watch for the lowest price across all scheduled showings.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING AVG(T2.Price) < 12;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of cinemas where the average ticket price is less than 12.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T3.Location = 'County Wicklow' UNION SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T3.Location = 'County Cork';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique titles of films scheduled to play in cinemas located in either 'County Wicklow' or 'County Cork'.

sql: SELECT T1.Directed_by, SUM(T2.Show_times_per_day) FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Directed_by;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each film director and the total number of daily showtimes scheduled for all of their films.

sql: SELECT Name FROM cinema WHERE Cinema_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Cinema_ID FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all cinemas that are not showing any films directed by 'Bill Schreiner'.

sql: SELECT Capacity FROM cinema WHERE Cinema_ID = (SELECT Cinema_ID FROM schedule ORDER BY Price DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the seating capacity of the cinema that hosts the single most expensive movie showing.

sql: SELECT T1.Directed_by FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Directed_by HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Date) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the director whose films have been scheduled for screenings on more than one distinct day.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Price > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM schedule);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique titles of films being sold at a price that is higher than the average price of all scheduled films.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, T1.Name, T1.Capacity FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Location, MAX(Capacity) AS max_cap FROM cinema GROUP BY Location) AS T2 ON T1.Location = T2.Location AND T1.Capacity = T2.max_cap;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows identifying the cinema with the largest capacity for each location, showing the cinema's name, location, and that maximum capacity.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM schedule AS T2 JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T2.Film_ID = T1.Film_ID AND T3.Openning_year = 2010);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the titles of films that were scheduled to play in any cinema that opened in the year 2010.

sql: SELECT Location FROM cinema GROUP BY Location ORDER BY AVG(Capacity) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single location with the highest average cinema capacity.

sql: SELECT * FROM track;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing all available race tracks, providing their ID, name, location, seating capacity, and the year they opened.

sql: SELECT * FROM race;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing all available races, showing each race's ID, name, class, date, and associated track ID.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating FROM track ORDER BY Seating DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each racetrack's name and seating capacity, ordered from largest to smallest.

sql: SELECT Name, Date FROM race ORDER BY Race_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and date of each race, ordered sequentially by their race ID.

sql: SELECT Name, Location FROM track WHERE Year_Opened = 1997.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and location of the race track that opened in the year 1997.

sql: SELECT Name, Date FROM race WHERE Class = 'DP/GT';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and date for each race belonging to the 'DP/GT' class.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating FROM track WHERE Seating > 70000;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and seating capacity for all high-capacity tracks that can hold over 70,000 people.

sql: SELECT Location FROM track WHERE Name = 'Chicagoland Speedway';
result_summary: Returns a single row with the location of the 'Chicagoland Speedway' track.

sql: SELECT Name, Year_Opened FROM track WHERE Location = 'Fontana, CA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and opening year for the racetrack located in Fontana, CA.

sql: SELECT Name FROM race WHERE Track_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all races that took place at the track with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name of each race and the corresponding name of the track where it was held.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Race_ID) FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each track by name, along with a count of the total number of races held at that track.

sql: SELECT SUM(T2.Seating) FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total cumulative seating capacity for all scheduled race events, calculated by summing the track seating for each race.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Year_Opened > 1990;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name of each track opened after 1990 and the name of the corresponding race held there.

sql: SELECT AVG(Seating) FROM track;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average seating capacity across all tracks in the database.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating FROM track ORDER BY Seating DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single track with the largest seating capacity, showing its name and number of seats.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Chicagoland Speedway';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of races that took place at the 'Chicagoland Speedway' track.

sql: SELECT T2.Year_Opened FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Rolex 24 At Daytona';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the opening year of the track that hosts the 'Rolex 24 At Daytona' race.

sql: SELECT Location, COUNT(*) FROM track GROUP BY Location;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each location along with the total count of tracks at that location.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of all tracks that have hosted a race.

sql: SELECT MIN(Year_Opened) FROM track;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the earliest opening year among all tracks in the dataset.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM race;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all races.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Location FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name of each race along with the location of the corresponding track where it is held.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T2.Seating FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Mexico City 250';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and seating capacity of the racetrack that hosted the 'Mexico City 250' race.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating FROM track WHERE Seating BETWEEN 60000 AND 80000;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and seating capacity for tracks with a seating capacity between 60,000 and 80,000.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Race_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the track that has hosted more than one race.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating FROM track WHERE Seating > (SELECT AVG(Seating) FROM track);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and seating capacity for all tracks that have an above-average seating capacity.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Seating > 70000;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of races held at tracks with a seating capacity greater than 70,000.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Race_ID) AS RaceCount FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Name ORDER BY RaceCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the track that has hosted the most races, along with the total count of those races.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Year_Opened = (SELECT MAX(Year_Opened) FROM track);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of races held at the tracks with the most recent opening year.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T1.Class = 'DP/GT';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of tracks that have never hosted a race of the 'DP/GT' class.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Seating) FROM track AS T1 WHERE T1.Track_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Track_ID FROM race);
result_summary: Returns a single row calculating the average seating capacity for all tracks that have hosted a race.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Track_ID IN (SELECT Track_ID FROM track WHERE Seating = 92000);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a race and the name of the track where it is held, for all tracks with a seating capacity of exactly 92,000.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T3.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN race AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID AND T1.Race_ID < T2.Race_ID JOIN track AS T3 ON T1.Track_ID = T3.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a pair of distinct races that were held on the same track, showing the names of both races and the name of their shared track.

sql: SELECT Location FROM track GROUP BY Location HAVING COUNT(Track_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the unique locations that contain one or more racing tracks.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 WHERE T1.Track_ID IN (SELECT Track_ID FROM track WHERE Year_Opened >= 2000);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of races held at tracks that were opened in the year 2000 or later.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Seating FROM track AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM race AS T2 WHERE T2.Track_ID = T1.Track_ID) ORDER BY T1.Seating ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and seating capacity for the track with the smallest seating capacity among all tracks that have hosted a race.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track UNION ALL SELECT Name FROM race;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows containing a combined list of all track names and race names, including any duplicates from both sources.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Seating > (SELECT Seating FROM track WHERE Name = 'Darlington Raceway');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of all tracks that have a larger seating capacity than Darlington Raceway.

sql: SELECT SUM(Seating) FROM track WHERE Location IN ('Joliet, IL', 'Fontana, CA');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the combined total seating capacity of all racetracks located in either Joliet, IL, or Fontana, CA.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Race_ID) FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Seating = (SELECT MIN(T3.Seating) FROM track AS T3 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM race AS T4 WHERE T4.Track_ID = T3.Track_ID)) GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of tracks with the lowest seating capacity among all tracks that have hosted a race, along with the total count of races held at each of those tracks.

sql: SELECT Name, Year_Opened, Seating FROM track WHERE Year_Opened < 2000 UNION SELECT Name, Year_Opened, Seating FROM track WHERE Seating > 80000;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name, opening year, and seating capacity for race tracks that either opened before the year 2000 or have a seating capacity greater than 80,000.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM track AS T1 WHERE T1.Year_Opened <= 1997 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM race AS T2 WHERE T2.Track_ID = T1.Track_ID);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of tracks that opened on or before 1997 and have hosted at least one race.

sql: SELECT Name FROM race ORDER BY LENGTH(Name) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the single race with the longest name, as determined by character length.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T2.Seating, COUNT(T1.Race_ID) FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each race track along with its seating capacity and the total number of races held at that track.

sql: SELECT T2.Location FROM track AS T2 WHERE T2.Track_ID = (SELECT Track_ID FROM race WHERE Name = 'Mexico City 250');
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the location of the track where the 'Mexico City 250' race is held.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Name LIKE '%Speedway%' AND Seating > (SELECT AVG(Seating) FROM track);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of tracks that contain 'Speedway' in their name and have a seating capacity greater than the average of all tracks.

sql: SELECT Location FROM track WHERE Year_Opened >= 2001 UNION SELECT Location FROM track WHERE Seating < 80000;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique locations of tracks that either opened since 2001 or have a seating capacity of less than 80,000.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating FROM track ORDER BY Seating DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and seating capacity of the track with the second-largest seating capacity.

sql: WITH RaceCounts AS (SELECT T2.Location, COUNT(T1.Race_ID) as NumRaces FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Location) SELECT Location, NumRaces FROM RaceCounts WHERE NumRaces > 0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each track location that has hosted a race, along with a count of the total number of races held at that location.

sql: SELECT Name, Sex FROM people WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and sex of all people identified as female.

sql: SELECT * FROM candidate WHERE Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all available polling data for candidates surveyed in the 'WNBC/Marist Poll', including their support, consider, oppose, and unsure rates.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people ORDER BY Height DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and height of each person, ordered from tallest to shortest.

sql: SELECT People_ID, Support_rate FROM candidate ORDER BY Support_rate ASC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each candidate's ID and their corresponding support rate, ordered from the lowest to the highest rate of support.

sql: SELECT * FROM people WHERE Name = 'Hubert Henno';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the full profile, including ID, sex, date of birth, height, and weight, for the person named 'Hubert Henno'.

sql: SELECT * FROM candidate WHERE Support_rate = 0.25;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all polling details for any candidate with a support rate of exactly 25%.

sql: SELECT Name, Weight FROM people WHERE Weight > 80;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and weight of people who weigh more than 80.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source, Date FROM candidate WHERE Oppose_rate > 0.4;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the poll source and date for polls where a candidate's opposition rate was greater than 40%.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Poll_Source FROM candidate;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique poll sources found in the candidate data.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name of every person in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Poll_Source, T2.Support_rate FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each candidate's name, their support rate, and the poll source that measured that rate.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Poll_Source FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and associated poll source for all female candidates.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Support_rate FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Poll_Source = 'FOX News/Opinion Dynamics Poll';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a candidate and their support rate as measured by the 'FOX News/Opinion Dynamics Poll'.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source, AVG(Support_rate) FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each poll source along with the average candidate support rate calculated from that source.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Candidate_ID) FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each person's name along with a count of their total candidacies.

sql: SELECT T1.Sex, MAX(T2.Support_rate) FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Sex;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the highest support rate achieved by a political candidate, broken down by sex.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people WHERE Height > (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names and heights of people who are taller than the average height of the entire group.

sql: SELECT People_ID, Support_rate FROM candidate WHERE Support_rate < (SELECT AVG(Support_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and support rate for candidates with a support rate below the average across all candidates.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate WHERE Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of people who are candidates associated with the 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT Sex, AVG(Weight) FROM people GROUP BY Sex;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each sex ('F' and 'M') along with the calculated average weight for individuals of that sex.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source, COUNT(*) FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each unique poll source along with the total count of candidates associated with that source.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate = 0.17;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name, height, and weight of the candidate who has a support rate of exactly 17%.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM candidate AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all people who are also registered as candidates.

sql: SELECT Date, MIN(Oppose_rate), MAX(Oppose_rate) FROM candidate GROUP BY Date;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, showing the range of candidate opposition rates (minimum and maximum) for each distinct polling date.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, (T2.Support_rate + T2.Consider_rate) AS Favorable_Rate FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY Favorable_Rate DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each candidate's name and a calculated 'Favorable Rate' (the sum of their support and consider rates), ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.People_ID, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Candidate_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of people who are registered as candidates, filtered to include only those with at least one candidacy record.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate = (SELECT MAX(Support_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the candidate with the highest support rate.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT People_ID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the poll sources that have surveyed at least two different people.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll' AND T2.Support_rate > (SELECT AVG(Support_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a candidate from the 'WNBC/Marist Poll' whose support rate is higher than the average support rate across all candidates.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people WHERE Sex = 'M' AND Height > (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM people WHERE Sex = 'M');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and height of all males who are taller than the average male height.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source ORDER BY AVG(Support_rate + Consider_rate) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single poll source associated with the highest average combined support and consideration rate across all candidates.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate WHERE Date = 'Feb 12–15, 2007') INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate WHERE Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of candidates who were both active during the 'Feb 12–15, 2007' period and associated with the 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate < 0.2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a candidate from the 'WNBC/Marist Poll' who has a support rate of 20% or greater.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Poll_Source FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.People_ID = 1 UNION SELECT T1.Name, T2.Poll_Source FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.People_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and poll source for two specific candidates identified by their People IDs (1 and 3).

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Support_rate, (SELECT AVG(T3.Support_rate) FROM candidate AS T3) AS Overall_Avg_Support FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each candidate's name and individual support rate, alongside the overall average support rate for all candidates.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Oppose_rate > T2.Support_rate;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of candidates whose opposition rate is greater than their support rate.

sql: SELECT T1.Sex, AVG(T2.Support_rate), AVG(T2.Oppose_rate) FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Sex;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the average support and opposition rates for candidates, broken down by gender.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID NOT IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of people who are not registered as candidates.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Date FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Unsure_rate = (SELECT MAX(Unsure_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the candidate and the date associated with the highest recorded 'unsure rate'.

sql: SELECT T3.Poll_Source, T4.Name FROM (SELECT Poll_Source, MAX(Support_rate) AS max_rate FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source) AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T3 ON T1.Poll_Source = T3.Poll_Source AND T1.max_rate = T3.Support_rate JOIN people AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each polling source alongside the name of the candidate who has the highest support rate for that specific poll.

sql: SELECT Name, Weight FROM people WHERE Weight < (SELECT AVG(Weight) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and weights for all people who weigh less than the overall average weight.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate > (SELECT T4.Support_rate FROM people AS T3 JOIN candidate AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.People_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Stéphane Antiga');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of candidates whose support rate is higher than that of the candidate named 'Stéphane Antiga'.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source ORDER BY (MAX(Support_rate) - MIN(Support_rate)) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single poll source with the greatest variation in candidate support rates, measured by the difference between the highest and lowest rates recorded for that source.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Height > 190 AND T2.Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a candidate who is taller than 190 and was included in the 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate > (SELECT AVG(Support_rate) FROM candidate) AND T2.Oppose_rate < (SELECT AVG(Oppose_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the candidate who has an above-average support rate and a below-average opposition rate when compared to all candidates.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate) AND T1.Weight = (SELECT MIN(T3.Weight) FROM people AS T3 JOIN candidate AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.People_ID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the candidate with the minimum weight among all candidates.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T4.Name, T1.Poll_Source FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.Poll_Source = T2.Poll_Source AND T1.People_ID < T2.People_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T1.People_ID = T3.People_ID JOIN people AS T4 ON T2.People_ID = T4.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing pairs of candidates who are cited in the same poll source, showing both candidate names and their shared poll.

sql: SELECT T2.Poll_Source, AVG(T1.Height), AVG(T1.Weight) FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Poll_Source;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each poll source along with the average height and weight of the candidates associated with that source.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM candidate AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Height > (SELECT AVG(T3.Height) FROM people AS T3 JOIN candidate AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.People_ID);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of candidates whose height is greater than the average height of all candidates.

sql: SELECT Name, State, Party FROM representative WHERE State = 'Indiana';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name, state, and party affiliation for representatives from the state of Indiana.

sql: SELECT Name, State, Party FROM representative WHERE Party = 'Republican';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing Republican representatives, including their name, state, and party affiliation.

sql: SELECT Name, State FROM representative WHERE Representative_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and state for the specific representative identified by ID 1.

sql: SELECT Date, Votes FROM election ORDER BY Votes DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing election dates and their corresponding vote counts, ordered from the highest number of votes to the lowest.

sql: SELECT * FROM election WHERE Election_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all recorded details for the specific election with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM representative;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total number of representatives.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM election;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all elections.

sql: SELECT Lifespan, State FROM representative WHERE Name = 'Dan Quayle';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the lifespan and represented state for the representative named 'Dan Quayle'.

sql: SELECT Representative_ID, Date FROM election WHERE Votes = 9423.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the representative and the date of the election that received exactly 9423 votes.

sql: SELECT Name, Lifespan FROM representative ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and lifespan of each representative, sorted alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Date FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each representative's name along with the date of an election they participated in.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Election_ID) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each representative's name along with the total number of elections they have participated in.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Votes) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each representative's name along with the total number of votes they received.

sql: SELECT T1.Party, AVG(T2.Votes) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Party;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each political party along with the average number of votes their representatives received in elections.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T2.Date = 'July 1942';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the representative who participated in an election held in 'July 1942'.

sql: SELECT T1.Party, SUM(T2.Seats) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Party;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each political party along with the total number of seats held by that party's representatives.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T2.Seats > 6.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of representatives who have won more than 6 seats in an election.

sql: SELECT T1.State, AVG(T2.Vote_Percent) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.State;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each state along with the average election vote percentage received by the representatives from that state.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T2.Place = 1.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique name of a representative who has won an election by placing first.

sql: SELECT SUM(T2.Votes) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T1.Party = 'Republican';
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total sum of votes for all representatives affiliated with the 'Republican' party.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Party, T1.State FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T2.Votes > 10000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name, party, and state for representatives who received more than 10,000 votes in their election.

sql: SELECT T1.State, MAX(T2.Seats) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.State;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each state along with the maximum number of seats held by any single representative from that state.

sql: SELECT State, COUNT(*) FROM representative GROUP BY State;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each state alongside the total count of representatives from that state.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING MAX(T2.Seats) <= 10;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows containing the names of representatives who have never won more than 10 seats in any single election.

sql: SELECT T2.Date, T2.Votes FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T1.State = 'Indiana';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Seats) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Representative_ID HAVING AVG(T2.Place) < 3.0;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T2.Votes > (SELECT SUM(Votes) FROM election WHERE Representative_ID = 1);

sql: SELECT T1.Party, COUNT(T1.Representative_ID) FROM representative AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM election AS T2 WHERE T2.Representative_ID = T1.Representative_ID AND T2.Seats = 10) GROUP BY T1.Party;

sql: SELECT Name FROM representative WHERE Party = 'Republican' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T2.Date = '1946';

sql: SELECT T1.Party, MAX(T2.Votes) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Party;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each political party alongside the highest number of votes received by any single representative from that party.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T2.Vote_Percent > (SELECT AVG(Vote_Percent) FROM election);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of representatives who won their elections with a vote percentage greater than the average across all elections.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE SUBSTR(T1.Lifespan, 1, 4) < '1923';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of representatives who participated in an election and were born before 1923.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 WHERE T1.Party IN (SELECT T2.Party FROM representative AS T2 JOIN election AS T3 ON T2.Representative_ID = T3.Representative_ID GROUP BY T2.Party HAVING SUM(T3.Votes) > 20000);

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Representative_ID HAVING AVG(T2.Place) < (SELECT AVG(T3.Place) FROM election AS T3 JOIN representative AS T4 ON T3.Representative_ID = T4.Representative_ID WHERE T4.Name = 'Dan Quayle');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of representatives whose average election place is better than the average election place achieved by 'Dan Quayle'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T1.Party = 'Republican' AND T2.Votes > (SELECT AVG(T4.Votes) FROM representative AS T3 JOIN election AS T4 ON T3.Representative_ID = T4.Representative_ID WHERE T3.Party = 'Democratic');
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the Republican representative who received more election votes than the average number of votes received by all Democratic representatives.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T1.Party = 'Republican' AND T1.State != 'Indiana' AND T2.Seats >= (SELECT AVG(T4.Seats) FROM representative AS T3 JOIN election AS T4 ON T3.Representative_ID = T4.Representative_ID WHERE T3.Party = 'Democratic');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a Republican representative, not from Indiana, who won a number of seats greater than or equal to the average seats won by Democratic representatives.

sql: SELECT * FROM election WHERE Votes > (SELECT AVG(Votes) FROM election WHERE Date = 'October 1942');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing all details for elections where the total votes received were greater than the average vote count from the October 1942 election.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 WHERE T1.Party = (SELECT Party FROM representative WHERE Name = 'Al Quie') AND T1.Name != 'Al Quie';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of representatives who belong to the same political party as 'Al Quie'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T2.Vote_Percent = (SELECT MAX(Vote_Percent) FROM election);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the names of the representatives who achieved the highest vote percentage ever recorded in an election.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN representative AS T2 ON T1.Party = T2.Party AND T1.Representative_ID < T2.Representative_ID;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing all unique pairs of representatives who belong to the same political party.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Representative_ID HAVING MAX(T2.Place) <= 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of representatives who have never finished worse than second place in any of their recorded elections.

sql: SELECT Name, State FROM representative WHERE Representative_ID IN (SELECT Representative_ID FROM election);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names and states of representatives who have participated in an election.

sql: SELECT Template_Type_Code, Template_Type_Description FROM Ref_Template_Types;

sql: SELECT Document_ID, Document_Name FROM Documents WHERE Document_Name = 'Summer Show';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the document ID and name for the specific document titled 'Summer Show'.

sql: SELECT Paragraph_ID, Paragraph_Text FROM Paragraphs ORDER BY Paragraph_Text ASC;

sql: SELECT Paragraph_Text FROM Paragraphs WHERE Document_ID = 3;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Template_Type_Code FROM Templates;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each distinct template type code available in the system.

sql: SELECT Template_ID, Template_Type_Code, Date_Effective_From FROM Templates WHERE Version_Number = 5;

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Paragraphs WHERE Paragraph_Text = 'Korea';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of paragraphs where the text is exactly 'Korea'.

sql: SELECT Document_ID, Document_Name FROM Documents ORDER BY Document_Name DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the ID and name for each document, ordered by document name in descending alphabetical (Z-A) order.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Paragraphs AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID WHERE T2.Paragraph_Text = 'Somalia';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any document that contains a paragraph with the exact text 'Somalia'.

sql: SELECT Template_Type_Code, COUNT(*) FROM Templates GROUP BY Template_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows showing the total count of templates for each distinct template type code.

sql: SELECT Template_Type_Code, AVG(Version_Number) FROM Templates GROUP BY Template_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each template type code along with the average version number for all templates of that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, count(T2.Paragraph_ID) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Paragraphs AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID GROUP BY T1.Document_Name;
