sql: SELECT T1.Template_ID, T1.Version_Number, T2.Template_Type_Description FROM Templates AS T1 JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T2 ON T1.Template_Type_Code = T2.Template_Type_Code;

sql: SELECT Document_ID, Document_Name FROM Documents WHERE Document_ID IN (SELECT Document_ID FROM Paragraphs);

sql: SELECT T2.Template_Type_Description FROM Templates AS T1 JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T2 ON T1.Template_Type_Code = T2.Template_Type_Code GROUP BY T2.Template_Type_Description HAVING count(T1.Template_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the descriptions of template types that have more than one associated template.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T3 ON T2.Template_Type_Code = T3.Template_Type_Code WHERE T3.Template_Type_Description = 'Presentation';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all documents that were created using a 'Presentation' template type.

sql: SELECT Document_ID, Document_Name FROM Documents WHERE Document_ID NOT IN (SELECT Document_ID FROM Paragraphs WHERE Document_ID IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the ID and name for all documents that do not contain any paragraph text.

sql: SELECT Template_Type_Code, MAX(Date_Effective_From) FROM Templates GROUP BY Template_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each template type code along with the most recent "effective from" date for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T1.Document_Description FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE T2.Version_Number = 9;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and description of all documents that were created using version 9 of a template.

sql: SELECT T3.Template_Type_Code, COUNT(T1.Paragraph_ID) FROM Paragraphs AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Templates AS T3 ON T2.Template_ID = T3.Template_ID GROUP BY T3.Template_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each template type code along with the total count of paragraphs associated with that template type.

sql: SELECT Template_Type_Code, Template_Type_Description FROM Ref_Template_Types WHERE Template_Type_Code IN (SELECT DISTINCT Template_Type_Code FROM Templates);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the code and description for each template type that is currently in use.

sql: SELECT Template_ID, Template_Type_Code, Date_Effective_From FROM Templates WHERE Date_Effective_From > '2005-01-01' UNION SELECT Template_ID, Template_Type_Code, Date_Effective_From FROM Templates WHERE Template_Type_Code = 'CV';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the ID, type code, and effective date for all templates that either became effective after January 1, 2005, or are of the 'CV' type.

sql: SELECT T2.Paragraph_Text FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Paragraphs AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID WHERE T1.Document_Name = 'Summer Show';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the paragraph text from the document titled 'Summer Show'.

sql: SELECT Document_ID, Document_Name FROM Documents WHERE Document_Description = 'u';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID and name for the document whose description is exactly 'u'.

sql: SELECT Template_Type_Code FROM Templates GROUP BY Template_Type_Code HAVING avg(Version_Number) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the template type codes where the average version number across all templates of that type is greater than 5.

sql: SELECT Template_ID, Version_Number FROM Templates WHERE Template_ID NOT IN (SELECT Template_ID FROM Documents WHERE Template_ID IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the ID and version number for templates that are not currently associated with any documents.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE T2.Version_Number > (SELECT AVG(Version_Number) FROM Templates);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of documents that use a template whose version number is greater than the average version number across all templates.

sql: SELECT T3.Template_Type_Description FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T3 ON T2.Template_Type_Code = T3.Template_Type_Code GROUP BY T3.Template_Type_Description ORDER BY COUNT(T1.Document_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the description of the most common template type, determined by the count of documents associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.Template_ID, T1.Template_Type_Code, T1.Version_Number FROM Templates AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Template_Type_Code, MAX(Version_Number) AS max_v FROM Templates GROUP BY Template_Type_Code) AS T2 ON T1.Template_Type_Code = T2.Template_Type_Code AND T1.Version_Number = T2.max_v;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying the most recent version of each template type, listing its template ID, type code, and highest version number.

sql: SELECT T1.Template_Type_Code, T1.Template_Type_Description FROM Ref_Template_Types AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Templates AS T2 WHERE T2.Template_Type_Code = T1.Template_Type_Code);

sql: SELECT T1.Document_ID, T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE T2.Template_Type_Code = 'PP' INTERSECT SELECT Document_ID, Document_Name FROM Documents WHERE Document_ID IN (SELECT Document_ID FROM Paragraphs);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the document ID and name for all documents that are of template type 'PP' and also contain paragraph content.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE strftime('%Y', T2.Date_Effective_From) = '2010';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the document whose associated template became effective in the year 2010.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_ID, T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID EXCEPT SELECT T1.Document_ID, T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE T2.Template_Type_Code = 'CV';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the ID and name for all documents, excluding any that are of the 'CV' template type.

sql: SELECT T2.Document_Name FROM Paragraphs AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID GROUP BY T2.Document_Name ORDER BY count(T1.Paragraph_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the document that has the highest number of paragraphs.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, count(T2.Paragraph_ID) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Paragraphs AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID GROUP BY T1.Document_Name HAVING count(T2.Paragraph_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of documents that contain more than one paragraph, along with the total paragraph count for each.

sql: SELECT Template_ID, Version_Number, Date_Effective_From, Date_Effective_To FROM Templates WHERE julianday(Date_Effective_To) - julianday(Date_Effective_From) > 730;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows identifying templates that were effective for more than two years, showing each template's ID, version number, and its effective start and end dates.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T2.Document_Name, T1.Template_ID FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE T1.Document_ID < T2.Document_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing pairs of different documents that share the same template, showing the names of both documents and their common template ID.

sql: SELECT Document_ID, Document_Name FROM Documents WHERE Template_ID = (SELECT Template_ID FROM Documents WHERE Document_Name = 'Understanding DB');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and name of all documents that share the same template as the document named 'Understanding DB'.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T1.Document_Description FROM Documents AS T1 WHERE T1.Document_Description = 'y' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Paragraphs AS T2 WHERE T2.Document_ID = T1.Document_ID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and description flag for documents that have a description available (flagged as 'y') and contain at least one paragraph.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T3.Version_Number, COUNT(T2.Paragraph_ID) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T3 ON T1.Template_ID = T3.Template_ID LEFT JOIN Paragraphs AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID GROUP BY T1.Document_ID, T1.Document_Name, T3.Version_Number;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, with each row showing a document's name, its associated template version number, and the total count of paragraphs contained within that document.

sql: SELECT T2.Document_Name, T1.Paragraph_Text FROM Paragraphs AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Templates AS T3 ON T2.Template_ID = T3.Template_ID WHERE T3.Template_Type_Code = 'BK';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the paragraph text and its corresponding document name for all documents that use the 'BK' template type.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T2.Date_Effective_From FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE strftime('%Y', T2.Date_Effective_From) = (SELECT strftime('%Y', Date_Effective_From) FROM Templates WHERE Template_ID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the document name and its template's effective date for any document whose template became effective in the same year as the template with ID 1.

sql: SELECT T2.Template_Type_Description, T3.Paragraph_Text FROM Templates AS T1 JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T2 ON T1.Template_Type_Code = T2.Template_Type_Code JOIN Documents AS T4 ON T1.Template_ID = T4.Template_ID JOIN Paragraphs AS T3 ON T4.Document_ID = T3.Document_ID WHERE T4.Document_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row for the specific document with ID 3, showing its paragraph text and the description of the template type it is based on.

sql: SELECT T2.Document_Name FROM Paragraphs AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID WHERE T1.Paragraph_Text = 'Korea' UNION SELECT T2.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T2 JOIN Templates AS T1 ON T2.Template_ID = T1.Template_ID WHERE T1.Version_Number = 9;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of documents that either contain the text 'Korea' in a paragraph or were created using template version 9.

sql: SELECT T3.Template_Type_Description, count(T1.Document_ID) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T3 ON T2.Template_Type_Code = T3.Template_Type_Code GROUP BY T3.Template_Type_Description HAVING count(T1.Document_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each template type that has at least one associated document, along with the total count of documents for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_ID, T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE '2002' >= strftime('%Y', T2.Date_Effective_From) AND '2002' <= strftime('%Y', T2.Date_Effective_To);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID and name of documents whose associated templates were effective at any point during the year 2002.

sql: SELECT T3.Template_Type_Description, AVG(T4.ParagraphCount) AS AvgParagraphs FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T3 ON T2.Template_Type_Code = T3.Template_Type_Code JOIN (SELECT Document_ID, COUNT(*) AS ParagraphCount FROM Paragraphs GROUP BY Document_ID) AS T4 ON T1.Document_ID = T4.Document_ID GROUP BY T3.Template_Type_Description;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each template type and the average number of paragraphs for documents associated with that template.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE T2.Template_Type_Code = 'PP' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Paragraphs AS P WHERE P.Document_ID = T1.Document_ID);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of documents that are based on a 'PP' type template and contain at least one paragraph.

sql: SELECT T_Counts.Template_Type_Description, T_Counts.DocumentCount, T_Versions.MaxVersion FROM (SELECT T3.Template_Type_Code, T3.Template_Type_Description, COUNT(T1.Document_ID) AS DocumentCount FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T3 ON T2.Template_Type_Code = T3.Template_Type_Code GROUP BY T3.Template_Type_Code, T3.Template_Type_Description) AS T_Counts JOIN (SELECT Template_Type_Code, MAX(Version_Number) AS MaxVersion FROM Templates GROUP BY Template_Type_Code) AS T_Versions ON T_Counts.Template_Type_Code = T_Versions.Template_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows summarizing each template type, including the total count of documents using that type and the highest version number available for that type.

sql: SELECT flno, distance FROM flight WHERE distance > 2000;

sql: SELECT T2.name, count(*) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid GROUP BY T2.name;

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name = 'Boeing 737-800';

sql: SELECT T1.name, count(T2.aid) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid GROUP BY T1.name;

sql: SELECT origin, AVG(price) FROM flight GROUP BY origin;

sql: SELECT name FROM aircraft WHERE aid IN (SELECT aid FROM flight WHERE origin = 'Los Angeles');

sql: SELECT name, salary FROM employee WHERE salary = (SELECT MAX(salary) FROM employee);

sql: SELECT flno, distance FROM flight WHERE distance > (SELECT AVG(distance) FROM flight);

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid GROUP BY T1.name HAVING count(T2.aid) > 1;

sql: SELECT SUM(T1.salary) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name = 'Boeing 747-400';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the total salary for all employees certified to work on the 'Boeing 747-400' aircraft.

sql: SELECT origin, destination FROM flight WHERE price = (SELECT MAX(price) FROM flight);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the origin and destination of the most expensive flight in the dataset.

sql: SELECT name, distance FROM aircraft WHERE distance > 7000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and maximum travel distances for long-range aircraft capable of flying over 7000 units of distance.

sql: SELECT T1.flno, T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid WHERE T1.destination = 'Chicago';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the flight number and the name of the corresponding aircraft for any flight destined for 'Chicago'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid WHERE T2.aid = 2;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of all employees who are certified to work on the aircraft with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T2.name, AVG(T1.distance) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each aircraft model name along with the average flight distance for that aircraft type.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid WHERE T2.aid IN (SELECT T3.aid FROM flight AS T3 WHERE T3.origin = 'Los Angeles');
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the unique names of employees certified to work on aircraft that have originated from Los Angeles.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name = 'Boeing 747-400' ORDER BY T1.salary DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the highest-paid employee who is certified to work on a 'Boeing 747-400'.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid GROUP BY T2.name HAVING SUM(T1.distance) > 3000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of aircraft models whose total flight distance across all trips is greater than 3000.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.distance > 8000 INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid WHERE T2.aid IN (SELECT aid FROM flight);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of employees who are certified on long-range aircraft (distance > 8000) and also have experience on an aircraft that has been used in a flight.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.salary FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid WHERE T1.salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employee) GROUP BY T1.name, T1.salary;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names and salaries of certified employees who earn more than the company's average salary.

sql: SELECT T1.flno, T3.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid JOIN employee AS T3 ON T2.eid = T3.eid;
result_summary: Returns 55 rows listing each flight number alongside the name of an employee who is certified to operate the aircraft for that flight.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name = 'Airbus A340-300' EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name = 'Boeing 747-400';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of employees who are certified for the 'Airbus A340-300' but not for the 'Boeing 747-400'.

sql: SELECT T3.name, AVG(T1.salary) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid GROUP BY T3.name HAVING count(T1.eid) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each aircraft model alongside the average salary of all employees who are certified to operate it.

sql: SELECT T4.name, T1.name, T2.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T3a ON T1.eid = T3a.eid JOIN employee AS T2 JOIN certificate AS T3b ON T2.eid = T3b.eid JOIN aircraft AS T4 ON T3a.aid = T4.aid WHERE T3a.aid = T3b.aid AND T1.eid < T2.eid;
result_summary: Returns 147 rows identifying all unique pairs of employees who are certified to operate the same aircraft, listing the aircraft name and the names of the two employees in the pair.

sql: SELECT T3.name, SUM(T1.distance) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid JOIN employee AS T3 ON T2.eid = T3.eid GROUP BY T3.name;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing each employee by name and the total flight distance they have accumulated.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid WHERE T1.destination = 'Washington D.C.' INTERSECT SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid WHERE T1.destination = 'Chicago';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT name FROM employee WHERE eid NOT IN (SELECT eid FROM certificate);
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of all employees who have not earned a certificate.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.NumEmployees, T3.NumFlights FROM aircraft AS T1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT aid, count(eid) AS NumEmployees FROM certificate GROUP BY aid) AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid LEFT JOIN (SELECT aid, count(flno) AS NumFlights FROM flight GROUP BY aid) AS T3 ON T1.aid = T3.aid;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each aircraft's name, the total number of employees certified to operate it, and the total number of flights it has completed.

sql: SELECT T1.flno, T1.price FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid WHERE T2.distance > (SELECT MAX(distance) FROM flight);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the flight number and price for a flight that uses an aircraft capable of flying farther than the longest currently scheduled flight distance.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name LIKE 'Boeing%' AND T1.salary > 150000;
result_summary: Returns 25 rows listing the names of high-earning employees (salary > 150,000) who are certified to work on Boeing aircraft.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM aircraft AS T1 LEFT JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid WHERE T2.eid IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the aircraft that does not have an associated certificate.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid WHERE T2.aid = (SELECT aid FROM aircraft ORDER BY distance DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all employees who are certified to operate the aircraft model with the longest flight range.

sql: SELECT origin, destination, price FROM flight WHERE price < (SELECT AVG(price) FROM flight WHERE origin = 'Los Angeles');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the origin, destination, and price for all flights that are cheaper than the average-priced flight departing from 'Los Angeles'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name LIKE 'Boeing%' UNION SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name LIKE 'Airbus%';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the unique names of employees who are certified to work on either Boeing or Airbus aircraft.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY count(T2.aid) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the employee who has earned the most certificates.

sql: SELECT T1.flno, count(T3.eid) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid JOIN employee AS T3 ON T2.eid = T3.eid WHERE T1.origin = 'Los Angeles' GROUP BY T1.flno;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each flight number for flights originating from 'Los Angeles', along with a count of how many employees are certified to operate the aircraft assigned to that flight.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid WHERE T2.distance > (SELECT MAX(distance) FROM flight) GROUP BY T2.name HAVING count(T1.flno) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.eid, T1.name, T1.salary FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid WHERE T2.aid NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT aid FROM flight);
result_summary: Returns 24 rows listing the ID, name, and salary of employees who are certified for aircraft that have never been used in any recorded flights.

sql: SELECT SUM(T1.salary) FROM employee AS T1 WHERE T1.eid IN (SELECT T2.eid FROM certificate AS T2 JOIN flight AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.destination = 'Dallas');
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total salary of all employees who are certified to operate aircraft used on flights to Dallas.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.salary FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid WHERE T2.aid IN (SELECT aid FROM flight WHERE distance > 2000) ORDER BY T1.salary DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and salary of the top three highest-paid employees who are certified on aircraft used for long-distance flights (over 2000 distance units), ordered from highest to lowest salary.

sql: SELECT Name, Year, Price FROM wine WHERE Winery = 'Robert Biale';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name, vintage year, and price for all wines produced by the 'Robert Biale' winery.

sql: SELECT Grape FROM grapes WHERE Color = 'Red';
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the names of all grape varieties that are red.

sql: SELECT Appelation, Area FROM appellations WHERE County = 'Sonoma';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the wine appellations and their corresponding geographical area for all regions located within Sonoma County.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM wine;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of wine records.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Winery FROM wine;
result_summary: Returns 281 rows, providing a unique list of all winery names in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Name, Winery, Year FROM wine WHERE Score = 93;
result_summary: Returns 55 rows listing the name, winery, and year for all wines that received a score of exactly 93.

sql: SELECT Name, Winery, Price FROM wine WHERE Year = 2008 ORDER BY Price DESC;
result_summary: Returns 130 rows listing the name, winery, and price for all wines produced in 2008, ordered from most to least expensive.

sql: SELECT avg(Price) FROM wine;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average price calculated across all wines in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Appelation, County FROM appellations WHERE isAVA = 'Yes';
result_summary: Returns 43 rows listing each officially designated American Viticultural Area (AVA) and the county in which it is located.

sql: SELECT Name, Winery, Drink FROM wine WHERE Drink = 'now';
result_summary: Returns 466 rows identifying wines that are ready to be consumed now, providing the name of the wine and its winery.

sql: SELECT Winery, count(*) FROM wine GROUP BY Winery;
result_summary: Returns 281 rows listing each winery along with the total count of its wines in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Winery FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape WHERE T2.Color = 'Red';
result_summary: Returns 347 rows listing the name and winery for all wines that are made from red grapes.

sql: SELECT Year, avg(Score) FROM wine GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, listing each vintage year alongside the average wine score for that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Winery, T1.Appelation FROM wine AS T1 JOIN appellations AS T2 ON T1.Appelation = T2.Appelation WHERE T2.Area = 'North Coast';
result_summary: Returns 353 rows listing wines from the 'North Coast' area, showing each wine's name, its winery, and specific appellation.

sql: SELECT Winery, sum(Cases) FROM wine GROUP BY Winery HAVING sum(Cases) > 500;
result_summary: Returns 202 rows listing each winery that has produced more than 500 cases in total, along with the corresponding total case count for each.

sql: SELECT Grape, max(Price) FROM wine GROUP BY Grape;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows, listing each grape variety along with the maximum price for a wine made from that grape.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM wine WHERE Price > (SELECT avg(Price) FROM wine);
result_summary: Returns 172 rows listing the name and price for all wines that are priced higher than the overall average wine price.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Appelation, T2.County FROM wine AS T1 JOIN appellations AS T2 ON T1.Appelation = T2.Appelation WHERE T1.Score = 93;
result_summary: Returns 21 rows listing the unique geographical regions (appellation and county) that produce wines with a score of exactly 93.

sql: SELECT Winery, count(DISTINCT Grape) FROM wine GROUP BY Winery;
result_summary: Returns 281 rows, listing each winery along with the total count of distinct grape varieties it uses.

sql: SELECT Name, Winery, Appelation FROM wine WHERE State = 'California' AND Appelation != 'Napa Valley';
result_summary: Returns 404 rows listing the name, winery, and appellation of wines from California, excluding any from the Napa Valley appellation.

sql: SELECT T2.Color, sum(T1.Cases) FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape GROUP BY T2.Color;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total number of wine cases produced, aggregated by the color of the grape.

sql: SELECT Name, Winery, Score FROM wine WHERE Grape = 'Zinfandel' AND Score > 90;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing top-rated Zinfandel wines (those with a score above 90), including the wine's name, the producing winery, and its specific score.

sql: SELECT min(Price) FROM wine WHERE Appelation IN (SELECT Appelation FROM appellations WHERE isAVA = 'Yes');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the single minimum price for any wine from an appellation designated as an American Viticultural Area (AVA).

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Winery FROM wine AS T1 JOIN appellations AS T2 ON T1.Appelation = T2.Appelation WHERE T2.County = 'Sonoma';
result_summary: Returns 107 rows listing the unique wineries located in Sonoma County.

sql: SELECT State, avg(Price) FROM wine GROUP BY State;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each state found in the dataset along with the average price of wines originating from that state.

sql: SELECT Winery, count(*) FROM wine WHERE Score >= 90 GROUP BY Winery ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top 3 wineries with the most highly-rated (score >= 90) wines, ordered from highest count to lowest.

sql: SELECT T2.Color, avg(T1.Price), avg(T1.Score) FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape GROUP BY T2.Color;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows that group wines by grape color (Red and White), showing the average price and average score for each color.

sql: SELECT Name, Winery, Price FROM wine WHERE Price > ALL (SELECT Price FROM wine WHERE Winery = 'Chiarello Family');
error_message: Discarded: SQL Runtime Error - near "ALL": syntax error

sql: SELECT T1.Winery FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape WHERE T2.Color = 'Red' AND T1.Winery IN (SELECT Winery FROM wine WHERE Grape = 'Zinfandel') GROUP BY T1.Winery HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.Grape) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of wineries that produce Zinfandel and also make at least one other distinct type of red wine.

sql: SELECT Appelation, avg(Score) AS AverageScore FROM wine GROUP BY Appelation HAVING count(*) >= 2 ORDER BY AverageScore DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single appelation with the highest average wine score, considering only those appelations with at least two reviewed wines.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Winery, T1.Appelation FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape JOIN appellations AS T3 ON T1.Appelation = T3.Appelation WHERE T2.Color = 'Red' AND T1.State = 'California' AND T3.isAVA = 'Yes';
result_summary: Returns 276 rows listing the name, winery, and appellation for red wines from California that are produced within a recognized American Viticultural Area (AVA).

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Grape, T1.Price FROM wine AS T1 WHERE T1.Price > (SELECT avg(T2.Price) FROM wine AS T2 WHERE T2.Grape = T1.Grape);
result_summary: Returns 213 rows listing the name, grape, and price of each wine that costs more than the average price for its specific grape variety.

sql: SELECT Winery FROM wine GROUP BY Winery HAVING min(Score) >= 90;
result_summary: Returns 129 rows listing wineries where every one of their listed wines has a score of 90 or greater.

sql: SELECT Name, Grape, Appelation FROM wine WHERE Appelation = 'Napa Valley' UNION SELECT T1.Name, T1.Grape, T1.Appelation FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape WHERE T2.Color = 'Red';
result_summary: Returns 291 rows listing the name, grape, and appellation for each wine that is either from the 'Napa Valley' appellation or is made from a red grape.

sql: SELECT Name, Winery, Year FROM wine WHERE Appelation = 'Napa Valley' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name, T1.Winery, T1.Year FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape WHERE T2.Color = 'Red';
result_summary: Returns 62 rows listing red wines from the Napa Valley appellation, showing the name, winery, and year for each qualifying wine.

sql: SELECT Name, Winery FROM wine WHERE Year = 2008 EXCEPT SELECT Name, Winery FROM wine WHERE Winery = 'Robert Biale';
result_summary: Returns 120 rows listing the name and winery for all wines from the 2008 vintage, excluding any produced by the 'Robert Biale' winery.

sql: SELECT County, isAVA, count(*) FROM appellations GROUP BY County, isAVA ORDER BY County, isAVA;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows providing a count of appellations for each county, broken down by their American Viticultural Area (AVA) status, and sorted alphabetically by county.

sql: SELECT Winery, count(DISTINCT Grape) AS GrapeCount FROM wine WHERE State = 'California' GROUP BY Winery ORDER BY GrapeCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single California winery with the highest number of unique grape varieties and the total count of those varieties.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Winery FROM wine AS T1 JOIN appellations AS T2 ON T1.Appelation = T2.Appelation WHERE T2.County LIKE '%Amador%';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and winery for wines from appellations located in any county containing 'Amador'.

sql: SELECT Name, Price, Price - (SELECT avg(Price) FROM wine) AS Price_Difference FROM wine;
result_summary: Returns 500 rows listing each wine's name and price, along with a calculated column showing the difference between its price and the overall average wine price.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Grape, T2.Name, T2.Grape FROM wine AS T1 JOIN wine AS T2 ON T1.Winery = T2.Winery WHERE T1.Grape != T2.Grape AND T1.No < T2.No;
result_summary: Returns 219 rows, each showing a pair of wines from the same winery that are made from different grapes, listing the name and grape type for both wines.

sql: SELECT Grape FROM wine WHERE Score = 93 EXCEPT SELECT Grape FROM wine WHERE Score = 91;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.Area, sum(T1.Cases) FROM wine AS T1 JOIN appellations AS T2 ON T1.Appelation = T2.Appelation WHERE T1.State = 'California' GROUP BY T2.Area;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the total number of wine cases produced in each geographic area within California.

sql: SELECT Winery, avg(Score) FROM wine GROUP BY Winery HAVING avg(Score) > (SELECT avg(Score) FROM wine);
result_summary: Returns 125 rows listing wineries whose average wine score is higher than the overall average score across all wines, showing each of these top-performing wineries and their calculated average score.

sql: SELECT T3.State, T3.Winery, T3.Score FROM wine AS T3 JOIN (SELECT State, max(Score) AS max_score FROM wine GROUP BY State) AS T4 ON T3.State = T4.State AND T3.Score = T4.max_score;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the highest-scoring wine from each state, including any ties for the top score.

sql: SELECT Name, Winery, Appelation FROM wine WHERE Grape = 'Zinfandel' EXCEPT SELECT Name, Winery, Appelation FROM wine WHERE Appelation = 'Napa Valley';
result_summary: Returns 65 rows listing the name, winery, and appelation of all Zinfandel wines while excluding any from the 'Napa Valley' appelation.

sql: SELECT T1.Appelation FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape GROUP BY T1.Appelation HAVING count(DISTINCT T2.Color) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 26 rows listing the wine appelations that are known to produce wines from grapes of more than one color (e.g., from both red and white grapes).

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Winery, T1.Price FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape WHERE T2.Color = 'Red' AND T1.State = 'California' ORDER BY T1.Price DESC LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the top two most expensive red wines from California, showing their name, winery, and price in descending order.

sql: SELECT T1.Winery, T1.Name, T1.Price FROM wine AS T1 WHERE T1.Price = (SELECT max(T2.Price) FROM wine AS T2 WHERE T2.Winery = T1.Winery);
result_summary: Returns 332 rows identifying the most expensive wine(s) for each winery, listing the winery's name, the specific wine's name, and its price.

sql: SELECT * FROM university;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each university along with its complete profile details, including location, founding year, enrollment, and primary conference.

sql: SELECT Team_Name, All_Games_Percent, ACC_Regular_Season FROM basketball_match;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing basketball teams along with their overall game win percentage and their ACC regular season record.

sql: SELECT * FROM basketball_match WHERE Team_Name = 'Duke';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete season performance statistics for the Duke basketball team, including their ACC and overall game records and percentages.

sql: SELECT School, Location, Enrollment FROM university WHERE Affiliation = 'Public';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing public universities along with their location and total student enrollment.

sql: SELECT School, Enrollment FROM university ORDER BY Enrollment DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each university and its enrollment number, ordered from the highest to the lowest enrollment.

sql: SELECT Team_Name, All_Games_Percent FROM basketball_match ORDER BY All_Games_Percent DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing basketball teams and their all-games performance percentage, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT School, Founded FROM university WHERE Founded > 1800;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of universities founded after the year 1800, along with their respective founding years.

sql: SELECT School, Location FROM university WHERE Location LIKE '%PA%';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the school name and location for universities located in Pennsylvania (PA).

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM university;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of universities.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Primary_conference FROM university;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each unique primary athletic conference that a university belongs to.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T1.Location, T2.Team_Name FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and location of universities along with the team name from an associated basketball match.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T1.Enrollment, T2.All_Games_Percent FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.Team_Name = 'North Carolina';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the school, its enrollment, and the all-games basketball percentage for the team named 'North Carolina'.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T2.Team_Name FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T1.Affiliation = 'Public';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing public universities along with team names from basketball matches they were involved in.

sql: SELECT T1.Affiliation, COUNT(T2.Team_ID) FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID GROUP BY T1.Affiliation;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each university affiliation and the total number of basketball matches played by schools with that affiliation.

sql: SELECT Primary_conference, AVG(Enrollment) FROM university GROUP BY Primary_conference;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each primary athletic conference along with the average university enrollment for that conference.

sql: SELECT School, Enrollment FROM university WHERE Enrollment > (SELECT AVG(Enrollment) FROM university);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing universities with an enrollment figure greater than the average enrollment of all universities.

sql: SELECT Team_Name, All_Games_Percent FROM basketball_match WHERE All_Games_Percent > (SELECT AVG(All_Games_Percent) FROM basketball_match);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and overall game win percentages for basketball teams whose win percentage is higher than the average across all teams.

sql: SELECT School, Nickname FROM university WHERE School_ID IN (SELECT School_ID FROM basketball_match WHERE All_Games_Percent > 0.8);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the school and team nickname for universities with a basketball program that has won over 80% of its games.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T2.Team_Name, T1.Founded FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID ORDER BY T1.Founded ASC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each university's name, their basketball team's name, and the year the university was founded, ordered from the oldest to the most recently founded institution.

sql: SELECT Affiliation, COUNT(*) FROM university GROUP BY Affiliation HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing university affiliations shared by more than one institution, along with the total count of institutions for that affiliation.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T2.Team_Name, T2.All_Games_Percent FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T1.Affiliation = 'Public' AND T2.All_Games_Percent > 0.7;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing basketball teams from public universities with a win percentage over 70%, showing the school name, team name, and their specific win percentage.

sql: SELECT T1.Primary_conference, AVG(T2.All_Games_Percent) FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID GROUP BY T1.Primary_conference;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each primary university conference and the average basketball game win percentage for the schools within that conference.

sql: SELECT School FROM university WHERE Founded < 1800 UNION SELECT School FROM university WHERE Enrollment > 19000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of universities that were either founded before the year 1800 or have an enrollment of more than 19,000 students.

sql: SELECT Affiliation, MIN(Founded) FROM university GROUP BY Affiliation;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each university affiliation along with the earliest founding year for a university within that affiliation.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T1.Nickname FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.Team_Name LIKE 'C%';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the school name and nickname for universities that have a basketball team name starting with the letter 'C'.

sql: SELECT School, Location FROM university AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM basketball_match AS T2 WHERE T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and location of each university that has participated in a basketball match.

sql: SELECT School, Location FROM university AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM basketball_match AS T2 WHERE T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and location of the university that has no recorded basketball matches.

sql: SELECT Primary_conference, AVG(Enrollment) FROM university GROUP BY Primary_conference HAVING AVG(Enrollment) > 15000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the primary athletic conferences where the average university enrollment is greater than 15,000, along with that calculated average enrollment.

sql: SELECT T1.Team_Name, T1.All_Games_Percent, T3.Avg_Win_Percent FROM basketball_match AS T1 JOIN university AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID JOIN (SELECT u.Affiliation, AVG(b.All_Games_Percent) as Avg_Win_Percent FROM university AS u JOIN basketball_match AS b ON u.School_ID = b.School_ID GROUP BY u.Affiliation) AS T3 ON T2.Affiliation = T3.Affiliation WHERE T1.All_Games_Percent > T3.Avg_Win_Percent;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the basketball team whose overall game win percentage is greater than the average for its university affiliation, listing the team's name, its win percentage, and the affiliation's average.

sql: SELECT School FROM university WHERE Affiliation = 'Public' INTERSECT SELECT T1.School FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.All_Games_Percent > 0.9;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the school names of public universities whose basketball teams have won over 90% of their games.

sql: SELECT School, Founded FROM university WHERE Affiliation = 'Public' EXCEPT SELECT School, Founded FROM university WHERE Founded < 1800;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and founding years for public universities that were established in 1800 or later.

sql: SELECT T2.School, T1.Team_Name, T1.All_Games_Percent FROM basketball_match AS T1 JOIN university AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID ORDER BY T1.All_Games_Percent DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the top basketball teams and their universities, ordered from the highest to the lowest all-games performance percentage.

sql: SELECT CASE WHEN T1.Founded < 1850 THEN 'Historic' ELSE 'Modern' END AS Era, COUNT(T2.Team_ID) FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID GROUP BY Era;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows categorizing universities by era ('Historic' for founded before 1850, 'Modern' otherwise) and provides a count of the total basketball matches played by the schools in each era.

sql: SELECT T1.Affiliation, T1.School, T1.Enrollment FROM university AS T1 WHERE T1.Enrollment = (SELECT MAX(T2.Enrollment) FROM university AS T2 WHERE T2.Affiliation = T1.Affiliation);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the university with the largest enrollment within each affiliation type, showing the affiliation, school name, and enrollment number.

sql: SELECT Team_Name, All_Games_Percent FROM basketball_match WHERE All_Games_Percent > (SELECT All_Games_Percent FROM basketball_match WHERE Team_Name = 'Clemson');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the team name and overall game win percentage for all basketball teams with a higher win percentage than Clemson.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T1.Enrollment, T2.All_Games_Percent FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T1.Enrollment > (SELECT Enrollment FROM university WHERE School = 'University of Delaware') AND T2.All_Games_Percent < (SELECT All_Games_Percent FROM basketball_match WHERE Team_Name = 'Duke');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing universities with a larger enrollment than the University of Delaware and a lower basketball win percentage than Duke's team, showing each school's name, enrollment, and win percentage.

sql: SELECT School, Primary_conference FROM university WHERE Primary_conference IN (SELECT Primary_conference FROM university GROUP BY Primary_conference HAVING AVG(Enrollment) > (SELECT AVG(Enrollment) FROM university));
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the school and its primary conference, for schools that belong to conferences with an average enrollment greater than the overall average enrollment of all universities.

sql: SELECT School FROM university WHERE Location LIKE '%, DE%' INTERSECT SELECT School FROM university WHERE Affiliation = 'Public';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the public university that is also located in Delaware.

sql: SELECT AVG(max_enroll) FROM (SELECT Affiliation, MAX(Enrollment) AS max_enroll FROM university GROUP BY Affiliation);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value: the average of the maximum enrollments found within each distinct university affiliation.

sql: SELECT School, Enrollment FROM university ORDER BY Enrollment DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
result_summary: Returns a single row identifying the university with the second-highest enrollment, listing the school's name and its total enrollment.

sql: SELECT Affiliation FROM university GROUP BY Affiliation HAVING MAX(Founded) - MIN(Founded) > 100 AND COUNT(School_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying university affiliations that contain more than one school and where the founding dates of those schools span more than a century.

sql: SELECT T1.Team_Name FROM basketball_match AS T1 JOIN university AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.Founded > (SELECT AVG(T3.Founded) FROM university AS T3 JOIN basketball_match AS T4 ON T3.School_ID = T4.School_ID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of basketball teams from universities that were founded more recently than the average founding year of all universities with a team.

sql: SELECT T2.Team_Name FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T1.Affiliation = 'Public' EXCEPT SELECT Team_Name FROM basketball_match WHERE All_Games_Percent < 0.8;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a basketball team from a public university that has a win percentage of 80% or higher.

sql: SELECT T1.Team_Name, T1.All_Games_Percent FROM basketball_match AS T1 JOIN university AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T1.All_Games_Percent > (SELECT AVG(T4.All_Games_Percent) FROM university AS T3 JOIN basketball_match AS T4 ON T3.School_ID = T4.School_ID WHERE T3.Affiliation = T2.Affiliation);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the team name and all-games winning percentage for any team whose performance is above the average for all teams within its own university affiliation.

sql: SELECT * FROM university WHERE School_ID IN (SELECT School_ID FROM university WHERE Affiliation = 'Public' INTERSECT SELECT School_ID FROM basketball_match WHERE All_Games_Percent > 0.8);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the full details for the public university that also has a basketball win percentage exceeding 80%.

sql: WITH PublicSchools AS (SELECT School_ID, School FROM university WHERE Affiliation = 'Public') SELECT T1.School, T2.Team_Name FROM PublicSchools AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing public universities and the names of teams from basketball matches they have been involved in.

sql: WITH TopTeams AS (SELECT School_ID, Team_Name, All_Games_Percent FROM basketball_match WHERE All_Games_Percent > 0.8) SELECT T1.School, T1.Location, T2.Team_Name, T2.All_Games_Percent FROM university AS T1 JOIN TopTeams AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the university, location, team name, and game win percentage for basketball teams with a win rate greater than 80%.

sql: SELECT T2.Team_Name FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM university AS T3 WHERE T3.Primary_conference = T1.Primary_conference AND T3.Enrollment > 19000);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of basketball teams whose university is in a conference that includes at least one university with an enrollment over 19,000.

sql: SELECT T1.Primary_conference, count(T2.Team_ID) FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T1.Founded < 1900 GROUP BY T1.Primary_conference HAVING count(T2.Team_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the primary conferences of universities founded before 1900, along with the total count of basketball matches associated with teams from those conferences.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T2.Team_Name FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID JOIN (SELECT AVG(All_Games_Percent) as avg_percent FROM basketball_match) AS T3 WHERE T2.All_Games_Percent > T3.avg_percent AND T1.Affiliation = 'Public';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the school and team name for a public university whose basketball team has a winning percentage greater than the overall average across all teams.
